She leaned closer, watching the sparkle in her wife’s eyes as hands landed on her hips, fingers playing with her own hem of her sweatpants. She saw the defiance and desire circle in those blues as she lifted her chin, but a smirk still played on her lips. 

“I just thought it would be best for the Dean of Faculty to maintain her decorum,” she teased, her tone laced with mock innocence.

Rio chuckled darkly, her free hand trailing down Agatha’s side, her touch light and teasing. 

“Decorum?” she repeated, her lips brushing against her wife’s jaw. “You think that’s what I’m concerned about right now?” 

Agatha shivered under the touch, her breathing quickening, but Rio watched her regain it, her smirk growing into that smirk she knew all too well. It was the one that told Rio she lost. She froze for a second, realizing what she had done. Her smile faltered as Agatha’s only grew. She played right into her wife’s trap. This was her plan all along.

She felt her body twitch, betraying her as it leaned into her wife’s touch. Hands were firm on her hips before one moved, pulling at the hem of pants before moving underneath.

“I think,” Agatha said, her voice dropping into a deep breath. “We both know what you’re concerned about right now– what you want, right now, don’t we?”

Rio couldn’t move, unable to breathe by the fire that engulfed her and she was hypnotized by the one who held the torch. Her wife’s smirk was devastating, a smirk perfected to kill, and Rio was her prey. A jolt of electricity ran through her as the hand moved below her navel, tracing the abs there and down. She steadied herself, her hands gripping tighter on her shoulders as her wife hummed, sucking in her bottom lip. 

This woman was going to be the death of her. 

Agatha leaned closer, teasing her ear with her teeth, causing her breath to hitch, “I want to make you forget all about work, all about that woman… forget everything except me.” 

She was so dead. 

With that, fingers moved underneath her boxers, slipping through her folds. She moaned, but quieted herself as her lips crashed against Agatha’s in a kiss that was anything but gentle. She tangled her fingers through her wife’s long locks, threading through the thick chestnut curls. Their glasses clicked together, causing a small laugh from both of them as Rio took them both off, throwing to the side of the couch, latching her lips back to her wife’s. She hummed into the kiss before groaning, something hot and wet prodding at her lips. She didn’t hesitate, opening her mouth for her wife’s tongue as her hips lifted into Agatha’s touch. Agatha refused to acknowledge it, her fingers swirling, touching her in every place but where she wanted the most. 

Her wife moved as Rio bit at her lip, trying to hold on to whatever dignity she had left. They hadn’t done something like this out of their bedroom– that was a total fucking lie, but normally Agatha was the one on top, riding her. Lips were hot on her neck, trailing around her as the arm slipped off her waist, pulling up on her shirt. Rio reached down, pulling herself free from it and throwing it behind her. She gasped when a hot mouth connected to the side of her breast, licking after dark marks were sucked into her skin. She didn’t care, a hand moving from her grip on her shoulder and tangling in the smaller hairs at the base of Agatha’s neck. She moved her hips again, trying to get her fingers inside her or on her clit; she didn’t care, but her wife ignored her still, reaching up to lick the fading bruise of yellow and green on her neck. 

“Agatha,” she gritted her teeth. “No marks.” 

Her wife simply hummed, moving her kisses back down to her breasts. She shuddered, her whole body arching into her touch as a hot tongue probed at her nipple through the green lace. Agatha pulled her bra down, her mouth wrapping around her. She gasped louder, holding her head there as her hips desperately sought more friction. She was growing impatient, every nerve on fire, waiting for the pleasure of water to douse them, but it never came. She could practically hear how wet she was, but her wife only teased her further, her teeth scraping at a pebbled nipple. 

“You should put your piercings back in, you’re so much more fun to tease when you have them in.” 

Rio glared at her, “You know why I took them out.”

Months ago, Agatha’s hair had tangled in her piercing for the last time. She remembered being jolted awake from the pain when her wife moved in her sleep. It wasn’t the first time, but it was certainly the last as she swore her hair almost ripped off her nipple. 

Her wife shrugged, “I’ll braid my hair back.”

Agatha leaned down again, capturing her nipple back in her mouth, releasing another gasp from her. 

“No, you–” fingers plunged into her with no warning “fuck! Agatha!”

Rio felt her hips move in their own accordance, thrusting on her wife as they filled her. She squeezed around them, her head foggy as her neck was bared. She moaned, her wife moving with her thrusts, scraping against her walls before they curled, hitting that one spot that made her moans echo off the walls of the house. Her wife hummed her content, pulling away from her breast and harsh fingers found her chin. She was tugged down, forced to look into those bewitching blues as she panted. 

“You feel so good, baby. You’re all nice and wet, squeezing around my fingers,” Agatha cooed at her. 

Rio felt her eyes widen, her movements picking up.

“Are you going to come so soon, sweetheart? Just from the sound of my voice?” 

She clamped her mouth shut, gritting out, “Fuck off.” 

Agatha laughed at her, shifting slightly and angling her wrist where her palm rubbed against her clit. Rio moaned louder, a current coiling in her lower belly. Her wife smirked, so Rio did the only thing she could do. She leaned forward, latching her lips in a fierce kiss, all tongue and teeth clashing as they both moaned. It was too much for Rio, the feeling of her wife inside her, the taste of her in her mouth, their tongues dancing a waltz, her nipple still free from her bra, dripping in saliva. 

Rio felt the current spread through her body, warmth taking hold as euphoria clouded her vision. She cursed as she came quickly, her wife riding her through it. Her forehead fell to rest against her shoulder as she came down, but Agatha wasn’t stopping. She gasped again, another finger stretching her, her wife guiding her faster. She buried her face into her neck, moaning and gasping before sitting up straight. She wouldn’t let her wife fully take control. She moved her hips, riding her wife’s fingers as the blue once more held her in place. 

“Such a good girl.” 

That’s my line, Rio thought bitterly, but then her mind was back to mush as fingers curled again, her wife forcing her to ride faster. 

Her body was shaking, each deep thrust met with the perfect curl inside her. The liquid sound of fucking harmonized with her moans and pants as a single line of sweat dripped down the side of her face. The pleasure was building, the current now more raw, visceral. It took up every part of her body. She moaned louder, closing her eyes but her wife slowed down, beckoning her to open them. 

Rio forgot about the world. Nothing else mattered, but the woman under her. Nothing was better than this, her wife’s fingers knuckle deep, her palm against her swollen clit– her wife fucking her senseless on their couch, in their home, and with her love. 

Rio came again, filling herself squeeze tighter, but her thrusting never stopped. Sweat was covering her, her moans louder as her wife guided her. It was too much, incredibly too much but her third orgasm was already building. She was desperate, rapid Spanish falling from her lips that didn’t even make sense. 

“Awe, you’re doing so well. Can you take one more?” 

She nodded her head frantically, staring deep into her wife’s eyes. 

“Let me hear you.” 

Rio creased her eyebrows. This was payback from Monday, and especially teasing her about the whining all weak. 

“ ¡Mierda! Sí!”

Agatha smirked at her, their lips meeting angrily. Rio swallowed her whines, not caring whose spit was going with it. Everything was perfect. She couldn’t stop even if she wanted to as her stomach clenched, and she tightened on her wife’s fingers that curled and pumped into her. The thrusts were fast, furious, hitting everything and more inside Rio. The knot in her stomach curled tighter, threatening to explode at any moment. She shook over her wife, barely able to keep up with her thrusts that didn’t waver. Her head fell to her shoulder again, her nails digging into her arms as she swore. She knew what was coming as her hand found the back of the couch, trying to direct some of the unrelenting pleasure there. 

“¡Mierda! ¡Mierda! Mi amor, if you don’t slow down–”

Agatha chuckled at her, “Don’t slow, you’ll what?” 

Rio went to pull away, knowing neither really wanted to clean up the mess but her wife simply wrapped her arm around her waist, urging her faster. She tried holding on, she really did, but white flashed through her vision, her body crumbling and falling into her wife. She cried out into her shoulder, muffling the sound as her body spasmed and stilled. She panted heavily, feeling as her wife slowly removed her hand. She shivered, feeling the soaked hand hold against her waist, keeping her up as the other moved with soothing circles on her back, lips pressing against the side of her head. 

Rio didn’t move. Her body was nothing but weight as her mind blanked. She barely registered the sweet nothings from her wife she held her. She took in the scent of lavender and something only that could be described as Agatha, allowing the familiarity of it to steady her breathing. Her hands released their death hold on the couch, allowing her to lazily hold onto her wife as her knees quivered. 

When the strength finally came back to her, Rio sat up, smirking down at her wife who raised her eyebrows as if to say, Really? Three orgasms and you squirting in your pants didn’t take it all out of you?

Rio leaned down, placing a kiss on her lips, “Shower with me?”

Agatha nodded, removing her hands and standing with her. Rio ignored the way her pants stuck to her, placing another kiss on her wife’s lips before playfully licking her cheek, “I’ll grab the strap, just promise not to throw it at me, okay?” 

 

Agatha would definitely throw it at her for that, but Rio didn’t care, because some deity listened to her and her wife’s mood swings were definitely on her side.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

Chapter 7: The Meeting and the Rings

Chapter Summary

“Somebody did!” Eva chuckled. “Okay, just be delusional with me for ten minutes. Dr. Vidal and Dr. Harkness, both women of power and very secretive about their personal life. Dr. Vidal, her boss, doesn’t want to make a scene or have her authority be undermined and they agree to keep their marriage a secret. Maximoff comes along and changes the game. Come on, it’s kinda cute too. Imagine Vidal going home with Harkness and they then come here and act like strangers.”
William rolled his eyes, “You are desperate for any love story.”
“Guilty,” Eva chirped.

 

OR:

The chapter is a summary of itself

Chapter Notes

okay one thing I would like to clear up: Nicky will be fine. this fic isn't sad, i can't do anymore sad, and i need my lesbians happy. but there will possibly be up and downs between Agatha and Rio, maybe.... I haven't decided, but this fic is relatively happy

Also, as you have noticed, this work has turned into a series, which I am already getting so excited about to write.

THANK YOU ALL FOR YOUR COMMENTS AND KUDOS 💚💜

Love,

rioallalong_719

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Wanda

 

Wanda sat back in her office, swallowing hard. The message glared at her, mocking her and replaying the memories of the weekend. She bit her lip hard as she couldn’t pull away. She had hoped the warning would have been an email, not this. Anything but this. Wanda would have done anything else– sit in front HR for hours, even the head of HR: Dr. Tony Stark, trainings, hell even sit in front of Agatha and apologize profusely– anything but this:

 

Subject : Mandatory Meeting - Professional Conduct 

CC : Stark, Tony; Davis, Sharon

 

Dear Dr. Maximoff, 

 

I am writing to formally schedule a meeting with you to address recent reports regarding professional misconduct. This meeting is mandatory and will take place today at 12:00pm in my office with an HR assistant. 

 

This meeting is due to an incident reported on and off-campus where the misconduct took place involving a colleague. Please be prepared to address your actions and understand that this conversation will remain professional, but formal documentation has been made and may be made further in lieu of the discussion. 

 

Your cooperation is appreciated. 

 

Rio Vidal, PhD (she/her)

Dean of Faculty 

Chairperson, Department of Biological Sciences 

Westview University



Fuck. 

She was so utterly fucked. 

Wanda sat back, the only real memory she had was the eyes from the woman, full of all the venom and rancor possible before she turned away walking into the darkness. She blinked a few times, rubbing her temples as her mind raced. It wasn’t supposed to go this far, and part of her hated her friends for not stopping her sooner. She blinked again, her cheeks flushing as she remembered placing her hand on Agatha’s shoulder and her pushing her hand off. She froze hearing the ‘no’ echo in her head and completely ignoring it. 

Wanda stood, needing to get out of there. She couldn’t face either of them today, and Dean Vidal especially. Worst, was that the dean was there, the fucking dean was at the bar. Wanda felt her fists clench as she realized she must have seen everything, but nothing burned hotter than jealousy and betrayal she felt. Agatha didn’t seem to have a friend in any of the departments, yet somehow it was Vidal? It didn’t even make sense. They had to have a clash. Their personalities couldn’t possibly have gotten along. And worst, how could Agatha pick that scary fucking bitch over her? 

She took a deep breath, running her hand through her hair. Agatha didn’t owe her anything, if anything Wanda needed to be on her knees apologizing, and she could do that right now. If Agatha and Vidal were friends, then she would just apologize, Agatha would forgive her or at least get Vidal not to do any more documentation. It was a drunken mistake. Mistakes happen. Agatha had to understand that, and then they could start over. Wanda could just be friends, and also get an insight on this Vidal woman. She smirked at that, her nerves settling as she thought of it. It had to be a cross something in the policies for Vidal to go out with her subordinates, right? Bosses don’t go out for informal gatherings like that. It would be too easy to undermine her position. She wasn’t the only one who made a mistake. She couldn’t have been. Vidal was right there with her. 

Upon stepping out of her office, she froze hearing the voices from the office lounge down the hall. There was light laughter, moving towards her, but Wanda recognized it. She stepped back, closing the door but pressing against it to listen. 

“Relax, Rio,” Agatha said, her voice calmer than she had ever heard.

There was a sweet note to it, something affectionate where Wanda wasn’t sure she could place or wanted to admit. 

“We will figure it out Wednesday afternoon. I am sure the Coven will be excited, but I am sure Alice is going to throttle you.”

A different sound echoed in the hall. It was oddly warm, and it sent a chill to her bones. The person couldn’t be– there was no way that sound came from her. Wanda felt her jaw drop, listening to the warmth and relaxation in her voice, her eyes widening. It had to be fake, honeyed. There was no way that woman could be so… alive, warm, breathing– Vidal was mean, cold, her eyes dead. This had to be an imposter. 

“Oh yes, I assume the moment she gets out of her lectures she is going to break the door down,” she hummed her own laughter. “You realize she is going to be as overprotective as me, right?”

Agatha chuckled lightly as keys jiggled into the lock, “I highly doubt that, knowing you, Dr. Vidal.” 

There was a pause and a light laugh from Agatha, “Oh, I think we both know you’ll be overprotective mom.”
 Rio scoffed, “Yeah, right, and I thought I told you, my little one will call me mami.”

The door opened and shut at that, leaving Wanda shocked. 

Overprotective mom… mami? ‘my little one’, the thoughts ran in circles as it dawned on her. 

Rio is pregnant?

 

***

 

Agatha 

 

“ Your little one?” Agatha teased, chuckling softly as she set her things down on her desk. 

Her wife smirked, folding her arms with confidence, “How much do you want to bet they’re going to look just like me?” 

Agatha rolled her eyes as she shrugged off her coat, sighing, “Did you forget I’m the one who’s pregnant?” 

“Did you forget they have both of our DNA?” Rio shot back, stepping closer. “And science doesn’t lie. Brown eyes trump blue. Dominant genes, my love.” 

Her arms circled Agatha’s waist, her smirk playful. Agatha felt a familiar warmth stir at her wife’s touch, but she didn’t let it show, keeping her scowl firmly in place. 

“Oh, great. Like I need two sets of those brown eyes staring at me, plotting together when you both want something.” 

The image immediately popped into her mind– Rio with one of her ridiculous schemes, and beside her, their child, wearing the same big brown puppy-dog eyes and a perfectly coordinated pout. Agatha could already feel her resolve crumbling under the imagined onslaught.

“It won’t be fair,” she added, shaking her head. “I carry them for nine months, and if they come out looking like you? Where’s the justice in that?” 

Rio’s smirk deepened as one hand slid to rest lightly on Agatha’s stomach. 

“Well, maybe our second can look like you,” she murmured, her tone softening. “Blue eyes, gorgeous hair, sharp wit– but for now, our son will be perfect.” 

Agatha’s eyebrows shot up. “Our second ? We haven’t even had our first, and you’re already talking of a second? Need I remind you who is going to be carrying that second child?”

Rio only smirked as her reply. 

“ And now you’re sure I’m carrying a boy? What are you, the pregnancy whisperer?” 

Rio shrugged, grinning, “Not exactly, but your hair is shinier. And don’t pretend I don’t notice these things.” 

“You’re obsessed with my hair,” Agatha accused, though her lips twitched into a half-smile.

“Completely,” Rio admitted, her hand rubbing gentle circles over Agatha’s stomach. “So you should trust me when I say I’m right. It’s a boy, and he’s going to have all my charm and my eyes.” 

Agatha tilted her head, pulling Rio closer by the collar of her shirt, “I can’t wait to wipe that smug grin off your face when I give birth to a girl with my blue eyes and my hair.” 

Rio chuckled, leaning in to brush their lips together. 

“Oh, really? Want to make it interesting?” 

Agatha raised an eyebrow, “A wager?” 

“Two wagers,” Rio corrected. “If it’s a boy, I get my trip to Yellowstone with you. If they have my features, you’re on diaper duty for a week straight.” 

Agatha groaned dramatically, rolling her eyes, “Fine. If it’s a girl, you’re taking me to England: Oxford and Cambridge. And if they have my features, I get to pick the Yule tree for the next three years.” 

Rio’s jaw dropped, her expression incredulous, “You’re joking. Agatha, picking the Yule tree is my thing.” 

Agatha smirked, tapping a finger against Rio’s chest, “So? Do we have a deal, or are you backing out because you’re scared you’ll lose?” 

Rio’s eyes narrowed, Agatha practically watching her wife’s competitive streak coming to life.

“Oh, we have a deal,” she said, her voice low with determination. 

“Good,” Agatha said, smirking as if she already won. “Because I’m going to win.” 

Rio’s hand came up, her fingers combing through her hair, “An I am going to be the one that wipes that smirk off your face, just as I did this weekend when I fucked–”

“Rio,” Agatha warned, trying to keep the heat out of her cheeks.

Her wife tilted her head, her smirk softening into something more tender, and Agatha’s resolve crumbled instantly. Those big brown eyes– warm, teasing, and full of love– held her captive. With a sigh of surrender, she leaned up, pressing her lips to Rio’s. Rio’s hands slid back to Agatha’s waist, steady but insistent, guiding her until the edge of the desk pressed against her thighs. As she sat, their lips moved in perfect harmony, a hum of contentment escaping her when one of Rio’s hands trailed upward, squeezing her breast lightly. Agatha’s own hand found its way to Rio’s hip, sliding over black nylon and gripping with purpose. She felt the heat of her wife’s body even through the fabric, grounding her in the moment. 

They broke apart briefly, and Agatha found herself staring into those mesmerizing eyes again. They were everything– comfort, fire, love. She didn’t let herself linger for long, though, leaning forward with more urgency this time. Her lips captured Rio’s again, her tongue brushing against her wife’s in a plea that was immediately answered. Their tongues danced, the kiss deepening as Agatha drank her in, the taste of her, the feel of her, the electric heat that spread through her entire body. She tugged Rio closer, arms sliding up to loop around her neck, pulling her in as though proximity alone could satisfy the yearning she felt. 

A loud knock shattered the moment, and both women sprang apart like guilty teenagers caught in the act. Rio’s eyes widened as she quickly stepped back, straightening her shirt with swift, practiced movements. Agatha stood as tall as she could manage, trying to calm the fiery blush on her cheeks even though every nerve in her body screamed to pull her wife back into her arms. Sliding into her chair, she adjusted her papers and cleared her throat, regaining her composure. Rio, with a perfectly casual air, dropped into the chair across from her, fixing her coat as though nothing had happened. 

“Come in,” Agatha called out, her voice calm and professional despite the storm raging inside her. 

The door opened, and to Agatha’s mixed relief and irritation, Mrs. Hart stepped into the room, her smile as cheerful and oblivious as ever. 

“Dr. Harkness, Dean Vidal– I thought I’d find you here,” Mrs. Hart chirped. 

“Mrs. Hart,” Agatha greeted, keeping her expression neutral, though she caught the scowl that flashed across her wife’s face. 

“It’s Sharon, sweetie, or Mrs. Davis now– you know, ever since my husband passed– ” 

Before she could continue, Rio shot to her feet, clapping her hands together with forced enthusiasm. The sharp faux-smile on her face nearly sent Agatha into a fit of laughter, and she had to lower her head to hide her grin. She could feel Rio’s glare even as she turned back to Mrs. Hart. 

“Mrs. Davis,” Rio interrupted smoothly, her tone polite but firm, “let’s head to my office. There’s so much we need to cover before the meeting.” 

Agatha stood as well, a polite smile gracing her lips as whatever-her-name-was gave a little wave and exited the office with Rio following close behind. Just before stepping out, Rio turned back, her hand brushing over Agatha’s desk as if it were a casual gesture. But her eyes locked on Agatha’s, her lips quirking into a mischievous smile. 

“Lunch, Mami Vidal?” Agatha teased in a hushed whisper, unable to resist. 

Rio’s true smile broke through, her eyes sparkling in that way that always made Agatha’s chest tighten with warmth. Without hesitation, she leaned down, brushing her lips softly against Agatha’s in a fleeting but sweet kiss. Agatha couldn’t stop herself from reaching up, her hand cupping Rio’s cheek as she savored the moment, her heart thrumming. 

“Twelve-thirty, Mama Harkness. My office,” Rio whispered, her grin widening as she straightened. She began to step away but, as if on impulse, swung back around and stole another kiss– brief, playful, and completely intoxicating. 

“ Te veo, mi amor ,” she murmured, her voice low and affectionate before finally slipping out of the room, leaving Agatha feeling warm and slightly dazed.

 

***

 

Rio 

 

Rio fell into step beside Mrs. Davis, her long strides slowing to match the older woman's. Mrs. Davis’ gaze drifted over the school gardens as they walked, her expression peaceful. Rio followed her line of sight, noting the azaleas still holding their own against the cold. The vibrant blooms caught her attention for a moment, but Mrs. Davis’ soft voice drew her focus back. 

“How is your wife, Dean Vidal?” Mrs. Davis asked, her tone gentle. 

Rio tilted her head slightly, considering her answer. 

“She’s better, though still upset about what happened this weekend.” She paused before adding carefully, “If I may ask, why did Dr. Stark give me the go-ahead to conduct this meeting? Are you sure it’s appropriate?” 

Mrs. Davis’ smile widened, the corners of her eyes crinkling.. 

“You know how Tony and I feel about your work, Dean Vidal. You’re an exceptional professor, and you have a knack for maintaining order. Tony trusts that you’re more than capable of handling something like this.” 

Rio nodded slowly but couldn’t hide the flicker of doubt in her expression. Her fingers absentmindedly toyed with the large amethyst on her ring finger– a habit Agatha often teased her about. 

“It wouldn’t be a problem,” Rio replied, her tone even. “But this matter involves my wife, Mrs. Davis. As I mentioned in my email, I would’ve preferred someone without a personal connection to handle it.” 

Mrs. Davis’ smile didn’t falter, but her expression softened with understanding. She stopped walking, turning to fully face Rio. 

“That’s exactly why we trust you. You’re fair, Dean Vidal. You’ll see the situation for what it is and do what’s right, even if it’s difficult.” 

Rio sighed quietly, looking away to the azaleas again, “It’s not about what’s right, Mrs. Davis. It’s about ensuring impartiality and avoiding complications.” 

Mrs. Davis chuckled lightly, placing a reassuring hand on Rio’s arm, “Sometimes, impartiality isn’t what’s needed. Sometimes, it’s someone who cares.” 

Rio turned back to Mrs. Davis, meeting her gaze. The sincerity in her words stirred something in her, but it didn’t entirely ease the tension in her shoulders. She offered a polite nod before they continued walking toward the science building. She couldn’t trust herself to bring into words how much she did, in fact, want to rip that woman’s head off. In some other life, Rio imagined she was a killer– a killer for Agatha Harkness. If it were possible…and legal, she knew she would do anything for her wife. 

If her wife said to walk, she would run. If she said jump, she would dive. If Agatha said to put a gun to someone’s head, she would shove it into their mouths and pull the trigger without a second thought. If someone laid a hand on Agatha– Rio shook out of her thoughts, knowing this was the exact reason why she wasn’t sure she could handle it. She would never admit aloud, but she was temperamental when it came to protecting her wife, and the thought of what her own voice sounded like that night– the way her best friend even flinched from her– still pranced through her memory. 

When they made it to her office, the woman smiled at her flowers, a hand brushing over the black petals before sitting down. Rio gathered her stuff, pulling out the report and passing it over to the HR woman before starting her computer. She couldn’t stop the calm smile, staring at her back screen. It was her and her wife in Salem, when they spent a weekend at a small cottage in the woods together. She had taken a picture of them cuddling in a hammock, Agatha peacefully asleep against her chest with a smile etched into her features. Agatha, at first, didn’t want to go, but Rio, like always, convinced her. 

At that moment, Rio saw it. A little boy curled against them on top of Rio’s chest, his brown eyes just as soft, and his hair thick, but ultimately Agatha’s hair color: golden chestnut when the sun hit it just right. She could picture him running around the backyard, Rio lifting him and swinging around as her wife watched from the porch, a bright smile on her face, before their boy convinced her wife to come out and join them in making a large leaf pile. At night, she wondered if he would have Agatha’s hunger for knowledge, maybe they could sit outside, and Rio would tell them about the stars, like she told Agatha on their dates long ago. 

She smiled, something flickering through her mind. They hadn’t had a true night in a couple weeks, and Rio was determined to change that. Wednesday night, after kicking her friends out of their house, she would take Agatha to see the stars again: just like their first official date over fifteen years ago. 

Just as she took a sip of her tea, Mrs. Davis spoke, “So, when are you and your wife going to have children?” 

Rio’s composure cracked at the unexpected question, her hand tightening around her mug as she hastily set it down. She coughed, tea catching in her throat as her mind scrambled for an appropriate response. The flush creeping up her neck had nothing to do with the heat of her drink. 

“Excuse me?” Rio managed, her voice slightly hoarse as she reached for a tissue to dab at her lips. 

Mrs. Davis tilted her head, her knowing smile only deepening. 

“Children, Dean Vidal. You and your wife. When are the two of you planning on having little ones? You know, someone to inherit that fiery determination of yours or her wit?” 

Rio blinked, forcing herself to maintain a professional facade. Her fingers began to toy with the amethyst ring on her finger as her eyes darted between the woman’s eyes, wondering if she somehow infiltrated her thoughts. 

“That’s… a very personal question, Mrs. Davis,” Rio said carefully, her tone neutral but polite. “And I’d rather not speculate on such matters during office hours.” 

Mrs. Davis chuckled softly, clearly undeterred, “Oh, come now, Rio– may I call you Rio? You’re a professor, but you’re also human. Surely, the thought of children has crossed your mind?” 

The image of Agatha’s slightly curved belly flashed in Rio’s mind unbidden, and her heart ached with the effort it took to keep her expression steady. It drifted to their wagers they place and how already she caught her wife looking for baby stuff online. 

They would only tell their close friends, and perhaps Rio’s sisters: Maria and Oriana. Maria… Rio liked less than Oriana. They were the beloved twins, each of their names derived from the sun. Their mother wanted to name them Infinito and Eternity, but papi shut that down– from the stories she heard about her parents. She flinched at the idea of telling them and worse, her abuelita, who would insist Rio and Agatha go straight to Puerto Rico so she could perform the birth– apparently having delivered Rio and her sisters. 

So no, Rio was not actually keen on sharing the news, even if every fiber of her being wanted to fly into the sky and celebrate like fucking Elphaba on her broom with a ridiculously long cape. 

“We’re taking things one step at a time,” Rio finally said, her voice steady but firm. “Agatha and I are both dedicated to our work. It’s a balancing act, and right now, our focus is on each other and our careers.” 

Mrs. Davis studied her for a moment, her smile softening, “You two make such a lovely couple. When the time comes, I’m sure you’ll be wonderful parents. You have so much love to give.” 

Rio swallowed hard, her fingers tightening briefly around the edge of her desk. The words struck deeper than she cared to admit, evoking memories of Agatha’s hand resting over her stomach, of their whispered conversations late at night about the life they were creating together. 

She forced a smile, nodding politely, “Thank you, Mrs. Davis. That means a great deal.” 

Mrs. Davis returned to the papers Rio had handed her, leaving Rio to gather her thoughts. Her gaze drifted to her computer screen, to the photo of her and Agatha in Salem. Her thumb brushed over the edge of her ring as her mind wandered, back to the vision of a little boy that would wrap Agatha and Rio around his little finger. 



 

Wanda 

 

Wanda hesitated outside the door to Dean Vidal’s office, her hand hovering over the handle. Her heart was pounding, not just from the looming confrontation, but from what she had overheard in the hallway earlier. 

We both know you’ll be the overprotective mom … Agatha’s voice echoed, the warmth and tenderness unmistakeable.  

…my little one will call me mami, the unfamiliar tenderness was delivered back. 

Rio Vidal was pregnant. 

The words had been spinning in Wanda’s mind ever since. It didn’t make sense– Vidal didn’t seem like the maternal type. She was cold, sharp, and unapproachable. And yet, the way she had spoken to Agatha in the hallway had been so… sweet. Affectionate, even. It was jarring, like seeing a completely different person. And Agatha– what the hell was that about? Wanda’s stomach twisted with jealousy. Agatha had been calm, even warm, in her response. The two of them had sounded so close, their laughter echoing softly down the hall. It didn’t fit. It didn’t make any sense for women like them, and it wasn’t fair.

My little one…  

Wanda shook her head, trying to push the thought away. It didn’t matter. What mattered was salvaging her job and her reputation. Vidal being pregnant had nothing to do with her. She stared at the door, wondering if Vidal’s reaction would be because of her hormones, and how bad it would be… would it even be fair? It was clear now that Agatha and Vidal were friends, best friends dare she say. Best friends were protective, but this? What she saw the previous night and the way the look in the woman’s eyes gave her nightmares… were best friends really that protective? It had to be a mix of the pregnancy and perhaps, because the woman herself seemed to have an emotional range the size of teaspoon… and that was being nice. 

She took a deep breath and opened the door. The moment she stepped into the office, she felt the air shift. It was cold, biting at her skin and she felt each individual bump rise on her arms. Everything in her screamed at her turn back, simply go home. She could pretend she was sick, but it was too late. She had sealed her fate. 

Vidal sat behind her desk, the epitome of power and control. Her dark eyes locked on Wanda the second she entered, and Wanda had to resist the urge to shrink under her gaze. She almost froze, but felt the quietest bit of relief seeing an older woman sitting across from Vidal.

She wouldn’t be left alone to the jaws of death. 

“Dr. Maximoff,” Vidal’s voice was low and clipped, a warning in itself. 

Wanda forced herself to step forward, her eyes darting around the room. The office was immaculate, every detail carefully curated. As she expected it was dark, minus the green couch to the side and a violet blanket draped over the back. Plants littered the room as well, vines from the ceiling, a bonsai tree sitting pristine on a dark mahogany cadenza. Behind Vidal on her cabinets hung dried roses and beside her computer monitor was a green glass vase holding a fresh bouquet of black roses– a gift from someone, perhaps whoever Vidal’s lover was. 

Then, her gaze to Vidal’s hands, where they rested on the desk, and her heart skipped a beat. The ring on her left hand was immaculate– an amethyst-cut heart that caught the light, flanked by smaller emeralds and what looked like diamonds. It was stunning, intricate, and undoubtedly a wedding ring. So, scratch that– whoever Vidal’s husband was gave her the black roses to match the darkness spilling from her. 

“Sit.” 

Vidal’s sharp command snapped Wanda out of her thoughts. She sat down quickly, her cheeks flushing as she realized she had been staring. The tension in the room was unbearable as Vidal’s piercing gaze pinned her to the chair. Wanda fidgeted, glancing again at the ring, and then quickly looking away. She couldn’t stop the questions flooding her mind, but now was not the time to ask.

 The room was suffocating and the silence that stretched was beyond uncomfortable. Vidal seemed to let the silence stretch until she fidgeted and it was then, she met Vidal’s dark eyes, sharp as a katana, with an intensity that made her feel like she was in a FBI’s interrogation room where Vidal was the bad cop, and the woman next to her, would let her partner go without stepping in. She had faced tough situations before, but this… this was different. She felt small, just by the mere presence of the woman as she couldn’t breathe. Her gaze flicked to Mrs. Davis, but her calm demeanor did nothing to soften the dark, oppressive weight of the Dean’s presence. 

Vidal finally spoke, her voice low and seething, “Dr. Maximoff, do you have any idea how close you are to losing your position here?”

Wanda opened her mouth, but no words came out. She swallowed hard, trying to form a response, but the Dean didn’t give her the chance. 

“I don’t think you do,” Vidal snapped, her tone cutting like ice. “Because if you did, you wouldn’t be sitting there looking like you just tripped and fell into this situation. This–”  she gestured sharply between them, “–isn't some accident . This is the direct result of your actions.”

Wanda shifted, her hands clenched tightly in her lap and her eyes blinking rapidly “I didn’t mean to–” 

“Stop,” Vidal’s voice rose, cold fury radiating from every word. “Do not insult my intelligence by pretending you didn’t mean to do what you did. You meant to corner her after that lecture. You meant to ignore her discomfort. You meant to approach her in that bar, and you sure as hell meant to put your hands on her when she made it clear she didn’t want your attention.”

Wanda flinched at the venom in Vidal’s words. 

“I was drunk. I wasn’t thinking clearly,” she tried weakly, her voice barely above a whisper. 

Vidal stood abruptly, slamming her hands on the desk so hard that the vase started to tip. She caught it, settling as she ran her hand back through her hair as she darkly. Wanda recoiled, her heart racing. The laugh from her was rancorous and somehow that laugh made the room temperature drop. 

“ You are an adult, not some college student with too much to drink! And not thinking clearly?” Vidal repeated, her voice sharp and mocking. “Is that what you’re going to tell me? That you weren’t thinking clearly when you decided to harass someone you work with? When you embarrassed them in front of students? When you humiliated them in a public space? Is that what you’re going with, Dr. Maximoff? Because let me tell you right now, it’s a pathetic excuse.” 

Mrs. Davis stirred, her expression cautious, but she didn’t step in. Vidal continued, her voice rising further.

“You don’t belong in this profession if you think it’s acceptable to treat your colleagues like that. You don’t belong in this university if you can’t respect basic boundaries. And if it were up to me, you wouldn’t be sitting in that chair right now and I would make sure you couldn’t even get a job within this state!” 

Wanda felt the blood drain from her face. She opened her mouth to defend herself, but the woman’s glare stopped her cold.

“Don’t. Speak.” Rio’s words were clipped, her tone a warning. “You don’t get to explain yourself to me. You don’t get to justify what you did. Because there is no justification for your behavior.” 

She leaned forward, her voice dropping to a dangerous whisper, “If you so much as look at her the wrong way again, you’ll wish this meeting was the worst thing that ever happened to you. Do you understand me?” 

Wanda nodded frantically, her voice caught in her throat. Mrs. Davis rose, her hand coming up to stop the Dean.

“Dean Vidal, I think that’s enough for now. Let me take it from here.” 

Vidal didn’t move, her eyes locked on Wanda, her breathing slow but heavy with restrained anger. Finally, she stepped back, her jaw tight as she turned her attention to Mrs. Davis. 

“Fine,” Vidal said tersely, her voice still simmering with rage. “But don’t let her mistake your kindness for leniency, Mrs. Davis. If it were my decision, this wouldn’t even be a discussion.” 

Mrs. Davis nodded, gently guiding Wanda to stand, “Dr. Maximoff, let’s go over the next steps outside.”

Wanda stumbled to her feet, her legs weak and shaky as she followed Mrs. Davis out of the office. She didn’t dare look back at Vidal, but the searing intensity of her words echoed in her mind, leaving her shaken to her core.



 

Students

 

William and Ireland exchanged glances as the doors slammed behind the professor. Her footsteps were loud, her usually stoic face now held her eyebrows pinched together. The class fell silent, feeling the frustration and anger radiate from the woman. She went on the computer, pulling up the files as her bag slammed onto the ground, echoing into the silence. 

“¡Mierda! Esto es tan jodidamente estúpido,” she muttered under her breath, causing some of the other students to shift uncomfortably. 

The whispers flew across campus about Dr. Maximoff. A student overheard an HR person talking to her outside Dr. Vidal’s office, hearing something about a warning being issued and for now, to stay as far as possible away from Dr. Harkness. William couldn’t believe what he was hearing. He knew the woman was flirting with the notorious strict professor, but he couldn’t fathom the extent of it, and worse, it seemed to have pissed off the Dean even more. He cringed, glancing at Ireland. 

“Do you think Dr. Harkness is going to be worse?” he muttered, but even then it felt too loud. 

Ireland simply took out a paper, writing down, You think it’s true then? Maximoff went too far?

Another note was passed from Ryan, I heard she harassed Harkness at a bar. 

Eva nodded, at that adding to her own paper, I heard that too. I also heard Vidal was there too. 

Ireland, tilted her head, writing: I told you they were friends. 

Ryan rolled his eyes, but quickly swiped the paper away when they heard the heel clicks behind them. The group turned to their professor, slamming packets on the table, her face now masked with her usual stoicism, but it didn’t hide the anger.

“You have the two full hours for the exam. Phones away. Do not start until I say so.” 

 

The group finished just as the two hours were up. It was hard, harder than any of them had prepared for and each one of them felt themselves shake as they approached the professor who was scrolling separately on her computer. When she noticed them approaching, she exited out of whatever it was, crossing her legs as her fingers played with something on her hand.

“Time’s up!” her voice rang over the silence, forcing the students to line up behind them. 

William had his gaze locked to her finger though, finding a giant amethyst on her ring finger, an unmistakable wedding band. He was right. Dr. Vidal was married, but to whom was the next question. He handed in his test, running out with his friends as they all looked at each other, seeming to have noticed the same thing. 

“That stone was huge,” Ryan said when they exited the building. 

“Oh my God, yeah, but why purple?” Ireland replied, fixing her bag on her shoulder. 

The boys stared at her, “What do you mean?” 

Ireland shook her shoulders, “I wouldn’t have guessed her to wear a color other than green or black. Purple is more…” her voice trailed off, looking up at Poets’ Haven. 

William groaned, “Don’t even go there– you guys are crazy if you really think that!”

Eva elbowed him lightly, “Oh come on, purple– the purple witch of Poets’ Haven?”

“That’s a fucking stretch,” Ryan agreed. 

William nodded, “Be so for real, right now. Dr. Harkness doesn’t even have a ring and who would be willing to marry either of them.” 

“Somebody did!” Eva chuckled. “Okay, just be delusional with me for ten minutes. Dr. Vidal and Dr. Harkness, both women of power and very secretive about their personal life. Dr. Vidal, her boss, doesn’t want to make a scene or have her authority be undermined and they agree to keep their marriage a secret. Maximoff comes along and changes the game. Come on, it’s kinda cute too. Imagine Vidal going home with Harkness and they then come here and act like strangers.” 

William rolled his eyes, “You are desperate for any love story.” 

“Guilty,” Eva chirped. 

Ireland stopped them, “Didn’t you mention Dr. Vidal graduated from Harvard, William?” 

He nodded, his eyebrows creased. 

“Where did Harkness study?” Ryan added, seeming to follow Ireland’s train of thought. 

William groaned, as they entered the building, “You guys are obsessed. Harkness doesn’t even have a ring. So, I am stopping your delusions in your tracks.” 

The group gathered into the classroom, finding most of the students already filling the seats. Looking at each other, they collectively sighed. The only seats left open were in the front. They sat down together as silence filled the room. It was the last class of the day and William couldn’t wait to go home and sleep. It had already been too long of a day and he was pretty sure Vidal’s test had destroyed him. He looked up the moment the professor walked in. She was in her signature purple, deep purple dress hung around her loosely, the sleeves rolled up, and a giant amethyst necklace wrapped around her neck. It was nothing new. He was pretty sure she wore it every day. But the thing that caught his attention– and he wasn’t the only one who noticed as other students’ jaws dropped, all of their eyes trained as Dr. Harkness’ fingers. On her left hand, sparkling faintly in the light was a giant emerald ring. Unlike Vidal’s, the band was gold, but it was unmistakable and this close, he could tell it cut into a heart. Black stones and more diamonds surrounded it, fading into the band itself. 

Dr. Harkness was married with a ring that was green…

The group all turned to look at each as William couldn’t seem to pick up his jaw. Eva kicked him from the side, Ryan slapping him on the arm from behind. He knew what they wanted him to ask. It was to everyone’s surprise that Dr. Harkness was almost kinder with him, as if he were the favorite. 

He cleared his throat, “Dr. Harkness?” 

The woman looked up, her icy gaze boring into him and freezing him on the spot. She didn’t bother to mask her irritation, and worse, she looked just as angry as Dr. Vidal. 

He swallowed hard, “Is the ring new?” 

“No,” she deadpanned, turning her gaze back to her notes. 

This time Ireland nudged him, “Oh, so how long have you been married?” 

The gaze was worse than Vidal’s. It wasn’t the stoic harsh gaze she had when she was annoyed, no this was worse. Her eyes glinted, her upper lip drawing into a sneer as she pinned him into his seat with the look. 

“I don’t see how that’s relevant to the talk of monstrosity in The Museum of Extraordinary Things. Open to page ninety-four in your textbooks.” 

He flinched as Ireland and Eva shared a look. They would definitely be taking a look at the staff page on the Westview University website later to see Harkness’ alma mater. 

 

 

Chapter End Notes

ANNOUNCEMENT : with a heavy heart, I have to announce that I will be starting classes again Monday. Unfortunately, that means a slow down in writing and posting. I appreciate your patience at this time.

Love you all

also, yes I jumped on the Rio's parents are dead train, but her sisters are here... drama ??????

Up next: the first ultrasound !!!

Chapter 8: The Little Alien

Chapter Summary

“Mmm, the little alien wants more,” Rio teased, her smile widening. “How about I go make some tea and slice up an apple or two? Keep that growing alien happy.”

 

OR:

Their first ultrasound and Lilia and Alice reacting

Chapter Notes

SURPRISE-- I had one class today and yesterday I went skiing, so my body is dying

I hope you like this, i don't think this is my finest work, I had imagined it soo many different ways, I struggled to put it to words.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

She hummed softly, a softer and deeper breath tickling her neck as she shifted slightly, pressing a gentle kiss in a curly brunette mane. Rio was pinned in place, her arms stiff and her bad knee ached with a fathom pain telling her to straighten it. She did slowly, wincing slightly as it cracked before a warmer leg was holding her in place again. She rolled her eyes fondly, the head against her neck nuzzling closer to her, as if to fuse with her very own body. Her shirt was tightened in Agatha’s hand, the other mindless drumming her fingers on Rio’s sternum. Rio continued humming as she stared at her phone over Agatha’s head, trying to read her emails for today. The past couple of days, she found her wife more cuddly. It was strange, Agatha usually liked her space most of the time, except for when they were in bed at night where both of them would swap being little spoons. However, something changed starting Monday, after they got home from work. 

 

Rio had set her papers down on the dining room table before going into the kitchen and starting dinner. It was Agatha’s turn, but she needed this. She figured her wife didn’t want to talk after she got upset at her for snapping and keeping things ‘unprofessional.’ It led to a small fight in Rio’s own office during lunch, and both of them were in a foul mood for the rest of the day. Of course, during her students’ quiz, she decided to take her mind off it by looking at baby things: cribs, carriers, toys, little outfits, a perfect stroller she already put in her cart, and even baby names. That was until a student approached with their quiz and she was snapped back to her reality of her wife and she had fought. The drive home was quiet. They didn’t speak, knowing the other one was finally cooling off and taking time to reflect. 

Making dinner relaxed her, her frustration coming to a halt as she prepared, grabbing the ingredients, potatoes, bacon, chicken broth, and some cream cheese. She started off simple, cutting up the potatoes and placing them in the broth before starting the stove. The bacon was next and just as she got them to cool, she went to turn, grabbing her bottle of water, warm hands wrapped around her waist, causing her to jump. She turned slowly, finding her wife but before she could see her face, it was buried into the crook of her neck. Warmth encircled her, lips pressed lazily against her neck as she slowly wrapped her arms back around her. Agatha murmured something against her neck, and the only reason she knew was the slight vibration there. 

“What did you say, mi amor ?” she asked quietly. 

“I’m sorry.” 

Agatha Harkness apologising… while it wasn’t unheard of, it certainly was rare after their fights. Normally, her wife would do something for her, like buy her plants, a ridiculously expensive necklace or bracelet, or packets of the freeze-dried strawberries she adored and could easily eat without stopping. When they weren’t fighting, Agatha had to hide the packets, making Rio have to ask for them like a child. It infuriated her, but her wife had a point. So apologising, instead of gift-giving caught her completely off guard. 

“What?” she couldn’t help but ask. 

Her wife scoffed, but kept firmly against her, “You heard me. Don’t push your luck.” 

 

Rio didn’t but her wife was firmly attached to her the rest of that night. It was strange, in the most loving way possible. Agatha didn’t leave her once she did apologize, instead, stayed leaning against her as she finished dinner. When they did eat, her wife was holding her hand. When Rio went to clean up, Agatha scowled at her until she sat down on the couch. In seconds, she was curled up against her side. Hell, Rio couldn’t even shower alone without her wife following her and holding onto her. By Tuesday morning, Agatha had somehow wormed her way on top of Rio, but the only issue was getting the woman out of bed and getting to work on time, which they were both late. During their lunch, her wife insisted on sitting on the couch and then leaned into her the whole time. And the previous night, it was the repeat of the night before. 

Now, Rio laid on her side, Agatha trying to somehow fuse their bodies together as she dozed in and out of sleep. One point earlier in the morning, Rio had gotten up to pee and the moment she was close enough to the bed again, Agatha dragged her in. Who would have thought pregnancy would make Agatha Harkness clingy? 

Rio shifted again, the stray hairs from her wife’s head tickling her chin. There was nothing new from work, but as Rio glanced at the time, she realized she had to start waking her wife to get to the ultrasound in time. She turned off her phone, placing soft kisses on her wife’s head, knowing she would get the kindest response that way. 

“ Mi vida, it’s time to wake up. We have to leave soon,” she spoke softly. 

Agatha huffed her annoyance, but instead of pulling away, she pulled Rio closer against her, refusing to let go, “Fuck off.” 

Rio chuckled, kissing down to her cheek before tucking her hair behind her ear. Leaning forward she whispered softly, “The ultrasound is today, mi vida. We get to see our baby.” 

Her wife sat up, her hair a mess as she grabbed Rio’s phone looking at the time. Without another word, she stood walking to the bathroom. Rio waited a moment before catching her wife was fine. She made her way into the kitchen, putting in toast and starting tea. Excitement ran through her, she was practically bouncing as she waltzed into their room, going through her draws to fish out her sweat pants, only to find her favorite pair missing. Cursing to herself, she found her black ones, putting them on and adding a dark green long sleeve. 

Walking into the bathroom she spoke, “ Mi amor, have you seen my green sweatpants? I swore I put them–”

She froze, seeing her wife brushing her hair in the mirror wearing none other than the green sweatpants she was looking for. They have been together for fifteen years and yet Rio swore something in her always short circuited when her wife wore her clothes. Her eyes widened as her mouth remained open, a rush running through her as her wife hummed softly, purposely ignoring her as she threw her hair up. 

“No, sorry, my love,” she teased, her voice still thick with sleep, before looking up at her through the mirror. 

The blue eyes rolled at her as she stepped back, facing Rio and placing a quick kiss to her cheek, “You can stare later, my love.” 

Rio blinked, momentarily caught off guard, before quickly shaking herself from her stupor. She had long grown accustomed to Agatha’s effortless beauty, whether it was in a sharp suit or a worn-out pair of sweatpants. Yet, every time she saw her wife in her clothes, there was something that made her heart stutter, a flutter in her chest that never got old. She fought to keep the blush from rising up her neck, her pulse quickening with that all-too-familiar warmth spreading through her veins. 

As the minutes ticked by, Rio found herself sneaking glances at her wife, each one feeling like a stolen moment. Agatha was stunning, radiating a quiet beauty that seemed to grow with every passing day. And today, they were about to see their baby for the first time. Even though their little one was still the size of a grape, the excitement was undeniable. Rio quickly gathered the necessary paperwork, her fingers trembling just slightly, before watching Agatha shrug on her coat and hastily slip on her sneakers. She tossed Rio the car keys with a grin, and Rio felt a rush of love swell in her chest. 

In the car, Rio’s nerves were a whirlwind, her emotions jumping from anxiety to excitement with every beat of her heart. Her fingers drummed nervously on the steering wheel, but the moment Agatha’s warm hand rested on her knee, everything stilled. That touch grounded her, soothing the storm inside. Soon, her wife was putting on music, something simple to push the minute hand faster. The gentle strums and harmony of Gigi Perez instantly filled the car, and following was the piano tune of Fleetwood Mac. 

“Can we get McDonald’s after this?” Agatha’s voice broke through the music, and Rio’s attention snapped from the road to her wife. 

Her gaze flicked from Agatha to the looming golden arches ahead, disbelief in her eyes. 

“Agatha,” Rio said, her voice half-laughing, “You hate McDonald’s.” 

Agatha shrugged, her shoulders lifting in a playful, exaggerated motion, “I want their fries.” 

Rio raised an eyebrow, smirking, “You hate their fries.” 

Agatha shot her a quick glance, a slight frown on her lips, “I’m pregnant. I get to change my mind.”

She chuckled softly, reaching over and placing her hand on her wife’s stomach, “Alright, my love. Anything else?” 

Agatha nodded, “Nando’s.” 

And how could Rio say no to that? 

***

Upon arriving at the clinic, Rio checked them in, and they found themselves in the waiting room. Rio watched as her wife bounced her leg, her hand becoming sweaty in hers. Her eyebrows were furrowed as she absent mindedly rubbed her stomach. Rio gazed around for a moment, knowing Agatha would feel even more uncomfortable if Rio tried comforting her in front of people. Across from them were a younger couple, the woman heavily pregnant, her eyes closed as her husband went over paperwork. To the right of them, sitting and bouncing her leg just as much as her wife was a single mother, maybe a few months along, her stomach only slightly round. 

Rio leaned in, murmuring softly in Spanish to her wife, “ Es solo un chequeo mi amor. Todo estará bien. ” She placed a hand on her knee, steadying her bouncing. “ Nuestro bebé está sano. Estás sano y ya sabes que él tiene la fuerza y ​​la lucha de Harkness. Aunque tendrá todo mi encanto."

She earned a small smile from her wife, who rolled her eyes and shook her head, “You are so dead set they are a boy.” 

Rio tilted her head, before nodding, “Of course, plus I think we should confirm at the eighteenth week mark, that way it will soften the blow when he comes out looking like me.” 

“You’re impossible,” Agatha snorted quietly, before the door opened from the side of the desk. 

“Agatha Harkness-Vidal,” a nurse called. 

Rio’s heart fluttered at the sound of her wife’s name, her protectiveness prickling as she stood up with her. Her wife smoothed her shirt for a moment, before stepping ahead of her. Rio followed closely behind, her hand on her back to offer a quiet reassurance as they entered long white halls. Pictures of fetuses lined them, each in a different stage. She could feel the tension radiating from her wife, but she kept her focus ahead, offering a calm presence. As the nurse neared the end of the hall, the nurse opened a wooden door, flicking one of the colored flags and nodding them in. 

“Go ahead and put on the gown. The doctor will be in shortly.”

Rio followed her wife to the table, looking around at the monitors that hummed faintly. A screen was on the wall, clearly meant to show the baby as a sterile scent filled her senses. Turning back to her wife, she came behind her, kissing her head softly before taking her clothes from her. She smiled faintly at her wife’s exposed skin making Agatha shove her playfully as she put the gown on. Rio carefully tied the back, her hands slightly trembling before her wife awkwardly adjusted herself on the table. Agatha fidgeted with her wedding ring, twisting it in circles nervously, and her heart swelled at the sight of her wife’s vulnerability. There was something about seeing Agatha like this– uncharacteristically unprepared, unguarded, and so entirely unsure that made Rio want to protect her more. 

“You know,” Rio spoke softly, breaking the silence, “We are doing this together, mi vida. It will be alright.” 

Agatha gave her a half-smile, though the nervousness was still visible in her eyes, “I know, it’s just… I keep thinking…” She paused for a moment, leaning back, her eyes darting to the ceiling as she let the words hang in the air. “I can’t help but think of my own mother. I don’t want her anywhere near this child. I don’t want them to have a childhood like me.”

Rio took a deep breath, stepping forward and cupping her wife’s cheek, “ Mi amor, we have talked about this before. You are nothing like your mother. This baby will be so loved by both of us and you know very well, I would end up in prison before letting that cunt near them.” 

Agatha hummed her laughter, “I don’t doubt that, my love.”

She closed her eyes for a moment, taking a slow breath, her hand coming to rest on her stomach, as if to reassure herself. Rio could see the silent fight in her eyes, the struggle between the overwhelming excitement of becoming a mother and the deep-rooted fears that clung to her from her past. Before Rio could say anything else, a knock echoed softly, shattering the intimacy between them, shifting into the clinical reality of the appointment. 

Her wife spoke to allow them in, welcoming a younger woman in a long white coat, her blonde hair tied neatly behind her as she held her hand out to Agatha. 

“Hello Agatha, I am Dr. Walsh,” she said brightly and shook her hand before holding it out to Rio. “And you must be the wife.” 

She shook her hand, “Rio.” 

“Today, we will be doing a transvaginal ultrasound for a seven week scan. Afterwards, we will set up your twelve week appointment. How are we feeling today? How are your symptoms as well?” she asked, clicking through the computer before the machine hummed to life. 

She watched her wife inhale, meeting her gaze briefly, before turning back to her, “Nausea is still there, but is it not as bad as before. Just… a lot of fatigue. It comes and goes.” 

Dr. Walsh nodded, her expression professional but warm, “That’s pretty typical in the early stages, but we’ll keep an eye on it. Anything else you’ve been experiencing? Any discomfort?” 

Agatha hesitated for a moment as Rio raised an eyebrow. Of course her wife was hiding something from her, “I’ve had some cramping, but nothing too severe. Just a lot of changes, I think.”

Rio’s hand instinctively reached out, her fingers brushing against Agatha’s, slightly annoyed that her wife didn’t tell her. It instantly vanished as her wife caught her eyes again, smiling softly. 

“That’s perfectly normal,” Dr. Walsh said reassuringly. “Your body is adjusting to the pregnancy, and cramping can occur as your uterus expands. Just be sure to rest when you need to. And if anything feels off, don’t hesitate to let me know. And cravings?” 

Rio smirked this time, “Oh, I think it kicked in today. She asked for McDonald’s when she typically hates it.”

The doctor laughed slightly as her wife glared at her, “Be prepared for the three am cravings. Just be sure not to eat too much fast food, but every now and then won’t hurt. Now,” she stopped, glancing between them “how about we see this little one?” 

 Agatha gave a nervous nod, her hand still clutching Rio’s. Dr. Walsh picked up the wand, placing a cover over it and turning to them. 

“Go ahead and set your legs up. Today, I just want to check on your uterus and ovaries, and get some measurements of your little one. We’ll see if we can detect a heartbeat, but don’t be alarmed if we don’t hear it. Your baby’s heart isn’t fully formed yet. Your little one might not look like a baby today, but at twelve weeks, when you come back, we’ll be able to count their fingers and toes.” 

As Dr. Walsh spoke, she adjusted the footrests, and Agatha carefully reclined back, settling her head on the pillow. Her gaze drifted toward the ceiling, her eyes unfocused. Rio stayed by her side, her grip on Agatha’s hand steady and strong, offering silent comfort as her wife’s breath became slightly quicker. The air in the room felt heavier, charged with anticipation, and Rio’s heart beat in sync with her wife’s. 

“ Estas bien, mi amor ,” Rio whispered, her voice low, steady, a calmness meant only for Agatha. 

Agatha squeezed her hand tightly in response, though her eyes were wide, still looking at the ceiling. She inhaled deeply, trying to steady herself as the doctor moved toward the ultrasound machine again. 

“This will be a little uncomfortable,” Dr. Walsh said, her voice professional yet gentle. “Are you ready?”

Rio nodded along with Agatha, her eyes briefly flicking to the screen before returning to Dr. Walsh’s focused expression. As the doctor positioned herself, Agatha’s grip tightened, her fingers pressing into Rio’s palm, her body tense. A flash of discomfort crossed Agatha’s face, and she muttered a quiet curse under her breath, her expression shifting from nervousness to something more vulnerable. Rio’s hand, the one closest to her, shifted to Agatha’s hair, her fingers weaving gently through it in a soothing motion. The moment Rio’s touch found her, Agatha relaxed, her tension easing, though she kept her grip on Rio firm, never letting go. 

“Okay, and there they are,” Dr. Walsh said softly, turning toward the screen and pointing at the image. 

At first, it was just a faint, blurry blob, barely discernible, and then it hit Rio, not just in her chest, but all the way down to her bones. 

 

Lub-dub. Lub-dub. Lub-dub . 

 

The unmistakable sound of a heartbeat.

 

A tiny, rhythmic pulse. 

 

Rio’s breath caught in her throat, and for a moment, it felt like time itself had paused. The rest of the world faded away as the sound of that heartbeat, fragile and strong at once, filled her senses. Her heart pounded in her chest as everything in her stilled. The words she had prepared felt foreign in her mouth, and her thoughts scattered in the face of this new, overwhelming reality. Her wife wasn’t just carrying a child– this was their child. The sound of life, so simple, so pure, grounded her in a way nothing else ever could. 

Her eyes darted toward the screen now, and there it was– the little white shape, nestled among shadows, small yet distinct, as the doctor took measurements, completely unaware of the emotional flood that was overtaking Rio. The baby was there. It wasn’t an idea anymore. It wasn’t a dream or a hope. It was real, and it was theirs . 

Rio couldn’t breathe. 

She watched the small, alien-like shape on the screen, trying to hold onto the moment while the world continued around her. Her hands trembled as she squeezed her wife’s, her fingers still brushing through Agatha’s hair as she fought to regain composure. It wasn’t until the soft click of the ultrasound machine stopped that Rio realized she was blinking back tears. When she turned her eyes to Agatha, they were already there– her wife’s cheeks wet with silent tears, falling freely. It was a rare sight. Agatha had always been so composed, so steady, that seeing her this exposed, this overwhelmed, felt like a gift. 

“ Mi amor ,” Rio whispered, gently wiping the tears from Agatha’s face with her thumb. 

She leaned down, pressing a kiss to her forehead, her heart pounding as she looked into her wife’s eyes. Agatha met her gaze, and the words she whispered broke Rio in ways she didn’t expect. 

“They’re real,” her voice cracked, thick with emotion. 

There was no denying the tenderness in the way she said it. Her eyes searched Rio’s for something, for reassurance, for understanding. Rio could only nod, her hand brushing against Agatha’s wet cheeks before her own tears came, unbidden and unstoppable. 

“Yes, mi amor . They’re real.” 

Agatha’s gaze softened, and a small, trembling smile tugged at the corner of her lips, “Our baby.” 

“Our baby,” Rio echoed, her voice hushed with awe. 



 

The doctor confirmed everything was healthy. Agatha and the baby were doing wonderful, and were on track. The next appointment was a week before finals, and there was a growing anticipation to see their baby look like a baby. But as Rio sat in the parking lot of Nando’s, waiting for their order, she found herself staring at the blob of white from the pictures their doctor printed. Agatha held her hand, still wiping some of her tears as they both stared in an awed silence. 

“We are really doing this,” her wife spoke, breaking the quiet. 

Rio nodded, turning to face her, “We are, and you’re going to be the best mama.” 

Agatha smiled for real at her, a blush creeping up her neck. Rio leaned forward, her wife meeting her half-way as they shared a tender kiss. More warmth washed over Rio, as her lips moved even and sure against her wife’s, speaking their love and promise to each other. Her wife kissed back in the same fever– the way they both wanted to kiss the moment the heartbeat filled the room. 

The sound of Rio’s phone interrupted them and she pulled back, a smile on her lips, “Alright, time to feed the little alien and you, of course.” 

Agatha chuckled slightly, “You’re such an idiot.” 

Rio only grinned, kissing her wife quickly and running into the grab their order.

***

After Rio grabbed everything her wife requested, they returned home, immediately falling onto the couch together. Agatha curled up into Rio’s side, digging in at her McDonald’s fries and gulping down her Nando’s chicken. Her head rested against her shoulder as Friends played in the background. They ate in comfortable silence, the occasional giggle or smile shared between bites. Once they finished, Rio threw the trash away and came back to pull Agatha back into her embrace, tucking her close. Agatha continued to stare at the photos, her thumb brushing over the tiny image of their baby. Rio kissed the side of her wife’s head repeatedly, feeling the softness of her hair and the warmth of her presence. 

“I love you,” Rio whispered, her voice filled with the weight of everything she felt– too much to be contained in just those three words, but she had no better way to express the depth of her heart. 

Her black heart beat for Agatha, for the family they were becoming, and for the life growing inside her. 

Agatha’s lips curved into a soft smile, her voice a whisper of tenderness, “I love you more.” 

Just then, her stomach rumbled, and both of them chuckled. 

“Mmm, the little alien wants more,” Rio teased, her smile widening. “How about I go make some tea and slice up an apple or two? Keep that growing alien happy.” 

Agatha laughed, resting her hand on her stomach as she leaned into Rio, “You spoil us both, you know that?”

“Anything for whom my heart beats for,” she replied, kissing her cheek and standing up. “Go ahead and send the coven the picture. I am sure they are dying to hear from us.” 

Rio tossed Agatha her phone, knowing Agatha rarely texted anyone. She heard the soft devious laughter from her wife as she started the kettle and pulled out the apples, cutting them easily. When she came back her wife immediately took them from her, leaning into her again as they waited for a response. They didn’t have to wait long as both of their phones started buzzing with an onslaught of messages. 

Below the single picture of the ultrasound, without a caption came Lilia and Alice: 

 

The Coven

 

Alice: What the fuck is that ? 

 

Lilia: Is that.. Is that- WHAT IS THAT ????? 

 

Alice: RIO ? AGATHA ? WTF IS THIS ?? IS THIS REAL ??? 

THIS IS A BABY RIGHT ??? 

 

Lilia: WHAT ? IS THIS YOURS ? THIS BETTER NOT BE A PRANK AGATHA !

 

 

Agatha snorted softly as she reached for the tea, “Why am I the one she blames for a prank?” 

Rio kissed her head, humming her own amusement, “Cause it’s you, my love.” 

 

The Coven

 

Alice: HELLO ? GUYS ? Are you being fr rn ?? 

 

Lilia: Who the hell is pregnant ? 

 

Alice: I AM IN THE MIDDLE OF A LECTURE. CAN YOU PLZ JUST TELL ME WTF IS GOING ON ? 

WHO TF IS PREGNANT ?

It’s Agatha, isn’t it ? After that reaction from the bar, Rio ? This is your guy’s explanation ? 

 

Lilia: No way, it’s Rio. Rio’s heightened emotions from her hormones. 

 

 

“Pfft, rude,” Rio huffed, annoyed as her wife laughed. 

A hand was placed on her chest, “Yes, my wife getting that overprotective because of her hormones.”

“Says the one who wanted McDonald’s,” she retorted as her wife mocked a wince.

“Low blow, darling.” 

Their phones buzzed again, pulling them out of their playful banter. 

 

The Coven  

 

Alice : RIO, I SWEAR TO THE GODDESS, ANSWER ME. IS IT AGATHA OR YOU ?! I AM LOSING MY MIND. 

 

Lilia : This is NOT funny. Okay, maybe a little funny. But seriously. Who’s pregnant ? 

 

Alice : OMG WAIT. Is it BOTH OF YOU ??? I SWEAR–

 

Agatha nearly choked on her tea, laughing, “Both of us? How does that even work?” 

Rio smirked, shaking her head as she typed something into her phone. But just before hitting send, she changed her mind, backspacing everything. 

Instead, she leaned into Agatha and whispered, “Let’s leave them hanging a little longer.” 

Agatha’s devious grin matched her wife’s, “Oh, absolutely. They deserve it after all the teasing they’ve done over the years.”

 

The Coven 

 

Lilia: Witches. Witches. Please. Just ONE WORD. Just say WHO. 

 

Alice: This is TORTURE. I can’t focus on my lecture, and it’s all your fault. 

 

Lilia: If we don’t hear from you soon, I’m coming over. I mean it. 

 

Rio chuckled, tossing her phone aside and pulling Agatha closer. 

“Let them stew. It’ll do them good.” 

Agatha hummed in agreement, nuzzling into Rio’s neck, “The longer they wait, the more dramatic their reactions will be. Perfect entertainment for a quiet night in.” 

Rio’s lips pressed softly to Agatha’s temple, her voice low and affectionate, “You really are wicked, my love.”

“And you married me anyway,” Agatha replied, a teasing lilt in her voice.

 

 

Agatha eventually fell asleep against her, Rio following with her, filled with utter contentment and warmth. By the time they opened their eyes, it was night and only a low glow from the setting sun casted into the room. Rio sat up, looking for the source that woke her when their front door rattled from someone banging on it. She chuckled, kissing her wife’s cheek to wake her up a little gentler. She rubbed her eyes as she turned on the lights, and the excitement of the day came back like a tsunami, crashing into her as the smile was infectious on her face. Glancing back at her wife, she made her way to the door. 

“RIO HARKNESS-VIDAL! OPEN UP YOU BITCH!” Alice screamed through the door. 

Rio paused at the door, stifling a laugh as she glanced back at Agatha, who was lazily stretching on the couch, still half-asleep. The golden light of the setting sun bathed her wife, accentuating her serene expression. 

“They really couldn’t wait, could they?” Agatha smirked, propping herself up on one elbow, her sleepy eyes sparkling with mischief. “Let them stew out there a little longer. They’ve earned it after all the years they’ve pestered us.” 

The pounding grew louder, echoing through the house like thunder. 

“We know you’re in there!” Lilia’s voice cut through the commotion, her tone bordering on desperation. “Don’t make us hex this door!” 

Rio raised an eyebrow, her lips quirking in a grin, “They sound desperate .” She glanced back at Agatha, her voice dropping to a whisper, “We should have recorded this.” 

“Would’ve been perfect for the scrapbook,” Agatha replied with a chuckle, her voice soft yet laced with her usual sass. 

With a dramatic sigh, Rio opened the door, revealing Alice and Lilia in all their disheveled glory. Alice’s face was flushed, strands of her usually neat hair falling messily around her face. Lilia, by contrast, looked less angry and more like someone on the verge of a breakdown. 

“Finally!” Alice burst through the doorway, pointing an accusatory finger at Rio. “What the hell was that photo? WHO. IS. PREGNANT?!” 

Lilia followed closely behind, her arms crossed tightly over her chest, a storm brewing in her dark eyes, “You two are insufferable. Insufferable!”

Rio stepped aside, gesturing toward the living room with exaggerated calm, “Please, come in. Make yourselves at home. Can I get you anything to drink?” 

Alice ignored the sarcasm, storming over to her wife, who was now sitting up, smirking up at their friends. Her calm demeanor only seemed to fuel Alice’s frustration. 

“Agatha,” she hissed, as though trying not to yell, “tell me right now. Is it you? Is it Rio? Or is this some kind of cruel, twisted joke?” 

Agatha tilted her head, her lips curling into a small, amused smile, “Why would we joke about something like that?” 

Alice threw her hands up in exasperation, “Because it’s you! Because you two live to mess with people!” 

Lilia’s voice was quieter but no less urgent.

“Seriously, guys. Is one of you actually pregnant? Because if this is a prank–” her voice cracked slightly, betraying the genuine emotion behind her words. 

Agatha’s teasing expression softened at that, her gaze shifting to Rio. Rio watched the strange softness span across her face in a quiet shock and if Rio didn’t move, then sure that expression would give it away. Instead, she sat down, bumping her wife playfully. 

“Guess,” she said simply, bringing back the playful expression onto her wife. 

Lilia almost snarled, raking her hands through her hair as she threw her coat to the couch, “Rio, I swear, if you make us guess, I’m going to lose my mind.” 

Agatha scoffed, “You mean what’s left of it.” 

She earned a sharp stare from both women and Alice spoke, “We have been overanalyzing that damn photo for hours! Who the fuck is it?”

Rio watched as her wife leaned into her, her eyes narrowing playfully as that beautiful smirk graced her lips, “Why are you two so dramatic? We send one little picture, and suddenly you’re banging down our door.”

“One little picture?” Alice repeated through her teeth. “You dropped a Peeta Mellark! What did you think we’d do? Sit back and sip tea?” 

“That’s exactly what we did,” Rio quipped, her smirk widening as she relaxed into the cushions beside her wife. “It’s been highly entertaining, by the way.” 

Lilia’s gaze softened for a split second before she snapped back to her stern facade, “Don’t change the subject. You’re stalling. Just answer the question!” 

Agatha’s lips twitched, suppressing a laugh as she turned to Rio, “Should we make them wait a little longer?”

Rio glanced at their friends, feigning thoughtfulness as Alice practically vibrated with impatience, “I don’t know. They seem close to breaking.”

She laughed more as Alice snarled, “You two are a match made in fucking hell, you absolute witches.”

“Yeah, we are, aren’t we,” Rio smiled at her wife, watching the way her blue eyes twinkled.

“Stop doing that!” Alice barked at them, forcing them to pull away. 

Rio sighed dramatically, “Fine.”

Then, she reached out, placing her hand protectively on her wife’s stomach. She watched as the simple gesture stunned the women before them into silence. Alice dropped to a nearby chair as Lilia stood frozen, her hands up to her mouth as Rio felt her wife’s fingers lace with her own over her stomach. 

“Agatha,” Lilia breathed, her voice catching as the realization hit her. “You’re… pregnant.”

Agatha’s lips curled into a small, knowing smile as she nodded, her eyes twinkling with mischief, “That’s right.” 

With an exaggerated tilt of her head, she gestured toward Rio, jabbing her thumb in her direction.

“And this one,” she said, her tone dripping with mock exasperation, “decided to knock me up.” 

Alice snorted, a laugh bursting from her before she clamped a hand over her mouth. Her shoulders shook as she struggled to contain herself. 

“Oh my goddess,” she wheezed. “Leave it to Rio to get another woman pregnant.”

Rio raised an unimpressed eyebrow, the corner of her mouth twitching into a smirk.
