“Naturally,” she replied with mock grandeur. “I am the green witch, after all. Miracles are kind of my thing.” 

“You’re insufferable, that’s what you are,” Lilia shot back, rolling her eyes.  But despite her tone, the fondness in her voice was undeniable. She shook her head as if trying to process the news, then let out a soft laugh, “Oh, to the Divine Mother. You two are really going to be moms.”

Agatha’s smirk only deepened, her confidence radiating as she exchanged a glance with Rio. 

“Moms,” she echoed, the word rolling off her tongue like a promise that Rio’s heart fluttered all over again. 

Rio watched Alice as she stood, her own glint in her eye, “Well, congrats, you two. I can’t wait for the day when your own spawn gives you a taste of your medicine. You two deserve it.”

 

 

Chapter End Notes

“Es solo un chequeo mi amor. Todo estará bien.”... Nuestro bebé está sano. Estás sano y ya sabes que él tiene la fuerza y ​​la lucha de Harkness. Aunque tendrá todo mi encanto." = It’s just a check up, my love. Everything will be fine....Our baby is healthy. You are healthy and you already know he has the Harkness strength and fight. Although he will have all my charm.

It should be a felony that the US has only like three Nando's and that only the England really has it like

ANYWAY- next up, their date and possible some Wanda POV... still haven't decided, but def their date

Chapter 9: Vega and Altair ~ The Star-Crossed Lovers

Chapter Summary

Rio’s smile softened, her heart swelling with love as she gazed at her wife, “Magic is real, Agatha. Just look at us.”
“You’re a fucking sap, have I mentioned that?” Agatha retorted, wrinkling her nose as Rio’s head fell back in laughter. “But, I guess you’re my sap. Tell me more about the stars.”

OR:

the coven grills Agatha, Rio sets up their date, and they talk about the stars

OR:

FLUFF GALORE AND SMUT (bon appétit)

Chapter Notes

OMG GUYS THANK YOU FOR THE 1K KUDOS YOU GUYS ARE AMAZING THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU 💚💜

Also, i think this is my favorite one I have written, like I made myself cry... in a good way... well kinda BC WHY CANT I HAVE THEIR LOVE ?!!! *cries in single*

The amount of research that went into this was a lot... my search history prob is so concerning between looking stuff up about pregnancies and then Vega and Altair. don't worry, if you don't know the story of Vega and Altair, Rio tells you !

 

Enjoy my 7.3k that I somehow found time to write 💚💜

I love you all. Your comments about Nando's has me cracking up. I haven't had Nando's since 2018 when I was in England, and then recently I was back, and ughhhh, i miss you Nando's. In the States (also im not in the States rn thank the gods) there is absolutely 0 Nando's where I live-- like wtf 💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

I don't quite know 

How to say 

How I feel 

 

Those three words 

Are said too much 

They're not enough 

 

If I lay here 

If I just lay here 

Would you lie with me and just forget the world? 

“Chasing Cars” ~ Snow Patrol

 

 

Agatha

 

Agatha sat back on the couch, her hand on her stomach as Lilia sat across from her, wiping the tears from her eyes. Agatha could only smile, her chest warm, as her hand remained over the life growing inside her– the life her and her wife created. They were really doing this. They were going to be moms and for the first time since staring at the stupid little positive pregnancy test, Agatha felt the reality hit hard. Never in her life did she imagine herself becoming a mother.

At eighteen, the day after she graduated high school, she packed her shit and snuck out of her mother’s house and into Lilia’s awaiting car. Her mother had her setup to go to Oxford, and as much as it was her dream, Agatha couldn’t go. One, she didn’t earn it. Her rich mother bought her way in and would be paying for everything and therefore, could hold it over Agatha and two, it wasn’t even for literature. It was some research program that would lead to Agatha working for her mother’s company. She had to escape after the abuse and homophobia. Sure, it would be out of the country and she would be free from her mother, but she knew her mother would have eyes on her even from there across the sea.

Agatha needed to start anew, away from Evanora Harkness. 

She planned it in middle school, working under the table at a dingy diner before working as a barista. The cafe was a thirty minute walk, but it was worth it, especially the long hours and overtime. Agatha took everything she could, on top of remaining the top of her class. When she applied for Harvard, it had been on a dare from Lilia, but when she received the email, learning she somehow received a full ride, she couldn’t turn it down. Lilia was beside her, admitted into Harvard and together, before they graduated with the help of Lilia’s parents, they rented a shared apartment and moved in together. She was finally free. She bought a new phone, changed her number, and deleted her social media, and shit, did she thrive in college… 

Then, she ran into Rio Vidal. 

Meeting Rio, hating Rio, and finally falling in love with the little shit changed everything. Agatha never planned on falling in love, in fact she actively avoided it. Too much was at stake; she was effectively on her own in college, no one to fall back on, her only reliance was Lilia, who paid more in the shared rent with the help of her parents. Her goal was to graduate early while also earning her masters, and throwing herself into a PhD program. Then, find the earliest job opportunity and stay the fuck away from her mother. There was no woman to fall in love with on her bingo card, no fiancée, no marriage, and certainly, there was no family on her fucking bingo card; but, Rio had appeared as if she clawed her way through the earth to arrive at Agatha’s feet, and take her down a completely different road. 

This infuriating little shit captured her heart, took her down a road, and showed her what true love was, and with that went all of her carefully crafted walls to protect herself with. And all Rio had to do with was show up to apartment with flowers, hold her when she fell asleep after Agatha marched to her dorm at three am, and have their first fucking date be under the stars. And then this infuriating little shit, after five years of being together, had randomly muttered that she wanted to marry and Agatha had agreed without a single doubt. They got married, and then Rio… oh her sweet darling Rio, had to go and hold a fucking baby, and it was that same night, the last of Agatha’s walls had crumbled. 

She remembered Rio’s old college roommate meeting up with them, holding her ten-month old daughter and she had grown stiff, uneasy by the tiny little human. Hela had simply handed her daughter to Rio, and in that moment, the world froze again. Agatha, most proud of keeping her emotions in check, of being in control, cracked. She sat across from her wife as Rio held that tiny, squirming bundle of innocence, and something happened inside her. Rio’s fingers, calloused from years of work, were impossibly gentle as they cradled the baby’s head. Her brown eyes, usually sharp with wit or blazing with intensity, softened to the kind of warmth that could melt glaciers. She cooed softly, brushing a thumb across the baby’s chubby cheek. Agatha, for the life of her, couldn’t breathe. 

“She’s beautiful, Hela,” Rio had murmured, her voice dipped in awe. “What’s her name?” 

“Lilith,” Hela had replied, her smile fond. “She’s a handful, but she’s worth every sleepless night.”

Rio had chuckled, the sound low and soft, and Agatha’s heart stumbled in her chest. She wasn’t sure if it was the way Rio held Lilith like she was the most precious thing in the world, or the way her wife’s lips curved into a tender smile, but something monumental shifted inside her. Agatha wasn’t even sure she wanted kids before this. She’d told herself it was impractical– messy, chaotic, and far too vulnerable. Yet, watching Rio cradle a baby, every argument she had carefully crafted crumbled into dust. 

“She likes you,” Hela had said with a grin, noticing the way Lilith’s tiny hand wrapped around Rio’s finger. 

“Of course she does,” Rio had smirked before returning the soft smile, her attention shifting back to the baby. “She has excellent taste.” 

Agatha wanted to roll her eyes, but she couldn’t muster the will. All she could do was stare at her wife, completely captivated by the scene unfolding in front of her. 

“She’s lucky to have a mother like you, Hela,” Rio added, her tone soft. 

“And you?” Hela had asked, her gaze flicking between the two of them. “You two ever thought about having one of your own?” 

Agatha had stiffened slightly, the question slicing through the haze of warmth that had wrapped around her. She remembered bracing herself for Rio to laugh it off or change the subject. After all, they’d never really talked about it, not seriously. But Rio simply had surprised her. A small, contemplative smile had tugged at her lips as she glanced at Agatha, their eyes meeting. 

“Maybe,” Rio had whispered. “Someday.” 

Agatha’s breath had been caught. It was just one word– maybe – but it carried a weight that slammed into her like a freight train. In that moment, she saw a future she had never dared to imagine: late-night feedings, tiny hands tugging at their clothes, first steps, first words, and Rio– always Rio– by her side through it all. 

When they left the café later that evening, Rio didn’t notice the way Agatha was quieter than usual, her blue eyes distant as if lost in thought. But that night, as they lay in bed, Agatha rolled over to face her wife. 

“Rio?” she murmured, her voice hesitant. 

Rio lifted her head, her brown eyes glowing in the dim light. 

“Yeah, mi amor ?”

Agatha remembered freezing, the words stuck in her throat as she stared at her wife. She swallowed hard, searching those eyes, but only finding a promise of understanding, and yet, she still couldn’t bring the words to her mouth. It was clear as day in her mind, how Rio moved closer, her hands caressing Agatha’s cheeks, her touch grounding for a moment, but it still felt too big to ask, too much to say. It felt life changing, and it was. It was another fucking road she wasn’t prepared to take, and even fathoming of taking, but here Rio was, holding her face, and staring at her with those stupid puppy dog eyes that she would follow anywhere. The image flashed again of Rio holding the baby and oh, how she wanted to straggle the little shit for breaking down her walls. 

“Iwanttohaveababywithyou,” she blurted out in one breath. 

Rio had blinked for a second, her eyebrows creasing in confusion, “ Mi amor, you know English is not my first language. I have no idea what you just said.”

Agatha had covered her face with her hands for a moment, but Rio moved closer, pressing their bodies together and pushing her hands away. Rio’s fingers were gentle, brushing the hair away as she stared into her eyes. Agatha had felt herself take a deep breath, closing her eyes to steady herself. 

When she met her wife’s eyes, her voice came out in a soft whisper, “Rio, my love, I want to have a baby. I want to have your baby. I can’t stop seeing the way you held Hela’s daughter, and to the Goddess– Rio, I want to see you become a mother and be one with you. I know we never talked about, fuck– I didn’t, or I least never– fuck I–”

Lips had pressed against hers, effectively stopping her rambling. Rio caressed her cheek, turning them and straddling her hips. Rio’s kiss was hungry, all consuming, but it poured a love Agatha still remembered as if it happened only moments ago. 

Rio had barely pulled away before whispering against her lips, “I want that too.” 

 

And now, Agatha sat in their home, pregnant with Rio’s child, her best friend across from her and her wife and Alice in the kitchen.  

“So how many weeks along are you?” Lilia spoke softly, knocking Agatha from her trance. 

She smiled, “Only seven, but our doctor said we can share with a few very close friends, plus, I needed to explain Rio’s behavior sooner than later.” 

Lilia’s laugh was warm, “Yes, I didn’t think the scary bitch could get scarier. If looks could kill, Agatha– that kid gonna have the fiercest moms looking out for them.”

Agatha let her gaze drift back to the kitchen where her wife and Alice were talking as Rio cooked their dinner. Her wife was always her fierce protector, but now with their little one, she coudn’t help but wonder if Rio would go as far to beat up a kid if they dared tried to bully their own kid. She could see it, unfortunately. She turned back to Lilia, realizing she was speaking again. 

“–I mean, there has to be so much to do, or did you and Rio already decorate?” 

Agatha blinked, “Decorate what?” 

Her friend chuckled, “Really, Harkness, pregnancy brain already?” 

Agatha shook her head, her eyebrow raising, “That’s rich coming from you, Dory. I was just distracted. Decorate what?” 

Lilia only shook her head as her eyes went down to where Agatha’s hand rested on her stomach, “The baby’s room, Agatha?”

“Oh, no, not yet. I think we are waiting for the eighteenth week scan. Rio and I have a bet placed on the gender–”

“Of course you two do,” Lilia rolled her eyes. 

Agatha ignored her, “Rio has this hunch that our baby is a boy. Lilia, we have known for barely three weeks and that little shit already thinks she knows everything.” 

“ Yo sí y esta pequeña mierda te trajo té, mi amor,” a voice echoed behind her, lips pressing against her cheek before a green mug appeared in front of her.

Agatha rolled her eyes as she took the mug, staring at the purple heart on the mug’s side, the one that Rio had painted during one of their old pottery classes together years ago. Rio loved the mug since, barely allowing Agatha to use it. Her heart did a little flutter as she couldn’t stop the smile. 

“And what do you think, Agatha?” Lilia asked the moment the footsteps faded behind her. 

Agatha blew steadily on the hot drink, “What do you mean?” 

Lilia sat forward, “I mean what do you think? Mothers– the pregnant one– usually have a sixth sense about their baby’s gender.” 

She sat back for a moment, her hand back on her stomach. She tried feeling for it, thinking of a little girl inside her but all she could think of was Rio’s words– that she was going to have a boy. She set the hot mug down, shaking her head. 

“I don’t know, Rio’s so damn adamant that I didn’t even think about it before she was pushing. She claims my hair is shinier and that is how she knows.”

Lilia laughed softly, sitting back, her eyes glinting at her, “Well, she does have a point and your skin is glowing. Divine Mother, Rio might be right for once.”  

Agatha scoffed, wrinkling her nose, “You’re just saying that because it’s me.”

Lilia held her chest in mock offense as their conversation turned more into the baby. Lilia practically grilled her on every detail from maternity leave for both her and Rio, to possible names, to themes of the baby’s room, to whether or not Agatha is breastfeeding (which she fully directed that question to another conversation), and even a baby shower. Most of the questions, Agatha hadn’t been able to answer. Rio and her had barely spoken in truth what the next steps were, they just settled that this baby– that their baby– was real. 

 

“Okay, so what’s the little one going to call you?” Alice walked in, holding a single plate of arroz con gandules. 

Agatha felt her stomach rumble as her mouth watered. She loved Rio’s arroz con gandules. Alice handed her the plate and Agatha practically swiped it off of her, her fork digging into the rice as Alice laughed sitting beside her as she ate.

She swallowed her food before speaking, “Yeah, Rio will be mami and I will be mama.”

Alice smiled, “I should have known this kid is going to be bilingual.”

Agatha laughed and waited a second, wondering if her wife would chime in, but she found her presence wasn’t near. She sat up, turning to look towards the kitchen and finding movement before turning back to her coven. 

“Yeah, Rio’s abuelita would throttle her if we didn’t.”

Alice sat up straighter as Agatha was already halfway through her dinner, “And what about Rio’s sisters? Do they know yet?” 

Agatha felt herself tense at that. She knew very well how Rio felt about her older siblings, especially Maria. Agatha, surprisingly, got along with Oriana just fine, but Maria was another story. She was exactly like Rio, if not more stubborn and more callous. The only difference between the sisters was she was sure the oldest lacked common sense. When Rio and Agatha moved in together, it was the middle of their senior year fall semester. Rio wasn’t home, she was at a meeting to talk about her graduate classes and Agatha stayed in their home, preparing their dinner for their movie night when Maria had knocked. Agatha remembered opening the door, listening to the woman’s demands for Rio and Oriana standing sheepishly behind her. Agatha had no idea Rio even had siblings. Let’s just say, Agatha didn’t take too kindly to Maria and when Rio returned home, she watched as her girlfriend’s face had paled upon hearing her older sisters had found her. 

Maria babied Rio, treated her like she was stupid, meanwhile she was the one who graduated from an Ivy League. Maria, in Maria’s opinion, always knew better than Rio, was always the right one, and was always supposed to be first. When Rio announced their engagement, Maria was pissed and jealous. Rio hated her for it, and almost didn’t invite Maria to the wedding. She knew Rio hadn’t told anyone in her family about their baby. Rio probably wouldn’t even tell them until their child was at least a year old, and even then, she was sure, Rio would hold out as long as possible. 

“I think we all know the answer to that, Alice,” Agatha replied smoothly, her gaze going back to the kitchen where she heard no movement. 

She strained her neck, listening for her wife’s footsteps, but she was only met with silence. Agatha didn’t like it. She couldn’t feel her wife’s presence in the house, and something slowly started to kick in. She wasn’t sure what it was as she felt like her breath was coming out shorter. Her mind entirely focused on her wife, her comforting coolness of her skin, her sweet pine scent, and Rio’s hand always on her, whether it was on her stomach, intertwining their fingers, or Rio playing with her hair. Now, she felt empty as the food became less interesting without her wife near. 

 

 

Rio

 

Meanwhile, Rio dragged a basket and bags to their backyard, hurrying up the hill to the view of the trees as the moon started to rise. The sky was brighter here than the hill where she took Agatha away from Harvard on their first date. She could make out more constellations as the owls sang around her with the occasional flutter of a bat. The air was chilly, but they would be fine. She brought out more blankets this time, and knew her wife would be cuddled against her. She felt herself sigh, a warmth entering chest as her mind flashed back to the picture of the ultrasound. They were really doing this, and in a little over seven months, they would be moms. 

Focusing back on the basket and bags in hand, she pulled out the blankets that she wrapped around her new telescope her wife bought her for Yule last year. They haven’t really used it together in a while and tonight would be perfect. She set it to the side, laying out the blankets and pillows. She reached back in, grabbing the fairy lights and lining the outsides of the picnic blanket before pulling out Agatha’s favorite snacks and tea in a thermos. Her phone buzzed in her pocket just as she rearranged the food. 

 

Alice : Kola mode is going off. Get your ass back here before your pregnant wife starts tweaking

 

Rio sighed, surveying her setup before switching the fairy lights on. She placed the bags in the basket before running back down the hill and into the house. She slipped back inside quietly and made her way behind her wife. Alice looked up at her, turning to Lilia and they both stood, making her wife sit up. 

“Well, as much as we would love to torture you more with questions, your wife is kicking us out,” Alice smiled down at her. 

Agatha turned and Rio watched as the tension left her shoulders the moment their eyes met. Alice was right. Agatha’s koala mode was going off again. When Lilia and Agatha were in the living room, she had told Alice about how strange and wonderful that her wife was being more clingy. Her best friend had laughed, comparing her wife to a koala, which Rio quickly denied, knowing Agatha would hate that, but now Rio saw it. When she moved around the couch to bid their friends goodbye, her wife was immediately leaning into her, forcing Alice to leave without a hug, and for once Rio was glad. Alice never left without hugging her, and most of the time, Rio hated it. She hated being touched in general, unless it was her wife (obviously). 

The moment their door closed behind their friends, her wife turned to her, her arms crossing over her chest, “Where did you go? Why did you leave me to entertain all their questions? You realize Lilia asked me if I was breastfeeding!” 

Rio smirked, staring at her wife lovingly, “Well, are you?” 

Agatha smacked her arm, giving her a cold glare. 

She laughed lightly, pulling her wife by her hips against her, “I am serious, mi amor. Are you going to?” 

Her wife froze for a moment, seeming to figure out Rio being sincere in her question, “I don’t know. I didn’t think about it that far.” 

Rio nodded, kissing her head, “You have time to decide, my love. Don’t worry, now as for where I was, how about I show you? Go put on your sweater.” 

Agatha didn’t move, she simply reached forward, her hand fisting on Rio’s and tugging at it. Rio couldn’t stop her chuckle as she reached down and pulled herself from it to give it to her wife. She quickly grabbed another from their room before taking her wife’s hand and leading her out to the backyard. The moon was higher, shining down on them as the owls continued to coo. For a moment, Rio wondered if she was in some type of movie or a book with how the scene played out. The wind blew, but to her surprise it was warm, not overly chilling and she silently thanked whatever author was writing her story. Then, with her hand in her wife’s, she thought back to the baby, truly wondering what he would look like. In truth, she didn’t care whoever he looked like more, but as long as he was a blend of them both. One thing was for certain, she knew they were going to have a boy. She couldn’t shake the feeling, no matter how hard she tried. 

They climbed the hill until the fairy lights were in view and her wife stopped, her hand tugging her to pause. She turned, looking to see how the moon lit up her features, her bright blues welling with tears as her hair blew around softly, framing her face. Agatha did look like she was in some type of movie and Rio the helpless audience as she stared at the scene, unable to take her eyes away from the beauty of it. She watched her wife take in the scene before finally looking at her, tears pouring down her face.

“I hate you,” she growled, but Rio could only laugh, tugging on her hand. 

“Love you too, cariño, ” Rio smirked, pulling her wife beside her and kissing her head.

Rio guided her wife to the blankets, getting her to sit down before crouching down and pouring the tea from the thermostat. She sat down next to her wife who immediately leaned against her, her head on her shoulder. Warmth encircled Rio as they took in the scene around them, watching a barn owl in the distance swoop down to catch a field mouse. With deliberate ease, Rio opened the few bags of chips and freeze-dried fruit, the strawberries mainly for herself. She wrapped her arm around her wife, reaching for the small telescope. 

“Need another lesson in the stars, my love?” she teased as Agatha rolled eyes, but didn’t budge as Rio lifted the telescope. 

Rio smiled softly, watching Agatha’s face light up even as she tried to act nonchalant. The night air was crisp, and the quiet of the hill wrapped around them like a comforting blanket, amplifying their small, peaceful moment. Rio’s fingers brushed over Agatha’s hair, still so soft despite the cool breeze. 

“I know you love me, cariño ,” Rio murmured, her voice a playful whisper against Agatha’s ear, knowing exactly how to get under her skin. “It’s written in the stars.” 

Agatha smirked, pulling away slightly to give her a look of mock annoyance, but her eyes were warm and filled with affection. “You’re insufferable, you know that?” 

“Only because you love me,” Rio teased, gently pulling Agatha closer again as she pointed the telescope towards the sky. 

Her movements were deliberate, and she made sure to guide Agatha’s hand to steady it before letting her peer through the lens. The stars glittered in the sky, so many of them like scattered jewels, each one seeming to whisper secrets of the universe. 

“See, there,” Rio said softly, her voice low and full of awe, “That’s Orion, and next to it... there’s the Pleiades, a cluster of stars you could almost touch if you could reach high enough.”

Agatha’s eyes softened as she stared at the constellations, her breath coming out in little clouds in the cool night air. 

“I don’t know how you do it, Rio,” she murmured, her voice barely a whisper. “You always make everything feel... magical.” 

Rio’s smile softened, her heart swelling with love as she gazed at her wife, “Magic is real, Agatha. Just look at us.”

“You’re a fucking sap, have I mentioned that?” Agatha retorted, wrinkling her nose as Rio’s head fell back in laughter. “But, I guess you’re my sap. Tell me more about the stars.”

She smirked, taking the telescope and looking for the star-crossed lovers. Her movements were slow as she adjusted the lens and as she searched, she asked, “Have I ever told you about Vega and Altair?” 

Agatha shook her head, a spark of curiosity sparking in her eyes. 

Rio’s smirk softened as she found them, “The Japanese and Chinese versions are my favorite, and they’re similar, but I think I like the Japanese version a little more, although it’s more heart wrenching.”

She sat back, adjusting her position slightly to face Agatha, as if she were about to tell her a secret that had been passed down through generations. 

“In Japan, the story of Vega and Altair is known as Tanabata , or the Star Festival. It’s based on the same myth of the Weaver Girl and the Cowherder from the Chinese version, but it carries a deeper connection to the harvest season, and the story is celebrated every year on the seventh day of the seventh month.”

Agatha’s interest deepened, her eyes fixed on Rio as she spoke. 

“The Japanese version begins much like the Chinese one,” Rio continued. “Vega is Princess Orihime , the silk Princess, the daughter of the Emperor of the Heavens. She lived by a river in the sky and wove beautiful robes for the gods. But she was so dedicated to her work, she became lonely, and her father, seeing her sadness, arranged for her to meet a man named Hikoboshi , a handsome cow herder who lived on the opposite side of the Milky Way.” 

Agatha’s brows furrowed slightly in thought, “They fall in love, right?” 

“Yes,” Rio said, her voice softening. “Princess Orihime and Hikoboshi were so in love that they neglected their duties. Orihime stopped weaving, and Hikoboshi stopped tending to his cattle. The Emperor was furious when he saw their neglect and, in anger, separated them by the Milky Way, forbidding them to meet. The only way they could reunite was if they worked hard and showed their devotion. But the rule was that they could only meet once a year, on the seventh day of the seventh month.” 

Agatha’s voice was full of sorrow, “That’s so cruel… to be so far apart.” 

Rio nodded, her expression growing serious. “Yes, but here’s the part that makes it different from the Chinese version. In the Japanese version, the magpies form the bridge across the Milky Way, but there’s a twist. If it rains on Tanabata , the bridge disappears, and Orihime and Hikoboshi cannot meet. The magpies can’t fly through the rain, so they can’t form the bridge, and the lovers must remain apart for another year.” 

Agatha looked up at the sky, her expression filled with the weight of the story, “So, every year, it depends on the weather if they can be together or not.” 

“Exactly,” Rio said softly. “It’s a symbole of hope and longing. But it’s also about patience and love, you should know that ‘Miss English Major.’”

Agatha playfully pinched her. 

“Hey,” Rio laughed before growing serious, her face falling into something solemn as she glanced up at the sky. “Sometimes, the universe makes us wait, and sometimes, the rain comes and we have to be apart. But when the sky is clear, and the magpies form the bridge, it’s like their love is shining brighter than ever.” 

Agatha let out a soft breath, as if she were processing the bittersweet beauty of the story, “It’s a reminder that love doesn’t always work on our timeline. But when it does, it’s worth the wait.” 

“Yes,” Rio agreed, her voice full of affection as she looked at Agatha. “Love, like the stars, can be distant, sometimes clouded by rain or obstacles. But it always finds a way back. And every time it shines, it’s even brighter than before.” 

They both sat in silence for a moment, watching the stars, the gentle sound of the night filling the space between them. Rio’s hand found Agatha’s, and she squeezed it gently. 

“We may not be separated by the Milky Way, cariño , but I think I understand what it’s like to wait for the perfect moment to be with someone. And when it happens... it’s like the whole universe aligns.” 

Agatha leaned in closer, resting her head back on Rio’s shoulder. 

“I think I’d wait forever for you, Rio,” she whispered. 

Rio smiled softly, her heart full, “I’d wait for you too, Agatha. Every lifetime, if I had to.”

 

For a moment they sat in silence, Rio’s thoughts on the story she told as her wife smiled into her, nuzzling against her as if breathing in her scent. Eventually, Rio guided them to lay down, pulling the blankets around them as her wife curled into her, pressing every part she could against her. Agatha’s head rested on her chest and Rio found herself playing with her hair as they gazed at the stars. Soon, Agatha prompted her to tell more stories of the stars. Rio knew how much Agatha loved her voice. She remembered on one of their dates, Agatha begged her to take her stargazing again. It was when they were first together and she remembered clearly telling Agatha about the star signs when she caught her staring at her. 

“What?” Rio had paused in her story, glancing at her girlfriend. 

Agatha had shaken her head, “I just– nothing. Continue the story.” 

Rio had smirked, pulling her girlfriend to sit between her legs, having her back against her and Agatha’s head against her chest, “No, tell me.”

Agatha had hid her face, refusing. She smiled fondly, looking at how far her wife had come with her emotions and feeling safe, and Rio thanked the stars at how lucky Agatha had grown to feel safe enough to express them to her. It was torture in the beginning trying to figure out her partner’s emotions or at least get her tell her what was on her mind. 

“Agatha,” she had drawn teasingly. “I won’t continue the story if you don’t tell me.” 

Her girlfriend had grumbled before saying quietly, “I just like the sound of your voice.” 

Rio smirked, but her heart melted as she leaned down, pressing a kiss to her girlfriend’s head, “Careful, beautiful, it almost sounds like you like me.” 

She had groaned when she felt the elbow in her ribs, causing her girlfriend to chuckle, “Shut up and continue the story.” 

 

As the night trickled on, Rio’s leg had fallen asleep and the cold now had set in. Her wife was dozing in and out of sleep against her. Slowly, she nudged her awake and began packing up the food and blankets Agatha beside her before making their way back to the house. Rio set the bags aside before Agatha slipped her hand in hers and dragged her to their room. She laughed as her wife turned off the lights as they went. Instead of the bed, her wife took them in their bathroom. 

Before Rio could open up her mouth, Agatha was already turning on the water, a sharp hiss echoed before the steady flow of water filled the room with steam. Her wife turned to her, a bright smirk in her eye as she lifted up the sweatshirt and shirt with it. Rio followed, her heart pounding as her wife stripped naked in front of her, revealing pale skin, glowing in the bathroom light. She paused, staring at her before her gaze lowered to her wife’s breasts. They looked a little bigger, her nipples standing up in the cold air as her wife smirked at her. Hands slipped around her waist before hooking into her sweat pants, and pushing them down along with her boxers. Agatha simply took her hand, leading her into the shower. 

The moment Rio closed the door, she pounced, pressing her wife up against the black tile, her lips latching to hers. Agatha moaned into her mouth, her head tilting back slightly as Rio melted into her wife. Her body pressed against Agatha’s, feeling how well they fit, the water cascading over them. “No, no, mi amor. Tonight is about you.”

Agatha scoffed at her, flicking hair from her face as Rio met those azure blues, “If tonight is about me, then I want you sitting on my face.”

Rio pretended like the statement didn’t just run straight to her core. Instead, she chuckled, leaning forward and kissing her wife’s neck up to her ear where her teeth pulled down gently. 

“Uh-uh, that’s not how this works, my love. Tonight,” she paused, moving her lips to trial her neck again, listening to Agatha’s breath hitch. “I am going to worship the mother of my child.” 

She trailed her kisses down around her throat and up to her lips, whispering softly in a tone that always made Agatha crumble.

Her hands traced delicate patterns over Agatha’s back, every touch sending warmth flooding through her. Agatha gasped softly, her body responding to the possessive, tender care of her wife as Rio continued her slow, deliberate kisses. She leaned back, letting herself melt into the sensation, her fingers curling into Rio’s damp hair. 

“Rio…” she breathed, her voice shaky as she struggled to hold onto her composure. Rio smiled against her skin, savoring the sound of Agatha’s voice– soft, breathless, completely undone. She nipped gently at the tender spot just below Agatha’s ear, enjoying the way Agatha’s breath hitched even more, before continuing her ministrations down the curve of her neck. 

“I’ve been waiting for this,” Rio purred, her lips curling into a playful, seductive grin as she pulled away just enough to look into Agatha’s eyes. “For you to let go completely, to trust me with every piece of you. And tonight, cariño , I’m going to show you just how much you mean to me.”

Agatha was a vision, panting heavily as her eyes stared at her, but she wasn’t really looking at Rio. She could see the happy haze in the blues as Rio slowly set her wife’s legs down. They shook underneath her and with a steady hand around her waist, she steadied her against the wall. Rio placed her head against her wife’s waiting for her to come down and for her legs to properly work again.

Agatha reached up, a single thumb brushing away at Rio’s chin before cupping her face tenderly. Rio stared, searching her eyes as she saw nothing but love. Agatha smiled at her, drawing their lips in a slow, delicate kiss. When they pulled away, Agatha was wiping more of herself off Rio’s cheeks, making her laugh softly. Her wife quirked a smile before placing a brief kiss on her nose. 

“I love you, Rio,” she murmured, although her voice was hoarse. 

Rio’s heart fluttered, hearing the raw tenderness her wife spoke, “I love you more, Agatha.”

 

They showered after that, Rio running her hands through her wife’s thick chestnut mane, placing gentle kisses along her shoulders as she went. Her wife did the same, both of them taking in the quiet intimate moment of their shower. When they finally exited the shower, they moved to the bed, not caring if they soaked the sheets as their love making was slow. From their celestial vantage, Vega and Altair, scattered across the Milky Way’s endless reach, gazed down with awe and longing, envious of the boundless love that connected the pair below.

Chapter End Notes

“Yo sí y esta pequeña mierda te trajo té, mi amor,” = I do and this little shit brought you tea, my love.

Also, randomly when I was researching the stars, Chasing Cars came on and I was OMG OMG THIS IS PERFECT

Hope to see you all soon. School sucks already 💚💜

Chapter 10: Have You Heard There’s a Rumor in Westview Uni

Chapter Summary

Vidal pranced around as if she owned the place, and with Agatha Harkness beside her, but nothing flashed hotter than the hurt and anger from the rumors. They filled the halls, filled the quad, the student center, the classrooms, dining halls, the libraries and study lounges, and worse, even the departments. No one, from student to professor, could keep it out of their mouths. And worse, the more she listened, the more an obsessive hatred of the Dean took over.

OR:

Wanda starts shit. Students are students. Ireland fucks up. Rio is stressed. Jen is a good friend... Rio...is not okay

Chapter Notes

guys i am sooo sorry for taking forever. I fractured my thumb skiing and then just like died this week because of classes. I HAVE 8 FUCKING HOURS WORTH OF CLASSES ON WEDNESDAYS. The gods need to have mercy on me.

Anyway, enjoy 9.3k words. I didn't mean to write so much, also why it took soooo long.

Also, yes, the name is from the movie Russian princess movie, "Anastasia" and the song "A Rumor in St. Petersburg."

Strap on... in. this chapter is a wild ride. The demon says that I have been spoiling you with too much fluff.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Have you Heard What They’re Saying in the Halls ?



Wanda

 

The professor sat hunched over her desk, staring at the screen blankly. She felt drained, her eyes drooping as the second hand ticked on in the corner of the room. Everything felt wrong, her whole body sagging as she rubbed her eyes, reaching for her cold coffee only for it to spill the moment she brought it to her lips. Groaning, she reached for the half empty tissue box, and for once she was glad she wore black instead of her usual reds. The weeks have passed in a blur together at this point as she moved in a haze, a third-scorned, a third-angered, and a third-hurt. She avoided Agatha like the plague, the Dean’s words ringing in her ears like a mantra. She cringed every time, fighting back a whimper like she was a kicked puppy in the streets. Then, came the bark of anger, her fists clenching anytime the scary bitch was near. She was humiliated, not only in front of the HR person, but then the department. They whispered about her as if she couldn’t hear them, staring at her as if she had a second head that looked as if it had been bashed in a couple times, just to add to the disgust she already felt. Everything in her wanted to get back at Vidal, but then came the scorn again, her own utter disgust with herself at how she acted, and finally just hurt. Agatha wouldn’t even look at her. 

Which, fair, she admitted she deserved the silent treatment, but it still didn’t change how she felt. What twisted the knife deeper was the consistency of Vidal being in Poets’ Haven. The name itself was a complete joke. Poets’ Haven was anything but as Vidal pranced around as if she owned the place, and with Agatha Harkness beside her, but nothing flashed hotter than the hurt and anger from the rumors. They filled the halls, filled the quad, the student center, the classrooms, dining halls, the libraries and study lounges, and worse, even the departments. No one, from student to professor, could keep it out of their mouths. And worse, the more she listened, the more an obsessive hatred of the Dean took over. 

It all started those weeks ago, the day when both Dr. Vidal and Dr. Harkness took off work on the same day and came in late the next. A student had seen them walking in the quad towards their buildings, sharing a few words before separating, but the student swore they saw them in the same car. And when she heard the rumor that spiralled from it, she laughed at first…

 

Wanda sat in the teacher’s lounge, making her fourth cup of coffee of the day and it was only ten in the morning. She didn’t care, her nerves were shot anyway, her eyes red from the lack of sleep and she swore her eye bags had eye bags. She threw make up on the cover, but she knew her movements were sluggish, uncoordinated as she dropped her spoon, breaking the peaceful quietness of the lounge. She felt the eyes on her, and it took everything in her not to run away. She had grown used to it. In fact, she was sure the whole literature department and science department heard the moment she walked her path of shame back to Poets’ Haven after Mrs. Davis told her the next steps. She was on thin ice, and while she was technically allowed to interact with Dr. Harkness on matters of work, she was warned to limit it as much as necessary. To her luck, she hadn’t seen the woman since overhearing Dr. Vidal was pregnant– which meant one thing: Dr. Vidal was taken. Rio and Agatha were just friends and that if there was any possible way of becoming close with Agatha, then she wouldn’t have to worry about an overprotective scary ass spouse. 

(author note: I am cackling. Denial is like her only friend right now) 

Well, that was the thought until two professors came in, their conversation easily reaching her ears. 

“Do you hear what they are saying in the halls?” Dr. Crawdad drawled, coming beside Wanda as she grabbed a mug as well. 

Dr. Lune joined her side, nodding, “The students are a nosy bunch this year. I never would have thought Harkness and Vidal would be this semester’s hot topic.”

Crawdad turned to Lune, laughing slightly, “Oh, yes well it makes a little sense with them being the witches of their departments. You think they know what their students are saying?”

Wanda felt herself twitch as she moved to sit at a table, next to an older gentleman who was the department head, “And what are the students saying?

Crawdad and Lune whipped towards him laughing, “You can’t say anything to either of them. I do not want to be the next victim of the Dean’s death stare.” 

Lune nodded her head with her, “Please, that woman is scary enough as is.” 

The department head cocked his head, “Go on then, and no I won’t say anything. I believe the purple witch already has it out for me.” 

The room chuckled as Wanda’s grip on her mug tightened. She slouched down slightly, but no one acknowledged her. She was a mere shadow thanks to Dr. Vidal. 

Lune giggled like a fucking school girl, making her head rattle. She squeezed her eyes tightly for a moment, still wondering why the hell Harkness and Vidal were on the student’s mouths. Then, as if on queue, Lune and Crawdad eyed her, but there was a glint of something dark– a malistic humor gleaming through their eyes that Wanda couldn’t miss. 

“They say Dr. Vidal and Dr. Harkness are married.”

The room laughed, except for her… except for the head who only turned his head to the side. Wanda almost dropped her coffee, trying to look for a hint of a lie, but none of that came out, instead, the professors– the grown ass adults with years of schooling– started gossiping like their own students. 

“No!” 

“Yes! I could see it too! Vidal never interacts with anyone willingly except for a few professors in the music department and Harkness.”

“Vidal even has a ring.”

“So does Harkness. I spotted it the other day.”

….. So does Harkness…. 

Wanda held back her laughter now as she sat back, tuning out their chatter. A ring? No, Wanda made sure the moment she laid eyes on the woman to check if there was someone else in the picture. Plus, Agatha never said anything– she would say something if it were Vidal, right? How could another professor not say that their boss was also their wife? And Vidal? She didn’t strike Wanda as anything but straight, but in truth, she never had the best time telling. The more she thought about it though, the more she could see it. A woman like Vidal would never allow a man near her. Moreover, the rings that adorned her fingers, the constant blazers, the doc martens… goddess, she was an idiot– but not idiotic enough to believe Harkness and Vidal were married. She swallowed hard, trying to keep her face neutral, but it came heavy on her. If Vidal was pregnant, and they were married… then the child she carried was Agatha’s… but, even that sounded ridiculous. 

Before she could ponder more, the room went quiet, heels clicking into the space and Wanda didn’t need to look up to know who it was. 

“Harkness, you okay?” 

She peered up, watching the woman in purple turn on the kettle, “Fine, Crawdad, why?”

Her voice was sharp, irritated as she reached up for her tea bag.

Crawdad took a step back as the room grew heavy, “Um, you just look a little tired.”

Agatha only hummed, reaching to grab the kettle that had finished boiling. Wanda watched her hand, her left hand that had something sparkling on it. She froze. Everything in her spinning as the emerald jewel caught the fluorescents and sparkled. There was a ring and it was on her left hand. 

“Hey, is that new?” Lune asked, and Wanda felt herself seethe. 

‘Make yourself more obvious, why don’t you?’ she thought, bitterly as she reeled from what felt like a slap in the face. 

Agatha looked up slowly before glancing back at the ring, “No.”

“May I?” Lune reached out. She watched as the woman hesitated before extending her hand out to her. “Wow, it’s beautiful, Harkness. How come I didn’t see this sooner, and we started at the same time? How long have you been married?” 

Agatha took her hand back, speaking slowly, “Um, I don’t always wear it, but um, it will be ten years in June.”

Wanda set her mug down, pulling out her phone to just do something. Storming out would be dramatic, put too much attention on her, but to the gods she needed to get out of there. Agatha was married… has been for ten fucking years. She chewed the inside of her lip, her heart aching as it felt like she couldn’t breath. She had flirted with a married woman. 

She swallowed hard, trying to calm her breathing. It wasn’t that big of news, she could still be friends with the woman. It explained why she wasn’t interested in the first place. And was not like Agatha had said who her spouse was, but she would bet her own fucking life that it wasn’t Rio Vidal.

Her headache only got worse as that figure appeared, as if the mere thought of her could summon her. A knock echoed on the room before the professor stepped in, her cold, calculated gaze sweeping the room, luckily ignoring her as the head of the department nodded in greeting. 

“Good morning, everyone,” she said smoothly. “I see most of you are here. I wanted to announce at the end of finals week there will be a faculty mixer and the literature department will be in charge of it this semester. I trust the department head can choose the candidates who wish to be in charge of planning. I just require a draft to affirm any plans to stick to the budget. Thank you.”

With that Vidal turned on heel, barely sparing a glance at anyone as she walked out. Wanda watched Agatha, and to her pleasure, found the woman rolling her eyes. It was proof that Vidal and Harkness weren’t married to each other. 

The department head sat up, “Alright, who wants it?”

“Crawdad and I are in. Harkness, you care to join?” 

Agatha rolled her eyes again, taking her mug and walking out the door, “No.”

Wanda sat up. She needed something to keep her sane at this point– something to spill the chaotic energy that engulfed her, “I will.” 

“Perfect, I’ll have Vidal send you three guides and a budget. She will share the rest of the details as well.” 

 

It had been going on for five fucking weeks, the rumor that Vidal and Harkness were married. Yet, she barely heard a peep from Vidal or Harkness from that matter. She wondered if they even knew. The students were terrified of them, especially Vidal, and so was she. Again, it was the end of the semester. Finals were about to move in full swing and Vidal still hated her. She joined the party planning, but every meeting, even with Gilliane Crawdad and Stephanie Lune by her side, she was shaking before the Dean. Her gaze was harsh, her words clipped with her, and most of her ideas were turned down, especially the assigned seating by department. Vidal had snapped at her; the whole point of the mixer was for the faculty to mingle among each other, not staying trapped in their retrospective departments. Vidal had eyed her like she could see straight through her. She wanted a chance to talk to Agatha again, and selfishly, she thought she could do it at the mixer. Wanda hated Vidal for seeing through it, and her hate only grew when Vidal mentioned being able to bring their plus one. She internally groaned, wondering who would be looped around Harkness’ arm that evening, and her jaw clenched at the thought of Vidal. 

But Wanda tried to move on. She really did. It didn’t help that no one really talked to her in the department anymore, and the thought of trying to make friends with others at the party made her want to gag. But then there was Professor Vision. He was new, a strange man whose movements were more robot than human, but he was charming, sweet, and always searching for a way to make her laugh. And if he knew what happened between Wanda and Harkness, he must have not cared. He was a physics genius, somehow without a doctorate, and he was her date to the faculty mixer in two weeks. The last day of finals for the students, and her time to escape the rumors and whispers of Westview University for a month and a half. 

The best part, aside from escaping the rumors, was escaping the Dean herself. She was fucking everywhere, as if the campus wasn’t big enough for the two of them. Wanda always caught her with Agatha. Hell, one evening, she went off campus to pick up her lunch and there Vidal and Harkness were, sitting at a booth, and looking over the menu. It was weird, to say the least. And strangely enough, she also couldn’t help but notice how fit the dean still was for being pregnant. She wondered if that day she decided to share it with Agatha was the moment she found it, so that meant Vidal would come back next semester with a baby bump and everyone else would find out… unless… 

She cracked her neck, looking at the time as a dark smirk graced her lips. It was still early in the day and part of her should have thought of it sooner. If she could just add to the rumors, get under Vidal’s skin. It was petty, but it would be worth it, especially if Vidal’s exterior cracked. She just had to slip it to the right student– one who wouldn’t go crying to another professor and spill that it was her who started the new rumor that would eventually end up being true.  

 

 

Students 

 

Eva slammed her head down on the desk. It was their first finals week in two weeks and she felt dead already. Hell week, as they decided to call the week before finals, had taken it out of them and it hadn’t even started. She was surviving between late night Nando’s, Monsters, and the shitty vending machine coffee. Across from her, William and Ireland fell down on the couch, Ireland wiping at her tears as Eva sat up quickly, ignoring the protest in her back. 

William raised a hand, stopping her from speaking as they both stared at Ireland. Her eyes were bloodshot, her frame shaking as she hiccuped for her breath. Ryan stepped into the circle, placing a bottle of water in front of their friend before sitting in the chair next to Eva. Ireland hiccuped again, taking the water and chugging it before looking at Eva. They eyes met, and Eva knew before it even came out of her mouth.

“Chad cheated on me,” her voice was a broken whisper, tears spilling down her cheeks again. 

Eva moved, shooing William away and cradling her friend in her arms as she sobbed into her neck. She never liked Chad. He was the stereotypical frat fuck boy. She held her friend tighter, trying to help muffle her sobs. The friend group sat like that, in silence as Eva rubbed Ireland’s back, her hand moving in steady circles as Ryan left to get snacks and William stayed silently with them. Then, he cleared his throat, pulling out his laptop. 

“Come on, Ireland,” he said carefully. “You have to pull yourself together. The first draft for Vidal’s is due in a few hours.” 

“I don’t care,” she bit out, the tears burning her cheeks as her chest started to heave with anger. 

Eva pulled away slightly, “Yes, you do. You are not failing a freshman biology class when it is your major and you are too smart to do so, especially not over crying for fucking Chad. He took enough from you. Don’t let that asshole take away your grades too.” 

Eva and William locked eyes. Sure, it was cold, probably not what they should be focused on, but at this point, with Hell week on Monday and finals right after, they couldn’t do the stuff they wished to allow their friend to feel in peace. Her feelings had to come after, and it was then Eva and William were already planning a weekend trip for Ireland, but first they were going to make sure Ireland passed her exams with flying scores. 

It caught her attention as she sat up, “You’re right. But, please not tonight. Give me tonight.” 

William shook his head, “We can’t, not when Vidal’s first draft is due. You still have a bit to write.” 

“Then, I will ask her for an extension,” she snapped, making the other two freeze and Ryan, who just came back from the vending machine, dropped his armful of snacks. 

There was a beat of silence before Eva was pulling at Ireland’s face, expecting it, “Did you fall on your head too? Oh my gods, please tell me you didn’t just say what I think you said.” 

William sat up, “Ireland, do you even hear yourself? Chad couldn’t have made you this stupid.”

Ryan was on the floor, picking up his candy and chips and setting them on the counter, “Yeah, and please don’t, especially not after what I just overheard by the vending machine.” 

The group turned to look at him, even Ireland who was still tense in her anger, cocked her head for him to go on. 

Ryan sat up, before looking around the study lounge warily. His voice dropped, low and urgent, his eyes never leaving the door. 

“I overheard two professors and then some students whispering it. They say Vidal is pregnant.” 

Eva, William, and Ireland all froze at once, their exhaustion momentarily forgotten and even the heartbreak from Ireland. For a long moment no one spoke as they all eyed each other. Ireland was the first to break out of the spell. 

“Vidal?” she whispered, her voice hoarse. “Like Dr. Vidal– Dean Vidal– our science professor?” 

Ryan nodded, his gaze darting around the lounge again, “Yeah, I didn’t catch much, but from what I heard, it’s a big deal. Some students were saying she has been acting differently, but the other professors? They sounded like they were even trying to be hushed about– like Vidal would swoop from the fucking shadows.” 

Eva and William shared a glance, their brains still rattling through the exhaustion to figure out if this was true. Dr. Vidal– their terrifying, no-nonsense, private life biology professor– pregnant? On top of the rumors that they accidentally started that Dr. Harkness and her were married? To be fair, William still firmly believed that it was true. They had gone to the university faculty page and found both Vidal and Harkness had a masters from Harvard and a PhD from the world’s elite university, The Salemite’s University. It was near impossible to get into the PhD program there, even with masters from Harvard, and even with a masters from their own school. They barely accepted anyone, but Harkness and Vidal were the top professors here… and it seemed they must have known each since their master’s education. Friends were unlikely at this point. 

“Maybe that’s why Dr. Harkness has been more of a bitch lately, stressed about her wife,” William noted. 

Eva snorted, “Not this again. You really still think they are married?”

William threw his hands up, “Come on, Vidal is always with Harkness. I swear I have seen them in the same car, and let’s be real, Vidal only smiles while she is with Harkness. Plus, the rings, and they graduated from the same schools- plural might I emphasize.” He pointed his finger at Eva, his own teeth clenched, “ You ’ re the one who told me to be delusional here, and the moment we find evidence now you’re telling me it’s too much?” 

Eva shook her head, “Alright, you got me there, but Vidal being pregnant… that’s a terrifying thought.”

Ireland wiped at her cheeks, “And Harkness being a bitch because her wife is pregnant is proof enough?” 

Ryan sat up, chocolate staining his lips, “Makes sense to me. Who wouldn’t be stressed if that woman is pregnant? Vidal is a nightmare on a good day. Imagine what happens when she is pregnant.” 

They laughed slightly, even Ireland, although she still didn’t lose her tears. They went quiet, the rest of the group pulling out their laptops except for Ireland. 

“So, what do we do with this information?” William finally spoke.

Eva shook her head, “Nothing. We are not getting on their radar.”

Then, Ireland sat up, grabbing her stuff and packing it away. 

“What the hell are you doing?” Eva reached out, her face panicked as her friend wiped at her tears again. 

“I am going to ask for an extension–” the group gasped. “If Vidal is pregnant, you know, more push of hormones, more emotional, then she developed a heart, and will actually grant me an extension for once.” 

“Ireland. No.” 

Ireland ignored her, already sitting up straighter, smoothing out her hoodie like she was preparing for battle. 

William shook his head aggressively, “Oh my god, no. That’s not how this works. This is Vidal. She does not care about our suffering. Pregnant or not, she’d probably fail you harder just for asking.” 

Ryan looked genuinely concerned, “Ireland, please. I’m begging you to let this go.” 

But she was already grabbing her bag, slinging it on her shoulder, “No. This is the best chance I have. I need an extension. I’m going to ask.” 

Eva physically grabbed her by the shoulders, “Have you lost your entire mind? Ireland, Vidal eats people alive for fun. You’re walking into a trap.” 

Ireland shrugged her off, standing up with newfound resolve, “You guys are overreacting. If she’s pregnant, she’s probably softer now. Maybe she’ll actually care.” 

Ryan groaned, rubbing his face, “We’re going to watch her die. She’s going to die in front of us.” 

William flopped back onto the couch in defeat, “She was so young. So full of potential.” 

Eva sighed heavily, pinching the bridge of her nose, “You better text us when you’re dead so we know how to divide your stuff.” 

“I will be fine,” Ireland insisted, gathering her things and striding toward the door. The group watched in silent, horrified resignation as she left, marching toward certain doom. 

Ryan exhaled slowly, “Should we… follow her?” 

Eva shook her head, “No. She made her choice. Now we pray.”

 

Ireland strided down the cool biology hallways, her tears were heavier though as she felt a few stares, but she had to do it. She needed tonight to process it. She blinked hard, choking back a sob that rose in her throat as she could still picture Chad moving over the girl. She had no idea who she was, but when she walked into his room, the girl and her locked eyes, and at first, she saw hostility, but as Chad threw himself off of her. He turned to Ireland to apologize, and a dawn of recognition came over the girl. The girl got dressed and walked out, leaving them yelling at each other and eventually, Ireland had walked up, running to find William, only to find the girl. She was shocked when the girl apologized and told her she deserved better. 

It didn’t matter though, it still hurt and tonight, she just needed to drown her sorrows before locking in. Her friends were right. She wouldn’t let Chad get the upper hand by failing. She was better without Chad, but first, she needed to nurse her heart for tonight. 

She froze when she stopped at Dr. Vidal’s door. She whipped at her eyes, smearing more of her mascara. It was pathetic, but she needed it. She needed the woman’s pity, whatever empathy the cold woman could give her. With a deep breath, she knocked and waited. She stood there for a moment more, before knocking again.

And finally, the cold voice echoed from the other side, “Come in.” 

She walked in, finding the professor standing with her back turned. She was watering her plants, preening at the leaves before moving to the next, refusing to turn. She wore her usual attire, black pants and green blazer. Her hair pulled up into its signature claw clip. 

“You have ten minutes for bothering me outside of office hours,” Vidal snapped, still watering her plants. 

She stepped inside, closing the door behind her. It got the professor’s attention. Vidal turned slowly, crossing her arms as she sat back against her desk, looking at her curiously and staring at her face with a raised eyebrow. 

“Miss McGrath,” the professor hesitated for a moment. “Is there something that you need?” 

Oh shit, so she does know my name, she panicked. 

She took a deep breath, staring into the eyes of the beast, “Professor, I– I need to ask for an extension on the draft.”

She watched as her sharp brown eyes flicked up, pinning Ireland in place like a bug under a microscope. 

 “An extension?” she repeated, as if the very word offended her. 

Ireland nodded, her throat tightening. She hadn’t actually planned on crying in front of Dr. Vidal, but the stress, exhaustion, and heartbreak all crashed down on her at once.

“I—” her voice broke. “Chad cheated on me.” 

Dr. Vidal blinked slowly, “Excuse me?” 

Ireland wiped at her face furiously, “Chad. My boyfriend– I mean my ex-boyfriend. He cheated on me. I just found out, and I can’t focus, and I just need a little more time to finish my draft. Please.” 

She expected a lot of reactions from Vidal– impatience, annoyance, maybe even outright rejection– but what she wasn’t prepared for was the sheer shock on her professor’s face. Dr. Vidal stared at her like she had just announced she was a time traveler. 

“Are you…” her tongue pushed at the inside of cheek for a moment. “Are you crying?” 

Ireland sniffled, “Yes?” 

Vidal looked deeply, profoundly uncomfortable. She stood up, walking behind her desk as she played with her ring, seeming to want to create distance. Slowly, she sat down, staring at Ireland like she was dealing with a wild animal rather than a practical normal human capable of feelings. 

“You are… crying in my office.” 

Ireland felt her eyes widened before they twitched with annoyance and her jaw clenched. She couldn’t keep the tears from flowing as she snapped. 

“I just said that.” 

Dr. Vidal leaned back in her chair, her expression unreadable, “…why?” 

Ireland gawked at her, her frustration melting over. Dr. Vidal was a doctor, an esteemed professor in sciences and here she was questioning her on why she was crying. There wasn’t a single amount of warmth and it was then Ireland realized her mistake, but there was no changing it. Her heart ached in the worst possible way and she couldn’t stop herself from yelling at the professor. 

“Because Chad cheated on me!” 

Vidal blinked again, not even phased from her outburst. 

Then she tilted her head slightly, “I don’t know who that is.” 

Ireland groaned, throwing her hands up as she began pacing before her, “He’s– ugh, it doesn’t matter! The point is, I’m an emotional wreck, and I just need a few extra days on the draft. Please.”

Vidal was still watching her like she was a bizarre specimen under study, “…You are requesting an extension because you are emotional?” 

Ireland froze, “…Yes?” 

A long, excruciating silence followed. 

Then, Vidal let out a slow, careful breath and reached for her pen, “No.” 

Ireland felt her stomach drop as she whipped around the face of the Dean head on, “ What? ” 

“No,” Vidal’s tone was flat, matter-of-fact, as if the very concept of an extension was an alien language. “Your deadline remains the same.” 

Ireland stared at her for a beat. Her mouth opened in protest but only hot tears came. Her fists clenched as a sob left her mouth for a moment, but Vidal merely turned her attention to the papers on her desk, picking up a pen as seeming to forget Ireland standing there. 

“You-you can’t be-be serious.” 

Vidal nodded, her pen scratching away on the paper, “Deadly.” 

And that was it.

The dam broke. 

Ireland let out a sharp, frustrated scream, dropping her bag onto the floor with a thud. Her body moved before she could think, pacing frantically in the small office, her movements erratic and wild. Her heart was pounding so loudly she could barely hear herself over it, but the words kept coming, faster than she could stop them.

“You can’t be– this isn’t just a little bit emotional! I woke up this morning thinking everything was fine! I was ready to turn in the draft tonight! This class– this stupid fucking class– has been one of the best I’ve ever taken, but of course it had to be you teaching it!” 

Vidal had stopped writing. Her fingers had gone still, the pen slipping from her hand as she stared at Ireland, her sharp, icy composure fracturing into something that almost looked like shock. 

Ireland didn’t care. 

“This day was supposed to be easy before you and all the other professors decided to torture us and act like we’re just fucking machines! But no!” she let out a bitter laugh, her voice raw and hysterical. “Because I went to Chad’s, and I found him in bed with someone else ! Someone else ! And I thought– I foolishly fucking thought– that he loved me!” 

Her breath came in short, sharp gasps. 

“And then– then!– when I thought, for one second, that I had even the smallest bit of hope, because you’re pregnant– that maybe you’d have a fucking ounce of empathy– ” 

Shit, shit, please just strike me dead, the bucket of water hit Ireland as she froze, the words dying on her lips. 

Silence filled the room, pure deafening silence as she peaked a gaze at the professor. Vidal’s expression shifted entirely. Gone was the indifference, the boredom, the unimpressed glare. Instead, she looked more than just stunned– she looked like she just swallowed the red pill and was now in the matrix. For the first time, Ireland saw Dr. Rio Vidal genuinely caught off guard. 

And then, after a long, agonizing beat– “ What ?” 

Her voice was different now. Lower. Sharper. Ireland’s rage sputtered slightly, her chest still heaving, but something about the way Vidal said it made her suddenly, acutely aware of the fact that she had just made a very serious mistake. Vidal blinked once, then leaned forward slightly, her piercing brown eyes narrowing. 

“Say that again.” 

Ireland swallowed, throat dry, “You’re pregnant?” 

Another long pause. 

Then– Vidal laughed. 

It wasn’t a happy laugh. It wasn’t even amused. It was sharp, cold, and furious. 

“Oh,” Vidal said, pressing her hands flat against the desk as she pushed herself up to stand. “Oh, you’ve got to be kidding me.” 

Ireland took an instinctive step back. 

Vidal was pissed. 

“You came into my office, crying over some pathetic little boy, demanding an extension because of a rumor?” Vidal’s voice was deadly quiet, but it burned with fury. 

Ireland opened her mouth, but nothing came out. Vidal let out a sharp, humorless breath, running a hand down her face before muttering something rapid and low in Spanish. Then, she turned back to Ireland, her glare scorching. 

“Get. Out.” 

 

Rio 

 

The moment the door slammed shut, Rio slouched, burying her face into her hands. She stared at the desk in between the crack of her fingers as hot frustrated tears began to well up. Today was already long enough. She had a series of meetings, dealing with incompetent idiots. She couldn’t even have lunch with her wife who was more than irritated by it, but luckily didn’t start a fight, and the rumors– the fucking rumors. Her and Agatha’s names were everywhere. The whispers instantly that they were married. It made her skin crawl with every turn in the hall, fuck, even in the professor’s lounge they whispered. No one had the fucking balls to just ask. No, instead it was rampant all over the halls, the classrooms, her labs– the rumors had taken over Westview University. And worse, it literally started the day after her and Agatha came back to work after her wife’s six week ultrasound. 

Rio was losing her goddamn mind, her mind circling from the rumors that seemed to undermine her every move, the lingering stares at her, and worse– the possibility of it stressing her wife out. She briefly wondered if this was her fault. Maybe, she should have said she was married from the beginning. Sure, her workers might have thought of the unfair advantage of her wife, maybe undermined her position in faculty meetings, or maybe the people who hated her, would also hate Agatha and vice versa– it would have been better than being left to gossip. Rio buried her face further in her hands. This wasn’t supposed to go this way. Who knew that wanting a little privacy would be this much of a fucking pain? And worse, maybe if Rio was just transparent in the beginning, Maximoff would have never flirted. 

This was all her fault. 

But, life wasn’t finished with her– no, it wanted her horizontal, in a grave, because the words from the student who had the audacity to sob to her, just felt compelled to do it, because of the new rumor: she was pregnant… Rio saw red, grabbing the closest thing– a pen– and hurling it across the room. 

Where the hell did they even get that from? 

She swallowed hard as a wave of self-consciousness passed through her. She looked down at her stomach, pulling her shirt back, but her own abs just greeted her. She knew she was being silly with that idea. She worked out almost every day, well sure she had been slacking since her koala bear of a wife decided she was her tree to cling on, but even then, her appearance hadn’t changed. Her wife was faintly showing, nothing major. It was like someone was pushing out their stomach and this time, it wasn’t bloating, it was their baby, making himself known. It was easy to hide, her wife wearing loose sweaters, some cardigans to pull over her body, even a crew-neck hid her belly, but it was there and oh, how Rio adored it.

Her gaze shifted to the ultrasound that sat proudly on her desk in a frame. She usually put the frame away during office hours, but it always came back out when it was just her time. In between grading, and even Zoom meeting, she found herself staring at the image, and in one week, her pequeño extraterrestre would finally look like a baby. Luckily, it was on the weekend, learning their lesson that they really couldn’t take the day off together. But at this point, Rio was wondering if it even mattered anymore. The rumor was there, running rampant like a wildfire through the halls of the university. If they could have just kept it, if Rio would have kept her fucking cool or if Stark could have just handled Wanda, then her and her wife could live their life peacefully, and the moment they left for maternity, they wouldn’t have to hear the rumors, the whispers aboutthem , or the stares like she was animal in a zoo… but no… none of that happened as she had planned, and Rio couldn’t shake that it was all her fault. It was her fault for the stress that she ultimately subjected her wife to, even though she tried her damn hardest to protect her. 

Rio had failed. 

Rio Vidal, the protector of her family, had failed, before she could even begin to have that family. The moment her wife had unveiled the positive pregnancy test, she vowed she would protect them: both their physical healths– to her wife’s mental health. She would never tell her wife, but she spent hours researching everything and anything. She poured over articles on symptoms, what to do when they hit, foods her wife could eat, to the vitamins and creams she would need, how the baby would grow inside her, the mental health aspects her wife would face, to what it mean to breastfeed or use a formula, the birthing process– even home birth if they couldn’t make it the hospital in time– anything she could do to make her sure wife would be safe, loved, and as comfortable as possible. Stress wasn’t good for her wife or for the baby, and she already failed at protecting her from that simple factor. 

Tears slipped from their hold as she sucked in her breath. If she couldn’t protect her wife from something so simple as stress from rumors, then how the fuck was she supposed to protect her own child? And with that thought, she crumbled. Rio placed her arms into her desk, muffling her soft sobs. What broke her further was that she couldn’t even go to her wife. It would go against everything that she had tried to do. She was supposed to be the strong one, and she wouldn’t let her wife see her crying over more rumors. The rumors that she was pregnant. 

Rio sat up, letting out a low confused laugh. Then, her tears died and her eyes darkened. Whoever started the rumor will wish they hadn’t even breathed life. She felt her eyelid twitch, something dark twisting within her. Whoever started the rumor, spread it among the students, and probably the faculty– oh, she would make their life not even worth living. Murder was off the table for legal reasons– but she would enjoy slicing their neck open in a different way. 



Jennifer

 

Jennifer sat back in the lounge, her head reeling as she nursed her coffee. Everywhere she went, all she heard was Rio’s and Agatha’s names between faculty and students alike, and it drove her crazy, especially knowing the truth. From what she knew, the pair had finally started wearing their rings, and Alice and Lilia were right– the rumors were all over the university that they were married– true as they might be, but fuck it was annoying. She might have not liked Agatha, but she was annoyed for her. But then, the new rumor was unearthed, and it spread faster than anything she had ever heard.

As if on queue her phone buzzed:

My Light 🧡: Babe, have you heard the rumors ? 

 

As if I could unhear them. :Me

How the hell did this even start? Is Rio actually pregnant? :Me

 

My Light 🧡: No idea, but she isn’t the one pregnant

 

What? Are you saying that the purple witch is? :Me

 

My Light 🧡: That’s for Rio to say. But I need a favor, beautiful

 

No, absolutely not :Me

 

My Light 🧡: You don’t even know what I was going to say

 

I already know. You must have a death wish for me :Me

 

My Light 🧡: You know that is not true. She is not that scary 

 

Lilia literally muttered the Death tarot that night at the bar :Me

 

My Light 🧡: Lilia can be a kook in the most loving way possible

My Light 🧡: Please, my enchantress

My Light 🧡: Ugh, I’ll do a face mask or whatever with you tonight please

 

It’s fucking Rio Vidal. How am I supposed to talk to her? :Me

 

My Light 🧡: Just tell her not carry all this stress alone.

My Light 🧡: knowing her, she is probably going to blame herself. 

                    That woman is fiercely loyal and protective of her wife. Right now, 

                    she probably thinks she isn’t doing enough for her. Just remind her 

                    that she is human, not a cosmic entity-incarnate. 

 

Fine, but if die :Me

It’s your fault :Me

With that, Jennifer stood up, going to dump her coffee when the cold voice found her. She froze for a moment, listening to her impending doom. 

“I am not pregnant. Do you even hear yourself? Stop wasting my time and yours by listening to these rumors!” 

The door to the lounge opened, slamming behind the Dean as she stomped in, going to the printer that began running. Jen turned slowly, swallowing her nerves as she followed Rio’s stiff movements. Her fingers tapped impatiently against the heavy machine, her neck even twitching as Jen could see the scowl from her side profile. 

“Long day?” she spoke softly. 

Dark eyes whipped towards her, “Don’t even start, Kale.” 

She flinched, watching the usual professional demeanor crack upon the woman. 

“I wasn’t, Vidal, but you are one more ‘congratulations’ from killing someone and you and I both know that won’t look good for your image.” 

Rio didn’t respond, she simply turned, grabbing her papers, “Not here, Jennifer. Please.”

Jen stood up straight, following the Dean down the hall. She watched as the students gawked, their mouths open as their eyes went to Rio’s stomach. She understood. She too would be just as furious if someone started a rumor she was pregnant when she wasn’t. It felt more than just invasive and worse, it was almost embarrassing, but from Rio, she could tell the woman was frustrated and tired, especially with how much she wanted her private life respected. 

The moment they walked into Rio’s office, they sat down, Rio reaching for a picture frame and turning around. Jen froze, staring at the white shape among the shadows. It wasn’t a full baby, but she studied with a midwife once upon a time. The baby in the image she guessed was six weeks old, its form still taking shape. With a deep breath, she collected herself and turned back to Rio. 

“I’ll start with this, so you don’t kill me. How far along is your wife?” 

Rio stared at her for a moment, her gaze intense before it dawned on her, “Alice told you, figures. She’s eleven weeks along now. We have the twelve week scan next weekend.”

“Congratulations, Rio, seriously,” she said, keeping her voice earnest. 

Rio only nodded, taking the frame and smiling softly down at it. Jen stared, having only seen something similar from the cold witch across from her when the woman looked at her wife. It was a rare moment, one reserved for those she loved and something grew in Jen for Rio, something that was a little bit more than respect– more like understanding. Rio was just a woman protecting her family. 

“Is there anything that I can do?” she said slowly. “I know Alice and I bullied you two for the rings but it only seems to have brought you more trouble.” 

Rio took a deep breath, her eyebrows pinched together, “Just, if you or anyone could find who started the rumor and also dissuade it. I don’t need it to stress my wife out.”

Jen nodded, sitting up, “Of course, and look, I am not Alice, but if you need anyone to talk to that isn’t your wife, I am here. You have a lot going on, try not to keep it all in. You don’t always have to be the strong one, even with your wife being pregnant. I admire your love for her, but even you have to realize, this isn’t in your control. You are only human.”

Rio snorted her laughter, sitting back in her chair, “Alice sent you.”

“Yeah, and she told me to remind you that you are human. People are cruel, they were going to talk, whether you and Agatha were out before or later.” 

Rio sighed, pinching the bridge of her nose, “I know. I know that, Kale.”
