Jen studied her, noting the dark circles under her eyes, the tension in her shoulders. She had seen Rio furious before– had even been on the receiving end of it more than once– but this wasn’t just anger. This was exhaustion. 

“You don’t look like you’ve been sleeping.” 

Rio let out a dry laugh, “No, really? What gave it away?” 

Jen rolled her eyes, “I’m being serious.” 

Rio exhaled sharply, setting the ultrasound frame down. She traced a finger along its edge, her expression softening just slightly. 

“Just worried for her. Who knew people could get obsessed with people who are basically strangers?”

Jen shook her head, “It’s more than that, Rio. You know it.”

Rio opened her mouth for a moment, then closed it, sitting back against her chair with a defeated sigh. 

“Look, I get it– you’re used to handling things on your own. But this is different. This is big. You’re gonna be a mom, and that means you don’t have to shoulder everything alone.” 

Rio exhaled through her nose again, rubbing at her temple, “I just… I want to make things easier for Agatha. She’s already dealing with enough between her classes, the pregnancy, and–” she gestured vaguely, “ – this bullshit.” 

Jen nodded, understanding, “And who’s making things easier for you?” 

Rio blinked. 

“You’re running yourself into the ground, Rio,” Jen continued. “That’s not gonna help Agatha. It’s not gonna help the baby. And it sure as hell isn’t gonna help you.” 

Rio looked down at the ultrasound again, her fingers tightening around the frame, “…I just want them to be okay.” 

“They will be,” Jen said firmly. “But only if you stop pretending you don’t need help.” 

Rio huffed, “You and Alice really are insufferable.”

Jen smirked, “Yeah, well, someone has to make sure you don’t implode.” 

Rio let out a slow breath, then leaned back in her chair, looking at Jen with something almost like gratitude, “Thanks, Kale.”

 

 

Rio 

 

Rio returned home late that evening. It was almost nine when she opened the door, kicking off her heels and throwing her coat on the hook before making her way into the kitchen. She threw her keys on the counter before finding food left for her in the fridge, dinner her wife saved for her. She had meetings that ran late, some with students, but mostly faculty. She hated it. All she wanted to do was to go home, lock herself in her greenhouse and fucking breathe. Worse, she knew she wasn’t in the mood to be touched and her wife, ever the koala bear… well, she hated the idea of her own needs getting in the way of her pregnant wife’s. Normally, Agatha never complained if Rio didn’t want to be touched. There were just moments where Rio felt trapped by the idea of someone touching her. It was overwhelming, too human. 

She had always been like this, where some days she couldn’t be touched at all. Her siblings were never physically affectionate and what she could remember of her parents, neither were they. Her abuelita was a different story, but she adored her. Sex was fine– great even… all before Agatha. With Agatha, she barely felt like this. She had her days, sure, and her wife was ever attentive, knew and never pushed or was offended. Agatha would just be there for Rio in other ways, making her meals, picking her up coffee or flowers, or allowing Rio to rant on whatever topic she wanted to talk about, even if they both knew Agatha had no idea what she was talking about. But most days, she craved her wife’s touch and it wasn’t just about sex. She craved the way her wife would cradle her face, hold her at night or allow Rio to hold her. The lingering touches on her shoulders, on her back, on her arm– it was their love language and fuck she loved it…. but she didn’t deserve that now… not when she was failing her own wife.

Rio swallowed the roast thickly before shoving it back in the fridge and making her way into the living room. It was quiet except for the faint hum of music from the television. She looked up, smiling as she saw Sailor Song playing. It was their song. One of many as her wife was playing their shared playlist full of all the songs that reminded them of each other. She smiled softly looking over the couch and finding her wife half-awake as she crocheted a green and purple blanket. 

“Good evening, my love,” Agatha smiled up at her, setting the needle and blanket down. 

Rio smiled faintly, her gaze going to her wife’s stomach. Her wife just wore shorts and her sports bra, leaving her belly open for her to see. She reached down, hesitantly, her hand shaking slightly, placing it on her wife’s growing bump, but she remained quiet, unsure how to tell her wife she needed a moment. 

“Long day?” 

She nodded, “Yeah, too long, cariño. ” 

Her wife nodded, but kept staring up at her expectantly, but Rio couldn’t. Something gnawed at her and she felt restless. She removed her hand, ready to walk away but her wife’s hand caught her. She tensed, feeling the warmth there, but didn’t she pull away like she usually did. She let herself freeze, meeting the confused blues.

“Come cuddle with me, my love?” her wife asked softly. 

Rio swallowed thickly, her eyes darting around for a moment before leaning down, and kissing her wife’s head to make sure she knew how much Rio loved her, “Later, cariño . I am going to go run. I’ll join you in bed after.” 

Agatha nodded, but she saw something in her eyes, but Rio recognized it wasn’t in the way Agatha usually looked at her. It seemed clouded, for once uncertain and almost like she was scared. It reminded her the first time Rio did this dance with her when they were dating, but then, Agatha was more patient and not clouded by how unbalanced her hormones were now. Rio hesitated, then leaned down again, brushing her lips softly over Agatha’s. Her wife hummed in response, her free hand ghosting over Rio’s cheek before pulling away, letting her go. 

“Don’t be long,” she said softly. 

Rio chuckled, “I’ll try not to be.”

She turned to their room, changing into shorts and her own sports bra before making her way to the basement where a makeshift cave was set up. It was large with a big couch in the center, a bar against the wall and a pool table to the side along with an actual table. A larger TV was across from the couch and in the back was Rio’s gym supplies. She climbed up onto the treadmill, throwing on her headphones. 

 

Rio ran.

The treadmill hummed beneath her as she pounded against it, her breath steady, her pulse thrumming. It wasn’t enough– not yet. The frustration still clung to her skin, a weight she couldn’t shake. So she pushed herself harder, ran until her lungs burned and her legs ached, until all she could focus on was the rhythmic slap of her feet over the music against the belt. 

 

She didn’t know how long she had been down there when she finally slowed to a stop. Sweat dripped down from her forehead as she braced herself against the treadmill’s rails, catching her breath. Her legs quivered, her bad knee aching in protest at what she had done and she silently cursed herself for tearing her ACL all those years ago. The pain always ghosted over her, haunting her on random days and most of the time when she ran. She stood there for a moment, staring at the blank wall in front of her, before exhaling sharply and heading upstairs to shower. 

The hot water helped. Some of the tension melted from her muscles, but not enough. By the time she stepped out, dressed in sweatpants and a tank top, her body felt lighter, but her mind still churned. She just needed to eat, cool down, and then she’d crawl into bed next to her wife. Things would be fine. 

She limped slightly into the kitchen, opening the fridge and grabbing the plate of food Agatha had set aside for her. As she sat down at the counter, light footsteps padded up behind her and Rio had to fight herself from tensing. She turned though, staring at the soft curve of her wife’s growing belly. The sight made something in Rio soften, but she was too tired, too restless, to fully give in to the warmth curling in her chest. Agatha stepped up behind her, hands gentle as they brushed over Rio’s shoulders, but Rio flinched. Just slightly. But it was enough. Agatha’s hands recoiled like she had been burned. Rio winced as she heard her wife’s sharp inhale.

“Oh,” Agatha said. 

Just that. One syllable, quiet and fragile, like a thread stretched too thin. Rio turned, guilt clawing its way up her throat. 

“ Cariño , it’s not–” 

“No, it’s fine,” Agatha cut in, stepping back, arms crossing over her chest like she was holding herself together. 

Her blue eyes– so full of warmth, of love, of everything that made Rio feel safe– were suddenly distant. Guarded. And then, Agatha smiled. It was a small, tight thing. Wrong. A shield. A defense. 

“I mean, I’ve just been carrying your child for eleven weeks,” she said, her voice light– too light. “But yeah. You can’t even handle me touching you.”

 Rio’s stomach twisted, her hands curling into fists. “That’s not fair–” 

“Not fair?” Agatha laughed, but there was nothing real about it. Just sharp edges and broken glass. “I haven’t seen you all day. I didn’t even get to have lunch with you. And then you come home, you don’t even look at me, and when I try to touch you, you flinch like I’m a fucking stranger.” 

Her voice cracked, just slightly, but Rio heard it. Felt it like a knife to the gut. 

“I get it, Rio,” Agatha continued, her arms tightening around herself. “I get that you don’t always like to be touched. I know that. But you flinching away from me?” her breath hitched, her eyes shining in the dim light. “That hurts.”

 Rio gritted her teeth, fighting the storm rising inside her. 

“I didn’t do it on purpose,” she said, her voice low, strained. 

Agatha scoffed, shaking her head, “Doesn’t make it hurt any less.” 

Rio exhaled sharply, her body aching with frustration. 

“I love you, Agatha,” she said, the words raw, desperate. “I love you more than anything. But some days, I just–” she swallowed hard. “I just can’t .”

Agatha’s eyes flashed, “Can’t what? Can’t let your wife touch you?” 

Rio grit her teeth, “That’s not what I mean. I just need some time to–” 

“Well, take all the fucking time you need,” Agatha snapped. 

Before Rio could say anything else, Agatha stormed past her, heading straight for their bedroom. 

“Agatha–” 

She moved to follow, but her leg buckled, a sharp jolt of pain tearing through her muscles. She caught herself on the counter, gritting her teeth, pushing forward– too slow. She watched as her wife slipped into their bedroom, the door slamming shut in her face. Rio sucked in a sharp breath, her entire body growing rigid. 

Then came the lock. 

Click. 

A sound so small, so simple, but it echoed in the house like a gunshot, aiming straight for Rio’s already bleeding heart. And then, from the other side of the door, muffled but cutting, came Agatha’s voice.

“Sleep on the fucking couch.”

Chapter End Notes

HAHAHAHAHA

Don't hate me too much, but don't worry guys. This story is very much happy. Angst is in the tags for a reason tho. but I will emphasis, nothing bad happens to them !!!!

Anyway, Love you all. Hope to see you soon 💚💜

Chapter 11: Run ~ Snow Patrol

Chapter Summary

cause I kinda felt bad... not really... but I did at the same time ???

ANY WAY

HERE

DAMN

(i love you all 💚💜)

OR:

sex... come find out if it's make up sex or not

Chapter Notes

okay this was my fault- Rio did NOT tear her ACL in that moment, past injury. that was my mistake with writing

but also,

I was cackling when I woke up to my email inbox being blown up. Thank you guys for your love and comments, you guys are so funny and sweet.

I realized that I could not let you guys go on another week without updating, so hear is 7k works from my sleep deprived ass... also I really wanted to write this scene 💚💜

ps, I used google translate and then put the translation afterward, i figured it would be easier. My RioSpeaksSpanish stans rise up, it's your time to shine

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Rio

She stared at the door, breath shallow, hands clenched at her side. She could hear Agatha moving inside– shuffling, the rustle of blankets, the sharp intake of breath that meant she was crying but trying not to. Rio wanted to fight. Wanted to completely blow the door off its hinges, fall to her knees, and beg Agatha to hear her…to explain, to fix it. But what was there to fix? She had failed her, and worse she had hurt her wife– her pregnant, exhausted wife. The woman she had promised to cherish, to love, to protect– the woman Rio’s black heart beat for. And instead– Rio pressed a trembling hand to her forehead, inhaling shakenly before leaning against the door for a moment. She knew Agatha heard her by the way she had gone still, not a sound behind the door now. With an unsteady exhale, she pulled away. Agatha told her to sleep on the couch. So she would. Even if it felt like a death sentence. 

Her body was pure weight as she moved down the hall, each step protesting under her as she turned off the lights in a broken haze. When she fell to the couch, she found it uncomfortable, but it didn’t matter, because Rio deserved it. She failed. She hurt her… how the fuck was she supposed to be a good mother? With that, she curled into herself, hoping to block the weight in her chest, but it didn’t stop. She turned, grabbing a pillow and burying her face into it, trying– failing– to stop the tears burning at her eyes. She never cried, not like this. This was a different pain, something she never felt before. Her shoulders shook, her breath hiccuping in sharp, uneven gasps, as she sobbed into the pillow like it was the only thing keeping her together. She could still hear Agatha’s voice in her head. The way it had wavered. The way she had pulled away. The way she had looked at Rio like she didn’t recognize her. 

And it was all her fault. 

Rio pushed away the only person in the world she cared about, the only person in the whole universe that she loved. Agatha was Rio’s everything. She was the earth, sun, sea, and stars. She would do anything for her, to the gods, she loved that woman so much it was painful. Agatha was the first person to understand her– if not the only person. Sure, she had her friends and Oriana was the closer sister, but no one compared to Agatha. She swore the woman knew her better than she knew herself. 

Rio clawed at herself, hating that she hurt her, hating at who she was. If only she could have been stronger, to be able to suck it up, and would have just held her damn wife like she requested. She was carrying her child and Divine Mother– Rio couldn’t breathe. 

She sat up, cradling her knees to her chest, the pillow now tucked between her knees and chest as she screamed her sobs into it. She couldn’t shake the hurricane of thoughts, the way her own mind felt like a hive of angry wasps, stinging their own home, and leaving nothing but hurt. She was going to fail as a mother. This child– gods, this child was going to hate her. She wasn’t enough for either of them. She choked again, clawing for breath, but refusing to lift her head from the pillow. 

Her hands shook, desperately clutching the pillow tighter as if it could somehow tether her to reality. Every thought felt like a dagger to her heart– sharp and relentless. The stinging, overwhelming guilt refused to leave. Her wife just needed her, Agatha who hated being vulnerable, who hated voicing what she needed, voiced what she needed and Rio just pushed her away. She wasn’t the rock she promised to be, she wasn’t the partner her wife deserved. She thought of their first date, how Agatha had made her feel seen, feel whole. The quiet moments between them, when the world seemed to fade away, leaving just the two of them in a safe, perfect bubble. Rio couldn’t remember a time when Agatha wasn’t in her heart, so deep it was embedded in her very soul. The idea of losing her– of failing her– was unbearable. 

She squeezed her eyes shut, trying to block out the image of Agatha’s face, how her wife had pulled back, as if she had been burned by Rio’s touch. Every word Agatha had spoken in that fragile, broken voice echoed in Rio’s mind, cutting deeper with each replay. 

Why couldn’t I just be here for her? she wondered. 

The answer was simple. She wasn’t enough. Not strong enough, not kind enough, not patient enough. She had always been one to keep her walls, to shut people out– worse than her wife. She learned to turn off her emotions, to just be something powerful, void of emotion… fuck, she had even shut Agatha out at times before. She had learned to survive by shutting off parts of herself, to keep people at arms length– cause in the end, they would just leave whether it was the road to death or just not being able to understand her… but she never realized the damage that would cause to the one that mattered most. Agatha had always given, always trusted her, and she had repaid that by being distant when her wife needed her warmth the most. 

Her chest ached, a deep, raw ache she had no words for, but the pain of it was consuming. Rio wrapped her arms around herself tighter, desperate for comfort that wasn’t coming. 

She ruined everything. 

All in one day, the universe stopped working around her. A single frayed thread… it was pulled and her natural order of life dismantled. Her vision blurred as her body fell back against the couch, and she just stared up at the ceiling. She couldn’t sleep. She couldn’t stop crying. There was no escaping the overwelcoming, crushing guilt. It was a nightmare with no end, just endless spirals, each one pulling her deeper into darkness. 

 

Rio drifted in and out of consciousness, never fully sleeping, never fully awake. She listened to her pounding heart in her head as the tension wouldn’t leave her. Everything was fuzzy as she imagined it all differently. If she and her wife would have just packed their shit and move to buck-fuck nowhere Alaska, or somewhere in French Alpes maybe the Swiss, or even somewhere deep in the German Black Forest. The Black Forest sounded nice. A cottage in the middle of trees, nature, and something deeply connected to the green. No noisy students. No fucking Wanda Maximoff. No immature colleagues. Just her, her wife, and their child. And she would be there for both of them, protect them, and love them like they deserved. She half wondered if in another life her and her wife had done just that. She couldn’t imagine a timeline in the multiverse where she wouldn’t be with her wife. And as she told her wife before, she would wait a thousand years, and love her for all to come. 

She drifted into the dream a little further, something to soothe ache and guilt, but it only twisted the knife deeper. She saw her wife in a simple purple dress, running in a meadow with a smaller figure with dark brown hair, darker than Agatha’s but lighter than her black hair. She saw herself explaining how rivers worked, what different trees and plants were before laying in the same meadow at night with both of them in her arms as she told stories of the stars.

 

The breath left her body. 

Her eyes flew open as she slowly registered something just collided into her, pinning her deeper into cushions of the couch. She was frozen, disoriented as the weight had punched the air from her lungs. It was warm though, gentle and rough all at once. Fingers clawed at her chest as it fought to fuse with her body. Then, a familiar scent surrounded her: lavender, cinnamon, and something that was distinctly her wife as the weight trembled on her. 

She blinked, wondering if it was just her desperate mind trying to fill in the absence of her wife, but as her head cleared she felt it against her neck. The breath was hot, blowing out raggedly, sending chills through her. Then there was something wet, a damp heat soaking her skin on her neck and running down to her chest. Her mind stuttered, trying to catch up– her hearing finally coming back. 

A soft sobbed echoed against her, breaking the silence of the house. It gripped her down to her very bones, as if hands had wrapped around her bones, pulling out her skeleton form, a form that wouldn’t change– a strange sense of begging, longing, praying that Rio wouldn’t disappear. Her mind was still catching up, trying to pull out of her own spiral, but her body worked. Her arms moved without thought, pulling the body closer to her, but a loud wail was the final punch that got her to regain her senses. 

Rio blinked. 

This was real. 

This wasn’t a dream. 

Agatha. Crying.

Her wife was here against her chest, sobbing loudly and clutching onto her as if she were to let go, Rio would disappear. 

Rio’s entire world tilted. 

“Agatha–” 

“Shut up,” Agatha growled, but her voice was thick and broken. She clung to Rio with trembling fingers, burrowing into her, pressing herself as close as possible. “Just– shut up and hold me.”

Rio obeyed, her arms wrapping tighter around her wife, tucking her face underneath her chin. Agatha continued her sobs, burrowing her face into Rio’s neck as Rio ran her fingers through her wife’s hair, a gesture that always calmed her. Within minutes, her wife’s breathing was steading. The violent sobs fell into something that was fragmented, a few hiccups and her breathing staccato.

She still refused to let go, the tears back in her eyes, but it wasn’t out of complete guilt, there was a small relief that her wife was here. Her Agatha was holding onto her like a lifeline. She was here in Rio’s arms where she belonged. Her wife nuzzled closer as Rio pressed a gentle kiss to her temple, knowing no words in Spanish or English could erase the pain she caused her wife. So she did what she knew would speak her apologies: gentle kisses, her hands gently caressing the curve of her stomach, and Agatha’s favorite of Rio running her fingers through her hair. She didn’t move, not until Agatha’s breaths grew into gentle sighs and her neck was only left sticky from her dried tears. The moment a soft pair of lips were placed underneath her jaw, Rio shifted. She pulled Agatha closer, moving her where her arm supported underneath her knees, the other cradling her back. Her wife moved instinctively, wrapping her arms around her neck as she stood, wincing slightly at the way her legs ached from her run previously. 

Rio moved easily around the couch and down the hall where their door was once locked by a gunshot to her heart. Her wife pressed another kiss lazily to her jaw again as she nudged the door open with her foot. Crossing the threshold, she placed her wife down slowly into bed on her side, a silent way of telling her wife she wasn’t leaving her. Agatha moved to her side, opening the quilts to Rio who climbed in beside. Before she could even settle, Agatha’s arms were around her, her face pressing into the crease of Rio’s neck and shoulder. A small smile graced her lips as she settled them back in their bed, holding her pregnant wife as close as possible as she trembled.

“Te amo, cariño,” her voice whispered softly against her wife’s head. “ Lo siento .”

Her wife only shook her head, against her, “Shh, lo sé. Te amo más. Simplemente no lo sueltes.” (I know. I love you more. Just don’t let go.)

With that, her wife fell into a deep slumber, her breathing fully steady, taking in Rio’s scent. She stayed up a little more, making sure her wife was asleep, her hand reaching under the blankets to her wife’s stomach once more. And it was like that, Rio fell asleep, finally with her wife in her arms, refusing to let go, even in her slumber. 



***

 

Agatha 

 

Agatha blinked against the heaviness in her eyelids, disoriented as she tried to make sense of her surroundings. She couldn’t quite place where her pillow had gone. The comforting scent of pine and cedar wood, which had wrapped around her like a blanket as she fell asleep, had faded into a faint trace. Her hand reached out instinctively, seeking the warmth she was used to. Her body ached from the remnants of the sobs that had wracked her the night before, and for a moment, her eyelashes resisted the urge to open. She kept them shut, brow furrowed, only to be met with the cold emptiness of the sheets beside her.

Oh that little bitch–

Before she could finish the thought, before the anger and hurt drowned her again, she felt a warm hand against her bare skin. All the animosity died from the featherlight touch on her stomach. Then it came more firmly, a thumb brushing over in a deliberate pattern. She almost shivered, her body warming at the sensation but what truly made her freeze was the press of lips against her belly. Soft. Reverent. 

Then a whisper. 

It was low, and heavy, Rio’s voice still deep with sleep, but nonetheless loving, “ Mi pequeño… mi amor más puro… ” (My little one… my purest love…)

Agatha held her breath. Rio’s voice was hushed, filled with something she rarely let slip, something raw and unguarded. 

“ No sé si puedes escucharme todavía, pero quiero que sepas cuánto te amo. ” (I don’t know if you can hear me yet, but I want you to know how much I love you.)

Another kiss, lingering, as if Rio wanted to pour all her devotion into it. 

“ Desde el momento en que supe que existías, todo en mi mundo cambió. No sé qué tan bueno seré en esto, pero te prometo esto: siempre estaré aquí para ti. ” (From the moment I knew you existed, everything in my world changed. I don’t know how good I’ll be at this, but I will promise you this: I will always be here for you.)

Agatha felt her throat tighten. Rio’s fingers traced a slow, swirling pattern just beneath her navel, her voice dipping even softer. 

“ Cuando des tus primeros pasos, yo estaré allí. Cuando caigas, te atraparé. Cuando tengas miedo, te abrazaré hasta que el miedo desaparezca. ” (When you take your first steps, I will be there. When you fall, I’ll catch you. When you are afraid, I will hug you until the fear goes away.)

Her lips brushed over Agatha’s skin again, warm and gentle. 

“ Tu mamá dice que soy insoportable. Que soy terca, que nunca sé cuándo rendirme… y tienes razón, mi amor. Pero eso significa que nunca dejaré de luchar por ti. ” (Your mama says I’m unbearable. That I am stubborn, that I never know when to give up... and you are right, my love. But that means I will never stop fighting for you.)

A small chuckle. 

“ Aunque sé que probablemente heredes mi terquedad y la vuelvas loca. Harás travesuras, la desafiarás, la pondrás de los nervios. Y ella fingirá que le molesta, pero la verdad es que le encantará. ” (Although I know you’ll probably inherit my stubbornness and drive her crazy. You will make mischief, you will challenge her, you will get on her nerves. And she will pretend that it bothers her, but the truth is that she will love it.)

Her fingers ghosted over Agatha’s stomach, a little more firm but still tender before she pressed one more kiss there. 

“ Siempre serás amado. Siempre serás protegido. Mientras tenga aliento en mis pulmones, nadie te tocará. Eres mi vida, mi alma, mi promesa eterna. ” (You will always be loved. You will always be protected. As long as I have breath in my lungs, no one will touch you. You are my life, my soul, my eternal promise.)

Agatha’s heart clenched, as she drew her lip in a thin line, scared to ruin the moment. She felt Rio exhale against her skin, as if grounding herself. 

“ Te amo, mi pequeño. Te amo más de lo que las palabras pueden decir. ” (I love you, my little one. I love you more than words can say.)

Agatha couldn’t stop the tears that pricked at her eyes, her chest tightening with something overwhelming. 

She swallowed, her voice barely above a whisper, “ Y ellos te amarán a ti.” (And they will love you.)

Rio stilled, her eyes widening as she sat up, seeming to want to pull away from her. Agatha didn’t let her. She grabbed her wife’s hand, keeping it firmly against her stomach, staring up into her eyes, watching in stunned silence as water began to fall from them. Agatha sat up, opening her arms to her wife. Out of all the reactions… out of all the scenarios in the morning, she didn’t expect to find her wife talking to her stomach on the verge of tears, like there was a fear Rio wasn’t yet ready to share. Rio rarely cried, even when their fights left Rio burned and beaten to the ground. This was new. This was strange. For a moment, Agatha wondered if they swapped bodies like Freaky Friday and she was actually Rio, her own emotional ass trapped inside her.

Her wife trembled in her arms, sobbing into her own neck, a mirror of last night and Agatha immediately felt a wave of guilt wash over. She should have given her wife some space and she knew her wife would come and talk when she was ready. But she couldn’t stop herself in the moment, everything in her seeking the comfort of her wife’s strong arms. Part of her wondered if it was her fault, if she put too much pressure on her and Rio thought she had to play the strong protective role, shielding Agatha away from anything that caused even the tiniest bit of stress. Agatha kissed her head repeatedly, allowing her wife to cry it out, before Rio took a deep shuddering breath. 

“ Lo siento , mi amor, ” Rio’s voice was hoarse, tugging at Agatha’s heart. “I shouldn’t have pushed you away last night– I-I never wanted to hurt you–” 

Agatha shook her head, holding her wife tighter, pressing her face into her soft hair. Gods, Agatha hated talking. They both did. Touch was easier, each kiss being able to heal everything they sucked at putting into words. Make-up sex was easy. They would fight, both of them say nasty things to each other before one of them dragged the other into a harsh kiss when there was nothing left to say and hurt would flash in their eyes. Speaking with their tongues made sense, especially to Agatha. She would explore Rio’s body all over again, pouring her love and apologies with every touch, every caress, every lingering kiss– until they crashed into a high together and the cycle would repeat until they were spent, one of them whispering how much they loved the other. Agatha could do that. This made her want to pull away, but she knew she couldn’t. Rio and her agreed years ago to stop running and fix anything they fought about as soon as they calmed down. 

But this was also different. Rio was crying. The strongest person she knew was crying. Rio didn’t even cry when she tore her ACL, and although Rio bragged about not crying, Agatha was sure it was just her body going into shock. But this time, she knew that for once, their love language wasn’t going to be the answer… especially after last night when Rio flinched from her. She internally cringed, the hurt flashing all over again, even though she knew her wife. It was a moment where Rio was carrying something that she wasn’t telling Agatha… but somehow that made it hurt more. 

Rio sniffled, her breath coming out in shaky, uneven bursts. Agatha could feel it against her collarbone, the way her wife was trying so damn hard to hold herself together, but the cracks were there– splintering, breaking, widening until there was nothing left to do but fall. 

“You don’t have to– ” Agatha started, but Rio tensed in her arms, cutting her off. 

“I do,” Rio’s voice was barely above a whisper, but the weight of it crushed Agatha’s chest. “I should’ve– I shouldn’t have shut you out.” 

Agatha swallowed, tightening her arms around her, “Then why did you?” 

Silence. 

Agatha could feel the hesitation in the way Rio’s fingers curled against her back, clenching the fabric of her bra as if she was afraid Agatha would pull away. She willed herself to stay. They couldn’t keep running. It couldn’t just be one of them saving the other. They had to save each other, but in the moment, she knew Rio needed extra strength, not because she was weak, but because she was too stubborn to admit she had taken on more than could carry. So she waited. 

Rio let out a slow, trembling breath. 

“I didn’t want to stress you out,” her voice cracked at the end, and Agatha shut her eyes against the sharp sting in her own chest. “You’re pregnant, Agatha. And you– fuck, you already have so much on your plate, I just– I thought I could handle it.” 

Agatha’s stomach twisted, a slow, creeping ache unfurling in her ribs. She had guessed correctly. Her wife was being her stubborn self. She pulled back just enough to see Rio’s face, and it nearly shattered her. Her wife’s eyes were red-rimmed, glassy with unshed tears, her lips pressed together in a wobbly, miserable line. She looked exhausted. She looked small . 

“My love,” Agatha whispered, reaching up to cradle her face. 

Rio shook her head, blinking rapidly, like she could force herself to keep it together if she just didn’t look at her. 

“I didn’t want to break down in front of you,” she admitted, the words spilling out in a rush, like she had been holding them back for too long. “I didn’t want you to see me like that especially over just these fucking rumors. I wanted to be strong for you. But I– ” her voice cracked, and this time, she didn’t try to swallow it down. “I ended up pushing you away instead. And I hate myself for that.” 

Agatha felt something in her chest snap . She didn’t think. Didn’t hesitate. She just kissed her– her lips caressing Rio’s as soft as a flower’s petal, and her hands framing Rio’s face as if she could siphon all the guilt away with the very press of her lips. Rio made a noise, something so soft… something so broken. Then, slowly she melted into the kiss, pressing her own lips softly against Agatha’s. It wasn’t the normal take, the fast pace they usually did. This was something perfectly new. 

When Agatha finally pulled back, she rested her forehead against Rio’s, their breaths mingling in the small space between them. She took in the familiar scent of her, morning breath and all, and part of her wanted to chuckle at it– how some mornings the other kicked the other out to brush their teeth before falling back into bed. She didn’t care this time, happily being close with her wife, but she knew Rio needed more than just a simple kiss. 

She remembered the one sided conversation, how scared Rio seemed, how she acted if she was failing both her and their unborn child. Rio could never fail in her eyes, and it scared her to think Rio ever thought that. 

“Then don’t shut me out next time,” her voice softened. “Let me carry it with you.” 

Rio let out something between a laugh and a sigh, her breath tickling Agatha’s skin, “You are already carrying enough, mi amor .” 

Agatha pulled back slightly, just enough to cup Rio’s face in her hands. 

“You are part of what I carry, my love,” she traced a thumb over Rio’s cheek, catching the last remnants of her tears. “Not just in the sense that I am physically carrying a part of you.”

She earned a weak smile, a hand back on her stomach. 

“But because I love you. Because I am your wife. Our wedding vows, my love, we promised to carry each other, not one of us just bearing all the weight.” 

Rio swallowed hard, her eyes flickering with something unreadable. But it was something like vulnerability, love, and perhaps, need. She turned her face slightly, just enough to press a soft kiss to Agatha’s palm. That small gesture made Agatha’s breath catch. She had touched Rio a thousand times before– brushed their fingers together absentmindedly, pulled her in by the waist, tangled their limbs together under the covers– but there was something different about this. Maybe it was the way Rio’s lips lingered on her skin, how her hands slid tentatively up Agatha’s sides, hesitant but wanting. Maybe it was the way their foreheads pressed together, how their breathing had synced without them realizing. 

Agatha tilted her head, brushing the tip of her nose against Rio’s, her fingers sliding down to curl around the back of her neck. Rio’s breath hitched. It wasn’t meant to be anything at first– just a soft kiss, slow and lingering… reassurance– comfort. But the moment their lips met, something shifted. Rio let out a quiet, shuddering sigh against her mouth, her fingers tightening in the fabric of Agatha’s bra. The small noise sent heat curling low in Agatha’s stomach. 

They weren’t fixing things with rushed, desperate touches this time. No dragging each other into a heated, messy kiss to erase whatever had hurt them. This was different, softer, deeper. 

Agatha pressed another kiss to Rio’s lips, slower this time, savoring the way her wife melted into her. She felt Rio’s hands slide up to her shoulders, pulling her closer, her body fitting perfectly against Agatha’s own. The warmth between them grew, humming under their skin, igniting in the spaces where their bodies touched. Rio was the one who deepened the kiss first, tilting her head to slot their mouths together more firmly. It was still slow, but now there was something else– something heavier, something that made Agatha’s fingers dig into Rio’s back, made her part her lips just enough to let Rio take whatever she needed. 

When Agatha finally pulled back, Rio chased after her, pressing a feather-light kiss to the corner of her mouth, then her jaw, then lower. A silent plea. Their eyes locked, speaking to each other with a language only they knew. They leaned in again, this kiss hot and wanting. Agatha sighed into it. This is what they knew. This was for her, for her Rio, and for their relationship. This was easier than talking. 

This was them. 

Agatha felt Rio’s lips move, trailing down her jaw and up to her ear. Her lips brushed against her neck, warm and purposeful, her breath still uneven from crying. She sighed softly, tilting her head, giving her wife more room to take whatever she needed. Rio started slowly, her mouth tracing a path down Agatha’s pulse, her hands sliding around the curve of her stomach, fingertips ghosting over bare skin. There was no rush, no frantic desperation– just quiet, lingering touches that spoke in the language they understood best. 

“I love you,” Rio murmured, her voice still hoarse, but steady now. 

Agatha exhaled softly, threading her fingers through Rio’s hair, “I know.” 

She kissed the top of her head, then tugged her back up, capturing her lips again, slower, deeper, letting the heat simmer between them. Rio melted into her, pressing closer, her hands gripping Agatha’s hips like she was grounding herself. But when Agatha ran her hands down Rio’s back, tracing the curve of her spine, she felt the smallest shiver. She pulled back just enough to look her in the eyes.

“Come here,” she whispered, tugging Rio to lay back down with her. 

Rio followed without hesitation, their bodies lining together without struggle. Her wife let her guide her, her gaze dark and unsure, but filled with something achingly tender. The moment they slotted their bodies together, Agatha rolled them over, pinning Rio beneath her. But, she didn’t move right away. She just looked at her, really looked at her. Her eyes studied the flush in her cheeks, the way Rio’s lips were slightly parted, her breath uneven that was no longer from her previous crying. Rio, her rock, her strong, stubbornly independent, unshakeable Rio, was now soft, open beneath her. Rio was trusting her to hold her– finally letting go, finally allowing Agatha to take care of her after months of Rio only taking care of Agatha, and refusing the care in turn. And gods, Agatha would take care of her. 

She leaned down, her hair creating a chestnut curtain around them as she kissed her, slower this time, dragging her lips over Rio’s with a deliberate patience. Her hands moved along Rio’s bare skin, her fingers rippling with the divents of ribs beneath her touch. Her fingers moved, going down the delicately carved rolling hills of abs, but she could feel the tension still coiled there, the weight she had been carrying alone for too long. She threw it somewhere behind her, pushing Rio back into the mattress, her lips trailing along sharp collarbones, up her neck, and to her lips once more. Rio sighed into her mouth, arching into her touch, her fingers curling into Agatha’s shorts in silent plea. 

“Mm-mm,” Agatha murmured against her lips, pulling away just enough to meet her gaze. 

She ran her fingers gently over Rio’s stomach again, moving over her rib cage and whispering, “Let me take care of you.” 

Rio’s breath hitched, something raw flashing in her eyes before she nodded, exhaling shakily. Agatha didn’t make her wait. She kissed down the column of Rio’s throat, slow and unhurried, her hands continuing their exploration, tracing every inch of familiar skin, relearning all the places that made her wife tremble. 

Agatha trailed open-mouthed kisses down Rio’s body, letting her lips and tongue map out every inch of beloved skin. She placed another kiss on her mouth.

“I swear it with all my heart.”

Another kiss.

“You are going to be a wonderful mother, my love,” she smiled down at her, taking her hand to rest against her stomach. 

Rio’s eyes glistened again before closing, allowing Agatha’s words to seep in. 

“Want to know how I know?” 

Rio shook her head, still refusing to speak. 

She placed a kiss to the corner of her lips, “I know because of how much strength you give me to go on.”

Agatha smiled softly, brushing a strand of hair away from Rio’s face, her touch gentle but full of affection. 

“I know because I see the way you love me,” she murmured, her hand moving to cradle Rio’s face, thumb gently tracing her cheek. “The way you protect me, even foolishly at the cost of yourself. I see the way you fight for us, for everything we’ve built. I see you. Te veo, mi amor. ”

Rio’s eyes fluttered open, meeting Agatha’s gaze with something raw, vulnerable, a silent understanding between them. She exhaled shakily, as if Agatha’s words had been a lifeline she hadn’t known she needed. Agatha’s voice softened, a tender smile curling on her lips as she continued. 

“You are so strong and you are doing so wonderfully, my love. You are going to be everything to our baby, all of that love you have for them already–”

She paused, taking her own deep breath as her wife’s hand shook slightly against her stomach, the words settling between them like a sacred promise.

“I am so in awe of you. You’re going to be an incredible mami. ”

Rio let out another shaking breath, her hand remaining on her stomach as her eyes refused to look at her now, “And what if I fail? Agatha, I can’t stand the thought of not being enough for you–”

The moment was tender, but the desire to continue what they had started was unmistakable in every touch, every press of her lips as they moved to Agatha’s neck.

Her wife was always like this, always wanting to make sure her love for her was known, but Agatha could see something else on her wife’s face. The energy was draining her from her, her eyes drooping still with tiredness, and the red still hadn’t fully faded from both exhaustion and emotion. She pushed back slowly, her fingers trailing down Rio’s arms, grounding her.  

“Rio,” Agatha whispered, her voice low but firm, “we’ll continue later, okay? You need rest. You’ve been carrying so much lately.” 

Rio looked up at her, her expression a mix of frustration and longing. 

“But I want to give to you… I want to show you how much I love you, too,” she said, her voice a little more pleading, her breath still coming in short bursts. 

Agatha sighed softly, brushing a few strands of Rio’s hair from her face, her fingers lingering on the warm skin of her cheek. 

“I know, my love. And you will. But right now, I need you to rest, okay?” she kissed the corner of Rio’s mouth, soft and lingering. “We both need to rest.”

Rio’s face softened, her gaze flickering between desire and exhaustion. She hesitated, but Agatha’s quiet insistence won. Slowly, albeit reluctantly, she sank back into the mattress. Agatha moved, burying her face back into Rio’s neck, taking in the scent of pine and cedar wood. Rio’s arms wrapped around her, one immediately going back down to her stomach, rubbing in smooth circles as Agatha felt her own exhaustion wash back over her. Her eyes fluttered closed, her body melting in the warmth of her wife’s body as her breathing slowed. She listened to her wife’s heart beat, it pumping slowly in a steady lulling thrum. She placed one last kiss to her neck, before allowing her eyes to close. 



***



Agatha’s body jolted awake, a sharp movement disturbing her peaceful sleep. She groaned, annoyed at the interruption. Her wife stirred beside her, the mattress dipping as Rio prepared to get out of bed. But Agatha wasn’t having it. With a swift motion, she reached out and grabbed Rio, pulling her back into bed. Rio gasped, momentarily caught off guard, before Agatha locked her legs around her, anchoring her in place. Her head found its resting spot on Rio’s bare chest, and a hand gently brushed through her hair. 

“Agatha,” Rio began, but Agatha mumbled into her chest, pressing herself deeper against the warmth of her wife’s skin. 

“Rio!” she groaned, her voice muffled. “Go back to sleep.” 

Rio shifted again, a soft thump against the nightstand as she reached for something. Agatha, with a sigh of frustration, yanked her wife back toward her, refusing to let her go. Gods, Rio could never sit still for long. 

“Agatha–” Rio started again, but this time there was something different in her tone. 

But she was too tired to care and found herself snapping, “What?”

Agatha reluctantly lifted her head, her tired eyes squinting in confusion. Her wife was holding up her phone. Agatha blinked a few times before her gaze fell on the lock screen: a picture of herself staring up at an owl in their backyard. The image was one Rio had sneakily snapped while Agatha was lost in thought. Her wife hadn’t shut up about it, proudly showing off the way she “ caught the perfect lighting” and “this is how I see you every day.” Agatha hated getting her picture taken. She almost shrugged, about to mutter something dismissive– probably just “cute” –but then her eyes widened as she noticed the time. 

It was mid-afternoon on a Friday. 

Friday. 

Not Saturday. 

They had slept in. 

On a workday. 

If Agatha wasn’t pregnant and exhausted, she would have shot up in a panic, rushing to get to the university. But one thought of her wife dealing with the rumors again, combined with the acknowledgment that she really didn’t want to get up, made her body sink back into the body beneath her and the warmth that was still there. With a contented sigh, Agatha threw her face back down into her wife’s breast, allowing the softness of Rio’s skin to pull her back into the comfortable embrace of sleep.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

okay, idk if this was my best work. I might re-write the chapter... i feel like I didn't add enough emotion in it, but also this feels very them... idk, tell me what you think 💚💜

Also, yes another Snow Patrol song bc it was too good not to add it.

Next up, 12 week scan, Yule and maybe also the 18th scan ?

idk, do y'all wanna see the 12th week scan, or do we wanna go and see Agatha lose her bet with Rio at 18th week scan ?

Chapter 12: And Life Goes On

Chapter Summary

“Buenos días, cariño,” her wife cooed at her, pressing a kiss to her lips before her hand found her stomach.
Oh, and she was showing now. It wasn’t outrageous, but the little baby bump was undeniable. She noticed it halfway through the week and Rio had been all over since.
“Y mi pequeño extraterrestre. Estoy emocionado de verte hoy.”

OR:

A LOT of shit happens here

Chapter Notes

So i stayed up late to post this for you guys since I won't be able to post this weekend. I have a friend visiting and well... this story is between me and you guys

So until next week my AgathaRio stans 💚💜

also, yes i changed my profile pic, i thought Rio with a knife was more fitting

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Rio

 

If there was one thing about Rio Harkness-Vidal, it was that she was ambitious, persistent, and according to her wife, she never knew when to quit. It was one of her best and worst qualities, but damn, it worked in the most of ways when it came to her wife– although Agatha would tell you differently, but not now… no, Rio was finally doing what she wanted to do earlier. 

 

Despite the emotional rollercoaster of the last twenty-four hours, Rio felt at peace. Her and Agatha actually talked. It was rare and fuck, it was scary. Rio laid everything bare: her fears, her worries, and her love… and Agatha, oh her beautiful fucking wife, just listened, even when she thought she wasn’t. She didn’t really want Agatha to hear her speaking to their child. It was between the two of them, if the child heard her, but she needed to say it, but her wife heard her, and oh, it brought Rio to tears hearing her wife tell her she would be an incredible mother. Something in Rio needed that, just those few words to cut through the fog. 

Her wife’s touch earlier lit something inside her and for a moment, it was like she was back on that small college dorm bed and Agatha was in her arms before moving down her body, exploring her for the first time, tasting her for the first time, really seeing her for the first time. The first time they had sex… for a lack of better words, it was magical. It wasn’t Rio’s first time, hell high school and leading up to Agatha, she was the classic lesbian fuck-boy. Sex was just sex at that time. She scored as many women as she wanted to and left the next day like nothing ever happened. The women were nothing, just someone to help let off some steam. But then, Rio met Agatha. She didn’t mean to, but she fell… hard. They weren’t even dating and Rio couldn’t even bring herself to look at another woman. And when they were dating, for the first time in her entire life, Rio was nervous. The idea of having sex with Agatha didn’t feel like just sex. It felt like she was taking another step in something huge. She couldn’t describe it at the time, but now she knew it was the first time she felt vulnerable during sex and still trusted someone to see her. 

And fuck, it scared Rio. 

To her luck for the first few months of dating, the furthest they had gotten was a messy make-out session with roaming hands, but Rio never wanted to pressure her. It was new. It was real. Rio normally pounced the moment she could with previous girlfriends and flings, but with Agatha, it made her want to do everything right. To her surprise, it was Agatha who initiated it. Hela, Rio’s roommate at the time, was out for the weekend and she figured they could have a nice time watching a movie in her room without having to walk out into the common area and Hela teasing her. 

They were lying side by side, Agatha in the center of her bed, Rio against the pillows that lined the wall. Her at-the-time-girlfriend had started small, a light kiss to her cheek, then a peck to her lips, before another was placed a little longer, and next longer. She felt the brush of Agatha’s tongue against her lips and happily obliged. Then, after what felt like hours, she felt a hand on her hip, warm and firm. It had traced around the bone before moving her shirt up. Sparks had flown, shooting up her very being. 

She could foggily remember what had happened next, but she remembered Agatha had moved to straddle her waist and Rio had looked up into those gorgeous eyes, searching them before she had whispered softly, “Are you sure?” 

Her girlfriend had simply nodded and well, that night led to a similar situation she found herself in now. 

Rio hummed, her fingers tracing along the outside of pale thighs that were wrapped around her head. Her tongue swirled, licking harshly up a side of a slick fold before delving back down, exploring with new enthusiasm. The taste of her wife consumed her senses, and she was right before, her wife did taste different. She had thought sweeter, and while it was true, there was something salty to her that she was quickly becoming addicted to. She smirked softly, but her tongue never wavered, pushing past the folds, searching for the burning heat of her wife’s inner walls. Fingers tangled in Rio’s hair, holding her in place as she shoved her tongue as deep as possible, her nose brushing across the soft bundle of nerves. 

“Fuck, Rio,” her wife moaned above her, her hips lifting into her. 

After promptly falling back asleep with her wife and deciding that work was a Monday problem, Rio had woken up hours later to her wife grinding against her thigh in her sleep. She knew she should have given her in return after her wife made her come, but her wife also needed the sleep as much as Rio did. Rio had smirked, moving her kisses along her wife’s neck, waiting for her to wake up and realize what she was doing. It didn’t take long. She watched as her wife’s eyes opened to reveal pupils so large they almost covered the piercing blue irises she adored. They kissed lazily afterward, Rio hoping to take things slow, to explore her wife as Agatha had done her, but her pregnant wife wasn’t having that. No, a tongue moved up against Rio’s cheek and her brain short circuited. The last time her wife had done that was in their kitchen when her wife wanted to get her back after Rio had left a hickey on her neck. 

Something in Rio had taken control, a primal need as she had turned taking her wife’s tongue into her mouth and sucking lightly on it. And well, hours later, in between food and bathroom breaks, they hadn’t left their bed. 

 

Rio pushed her wife’s hips down, pinning her in place as her mouth moved up to her clit flicking at it with her tongue as her fingers came up, slipping two inside her wife with ease. Agatha gasped above her, more moans falling from her lips. She pulled her face away, watching her fingers disappear into pink silk folds. Her heart leapt as she stared, pushing her fingers in and out quickly, feeling the softness of her walls squeezing around her fingers. She scissored her fingers apart, stretching her wife, watching as a droplet of arousal ran down to her wrist. She almost moaned herself, her mouth open as she practically drooled at the sight of it. Rio added a third finger without resistance. Sweet wet noises filled the room along with her wife’s moans. She curled her fingers, retrieving another gasp before the hand on her head, tugged her back to her awaiting clit. She didn’t tease, no, Rio doubled her efforts, pumping her fingers faster and making her tongue flat as her wife rutted against her face. 

“Fuck, fuck, yes, oh, just like that,” her wife’s words ran straight to her core. 

She curled her fingers again, touching the spongy spot deep inside her wife and her wife jerked. She was close, she was so close. Thighs tightened around her head, the fingers laced through her hair, tugging harder, and above her, her wife started rambling incoherently. Her thrusts were erratic, desperate against her face and Rio took every breath she could in between but refusing to pull away until–

Knock. Knock. Knock. 

Rio lifted her head up, her face turned towards the hall where their front door was, but a sharp tug and a low growl from her wife forced her back to what she was doing. Her mouth returned, but she heard it again, this time insistent and louder. She groaned, all the annoyances of yesterday burning back through her body. Before she could pull away, there was another tug on her hair, her wife’s pussy clenched on her fingers, keeping her inside as legs tightened around her head, locking her in place. 

“Rio, I swear to the fucking gods if you stop, you won’t– AH!” 

She cut her wife off, plunging her fingers deeper, curling them with every thrust. She tuned out everything– everything except her wife’s moans and the loud squelching sound that echoed with every thrust of her fingers. Her tongue swirled around her, licking up, down, to the side and the other. She smirked, her teeth nicking the pink moistened skin before her lips wrapped around the angry bud and she sucked hard. Rio closed her eyes as hot liquid splashed onto her lips, splattering on her cheeks and thoroughly soaking her chin and hand. She continued licking, her long tongue gathering each drop as her wife shook, her thighs squeezing Rio’s head painfully. 

What a wonderful way to die, Rio mused to herself before pulling away as her wife went limp on the bed. 

Pale thighs flopped down to the mattress on either side of her as her wife was taking deep breaths, her eyes closed, and her head rested back in their pillows. Rio kissed a thigh lovingly before making her way up her wife’s exhausted body. She paused at her stomach, leaving a longing kiss there before kissing up to her wife’s cheeks, coaxing her gently from her high. Getting Agatha to squirt wasn’t something Rio could always accomplish. It wasn’t something her wife usually did, but fuck, when she did, Rio was smug about it for the rest of the day. Sue her, it was hot, especially with her belly slightly rounded. Oh, Rio couldn’t wait until her wife was more sensitive, more needy– she half wondered if it was already starting. She read somewhere that women usually get an influx of their libido and Rio was more than happy to appease her wife. 

Hands came up, caressing her jaw before pulling her into a sweet kiss. She moaned when a tongue came out, her wife tasting herself on Rio’s tongue, lips, and cheeks–

Knock! Knock! Knock!

Rio audibly growled as her wife pushed her away, but placed a peck on her cheek, “You should check that.” 

She shot her wife a glare but Agatha was already curling up on her side, her eyes fluttering close as she tried normalizing her breathing. She sighed, kissing her wife’s lips quickly before running into their bathroom. Rio cleaned her mouth and chest clean before reaching down and unbuckling the harness around her hips. It fell to the floor with an unceremonious thud before she grabbed the mouthwash and looked around the bathroom, swishing the minty liquid around her mouth. To her dismay, it washed the taste of her wife away, but if she was forced to open up the fucking front door, whoever it was didn’t need to know where her mouth just was. She could still hear the knocking and everything in her made her want to open the door with a gun in her hand for disrupting her night. Spitting into the sink, then grabbed the first robe on the door, finding it was her wife’s purple and black satin one she had bought her for Valentine's. 

The silky fabric was cool against her flushed skin, in fact Rio was all too aware of the state she was in. Her hair was probably in a rat’s nest around her shoulders and she knew how deep the marks on her chest were where the robe did nothing to hide them. She closed their bedroom door quietly behind her before marching towards the front door. The knocking was sharper now and she swore she heard muffled voices on the other side, but it was all drowned out by herself. 

“ ¿Por qué diablos está pasando esto? Lo juro por los dioses, si no es importante, apuñalaré a quien esté aquí, ” she muttered to herself before pulling the door open with more force than necessary. “¿ Qué carajo quieres? ” (Why the hell is this happening? I swear to the gods, if it’s not important, I will stab whoever is here. What the fuck do you want?)

Jen and Alice stood there, shivering as in the background, with only the view of the porch lights, snow rained down. They pushed past her, not looking up at her as they rubbed their arms, peeling off their boots already. 

“Gods, Vidal, what the fuck took you so long? It’s fucking freezing out there,” Alice shook before finally looking up at her, her worried expression melting into shock.

Jen stood stiff beside her, her eyes wide and it seemed she wanted to bolt back out into the cold. Their eyes dragged over Rio’s dishevelled appearance– the tangled black hair, the faint sheen of sweat still clinging to her skin, and most notably, the deep, dark hickeys trailing down from her collarbone, barely concealed by the satin robe. A slow, knowing grin spread across Alice’s lips as Jen’s lips grew into a disgusted purse. 

“Damn, Rio,” Alice drawled, tilting her head as she crossed her arms. “You could have at least hid the evidence instead of acting like we interrupted something.” 

Rio exhaled sharply through her nose, leveling Alice with an unimpressed stare. 

“You did interrupt,” she said flatly, closing the door behind them. “In fact, Agatha was–” 

“NOPE,” Jennifer practically jumped in place, hands flying up as if she could physically stop Rio’s words. “Do not finish that sentence.” 

Rio’s smirk turned wicked as she purposefully leaned against the door, eyes glinting with mischief, “Oh? But you came all this way. It’s only fair you know exactly what you interrupted.” 

She dragged a finger along the collar of the robe, deliberately drawing attention to the bruises dotting her skin. “I was on my knees in between Agatha’s–” 

Jennifer let out a strangled noise. 

“ Enough !” she grabbed Alice’s arm, as if trying to physically haul her away before Rio could continue. “We came to check if you were alive, not to be scarred for life!”

Rio rolled her eyes, pushing herself up off the door, and going into the kitchen. They followed her as she pulled out two wine glasses and grabbed a bottle from a higher shelf. She turned, finding them already sitting at the counter as she passed them the wine glasses before preparing tea for herself. She kept her back turned, slightly wishing she could just throw the bottle back, but Rio already promised her wife if Agatha wasn’t drinking, neither was she. She missed the exchanged look from the couple behind her, staring at the kettle as it heated up. She knew why they were here, but fuck, Agatha and her still had yet to talk about the rumors. 

Alice cleared her throat, her voice losing the usual tease, “You do know people are talking, right?”

Rio felt her fists clench as she stopped herself from stalking back to their bedroom and taking her wife right there to let out her frustrations. She gripped the counter, muttering a few curses out in Spanish before growling. 

“Yes, I am fucking pregnant. Agatha and I are married. Anything fucking else?” she whipped around, finding the couple shrinking back slightly. 

Jen and Alice looked at each other again, before Jen put her hand on Alice’s back, “Yes, actually. It grew worse today when you and Agatha didn’t show without notice.”

Rio froze, tilting her head, “‘Grew worse’ how?” 

Jennifer sighed, “Honestly? It’s not great, Rio.” 

“What exactly is being said?” she couldn’t keep the growl out of her voice. 

Alice hesitated for a second, taking a breath, “Rio, please don’t stab me–”

“Out with it!” she snapped, turning back to the tea. 

“They’re saying you and Agatha are having an affair– that she’s sleeping her way through the faculty, but more importantly, taking advantage of you,” Alice rambled out quickly.  

The kettle clattered against the counter. 

Rio’s jaw locked, her hands curling into fists, “Excuse me?” 

Her voice was low, dangerous. 

Alice shifted slightly, eyeing her warily, “It’s bullshit, obviously. But it’s getting around.” 

Rio’s nostrils flared, a single eye twitching as rage spilled from her, vibrating her very bones. 

She started pacing, her hands flying in the air– a habit she slowly picked up from her wife, “Agatha doesn’t need this fucking shit! She’s pregnant for fuck’s sake and now, they want to slander her through the mud?”

She laughed, but it was dark, emotionless as she ran her hand down her face, “I was fine with them thinking I was pregnant–”

Rio was not.

“Fuck, they can say whatever about me.” 

Over her dead body.

“But my wife? ” she snarled, slamming her hands down on the counter, rattling the wine glasses. 

Jen sat up, reaching her hand out but ultimately decided to pull back, “We know. That’s why we’re telling you this. So you’re both prepared.” 

Rio let out another sharp, humorless laugh, “Prepared? You think we need to be prepared to deal with a bunch of pathetic, gossiping groups of cunts.” 

She shook her head, trying to keep herself calm, but everything was screaming to go to that fucking university and slit every single one of their throats. 

“Agatha shouldn’t have to deal with their bullshit just because they can’t keep their miserable lives interesting without dragging us into it.” 

Alice gave her a look, “You’re not exactly known for being approachable, Rio. People don’t know much about you two, and when people don’t know, they speculate.” 

Rio scoffed, “Right. So, what? We’re supposed to just play nice, let them talk shit, and hope it dies down?” 

She laughed bitterly, “Fuck that.” 

Jen sighed again, “No, but–” 

“Because let me fucking tell you something,” Rio continued, venom lacing her tone. “If I hear one more fucking word about my wife from some jealous, washed-up–” 

“Down, girl.”

Rio’s breath hitched as everyone else in the room stilled. Rio looked up, finding her wife leaning against the door frame, her eyes sparkling. She was the picture of composure compared to what Rio looked like. Her hair was brushed, tame and curling down to her waist. She wore Rio’s crew neck and Rio’s sweatpants. But something in Rio softened and for a moment, the anger dissipated as she found her wife’s hands on her stomach, rubbing the small bump that showed as she flattened the pine green sweatshirt around it. 

She felt frozen in place as Agatha pushed herself off the wall, rounding around the counter to face Rio. She cupped her cheeks gently, grounding her and easing her racing heart ever so slightly, “Breathe, my love. Go, get dressed and we all will talk when you’re ready.” 

Rio grumbled, but kissed her wife quickly before stomping back to her room, muttering more curses underneath her breath as she went. From the kitchen, she still heard them. 

“Jesus, Harkness, I don’t think anyone could reel Rio back like that,” Alice snickered. 

She could already see her wife’s smug face, “Well, she’s well trained.” 

That made Rio only mutter more curses as she slammed the bedroom door behind her. Rio huffed as she yanked open the dresser drawers, pulling out a fresh pair of sweatpants and one of her old college hoodies. She stripped off the silky robe, tossing it onto the bed before changing, her mind still fuming with everything Alice and Jen had just told her. Who the fuck was spreading those rumors? She was already trying to wrap her head around about the fact that people were stupid enough to think that she was pregnant and why the fuck did they care. Her life wasn’t some goddamn spectacle or some fucking story for someone to write fanfiction about. Rio had been dealing with people’s assumptions her whole life– cold, unapproachable, a bitch. Fine. Let them think that. But Agatha? Pregnant, exhausted, and now the center of these fucking rumors? Absolutely fucking not. 

She shoved a hand through her messy black hair, forcing a deep breath through her nose before she stormed out of the bedroom, only somewhat more presentable. When she stepped back into the hall, she found the group had migrated into the living room. Her wife had settled herself in the love seat, curled up and sipping tea like she wasn’t the goddamn center of a vicious university-wide scandal. Jen and Alice turned when they heard her, their shoulders visibly tensing. Probably because Rio looked like she was two seconds away from committing murder. Agatha, on the other hand, barely reacted. She simply tilted her head at her wife, then patted the empty seat beside her. 

“Sit, mi vida .”

Rio grumbled, but obeyed, dropping into the seat with a heavy sigh but ultimately curled herself where her wife was quickly moving back against her. Her hands automatically landed on her stomach, tracing gentle circles as her wife nodded to the tea on the coffee table in front of them, clearly for her. She ignored it, pulling her wife closer, her hands remaining on her stomach. 

Alice, perhaps foolishly, was the first to break the silence, “So… do either of you know who might be spreading this shit?” 

“Don’t have to,” Rio muttered darkly, resting her chin on top of her wife’s head. “I can just go to work Monday and let them all know exactly what I think about their little gossip circles.” 

“Darling,” Agatha sighed, resting a hand on Rio’s thigh, “as much as I would love seeing you go full cold, bitchy Dean Vidal on people, that might not be the best strategy.” 

Rio scowled, “Then what, mi amor ? We just let them think you’re fucking your way through the faculty while pregnant with our child?” 

Jen, wisely, took a gulp of wine to avoid responding. 

Alice, however, scoffed, “I mean, to be fair, Harkness, you do have that whole mysterious, hot professor vibe–” 

“Babe,” Jen warned. 

“–and people are nosy.” 

Rio shot her a glare, “Oh, thank you, Alice. That makes me feel so much better.” 

Alice held up her hands, “I’m just saying, people will talk, and if you keep shutting them down the way you always do, they’ll only make up worse shit. You two don’t have to go on stage and make out in front of everyone–” 

Agatha hummed, “Tempting.” 

Alice made a face,  “– but maybe it’s time to stop pretending you’re just colleagues.” 

Rio exhaled sharply, looking down at where Agatha’s hand still rested on her thigh, “You really think just admitting everything will make them shut up?” 

“No,” Agatha said simply. “But it will take away their fun. If they have no mystery to unravel, they’ll get bored and move on.” 

Rio tongued at the inside of her cheek, the muscles in her jaw ticking. 

Then, finally, she muttered, “Fine.” 

Agatha squeezed her thigh, “Good girl.” 

Rio whipped her head down towards her wife, her eyes darkening slightly with annoyance, as Alice and Jen both choked on their drinks.



 

Agatha

 

Her wife hated the idea, she knew it. Even when their friends left, she could practically hear her wife’s thoughts buzzing in her head while she cooked dinner. Agatha had sat at the counter, listening to Rio mutter to herself, too low and too fast in Spanish for her to catch, but she knew exactly what Rio was thinking. They had two more weeks. Two more weeks and they were on break. If they could just hold on for a little bit longer and then it would be just them for a month and a half– a month and half for her to also start noticeably showing. She swore the kid in her was growing by the day though, and that it wasn’t just her being bloated. Her favorite purple pants were already becoming too tight, not to mention her wife already had to go out of her way one day to get Agatha new bras. She was approaching her twelfth week and the curve of her stomach was definitely there now. She half wondered how big this baby would be. She had no information on her side of the family and she wasn’t about to call Evanora Harkness and ask her what her pregnancy was like. Surprisingly worse, Rio couldn’t ask her abuelita . They still had yet to tell anyone outside of the coven and she knew Rio really didn’t want to tell her sisters. 

By Sunday evening, Agatha spoke what was on her wife’s mind, telling her they would wait the two weeks out. Agatha was perfectly capable of listening to the rumors, in fact, if her wife wasn’t so upset, she probably would have laughed… well, she did laugh when she heard the rumor that Rio was pregnant. She didn’t fully expect her wife to be angry and upset over it. Rio could handle anything, but after their talk, she saw how nervous Rio was to become a mother, and every day since, she would casually sneak in some compliment of how wonderful of a mother Rio would be. 

They were just little things, like when Rio fetched her craving food in the night, she would casually say, “You know, you’re really going to spoil our kid with all these midnight errand runs. I can already hear them complaining when they can’t get a midnight Nando’s if you’re not around.” 

Or when Rio was tucking her in with a blanket during her midday nap: “Ah, yes, the maternal instinct is strong in you,” she said, her voice dripping with playful mockery. “Keep this up, and our little one will have you running around doing their bidding before they even know how to speak.”

They were teasing, but it was what Agatha knew best, and she knew her Rio would understand the true meaning behind them. 

 

By the time Agatha was wrapped around in her wife’s strong arms that Sunday night in bed, she kissed underneath her jaw, telling her that they would be fine. The baby would be fine and when they came back from break, they would handle it then. If anyone asked about her pregnancy, Agatha would simply say, “Yes, my wife, Rio…” It seemed to relax her wife and she knew it always made the possessive side of her wife smirk. 

 

When Monday came around, they were happy for a change of schedule. It was Hell Week, and that meant no classes, just office hours and grading. Agatha snuck into Rio’s office, leaving a note on her door for students to email her if they wanted to meet. It wasn’t ideal for them, but she didn’t care. To her surprise, she was able to sneak into the science building without being spotted, and happily slipped into her wife’s office, sitting on her couch and grading in silence. That was until her wife’s phone rang. Her wife barely paid attention, already elbow deep in preparing for the next semester as she answered the phone putting it on speaker. 

“Dean Vidal,” she answered sharply. 

“Rio, glad you’re answering,” the light voice of Pepper Stark replied, making both of them freeze. 

Pepper Stark was technically Rio’s boss. She was the Chancellor of the university, one of the officer positions that Rio had tried explaining to her many times, but eventually Agatha didn’t care enough to actually listen to her wife talking about another woman. They were somewhat friends and they knew each other from the PhD program, and having been invited to their wedding. 

Rio sat up, running her hand through her hair, “Pepper, sorry, I didn’t look at the caller ID. Something I can do for you?” 

Agatha stopped writing, watching the tension coil in her wife. She stood up, walking around her desk and placing a gentle hand on her shoulder to help ground her. Her wife turned, pulling her between her legs, her hands immediately going to her stomach. She let her, realizing how much her pregnant stomach, no matter how small it was, grounded her wife and it made something in her chest do a few funny flips. 

“Yes, why the hell are your and Agatha’s names flying through campus like a hurricane. I thought you two were keeping your marriage on the DL and then I heard you’re pregnant. Is this a congrats or not, Vidal?” 

Their eyes met, and Agatha simply nodded, telling her wife it was fine to continue, “I don’t know what is happening, Pepper. It’s not even like we have done anything to cause it. I heard it from a student–”

“Yes, she was crying her eyes out. I heard it from Kale who comforted the girl. Jesus, Vidal, do I even want to know what you said– actually no, I don’t, but the pregnancy?” 

Agatha leaned down, placing a kiss on her wife’s head, “Yes, well, I have been meaning to talk to you and Tony about that–”

“So it’s true! Rio, congratulations! You should have told me sooner. We can start talking about your maternity leave for next semester and then we can talk about your wife when she should leave to join your side–”

“Pepper,” her wife groaned, slumping back in her chair, running her hands through her hair again. “ I am not pregnant. I don’t even know how that started. It’s my wife who is pregnant. We were going to send a draft to you for our plan for next semester.”

There was a moment of silence before Pepper gasped, her voice echoing in Rio’s office as Agatha reached over, turning the volume down, “Agatha is pregnant?! Oh, Rio, congratulations and send my congrats to Harkness.”

“Thank you–”

“Just send me her due date and then I will plan the rest from there. I will be letting you both go at the same time and before you fight me on that: can it, Vidal. You deserve as much time off as your wife. Now that’s all settled, are you both coming to the mixer next Friday?” 



 

Rio wanted no part of the mixer and they were both sure that by then, Agatha would be showing a good bit– at least to the point that her tight dresses were not going to fit without showing off her stomach… if she could even fit her breasts into them. The rest of the week had gone on smoothly, despite the interruptions of their students checking their work with them. Rio didn’t mind, partially surprised her Freshmen students were brave enough to check in with her, but Agatha cared, because it meant either staying in her office, away from Rio, or doing her walk of shame across campus back to her office just to sneak back into her wife’s office. Both of them stayed away from their colleagues and it worked… for them. Alice, Jen, and Lilia texted them their reports and the rumors stayed the same. 

 

It was Saturday morning, the day of their twelve week ultrasound. Her wife was already up, making them tea and preparing Agatha’s craving food– pickles with peanut butter and caramel ice cream on the side. Agatha thought Rio was going to kill her last night though. She had tried sneaking out at three am to run to the gas station where they had fresh churros and potstickers. It was only a ten minute drive, so twenty minutes driving, maybe about another total of ten minutes of waiting on her food and back home. Thirty minutes and her wife wouldn’t even notice. 

Rio did. She practically ripped their bedroom door off its hinges just as Agatha was putting on her shoes to go and keys in hand. Her wife had groaned, yelling at her to stay put as she went and changed before swiping the keys off of her and driving them. It took an hour in total because of the college students that decided to stay up then to study and needed a snack. Her wife forced her to stay in the car and came out twenty minutes later and it took them an extra twenty minutes to get home because Agatha then decided she wanted a milkshake from McDonald’s. For the first time ever, the ice cream machine was working. By the time they came back through the door, it was a little after four and Rio had collapsed back into bed, firmly passing out while Agatha finished her milkshake and crawled back into her wife’s arms. 

 

Agatha was tearing through their drawers, trying to find a pair of stretchy sweat pants because she was tired of her pants digging into her. Her wife had already planned them to go shopping for maternity clothes after the ultrasound and she really wanted to kill her. Agatha hated shopping, despite the outrageous amount of clothes she had– to be fair most of them were gifts from her wife. She groaned, throwing on her wife’s sweat shirt and making her way out into the kitchen in just that in her underwear. Before she could go to the basement where the laundry room was, her wife was already approaching her, her eyes trailing on her legs.

“ Buenos días, cariño ,” her wife cooed at her, pressing a kiss to her lips before her hand found her stomach.

Oh, and she was showing now. It wasn’t outrageous, but the little baby bump was undeniable. She noticed it halfway through the week and Rio had been all over since. 

 “ Y mi pequeño extraterrestre. Estoy emocionado de verte hoy.” (And my little alien. I’m excited to see you today.)

Agatha couldn’t help but roll her eyes, but placed a hand over Rio’s, watching at how her wife’s brown eyes were sparkling as she stared down at her stomach.

“Oh,” Rio made a mock gasp. “ Sí, estoy de acuerdo, pequeño extraterrestre. Tu mamá necesita unos pantalones.
