Ella no está contenta porque tenemos que ir de compras después de verte. ”  (Yes, I agree, little alien. Your mom needs some pants. She isn’t happy because we have to go shopping after seeing you.)

She shook her head, “You realize they can’t hear you, right? Not until I’m–”

“ –veintitrés semanas. Lo sé. Dioses, tu mamá no es divertida, ” her wife rolled her eyes at her. (twenty-three weeks. I know. Gods, your mama is no fun.)

Agatha smirked as she cupped Rio’s cheeks, tilting her head up to meet her gaze. Without hesitation, she crashed her lips against her wife’s, slipping her tongue into her mouth with a deliberate intensity. She deepened the kiss, her tongue teasing and swirling around Rio’s until a quiet gasp escaped her. Rio’s hands instinctively found Agatha’s hips, while Agatha’s own hands roamed down the curves of her wife’s figure. Just as quickly as she started, Agatha pulled away, smirking as she gave Rio’s ass a firm squeeze before stepping back.

 “ ¿Qué te parece eso de ‘no es divertida’? ” she teased, raising an eyebrow before sauntering past her stunned wife, punctuating her departure with a playful slap on Rio’s ass. “ Te olvidas que aprendí el idioma por ti, mi corazón negro. ” (How’s that for ‘no fun’? Did you forget I learned the language for you, my black heart.)

She chuckled darkly as she turned towards the basement before feeling hands on her waist, pulling her back. She laughed loudly, soft lips attacking her neck and head as her body warmed at her wife’s affections. Her wife laughed with her before pulling away, leaving with a longing kiss to her head. 

“ Come, mi amada esposa,” her wife replied, before running down the steps. (Eat, my darling wife.) 

 

***

 

Agatha fidgeted with her hands, picking at her nails as her and her wife sat back in the teal chairs of the clinic. She couldn’t keep her leg from bouncing, and it took everything in her to not bite at her nails. Her wife reached over, breaking her hands apart with ease, giving it a gentle squeeze as she continued filling out the paperwork for her. 

“Relax, mi vida, everything is going to be fine or is this about the shopping?” her wife whispered as she clipped the pen back in place. 

Agatha shot her a glare before rubbing her hand over her stomach, “Shit, we have to shop for the baby too.” 

Rio laughed at that, a genuine light hearted chuckle that made Agatha’s heart do that little flip again, “Yes, cariño. Don’t worry, we still have time. I might have already bought a stroller though.” 

She whipped her head towards her wife, her eyes wide, “What? When?”

Her wife shrugged, “I don’t know, during one of my students’ exams. I was bored and I already had it in the shopping cart and it went on sale. Some special deal. Don’t worry, cariño. You’ll love it. It evolves with them as they grow and it has a storage thing underneath.” 

She shook her head, chuckling, “Imagine if they saw their intimidating professor gushing over baby things online while they were testing– Vidal, you’re losing your edge.” 

“Well, they didn’t see and no, I’m not.” 

“Says the woman who was just talking to her wife’s stomach.” 

Before her wife could quip back at her, the door opened, revealing a younger nurse, “Agatha Harkness-Vidal.” 

They both stood up, Rio handing the clipboard to the nurse before following her back. They were led down the same halls, and into a room where the machines hummed with life.

“Dr. Walsh will be right in. You can sit on the table, take your shoes off, and then just pull your shirt up.” 

The moment the door closed, Agatha handed Rio her longer coat and slipped off her shoes as she pulled herself up. Rio set their stuff down in the chair that was meant for her to sit in but Rio was back by her side, her hand taking hers and kissing her knuckles softly. She smiled, everything in her warm as her wife practically bounced beside her with excitement. 

A sharp knock echoed on the door before Dr. Walsh stepped in, a smile on her face, “Agatha, Rio, good morning. How are you doing today?” 

Rio smiled as Agatha spoke, “Well, the morning sickness has passed for the most part and the cravings have been hitting.”

“That’s good, and look at that, you’re showing already. Every mother is different, just keep that in mind. Any discomfort?” she asked while pulling up her computer, already typing away. 

Agatha shook her head, “No, just tired and a little dizzy sometimes.” 

Dr. Walsh shook her head, moving to the equipment around them, “That’s perfectly normal. Any concerns or questions?” 

She shook her head as the ultrasound machine switched to life, a louder humming echoing in the room. Agatha squeezed her wife’s hand, a smile stretching her lips that she couldn’t hold back as Dr. Walsh placed her gloves on before grabbing a bottle to the side. 

“Alright, now, how about we have a look at your little one? We should be able to find their hands and feet. They’ll look more human this time,” she moved forward as Agatha pulled back her shirt more, revealing her stomach rounded by life. “Alright, this will be cold.” 

Dr. Walsh applied the gel to Agatha’s stomach, and a slight chill ran through her before the coldness of the ultrasound wand followed. She flinched just a little, but Rio’s thumb against the back of her hand kept her grounded. The machine hummed as Dr. Walsh gently moved the wand over her skin. The screen flickered to life, showing a blurry, greyish image, but soon the shapes began to sharpen, revealing tiny limbs and a little outline of the baby’s head. Agatha gasped, her breath catching in her throat. 

“There’s their head,” Dr. Walsh pointed out with a soft chuckle. “And here are their arms... and their legs. You can already see their little feet.” 

Agatha couldn’t tear her eyes away from the screen, her fingers tightening around Rio’s. She could barely believe it, the life inside her was real, and sure they had seen it six weeks ago, but finally seeing their little arms and legs, Agatha could hardly put words to it. Everything in her stilled, a strange sense of calm overtaking her as Rio’s hand tightened around hers, both of them staring with stupor as the heart beat entered the room again. 

 

Lub-dub. Lub-dub. Lub-dub . 

 

Agatha froze, once again, tears streaming down her cheeks again, the weight hitting her all at once. The world came to a pause, the same feelings washing over her as she stared on. No matter how many times she heard that heartbeat, it would hit her all the same. It was a rush, an overwhelming sense of love and pride that she felt within her very soul… cause in that screen– inside her , was the very product of Rio and her, the truth of their love spelling out over and over again. 

Dr. Walsh moved the wand slightly, and the baby’s tiny feet became clearer, kicking gently on the screen. 

“They’re already active. Look at that,” she said with a smile, her tone warm and encouraging.

Rio laughed and it was a sound Agatha had never heard before– it was softer than the snow outside, lighter than the clouds, and a sweeter tune than a loon could ever sing. Her heart rushed as the world was solely them. She didn’t bother to stop the tears, watching as tiny hands moved out before the little feet started kicking around again. 

This was real. 

This was their love. 

This was all of their dreams coming true with just the mere movement of a child no bigger than a lime. 

“Everything looks great. The baby is strong, their heartbeat steady, and they’re the perfect size,” Dr. Walsh smiled, clicking a few more things on her computer before switching the ultrasound off. 

Agatha turned back to Rio who had that perfect, lopsided smile on her face. Everything in her fluttered as her wife wiped her tears before taking the towel from Dr. Walsh and cleaning off her stomach with the utmost care. 

“If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to call. The pictures will be at the nurses station for you to make your next appointment between eighteen and twenty weeks. You may start to feel them move as early as sixteen weeks and by your next appointment, hopefully they’ll be in a position to see their gender if you want that.”

Rio smirked, “Yes, we have a bet that I would very much like to win.” 

Agatha playfully smacked her arm as she sat up, making both her wife and the doctor laugh, “Don’t get your hopes up, Mrs. Harkness-Vidal. The mothers who carry normally have sixth sense. I’ll see you both in six weeks.” 

With that, the Dr. Walsh stepped out, leaving Agatha to put her shoes on. The moment she stood up straight, Rio’s arms were around her, her lips on hers. The kiss was sweet, grounding as her wife’s hands found their perch on her stomach. 

“ Te amo, mi hermosa esposa, ” Rio whispered, and the words hit Agatha’s heart with a weight she hadn’t expected. 

It was the same feeling as the first time Rio had said it, at the altar, when she had promised her heart to Agatha forever. The same rush from their wedding day, when Rio had dipped her back, whispering promises to hold her soul for all time.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

up next, Yule and Rio winning her bet 😈... also... DRAAAMMMAAA

 

so sorry for any mistakes, im half awake rn 💚💜

Chapter 13: Winter Solstice Whispers and Shattered Delusions

Chapter Summary

SURPRISEEEEEEEEEEE

my friend is asleep rn and I worked as much as I could during the week on this.... so hey, the devil works hard, but desperate lesbian writers work harder or whatever the saying is.

Anyway, here's a shit ton of fluff and

what ?

what was that ?

shattered delusions ????

... Wanda ?

Chapter Notes

Also, I know some of you are going to be upset that they (SPOILER) don't go to the mixer, but I promise this is better... okay, well i hope this is better... please don't be mad at me

Some cheesy moments, because I got sad this week so FLUFF

Also, i think I overused "my wife" because "THEY WERE MARRIED IN SOME WITCHY WAY"

UGGGHHHHHH MY HEART 💚💜

TELL US SOMETHING THAT WE DON'T KNOW JAC

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Rio

 

She groaned, staring at the two suits. Tonight was the mixer and Rio was one more inconvenience away from skipping completely. Her wife wasn’t going, which it took forever to convince her not to as listed off the reasons to her too many times. One: mainly she knew she wouldn’t be able to not be by her side or keep her hands to herself. Two: Agatha was a nightmare while shopping and wouldn’t even try on dresses so that left her other dresses that were now too tight. Three: she knew how her colleagues were and while Agatha could handle herself with the rumors and speculations around her, Rio could not. She knew if she went by herself there wouldn’t be as much talk. 

Her wife sat on the bed behind her and Rio could practically feel her eyes burning into the back of her head. She eyed the black and green pieces again, frustration smothering her at the thought of having to deal with everything— forcing her way through conversations, her fake smile, charming her own bosses, and actively avoiding questions about her supposed pregnancy. 

“Rio,” her wife spoke softly.

She only returned her acknowledgement with an indignant grunt before throwing both of her suits back into their closest, absentmindedly searching through as the clatter of hangers echoed. 

“I can tell you don’t want to go,” her wife called from their bed. 

Rio stepped out, her arms crossed as she stared at her wife. She was sitting at the edge of their bed in Rio’s shorts and the new bra Rio had bought her a couple days ago. Of course, her eyes refused to meet her wife’s, instead she was staring at the curve of her stomach. Her wife was thirteen weeks and she was stunning in every way. She partially wished they were going together, to put an end to all this bullshit, but it wasn’t energy they wished to waste… no, they had a new problem. It was Agatha and Rio’s turn to host Yule this season and that meant not only Alice and Lilia, but also Rio’s sisters. Hela wasn’t coming this year and the rest of their old friends were scattered across the world. Jen was going to be there, though, which was a new addition and Pepper and Tony said they would try to stop by. They weren’t the issue no. The problem was Rio’s older twin sisters. 

Despite how much Agatha had told her to just “woman-up” and tell her sisters so they could tell her abuelita , Rio found herself frozen. Oriana would be thrilled, she knew that, but if Oriana knew that they told everyone else first, well, Rio was sure her sister would hold it over her for the rest of her life. Maria, however– ugh, Rio wishes she throttled the bitch when she was younger. Maria was the complete opposite of her. According to Maria, Rio wasn’t even a real doctor, just a fancy title and nothing special. Maria did well for herself, sure, she was working in some high level corporation that Rio never bothered to remember. Her sister was always supposed to be the golden child of their family– well, that all went away when Rio got engaged and eventually married. 

The matriarch of the Vidal family, the very beating heart that held them all together, their abuelita, adored them all, but when they were younger, she always gravitated towards Maria. She could cook, knew how to sew and knit, played the piano, got straight As– see? The golden child. What made Maria even better in their abuelita’s eyes was how much Rio got in trouble, but once again, that was because her older sister always told on Rio for everything. In Rio’s defense, most of the things she did weren’t even true… minus the amount of fights she got into… also the amount of times she was caught having sex with other women… also that one time she robbed a gas station and got away with it except when it came to abuelita … also the piercings… also the weed… 

Okay, so Rio was nowhere near being the golden child of the family, but every time she got caught, it was Maria who ratted her out. So, naturally nobody expected much of anything from her. She was the black sheep– la oveja negra . No one expected her to get a full ride to Harvard, no one expected her to be the first one to settle down, and no one had expected her to get a PhD from one of the world’s prestigious universities: The Salemites' University. Rio was supposed to be nothing but, fuck, did she love proving people wrong. So when “ la oveja negra” had the first grandchild of the family… she knew her abuelita would be so proud of her, so excited, but most importantly, she knew Maria, Oriana, and their cousins would never hear the end of it. 

She could already hear her abuelita scolding them: “ ¡¿Y qué hay de ti, eh?! ¡Mírala, mi Riocita, dándome un bisnieto antes que tú! ¿Y tú? ¡Nada! Ni un novio o una novia, ni un marido o una mujer, ¡ni siquiera un intento! ¡Qué vergüenza! Hasta el más problemático de todos vosotros me da un bisnieto, pero vosotros, nada. ¡Nada! ¡Bah! ¿Qué esperas, que muera antes de ver más bisnietos? Ponte manos a la obra, estúpido.” 

(And what about you, huh?! Look at her, my little Rio, giving me a great-grandchild before you! And you? Nothing! Not a boyfriend or girlfriend, not a husband or wife, not even a try! What a shame! Even the most troublesome of you all gives me a great-grandchild, but you, nothing. Nothing ! Bah! What do you expect, that I die before seeing more great-grandchildren? Get your damn act together, stupid.)

Rio smirked just imagining it. She could picture the way Abuelita would puff up with pride, how she’d hold Rio’s face in her wrinkled hands and kiss her forehead like she used to when Rio was little– before all the trouble, before Maria’s constant tattling, before the reputation. Agatha, her poor wife, would immediately be smothered by her abuelita worrying. She thought Rio was obsessed with her stomach? The moment Ximena Vidal got her hands on Agatha, her wife was done for. But she could see it now: her cousins shifting uncomfortably, Oriana trying not to laugh, and Maria gritting her teeth so hard it was a miracle they didn’t crack. 

Gods, it would be worth it. 

But first, she had to tell them. 

And that was the part that made her stomach twist. 

“No, I don’t,” she admitted, rubbing the tension in her head. 

Her wife tilted her head at her, sitting back with her arms propping her up, “Then don’t, my love. You have a lot going on–”

“But I am the dean and Jen, Alice, and Lilia–”

“Can survive without you, love. Look, they know how much stress has been on you the past two weeks. They are not going to be upset if you rather stay in or if you decided to let your dear pregnant wife surprise you.” 

Rio opened up her mouth before closing it. For a moment, she looked like a fish out of water, opening and closing her mouth before finding the right words in English. 

“Surprise me?” she finally questioned.

Her wife smirked, nodding, “You let me dress you for tonight.” 

Her wife stood now, walking towards her and Rio was entranced. It was like a cosmic entity trapped her under a spell. She swallowed hard, feeling as if her wife casted a spell where her feet had decided to grow roots, and hold her where she stood. 

“You let me take you to your surprise,” her wife practically purred, brushing Rio’s hair from her face before cupping her jaw. “And then, you take me home…”

Her wife drawled out the words, her lips kissing up her neck as Rio’s breath hitched. Her wife’s teeth scraped at her skin as her lips reached her ear. A hand came down to her hip before gripping her cheek, and fuck, Rio was nothing but a puddle as her wife nipped and teased her. 

“...lie back in bed with that delicious strap on your hips, and just sit back and watch, mmm? How does that sound?” her wife whispered in her ear before abruptly pulling away. 

Fuck, her wife wanted to kill her… her wife was actively killing her and somehow this seemed only the beginning of it. Rio felt her mouth drop, her eyes dark as her wife wore her signature smirk, chuckling darkly. Yeah, she definitely wasn’t going to survive her wife’s pregnancy. She was either going to die from her wife’s libido or from mood swings… and at this point, both were an even percentage of chance it would happen. She couldn’t pick her jaw up off the floor as her wife kept her smirk. A few condescending pats on her cheek snapped her out of it as her wife stepped around her. She flinched, rubbing her cheek as her wife disappeared into the closet, coming back with Rio’s black cargo pants and black long sleeve. She dropped them in her arms before fetching a wool green flannel. 

“And your green Doc Martens will do, love,” her wife kissed her cheek before disappearing back inside. 

She couldn’t stop the soft smile, as her tongue prodded the side of her cheek. She got dressed, pulling her hair up in a tail as she did her eye liner in their bathroom. Just as she was about finished, she heard a groan from their room. Panic sinking in for a moment, she rushed out, finding her wife laying on her back with her legs dangling off the bed. The new purple turtleneck sweater fit her beautifully, but her black jeans now sat around her waist unbuttoned. She chuckled softly, sitting next to her wife who was covering her face with her hands.

“What’s wrong, mi vida ?” she asked sweetly. 

Her wife peaked an eye at her, seeing her smirk. Agatha swatted at her lightly, refusing to sit up. 

“They don’t fit,” her wife mumbled into her hands. 

Rio couldn’t help but laugh, her fingers tracing light, soothing circles over the soft skin beneath Agatha’s sweater, “ Cariño, I just bought you new pants that will fit.”

Her wife rolled her eyes even though Rio couldn’t see it, “Yeah, but these jeans make my ass look good.” 

Rio hummed in agreement, her hand trailing lower to squeeze Agatha’s hip, “Your ass always looks good, cariño . But I distinctly remember buying you a pair of jeans that you can button.” 

Agatha scoffed, her hands dropping to rest over hers, “You’re enjoying this way too much.”

“I am enjoying you, my love,” she corrected, her hands slipping further underneath the sweater. “And our pequeño. ”

Her wife tried to hide her smile, but Rio caught the way her blues sparkled up at her. She smiled down at her, placing a quick kiss on her nose before finding the brand new jeans she bought last week. Her wife was still lying there. She didn’t speak, reaching forward and taking her wife’s pants off of her. Her wife raised an eyebrow, sitting up in surprise as Rio gently pulled them off. 

“Love, I can do it. You don’t–” 

Rio simply kissed her, silencing her as she kneeled down before her, “I know, but I want to.”

Her wife’s cheeks grew a bright shade of pink as she kissed the side of her knee, grabbing the new ones and pulling them up her legs. Rio’s heart was beating steady, a calm sense surrounding her as she felt nothing but love for the woman in front of her. It was a simple, intimate gesture, and sure they have helped each other zip up a dress countless times, or tie the back a swimsuit, or dress each other for bed after too many drunken nights, or even the hundreds of times they have undressed each other– Rio felt for some reason this was more than that. Something strengthened their connection as she stood, kissing her wife’s lips before pulling away. 

“Should I pack your craving food, mi amor ?” she asked softly. 

The tips of her wife’s ears were pink as she nodded. Rio smiled, kissing her lips again before making her way out into the kitchen. She grabbed her wife’s goldfish and pretzels, sealing them in a bag before cutting up some fruit just as her phone buzzed. 

The Coven

 

Alice: Where are you guys? 

 

Jen: I’m by the bar. 

 

Lilia: I got us a table. Jen if you want to grab the drinks? 

 

Sorry, the mrs is keeping me tonight: Me

 

Lilia: Seriously? You two are going to add to the fuel by not being here. 

 

Jen: Unfortunately, Lilia is right. 

 

Mi amor💜: Don’t care. Not feeling great. Rather stay home with wife. 

 

Alice:  You text like a toddler Harkness. 

 

Lilia: And what a shitty excuse. You used to be so much more convincing than that Harkness

Lilia: What happened to you?

 

Jen: Rio happened

 

Mi amor💜: Go fuck yourself, Calderu. I am literally growing a child inside me. 

 

Alice: Yeah, Rio’s demon spawn.

 

Rio chuckled at that, rolling her eyes as she packed her wife’s purse with the snacks– her wife’s purse that she would be carrying. Rolling her eyes, she unpacked her essentials, putting them in her backpack. Just as she shoved their wallets inside, her wife came out of their bedroom. Her hair was pulled up in a bun, a blue heavy long coat framed her body well enough. In her hands was a black scarf that her wife quickly wrapped around her neck. 

“Thank you, mi amor . Do you have your gloves and scarf?”

Her wife nodded, picking up their keys to Rio’s truck. Rio raised an eyebrow. They usually took Agatha’s black Subaru everywhere. Rio used her truck for her in the summer during their camping trips, if the winter was bad enough and for skiing (which they both were taking the year off from) and their vacations. Rio herself used it when she was chopping wood in the backyard for the fireplace. 

“Already in your truck, now, vámonos !” 

Rio chuckled lightly, following her wife to the garage where she slid in the passenger seat. She sat back, her hand across the dial on her wife’s thigh as she drove, humming to Rio’s heavy metal playlist as Rio stared out into the world. All the houses were already decorated for Christmas and her and Agatha had yet to decorate for Yule. Rio was working on their wreath in the greenhouse, along with her mistletoe. Agatha was already prepping the altar, the candles bringing a comforting scent into the home. Rio was researching cleansing rituals for her wife and the baby. They were still planning the dinner but to their luck, Lilia was going to bring the Yule ham. She sighed, realizing everything they still had to do between the holiday, the baby, and her sisters. 

“My love, you’re thinking very loudly.” 

She hummed her response, not tearing her eyes away from the large blow-up Grinch decoration in a drive-way. She felt her wife’s hand in hers, making her look at her. Her wife’s eyes were still on the road, but there was a softness there reserved for her. 

“My love, what if you invite your sisters over for dinner in a couple nights instead of telling them during the party?” her wife then chuckled. “Mm, maybe Maria won’t even come this year.” 

She chuckled as well, slightly hoping it was the case, “That would be nice. I think Oriana is arriving tomorrow. I don’t know when Maria will be here.” 

“What about Abuelita Vidal? You haven’t told me if she is flying out this year.” 

Rio smiled softly, thinking of the woman who raised her, “I think she is. I haven’t heard from my cousin Elio. Normally, he texts me updates with Abuelita.” 

She watched as her wife smiled faintly, “I hope so. I can already imagine her freaking out realizing she is getting a great-grandbaby.” 

This time Rio laughed, not able to stop the image in her head from filling her with such warmth, “ Mi amor, if she comes this year, she is going to be all over you. She isn’t going to let you do anything other than rest. Oh, and she might talk you into a home birth, just warning you.”

Agatha looked at her for a moment, her eyes wide before turning back to the word, “What? Why?”

She chuckled, running her hand through her hair as she leaned back in her seat, “Apparently, Abuelita delivered me and my siblings before our mother took us to the hospital. She’s a big firm believer in the natural way of doing things– being more connected with nature. So, she will also be talking to you about feeding naturally. You don’t have to tell her if you’re using formulas, just let her talk.” 

She offered sweetly, remembering the countless corrections Rio and her sisters had for not doing things in the way nature intends. So, she was surprised when Abuelita didn’t even blink when she was caught with another woman in her bed. She had merely commented that love was natural, why should she care who Rio loved as long as she was happy– a huge difference from what her mother-in-law believed. But to Abuelita? Loving was a part of what nature intended and when they got married, her first words to Rio was when she and Agatha would have her great-grandchild. 

As much as her sisters cared less, Rio found comfort in the natural order of life. Her grandmother had taught her from a young age what it meant to live and what it meant to die. So, when her parents died, she wasn’t overly upset. Sure, she missed them, but she understood, but that was also where Maria’s hate came from. Maria, the oldest and twelve at the time, was a wreck when mami and papi died that night. Rio was eight and with them when it happened. She couldn’t remember it well, but she remembered a firefighter blocking her path into the burning hotel.

Rio had a basketball tournament in San Juan– it was playoffs and Rio remembered sneaking out of the hotel to meet up with the team to get late night ice cream. It was Rio’s idea, as her parents were already asleep and the rest of the team was staying in nearby hotels, a little scattered. But Rio, the ever rebellious and resilient child managed, walking down the streets with her team before returning to sirens and firefighters surrounding the burning building. She didn’t know what happened after. Only trying to go into the hotel when a firefighter had caught her. The rest of it was blurry and she didn’t remember any of it, only the funeral in which she just sat there, unable to cry. She wondered if it was trauma, but Rio didn’t react to fires, hotels, or even hospitals. They were part of the natural order and sometimes shit just happened. 

Sometimes people die.

“I think I want to,” her wife broke her from her thoughts.

She sat up, looking over at her, seeing the shyness creeping around her. 

“Mm, what?” she asked, shaking her head to focus. 

Her wife shot her a quick look before stating, “I think I do– want to breastfeed I mean.”

 Rio glanced over at her wife, both of their hands entwined lightly on her belly as the car hummed along the road. The street lights flickered past in a rhythmic glow, illuminating Agatha’s face in soft, golden light. She had one hand on the wheel, her fingers tapping idly as she focused on the road ahead. 

“You do?” Rio finally asked, her voice gentle but laced with curiosity. 

Agatha nodded, eyes never leaving the road. 

“I mean, if I can. I know it’s not always easy, and I don’t know how it’ll go, but…” she exhaled, gripping the steering wheel a little tighter before finally stealing a glance at Rio. “I think I want to try. I like the idea of it. Of that connection.” 

Rio felt warmth spread through her chest, and squeezed her hand lightly in hers. 

“ Mi amor, that’s beautiful,” she murmured. “And whatever happens, we’ll figure it out. No pressure, no stress. Just what feels right for you and our baby.”

Agatha hummed, but Rio could see the small, pleased smile tugging at her lips. 

“ Abuelita will be thrilled,” she admitted with a chuckle. “She’ll probably try to give me some old herbal remedies to help.” 

“Oh, without a doubt.” Rio laughed, rubbing her thumb in slow circles against Agatha’s belly, “You should’ve seen the teas she made for my aunts when they had their babies. Some of them smelled awful, but apparently, they worked.” 

Agatha smirked, “I’ll take the teas. Just don’t let her try to convince me to have the baby in a river or something.” 

Rio snorted, “I’ll do my best.”

Silence filled the car once more as the snow started twirling down. Rio watched the flakes before eyeing her wife sharply when she pulled her hand out of Rio’s. Her wife reached for the radio, changing Rio’s playlist. She chuckled as “Baby, It’s Cold Outside” came on, filling the car with the opening notes. Agatha’s fingers hovered over the skip button, ready to change the song. 

“Eyes on the road, Harkness,” Rio slapped her hand away, a playful smile tugging at her lips knowing very well what her wife was capable of. “I want to hear you sing, mi amor. ” 

Her wife shot her look, rolling her eyes, “Seriously, Rio? What are we? In a Hallmark movie?” 

Rio laughed, shaking her head at her wife as her eyes sparkled with amusement. She leaned over, giving her wife the best pleading look she could manage, knowing she would crack. 

“Please, my love. You know how your voice makes me fall in love with you all over again.”

Her wife groaned, but her fingers were already moving away from the controls. Rio hummed with excitement, the mischief in her eyes turning to adoration as her wife cleared her throat, obviously getting ready to sing for her, but she knew her wife was trying to play it off. 

“Laying it on thick, Vidal. Flattery will get you nowhere.” 

Rio shook her head, sitting up in her seat, “I’ll sing it with you– por favor, mi amor. ” 

Her wife eyed her from the corner of eye, but Rio already saw the cracks deepening, “Rio–”

She cut her off, “Our little one will love it!” 

Without another word, Rio slid her hand into her wife’s, and as the music played on, she began to sing, her voice low and playful, mimicking the lyrics as she wiggled her eyebrows at her. 

“I really can’t stay… but baby, it’s cold outside.”  

A soft smile tugged on her wife’s lips, but she acted annoyed as her sweet voice filled the car, easily taking over Rio’s.

  “I’ve got to go away… but baby, it’s cold outside.” 

Rio’s eyes sparkled with delight as she sang along, listening to the smoothness of her wife’s voice. It wasn’t that Rio couldn’t sing, but her wife was like a siren compared to her. She would never forget the first time she had heard her at a karaoke bar singing “Shallow” and how Rio almost fell to her knees the moment her girlfriend-at-the-time kept her eyes. 

She latched onto the warmth of her wife’s voice mixing with her own as they continued the back and forth, laughing in between. The lyrics danced between them, their voices blending effortlessly 

“ This evening has been… so very nice,” Rio’s voice softened with affection, glancing over at Agatha. 

“I’ll hold your hands, they’re just like ice,” her wife replied, her voice light and teasing, turning her head to smile at Rio before returning her focus to the road. 

Rio laughed and leaned over slightly, still singing as she moved closer, “My mother will start to worry…”  

“Beautiful, what’s your hurry?” Agatha tilted her head, the smile still on her face. 

The duet continued, their voices blending and filling each other with a warmth like no other. With every note, Rio felt more connected to her wife in a strange way. She didn’t always sing, she preferred catching her wife humming or singing to herself when she thought no one was listening. Her favorite was when they would watch a movie together and her wife would sing along with the musical whether it was Les Misérables, The Phantom of the Opera, or even Wicked. She had even tried to get her wife to sing at their wedding party, even planning a karaoke set up, but her wife shut that down. 

As they finished the final lines together, both of them laughing softly, Rio couldn’t help but lean over, pressing a kiss to Agatha’s cheek. 

“That was perfect,” Rio whispered, her voice filled with affection. “I love hearing you sing.” 

Agatha grinned, her cheeks flushed from the warmth of their shared moment. 

“You’re lucky I love you,” she teased. “But… I kind of enjoyed that.” 

Rio chuckled, her hand once again resting on Agatha’s belly, a quiet, contented smile on her face, “Good. Because we’re doing it again next time. Plus, I think he’ll like it when he can finally hear.” 

Agatha laughed, shaking her head, but there was no denying the warmth between them, both in the car and in their hearts. 

***

When Agatha turned off the road, onto a gravel one, Rio knew immediately where her wife was taking her. It was to the Yule market where a village would pop up like magic right before the holiday with trinkets to buy like candles, sun and moon ornaments, fresh wreaths and mistletoes, yule trees, statues of goats, and Rio’s favorite, the food: venders of wassail, Yule log cakes, gingerbread, nuts, berries, beef stews, and so much more. 

But something made her uneasy. Tonight was the opening night, which meant college students would surely be filled here. It was all too easy; she knew that they would run into their students. At least their co-workers were at the mixer, but that still didn’t settle her. Her wife must have known her thoughts as she took her hand, kissing the top of her knuckles.

“It’s okay, my love. We both knew our secret was going to come out sooner or later.”

Rio’s heart skipped a beat as Agatha spoke those words, and for a moment, it felt as if the weight of the entire world had shifted. Her instinct was to pull back, to keep the secret a little longer. But Agatha’s calm, unwavering confidence settled over her like a warm blanket. As much as she wanted to protect her wife, to shield her from looks and gossip, Yule was the perfect time for new beginnings– fresh starts. 

“I’ve been ready for the gossip to stop, mi amor. But, with how nosy this group is– I don’t want them saying anything more about you,” she murmured, glancing out the window at the small flickering candles that decorated the parking lot of the market. 

“Then, let them talk, my love,” her wife hummed, her thumb brushing circles over the back of Rio’s hand. “Plus, I think if it’s out now, by the time we get to the next semester, the excitement will have died down.” 

They both fell quiet as her wife rolled down the window, talking to one of the people directing traffic. Her wife was pointed closer to the pines that were growing in neat rows, people already running through them and picking their trees. Rio let out a breath, calming her nerves at her wife’s words. She had a point, and they wouldn’t have to hear the gossip anymore. They could just be together– Rio could watch her wife bloom with life over the month and a half break and it be just them… just in a place for them… 

Her wife backed the truck into a space and placed it in park before turning to her. Rio watched as her eyes sparkled with that familiar gleam– one Rio had fallen for all those years ago. 

“Besides, I want to spend tonight with you, my love. Walk the market, start picking our decorations, and for you to pick the last Yule tree before I take over for the next three years,” Agatha smirked at her. 

Rio raised an eyebrow, “I don’t think so, Mrs. Harkness-Vidal. The Vidal genes are stronger than you realize. You will be losing both bets, my love.” 

Her wife rolled her eyes, “I am carrying the kid, darling–”

Rio tutted, “Yeah, with my bone-sperm–”

Agatha tilted her head, a gleam in her eye, “Do I need to remind you what you were like after sedation?”

Rio scowled at that, remembering her wife laughing at her once she was fully conscious in their home. 

 

Rio grumbled as a light poked at her eyes. Her eyes felt too heavy, like they were fighting her brain from wanting to wake up, but there was something that was telling her to wake up. A gentle, angelic voice calling to her. She hummed for a moment, not understanding where she was– and for a moment she figured she was dead with this beautiful voice above her. Fingers brushed through her hair before cupping her cheek. She instinctively leaned into the touch, the grounding warm touch that triggered her eyes to open. She squinted, finding chestnut hair spilling over someone’s shoulders, and the light behind them, made their hair shine. It was like a soft halo crowning her head and Rio slowly took in how long and thick the chestnut locks were. Her eyes followed the waves up to a pale beautiful face and Rio felt like her mouth dry. 

Her cheekbones were sharp, her jawline must have been carved by the gods above and that adorable dimpled chin. Her lips were plump and a pale pink that were curved in a soft smile, a smile looking down at her as if she were the world. She blinked again, moving up to that nose– oh, it was cute. And then, Rio’s heart stopped. Her eyes grew wide as she met cerulean blue– a blue that was cleaner than any river in Switzerland– not that she had been there… had she been there? They were electric, something enticing– something that put a spell on her and for a moment, she almost fell for– what she decided– this witch. That’s what she had to be, the way she was trying to lure her in. This person was nothing but a predator staring down at her prey, ready to take her life for her own.  

Rio weakly smacked the hand off of her. Her arms flared out, trying to push the witch away, but it was a weak attempt as hands wrapped around her wrists, stilling her as the witch’s lips moved as if speaking to her. She only blinked again, squinting as if she made her eyes work better, then she could hear better. She stilled and the hands dropped her wrists, moving to cradle her face as the woman sat on the bed next to her. It was then, the sound rolled in like the slow rolling waves of an ocean coming to greet you. 

“Sweetheart, it’s time to get up, okay?” the witch spoke, the waves tugging at her feet, trying to lure her in closer.

Rio tilted her head as one hand remained on her face, a thumb brushing her cheek as the other held her hand. She blinked slowly again, unsure how to approach the witch. She was so fucking beautiful– like something out of one her dreams. It was almost unfair as she could barely put words to the etherealness above her. But one realization hit her hard. She was married. She had a wife… somewhere… someone married her, but it couldn’t be this beauty. And Rio Vidal was a respectful, loyal woman. So whatever this witch had planned for her, it wouldn’t work.

She slapped at the hands again, “You’re…you’re like really pretty– like really really really pretty” her voice slurred “but I-I… have a wife, querida.”

The witch cladded in purple and black laughed– perhaps in a loving manner as she took Rio’s wrists again, “I am your wife, my love.” 

Rio blinked. And then again for good measure because that was all she could do as the witch slowly morphed into an angel again, the halo crowning her rightfully as Rio felt her jaw go slack. She lifted her hand, reaching weakly to touch her face but ultimately failed, leaning back into the pillows.

“Wifey…” she murmured and Agatha put her hand to her mouth, seeming to cover her laugh.

She nodded, a smile still shining, “Yes, my love. I am your wife.”

Before Rio could respond, she jumped to the sharp scrape of metal and the fluttering of curtains flapping together. A nurse came in with pink scrubs and Rio scrunched up her nose. It didn’t make sense why there was another person with them. Why couldn’t they just leave her and this goddess– well she guessed if the woman said she was her wife, who was she to question it. 

“How are you feeling, Mrs. Rio Harkness-Vidal. You still seem a little out of it.” 

She grumbled out something but felt her eyes be drawn back towards the witch beside her. She couldn’t stop the smile that stretched her face as she took in the sight before her. Yep, this was definitely the last thing she wanted to see before she promptly kicked the bucket.

“How did it go?” the witch asked the nurse and Rio flared her nostrils. 

She didn’t like the witch paying attention to someone else. She felt something inside her, a need to turn the witch back to her. She couldn’t understand why another woman was talking to her if she was hers. She tried sitting up, to tell this random bitch off, but the room immediately spun. She groaned but then the hand was there, on her shoulder and holding her in place. 

“Doctor said the procedure went great. They were able to scrape enough bone marrow to use as sperm and we can begin the next phase next week. You can make an appointment up front once she’s a little more awake.”

Rio tugged at her wife’s arm and met worried blue eyes. 

She tried flashing a smile at her, reaching her arms out and moving her fingers in a grabbing motion, “Hug mm… huggie.”  

 

Rio snapped out the faint memory, grumbling to herself. She didn’t remember much else of it, but apparently Rio had tried to flirt with her wife when they were in the car. And that then led to something about her tongue being involved. Agatha wouldn’t let her hear the end of it for weeks, especially seeing Rio’s rare softer side. And even now, she brought it up every now and then to win an argument.

She turned her head away, muttering soft curses under her breath as she adjusted the scarf around her neck, but ultimately melted when she felt warm lips against her cheek. They got out of the truck, Rio coming around and fixing her wife’s scarf before handing her gloves. Before her wife could drag her off, she wrapped her arms around her waist, pulling her closer as Agatha’s nose was already growing the slightest shade of pink from the cold. She kissed her lips slowly, pressing her warmth and love into her wife, feeling her fall into her embrace. 

“Thank you, my love. For doing this, I mean,” she murmured against her lips, her head tilted down due to the heeled boots. 

Her wife smiled at her, her eyes meeting hers as her breath was hot against her mouth as she spoke, “You have dealt with enough, my love. You deserve this– we deserve this.” 

With that, Rio placed another fleeting kiss to her lips, leading her into the entrance of the market. The Yule market was alive with activity, a blend of voices and laughter swirling in the crisp winter air. Rows of wooden stalls stretched across the square, their roofs dusted with snow and adorned with twinkling fairy lights. Garlands of pine and holly draped across their edges, tiny golden bells tied into the branches jingling softly whenever the wind picked up. Vendors had decorated their stalls with wreaths and lanterns, their warm glow casting flickering shadows on the hay covered ground. 

The scent of roasted chestnuts, cinnamon, and mulled wine wrapped around them like a comforting embrace, mingling with the fresh pine from the trees scattered around the square. Somewhere in the distance, the sound of a violin played a soft, cheerful tune, adding to the enchantment of the evening. Rio wrapped her arm around her wife’s waist, guiding her through the bustling crowd. The food stalls were calling to her, their aromas thick in the chilly air, but before she could make her way over, Agatha lightly tugged on her sleeve. 

“I’ll get us wassail,” she said, already glancing toward a vendor lined with wooden barrels, steam rising from the large cauldrons where the spiced drink was being ladled into mugs. “And yes, my love, I know. No alcohol.”

“Good,” Rio nodded, taking off her backpack and handing her wife her wallet, “And I’ll get us food. You know, the important part.”

Agatha rolled her eyes, but there was warmth in them as she let go of her wife’s sleeve and drifted toward the cider stand. With her hands now free, Rio made her way toward the food section, already eyeing a stall selling roasted nuts glazed in honey and cinnamon. Across from it, another vendor arranged fresh-baked bûches de Noël, their scents mingling with the smoky aroma of sizzling sausages and golden tartiflettes. But before she could take another step, something caught her eye– a flash of a lighter green among the reds and golds of a nearby booth. She turned her head, her feet already moving on their own as she approached the stall. 

Nestled among soft woolen scarves and hand-knitted socks was a section full of baby clothes, and a onesie hung among the rest. Rio took it down, her hands moving over the soft, thick wool of the onesie. It was perfect for winter with delicate little embroidered leaves on the collar and holly berries decorating throughout. Something inside her clenched, warmth spreading through her heart as she turned to the tag, finding it was for a baby that was at least eight months old. She felt a soft smile take over her face as she couldn’t will herself to put it back. Her child wasn’t even born yet, but she could already picture them wearing it, small and snug in her wife’s arms. 

Before she could even try to reason with herself she didn’t need to buy it, an old woman approached, her smile tender and her eyes bright with the candles that surrounded them, “Do you want that wrapped, dear?” 

She nodded quickly, but quickly found her voice. 

“Yes, please,” she murmured, her fingers reluctantly letting go of the onesie as the old woman took it from her hands. 

“You must be expecting a little one soon,” the woman said kindly, carefully wrapping the onesie in soft brown paper tied with twine. “How far along are you?”

Rio exhaled, a rare softness in her expression. 

“My wife is carrying our little one,” she corrected, but it wasn’t with the usual irritation. “Our baby is due in May.” 

The old woman’s eye lit up with interest as she set the package into a green tote bag. 

“A spring child,” she mused, nodding approvingly. “Blessed by Betlane’s warmth and the turning of the wheel.” 

Before Rio could ask how much, the woman turned, sorting through the wooden drawers behind her counter. She frowned slightly as she watched the woman collect bundles of dried herbs and small vials of oil. 

“What are you—?” 

“Hush now, dear,” the woman said, gently but firmly. “You young mothers always think you don’t need looking after. But a mother and her child deserve all the protection they can get.” 

Rio stared, her mouth hanging open, startled. The woman turned back around with a small cloth pouch and bundle of tiny herbs in jars. 

“This,” she held up the pouch, “is an anointing oil– made with rosemary, lavender, and myrrh. Draw protection symbols on your wife’s belly, especially during full moons. It will strengthen the bond between mother and child and protect her.”

She put in the tote bag and reached for the jars of herbs, “These are also protective. Hang them above your door, burn a little in the evening, and add some to her baths. Sage for cleansing” she held the jars up one by one as she named them “mugwort for dreams, and rose petals for love. Now, it’s only fifteen for the onesie.” 

Rio reached for her cash, pulling out more, “No, please. Allow me to pay for the oils and herbs too–”

The woman clicked her tongue, waving a hand, “It is a gift, dear. From an old mother to a new one.” 

Rio shook her head, a small laugh escaping her as her head dipped in gratitude, “Thank you. Happy solstice.” 

“May your little one be born under the blessed stars.” 

She took the bag, thanking her once again, before turning and heading to the food. She picked up some tartiflette before grabbing two bowls of stew and searching for her wife with the tray in her hands. She ended up finding a table, setting their things down and pulling out her phone. 

Mi amor💜 : The line is long and they messed up on my order. Got gingerbreads too! 

 

Found a table at the end of the food section :Me

Rio blew at the stew in front of her, her phone on the table waiting to see if her wife could find her when a familiar figure made her stop. She turned, spotting a tall man with strawberry blonde hair. He wrapped his scarf tightly around him, carrying a tray of food himself before spotting her. He smiled, moving quickly towards the table. 

“Dr. Vidal,” he said cheerfully. “Skipping the mixer too?” 

She swore in her head, feeling her professional façade slipping into place as he sat the tray down on the table, rubbing his hands together.  

She nodded her head slowly, “Yes, figured I could use some air to breathe, Professor Vision.” 

The rush of anxiety flooded her as she glanced around for her wife. This wasn’t how this was supposed to go. Tonight was for her and her wife, away from work life and here was the physics professor she had hired recently, approaching her without fear. She straightened up, trying to ease the pounding in her heart and keeping herself from biting at her cheek. 

I swear if this fucker asks if I am pregnant–

“Please, Vision is fine. I must say, I am so sorry to hear all the rumors about you. It must be overwhelming, especially as the dean.” 

She glanced at her phone, swallowing the tension that set in her jaw, “Yes, but um– why are you here? And not at the mixer?” 

 

Wanda

 

She took a deep breath, taking the cold air to help ease the overwhelming sensation of everything eating at her. She couldn’t handle the thought of seeing Agatha tonight, as much as it was the plan to talk to her– to apologise, any thought of Rio Vidal made her want to throw up. The rumors grew worse, and it wasn’t entirely her fault. All she wanted to do was stir trouble for Vidal, but no, the professors decided to take it in a whole new direction. She had walked in on that Friday morning hearing endless whispers that Agatha wasn’t there. As the day drew on, the whispers grew louder. The Dean was absent too, both of them were absent without notice. And then came the rumors, crawling through the department like an infestation of rats and spread like the fleas they carried. The plague hit Wanda hard, and she couldn’t shake the guilt that clouded her. From Agatha and Rio being married, to her own rumor that she started with Rio being pregnant– and now she had dragged Agatha into it in the worst possible outcome. 

Agatha Harkness was sleeping through the faculty and taking advantage of the pregnant Dean. It was ridiculous and something that Agatha Harknes would be the last person to even try, and the thought of Agatha wanting to touch Rio in any way made her sick further. Vision had shown up to her apartment with roses and a warm smile, his cheeks pink from the cold. It was endearing, but it still didn’t ease her nerves. It was her fault now that Agatha was now the center of a scandalous and completely untrue rumor. Rio Vidal was nothing but a friend to Agatha, and it was only purely coincidence that they both were absent that day… right? 

She shrugged her shoulders back, rolling her neck in a circle as she settled herself. Tonight, she could forget and be present on this date. Vision was a wonderful man. Agatha was married… 

Stop thinking of her.

Vision was sweet, funny, and sure a little awkward, but he was cute. He made her heart flutter in a nice way. And he was understanding. They had called before he came to pick her up for the mixer and he must have heard her hesitate. He had simply offered to take her somewhere else, make it a real date and Wanda had agreed. So, that was how she found herself at the Yule market, away from her hosting duties with a simple text to Crawford and Lune that she was sick. They could manage without her, in fact, they had been running the whole thing without her. They rarely listened to her ideas and it wasn’t like the dean was going to listen to her either. 

Wanda smiled as she grabbed their beverages from a man, thanking herself for wearing gloves as the heat radiated through the wool, seeming to already want to burn her. Vision had said he would meet them at the nearby tables, hoping to find one open. She looked around, over the crowds of people carrying endless bags of goods and sweets. She smiled softly, the smell of cinnamon calming her senses as she quickly spotted him. His back was turned and seemed to have found a table. She put on her best smile, forgetting all about work as she weaved herself around the people. 

Just as she was about to reach them, she ran into someone, almost spilling their drinks, but she held them out, keeping the scorching liquid steady as the passenger-by mumbled out a weak apology. She groaned, looking up but this time she noticed Vision was talking to someone. She stepped to the side, trying to see who it was and the sight made the blood dissipate from her body. 

They stood apart by a table, a tray placed in front of each of them. Her eyes narrowed to the large dish of potatoes with melted cheese and bacon to the two bowls of stew before moving up the woman. Her winter coat was black and underneath the signature colour of green, a thick wool flannel. Her eyes continued, reaching the face of the Dean… the dean who was supposed to be at the mixer. Wanda’s mind raced, each heartbeat thundering louder than the bustling crowd around her. This couldn’t be… she had avoided the mixer to avoid her … the woman who had done nothing but ruin her life 

Rio Vidal, a picture of perfect grace and power, standing at the table with Vision. The two were deep in conversation and she watched as Vision motioned his hands around as he spoke. It made her stomach twist. Tonight, it was just supposed to be him and her… but no, Vidal had to ruin not only professional life, but her personal life. Irritation flooded her, the heat in chest intensifying as she stood frozen in place. Her thoughts were sharp, cutting her as she watched Vidal’s somehow relaxed demeanor. It wasn’t as cold, as harsh, but she still demanded respect– but yet they spoke as equals, even Vidal cracking a small smile. Vision’s easy, relaxed smile contrasted with the sharp edges of Wanda’s thoughts. All she knew was this had to be a joke. This couldn’t possibly be happening, but the more she shook her head, reached for the heat in her hands to wake her up, the more Vidal was there, speaking kindly with Vision… and for some reason, it felt personal– more than her just being an insufferable boss.

Wanda held the drinks tightly in her hands, her pulse quickening as she watched the way Rio gestured animatedly, flicking the raven curtain bangs out of her face as she leaned on the table and fixed the tote bag on her shoulder. 

What’s so special about her? Wanda thought bitterly. She is nothing but a cold-hearted bitch. Why is Vision so at ease with her? 

Her gaze shifted back to Rio, a flicker of something she couldn’t quite identify stirring inside her. Was it envy? Or was it something deeper? Something darker? As much as she wanted to avoid these thoughts, she couldn’t. She hated Vidal. She hated her face, her stupid smirk, the rise of an eyebrow, the power she carried, the way she had something with Agatha that Wanda didn’t– if it wasn’t for Rio Vidal, her life would be perfect… well, minus that Agatha was married. But it was fine, because even then she could have at least had the woman as a friend– fuck, she would have had friends at work if it wasn’t for Rio. 

Her grip tightened, And people really thing that fucking woman is one being taken advantage of? She is obviously taking advantage of Agatha. 

Just as she was about to step forward, probably about to do something that would end her career, a figure out of the corner of her eye caught her. Like a magnet, her eyes were drawn behind Vidal. Her blood came rushing back, but this time her heart dropped. Her feet carried her forward unsteadily as she watched as Agatha Harkness came beside Rio, smiling so softly at her.

No , she thought, her breath catching. Not now. Please, not now.

The truth that had been gnawing at her since the rumors first started felt so much sharper in the moment. Her mouth opened and closed, not a single sound being made as Rio’s arm wrapped around Agatha’s shoulders, pulling her closer. And then, Agatha leaned into it, a smile lighting up on her face as they turned back to Vision. 

As her feet carried her closer, she almost dropped the drinks, “ Mi amor–”

Mi amor… my love? her mind raced, not fully understanding the weight of the words until Rio continued. 

“This is Vision. The new physics professor. Vision, this is my wife, Dr. Agatha Harkness.” 

Wife?

Wife.

“…this is my wife, Agatha Harkness.” 

Wanda couldn’t speak. 

Wife.

She couldn’t breathe. 

Wife . 

She couldn’t move.

Wanda stood there, feeling every breath catch in her throat as the weight of the words hit her. Wife . The word clung to the air between them, thick and suffocating. She couldn’t even begin to process the whirlwind of emotions that flooded her– rage, confusion, bitterness– all wrapped around a core of deep, undeniable hurt. Her gaze remained fixed on Agatha, the woman she had admired, the one who had unknowingly drawn her in with just a glance. But now, in this moment, that admiration was twisted into something darker. Wanda’s mind was reeling, the anger welling up in her chest, each thought sharper than the last. She’s married to Vidal. To Rio fucking Vidal. It felt like a cruel punch to her gut, a sharp reminder that all her unspoken feelings, all her yearnings, had been for nothing. Agatha was gone, claimed, and the world she had imagined in her head– the one where Agatha might look at her, smile at her, even just talk to her– was a fantasy that had been shattered by the cold reality of the woman’s real relationship with Rio Vidal. 

“Lovely to meet you, Agatha,” Vision’s voice broke through the haze in Wanda’s mind, a bright and welcoming tone that contrasted with the storm brewing inside her. 

She watched, almost in slow motion, as Vision reached out to shake Agatha’s hand, his polite, unaware smile spreading across his face. Wanda’s stomach twisted painfully at the sight of it, feeling every discomforting ripple like it was an electric shock. Agatha, ever composed, smiled warmly at Vision, returning the handshake with ease. 

“The pleasure’s mine,” she said, her voice smooth, her presence radiating confidence. 

But even in the midst of that calm, Wanda could see the subtle way Agatha leaned into Rio, the quiet connection between them that no one could miss. Wanda felt as if she were watching a scene play out from behind glass, unable to break through the wall that had suddenly formed around her. She had never felt so out of place in her entire life. The man she was on a date with, the woman she had longed for, and the woman she hated with every fiber in her being– now all tangled together in a way she never could have imagined.

Before she could run, before she could hide away from the crumbling world, Vision turned, meeting her gaze with a smile, “Wanda, darling, there you are.” 

She smiled back and in truth she didn’t know how she did it. She let something take control, a part of her that was an actress, refusing to let it show how much she wanted to lunge across the table or maybe cry or maybe scream or maybe all three. But she would win a Grammy…a Golden Globe? Whatever the fuck it was for this performace. Her smile hurt her cheeks as she stood beside Vision not missing the way Vidal’s jaw clenched or how she seemed to pull Agatha closer against her. 

“Vis,” she smiled up at him, refusing to look at them. “Dr. Vidal, Dr. Harkness, sorry if he was bothering you.” 

Vision laughed but Agatha only gave a fake laugh and Vidal, she barely reacted, tilting her head at Wanda with a look that could kill. Vidal’s jaw was still tight, her gaze never leaving Wanda, as though she could see right through her, sensing the crack in Wanda’s calm façade. Agatha, on the other hand, seemed unfazed. She was still leaning slightly into Rio, her hand brushing against the woman’s arm in a way that made Wanda feel like an outsider, a stranger in a space she once thought she could belong. Agatha’s gaze met Wanda’s for just a moment, and there was a flicker of something there– something that might have been pity, or maybe it was just indifference. Wanda couldn’t tell. All she knew was that it hurt. It hurt so much.

“Yes, we should let you two be. It was wonderful to meet you, Dr. Harkness,” Vision said, grabbing the tray as Wanda stayed by his side. 

She didn’t hear what else was said as they turned, walking back into the crowd. Vision was talking about something to her, but she barely registered it. She looked back over her shoulder, something foolish in her hoping to see if Agatha was looking at her, but she was not. Agatha was looking up at Rio like she was the world– like she was the moon, the stars, the sun, the sea– like she was life incarnate. She was saying something and Rio nodded, a strange warm smile gracing her lips and then it happened. Rio leaned down, Agatha closing the distance, and when their lips met, Wanda staggered.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

Also, I did a shit ton of research on Yule. I want to celebrate Yule one day, but I grew up in a christian household like so I have been trying to explore more Pagan roots. if you guys have any notes on Yule, please send them my way.

Also, we are just going to pretend that the bone marrow way is capable of producing the Y chromosome. I thought about IVF, but then I figured "made from scratch" so like made from Rio's bone marrow and that it just tied well together with her also being Lady Death. I believe scientists are still working on the bone marrow method of pro-creating, but it also just seemed very AgathaRio to me.

Yes, I jumped on Rio's parents are dead train, but ya know, we love abuelitas sooooo, more Vidal family incoming and Yuletide festivities and soon the 18 week scan. (was gonna do more for this chapter, but then I hit 9k words and was like I need to calm down)

Finally, I realized I fucked up. I wanted Rio to have tattoos, but idk if it would be weird to mention them now ? let me know what you guys think

with love,

Rioallalong_719💚💜

Chapter 14: Love Unearthed

Chapter Summary

The sensation of Rio’s hand on her skin, her kisses, and the soft whisper of words in Spanish– their sacredness, the trust Agatha felt in this moment– it all collided into something overwhelming, tender, and raw. Her hands reached for Rio then, her fingers curling into the softness of her wife’s hair as she pulled her closer, desperate for more of this, more of Rio’s presence, more of this love that seemed to be unearthed from her wife’s very soul.

OR:

Fluff, smut, and....

ha, you really thought I was going to say everything nice 😈

Chapter Notes

IM BAACCKKKK

Here is your weekly update my darlings. I have read your comments and yes, Rio's tattoos are here, and we are gonna pretend they were here the whole time.

Also, ironically, the students in this piece are so real, because my friends and I just figured out our professor is pregnant and the whole class we were gossiping about it sooooooo... yeah

I also don't know why I keep posting these long ass chapters, i keep telling myself to pace myself because I don't want to make the chapters too long... but it seems they keep getting longer with every post... i have issues... also I feel like I really am making Agatha a bottom here, but like she's pregnant... idk what do you all think ?

Additionally, sorry I was gonna post last night but I past out so here is my late Valentine's day gift to you all 💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha

 

Snow poured from the heavens, smothering the roads, the trees struggling like Atlas to hold up their own weight from the assault. In the distance, a tree failed, crumbling to the ground and taking wires down with it who snapped from the first touch of a branch. Across Westview, candles lit every window, families huddled together inside their homes, telling Christmas ghost stories or setting up their altar for Yule. A few were lucky, powering on their generators to keep the heat while most scrambled to flick on their faucets to keep their pipes from freezing. It was a strange winter– by a strange winter, really a winter the Northeast of the States haven’t seen in years. Decembers of late kept their freezing miserable fall weather, and once or so in January or February, they were gifted a good snow fall to christen the slopes for a semi-good ski day. 

The walls of the living room flickered with light from the flames of the fireplace. They danced and bowed with a rhythm befitting the Imperial Russian Ballet, casting a spell to at least warm the single room. The house was already filled with decorations for Yule. A tree stood proudly in the corner, waiting to be decorated. The mantle above the fireplace was lined with holly and sprigs of mistletoe, their vibrant green and red standing out against the deep mahogany of the wood. Garlands of pine draped around the house, along banister in the basement, on the chairs of the dining room and kitchen, its scent filling the air with the unmistakable aroma of winter. 

On a nearby small credenza sat their altar for the Triple Goddess and for solstice, Agatha rearranged it with pinecones, evergreen boughs of fir and cedar. Rio had added fennel seeds for new motherhood and inner strength, a single raspberry leaf to symbolize birth, and a strawberry leaf to aid pregnancy. In the center of it, sat a ceramic dish with ashes of bay leaves from their wishes. 

A tray of cider steamed gently from a coffee table that was pushed to the side, the cinnamon and clove mingling with the woody fragrance of the fire. A plate of gingerbread sat beside it, their icing hard and cold from their journey from the market. On the couch sat a green tote bag, still yet to be revealed of the contents inside and beside it, a small crocheted green blanket with a few purple azaleas still yet to be finished and the needle wrapped in the yarn of wool. In the place where the coffee table should have been sat heaps of blankets and pillows, tangled and ruffled as heavy panting filled the air, another heat setting into the room that wasn’t from the fire. Bodies slid languidly against each other, fitting together with ease as soft breaths and moans slipped along with a small laugh or a simple hum of amusement.  

Agatha stared down into deep brown eyes, watching as her wife kept her eyes with her as she moved. Calloused but soft hands framed her hips, guiding her up and down in a slow deep pace. Sweat formed on their bodies, the fire lighting a golden sheen as Agatha felt every slow drag inside her, filling her over and over again. Her eyes traced down her wife’s face, to her lips and to the black ink on her shoulders. Vines wrapped around her collarbones, an allusion of them seeming to have disappeared into her skin. The phases of the moon were placed right under her breasts and her ribs were done, each a typiction of what lies beneath the skin. The contrasts of blacks and whites making it seem Rio’s ribs were on display. 

She was so used to them at points that she forgot they were there, but something in her brain triggered her eyes to them. Her eyes flicked to the deep purple azaleas on her wife’s bicep and then to the snake on the other, but Agatha’s favorite was Rio’s back tattoo– the way the black ink would flex with her muscles: an intricate design of the tree of life. The trunk lined with Rio’s spine, subtly made of human skulls, its roots twisted and gnarled at the base of her back, curved into protective runes. The branches danced along her shoulder blades, some leaves, some bare like bone. She was with Rio at every session and Agatha was obsessed ever since. 

Agatha’s fingers moved with deliberate slowness, tracing the edge of Rio’s collarbone, lingering over the tattoos that danced along her skin like an intimate map. Her breath was quick, warm against Rio’s body, but there was a subtle shift in her expression– a slight tightening of her jaw as she tried not to let her gaze wander, knowing her wife would always get smug if her gaze lingered too long. 

Too late apparently as her wife chuckled darkly, “Ah, this phase again, mi amor ? Was wondering when that would come back.” 

Agatha tilted her head, “What phase?” 

Rio’s eyes sparkled mischievously, a teasing grin curling at the corner of her lips. 

“The one where you’re obsessed with my tattoos,” she purred, her voice low and sultry, the heat of her words matching the fire that crackled in the background. 

Her fingers, still resting on Agatha’s hips, tightened slightly, subtly guiding her deeper into their shared rhythm, but it was clear Rio was enjoying the teasing moment. Agatha tried to hide the flush that crept up her neck, but let out a soft moan as her wife pushed the strap a little deeper.

“I’m not obsessed,” she replied too quickly, though the way her fingers lingered, tracing the designs on Rio’s skin, gave her away. “I’m just… appreciating the artistry.”

Her voice faltered just a little as her gaze flickered back to the intricate design of her ribs. It was impossible not to admire the way they twisted and turned with each movement, lining with her real ribs perfectly. Agatha moved her hands down from her collarbones, steadying herself with her hands splayed over contoured abs, ignoring the wide smirk of her wife as hands moved up and down her sides.

“You don’t fool me, mi amor . You’re practically memorizing every line, every curve. It’s like you can’t get enough.” She sat up, her breath ghosting over Agatha’s ear. “I like it though. You look so damn beautiful when you’re lost in me, in my body.”

Agatha could feel the heat of her words settling deep within her, and for a moment, her resolve wavered. She could deny it, but it was undeniable– the way the tattoos made Rio’s body even more intoxicating, how the ink and skin merged into something almost sacred to her. She shook out of it as she bared her teeth in the way her wife would and pushed her back down. Rio grunted as she landed, her head in the mess of blankets beneath her, black hair fanning out.

“Don’t get all cocky now, hun,” she curled her lip. “Need I remind you how obsessed you were when I got the tattoo on the back of my neck?”

The image flashed quickly through her mind, Rio holding her hand as the blood moon was placed at the base of her neck, before flashing to when they got back to their apartment. Rio had Agatha bent over on the bed, fingers roughly in her hair, clearing the view for the tattoo, and pulling her back with every thrust. 

Her wife didn’t even blink, “Need I remind you when my back tattoo was finished– fuck, Agatha, you bent me over the counter the moment we got home. I didn’t even realize you were wearing your strap until it was inside me, and the way your ripped my shirt off–”

 Agatha leaned down, kissing her wife’s lips to get her to stop talking. Red burned her cheeks as she remembered thoroughly planning it out before leaving to the tattoo studio with Rio. Okay, so she had done that, but who could really blame her when her wife’s back was already hot and then the ink was added– sue her. She did what any other sane woman, any other loving wife would do and bent her wife over their kitchen counter just to watch the way the ink moved with her muscles. 

She pressed her hips against her harder drawing a soft moan from her wife and wiping the smugness off her face. When she pulled away, sitting up on and moving up and down in her wife’s lap, Agatha watched as brown eyes trained to her stomach. 

She smirked, knowing she could get the last word, “And look who is staring now, all obsessed.”

But her wife had other plans.

Rio only tilted her head, her eyes softening, “Mmm, how could I not, my love? How could I not be obsessed with the mother carrying my child?”

Hands landed on her stomach before one guiding her to lean forward again, their lips met softly as Agatha melted into her wife. Her heart fluttered at the sudden change of tone, and she couldn’t stop her smile as her hand cupped Rio’s face, pressing her body flush against her.

“You say the sweetest things sometimes,” she murmured against her lips before attaching them back to hers. 

Hands moved up and down her sides as her pace quickened, but their lips never left. Her tongue swirled with Rio’s as their bodies moved in sync. Hands were in her hair, smoothing it down her back, caressing her shoulders to pull her impossibly closer. Her wife’s tongue dominated hers as she let her moans fall into her mouth without care. 

Fingers traced the delicate curve of Agatha’s spine, pressing, mapping, memorizing. Rio’s grip was firm, her touch both grounding and electrifying, a contradiction Agatha had long since surrendered to. She shivered as Rio’s hands roamed, as if she were reading her body like scripture, committing every breath, every twitch, every sigh to memory. Agatha pulled back slightly, her lips swollen, her breath mingling with Rio’s in the space between them. She tilted her head, taking in the way her wife looked beneath her– flushed, dark eyes half-lidded, a lazy, knowing smirk playing at the corners of her mouth. It was that smirk and Agatha would never tire of it, not that she would admit. 

“You’re insufferable,” she murmured, dragging a teasing hand down the expanse of wife’s torso, her nails barely scratching over warm skin, over ink and muscle that tensed under her touch.  

“And you love me,” her wife shot back, arching ever so slightly, chasing her touch, her breath hitching when Agatha’s hands settled on either side of her head.

Agatha hummed, pressing an open-mouthed kiss to the column of Rio’s throat, feeling the way her pulse thrummed beneath her lips. Her hands wandered, learning her all over again, marvelling at how her wife fit so perfectly beneath her, beneath her hands, beneath her lips. Rio exhaled sharply when Agatha bit down, just enough to leave a mark, just enough to make her gasp. Her fingers curled into Agatha’s hair, gripping, pulling, guiding her mouth back to hers. 

Their kiss was slow but deep, filled with unspoken devotion, with a hunger that burned between them like wildfire. When Agatha pulled back again, her hips still grinding down against her wife’s, she felt a hand back on the side of her stomach, a thumb brushing in circles over the expanse of her naked skin. 

“You’re carrying everything I love most in this world,” Rio whispered like it was a precious secret that came from her soul. 

Agatha’s breath caught. It was always like this with Rio– one moment teasing, the next overwhelming her with the sheer depth of her love, her devotion, her need. She almost didn’t know how to respond, but her lips claimed hers again, her tongue spelled out all the love she didn’t quite know how to put into words. 

Their moans entwined in a harmony, hands roaming, sparking against each other’s skin hotter than the fire that burned beside them. Her wife’s hips met her with every thrust, hitting everything inside her as she pulled away from her lips, searching for those rich soil eyes. Agatha never looked away as her brow furrowed, a pressure coiling in her lower stomach. A hand reached up, cupping her cheek tenderly, their foreheads touching, Agatha breathing in Rio’s panting breath. 

The lasting eye contact– the way Rio was looking at her like she truly was carrying everything Rio cared about–  made her hips stutter, a loud moan falling from her lips, but she forced her eyes to remain open as Rio’s brow furrowed. 

“Rio,” she gasped as the coil finally snapped, a bliss washing over her body as for a moment all she could see was a sea of soil. 

She heard her name echo into the house, her wife’s hips arched up into hers as her wife trembled, but the soil remained, their love unearthed. For a moment, Agatha swore she could hear their hearts beating in sync, beating for each other, beating for the life they were making. It was just them and their love before the fire, tangled in the middle of their living room in the middle of a power outage. 

She smiled as she felt lazy, lingering kisses to her cheeks, and a final one to her lips before hands were helping her lift off of her. Agatha gasped as the toy slipped out of her, a feeling of emptiness hitting her and it took everything in her not to sink back down. Strong arms guided, her body pressing up against a muscular one, her stomach somewhat resting on Rio’s as her leg hitch up on a thigh. She buried her face into her wife’s neck, smelling that perfect scent of pine and cedar-wood. 

Arms wrapped around her, holding her close as they both caught their breath, their chests rising and falling in cadence. Her scalp tingled as fingers brushed through her hair and a hand found its way back to her swollen stomach. Warmth remained inside her as she pressed lazy kissing to the underside of Rio’s jaw, her wife humming in contentment. 

“I love you, Rio,” she whispered over the crackling of the fire. 

Her wife’s arms tightened around her, protective, possessive, reverent. She tilted her head slightly, giving Agatha more space to press her kisses, her breath warm against sweat-slicked skin. The firelight flickered, casting golden shadows over them, over the ink winding across Rio’s body, over the curve of Agatha’s stomach where their unborn child lay nestled between them, safe, loved. 

A soft hum rumbled from Rio’s chest, deep and satisfied. She turned her head, brushing her lips over Agatha’s temple, then down to her cheek, her nose, until she found her lips again. It was a slow kiss, unhurried and tender, a silent answer to the whispered confession between them. 

“I love you too, mi vida ,” she promised against her lips, her voice rough with emotion, with exhaustion, with the quiet certainty of devotion. 

Agatha sighed, melting further into her wife, her body aching in the most delicious way, her heart brimming with something too vast to name. She traced lazy patterns over Rio’s chest, fingers gliding over muscle and ink, over the heart that beat only for her.

 

She wasn’t sure how long her wife held her like that, her fingers grazing over her stomach and combing through her hair until lips pressed to the top of her head and the body beneath her shifted. She groaned her annoyance but ultimately stood with her wife, but found herself in her arms immediately, searching those brown eyes. They were soft, filled with adoration as they stared back at her. Rio placed a kiss on her forehead, brushing a few strands of hair away from her face.
