“Tan apretada, tan perfecta para mí.” (So tight, so perfect for me.) 

She adjusted her hand now, knowing exactly what her wife needed. The sodden silk stretched with her hand, bunching against her knuckles as her thumb rubbed circles there on that sensitive bundle of nerves. It was deliberate but not rushing, pressing just how her wife liked. Her wife’s moans filled their bedroom, louder than usual for morning sex, but something in Rio beamed at thought knowing her wife was more reactive, more sensitive. 

Trailing kisses along her jawline, she murmured, “Quiero verte venir, sentir cómo te desmoronas en mis manos.” (I want to see you come, to feel how you fall apart in my hands.) 

Agatha’s breathing turned ragged, her body trembling. Her pussy squeezed Rio’s fingers painfully together as blue— almost black eyes snapped shut, her head shaking as her words were a babbling incomprehensible echo. 

Rio smirked, whispering, “Déjalo ir, mi vida. Dámelo todo.” (Let go, my life. Give it all to me.) 

With that, the dam snapped and Rio’s fingers were pushed out from the sheer strength as her wife came hard, squirting and yelling her name. Rio caught her, easing her through it as pressing kisses to her cheeks, whispering sweet nothings.

“Eso es… tan hermoso cuando me lo das todo, mi amor. Te tengo— lo hiciste tan bien para mí.” (That’s it… so beautiful when you give me everything, my love. I have you— you did so good for me.) 

Her wife finally went still, her eyes remaining closed as her breath was heavy. Rio pulled her hand out from between them, sucking on her fingers watching as a blue peaked open to watch her. She smirked as her cheeks hollowed out, her tongue wrapping around the sweetest of her wife’s cum before pulling her fingers out with a pop. Her wife huffed at her but to her surprise, she felt a soft push on her chest, an ask for her to move. She did, her eyebrows furrowing with confusion as her wife simply shifted on her side, her eyes closed once more as her breathing evened out. 

This time Rio didn’t hesitate to reach out, caressing her wife’s cheek, pressing soft kisses to her cheeks until Agatha came down. Her wife leaned forward there, kissing her lips, her tongue tasting herself inside Rio’s mouth. 

“Are you alright, mi vida?” she asked, a hand going down to rub her wife’s belly. 

Agatha nodded, saying softly, “Yes, I just don’t think I can be on my back anymore.”

She nodded, realizing her wife was truly growing. She smiled to herself, her gaze down at the swell of her stomach. She couldn’t wait to feel him kick, to feel the press of his tiny hands against her wife’s stomach, but something in her knew if he was anything like his mama, he was going to be handful. 

She was brought out of her thoughts when velvet lips pressed against her neck, a hand moving along her ribs, but the realization of last night crept in slowly. They had barely eaten, especially Agatha who always ate at least two servings at dinner. Carefully, Rio pulled her away, kissing the pout off her lips. 

“You need to eat, cariño. You barely ate last night.”

Her wife pouted again, leaning to kiss her neck again, but Rio placed a finger to her lips, raising a brow, “After, cariño. Please, you need to eat.”

Her wife finally groaned, rolling her eyes making Rio chuckle. Placing a delicate kiss to her cheek, Rio stood, heading out to their kitchen to make a large brunch. She moved into the rhythm, finding it calming her as it always had. She started the tea, and fetched the bacon from the fridge, allowing the sizzling of grease to fill the kitchen. Once they were done, she went back to mixing the last of the batter, but footsteps caught her attention and they weren’t the familiar foot falls of her wife. They were slow, too light, and too precise. 

Rio snapped her head to meet the steady gaze oft Abuelita and her face drained of color. Her whisk dropped from her hand as she stared at her, seeing the rise of an eyebrow from her. She felt the increase of her heartbeat as her mind shifted to earlier— to how loud her wife was, how Rio talked her wife through it in Spanish, and how Agatha screamed her name as she came. Their walls were not entirely thin, but they weren’t thick either, and she had learned first hand that their voices tended to echo down the hall, having the one time came home late and her wife was enjoying the new vibrator Rio had gifted her. 

“Abuelita,” she squeaked. “¿Qué sigues haciendo aquí?” (What are you still doing here?)

Her grandmother merely smirked at her, coming to the stove and taking the bacon off the pan. She laid the strips out with grace, lining them evenly on the plate with paper towels down to soak up the oil. 

“Bueno, tu hermana se fue con el coche y una vez que Oriana te llevó a la cama, tu esposa se desmayó contigo.” (Well, your sister took off with the car and once Oriana carried you to bed, your wife passed out with you.)

She nodded slowly, beginning to mix the batter again as an uncomfortable silence set it. Rio’s movements were stiff, cringing away from Abuelita as this morning’s events played sharply in her mind. Maybe they were lucky and her sister and Abuelita slept downstairs in the basement, but she doubted it, knowing her abuelita liked the chair in their living room to sleep— hopefully, Oriana slept downstairs.

Rio heard another set of footsteps, looking up to see her older sister come in, a strange look on her face. Normally, Oriana was one of the worst morning people you could meet. She would be loud, laughing, a bundle of energy somehow, pulling those she loved into tight hugs talking immediately. Now, she refused Rio’s gaze as she made herself coffee in uncharacteristically silence. She let her go, finishing breakfast in a tense silence that had her nerves on fire. 

A final familiar pair of footsteps padded into the kitchen, and Rio was quick with her tea, sliding it across the counter to her wife. That’s when she noticed Abuelita had left the kitchen some time ago, leaving Rio to simmer with her embarrassment. She was too caught up in it, she hadn’t realized both Oriana and Abuelita took their awkward presence to the living room. 

Rio turned back to the stove, flipping the pancakes just as her wife wrapped her arms around her, pressing a kiss to the back of her neck. In that moment, she surrendered to the grounding touch as her wife’s nose dragged along the skin there, her hands moving back to trace the back tattoo. Rio pulled the pan off, turning in the wife’s hold. Their eyes met, Rio staring into tired adoring blue. 

Arms wrapped around her neck, pulling her into a soft kiss, and like always, Rio melted, the racing of her heart easing to a steady rhythm. She pulled away though, quicker than her wife liked who immediately scowled at her. 

“Mi niña, ¿debería poner la mesa?” Abuelita asked from behind her wife. (My girl, should I set the table?)

Agatha’s face went pale as the same realization dawned on her. Her eyes grew wide at Rio who kept a steady hand on the curve of her stomach. Rio nodded at her abuelita, refusing her gaze as her thumb traced careful circles there. Her wife’s hand came over hers, her eyes wide with mortification. 

Before either could say a word, Abuelita came back into the kitchen, placing a hand on her wife’s back, “Ven, no deberías estar parada tanto tiempo, mija.” (Come, you shouldn’t be standing this long.)

Her wife was led away from her and into the dining room where Oriana grumbled passed her and followed them. Rio shook her head, her fingers pinching the bridge of her nose as she took a few calming breaths. Perhaps, they hadn’t heard anything and Oriana was just in a mood because of Maria. Rio grimaced at the thought of her eldest sister, the anger starting to boil as her fists tightened. Nails left bloodied crescents but ultimately soothed as her thoughts led her to the image of Maria getting hit by a bus… Regina George style. She smirked at the thought of getting that phone call. 

She returned to their food, grabbing the stack of pancakes in one hand and the bacon in the other. No one spoke as she set it down, returning to the kitchen to bring the hot tea kettle over, some syrup, and cut up strawberries. As her usual routine, her body moved, coming beside her wife and placing a soft kiss on her cheek. Oriana made a noise, making Rio snap her head up at her. 

“What?” she snapped.

Oriana shook her head, placing some bacon on her plate as Abuelita piled pancakes on her wife’s plate. Once everyone had their food in front of them, Abuelita straightened, regarding her with surprisingly tender eyes.

“Agatha me dice que aún no has ido de compras para el bebé. Vamos después del desayuno, Riocita,” she stated sternly. (Agatha tells me you haven’t gone shopping for the baby yet. We are going after breakfast, little Rio.)

Rio groaned, “Abuelita, solo tiene quince semanas de embarazo. Tenemos tiempo.” (She’s only fifteen weeks pregnant. We have time.)

“Solo le quedan veinticinco semanas,” Abuelita snapped. “¿Has empezado siquiera una guardería? ¿Elegiste un tema? Además, necesitas que me asegure de que consigamos el extractor de leche adecuado para ella.” (She only has twenty-five weeks left. Have you even started a nursery? Picked the theme? Plus, you need me to make sure we get the right breast pump for her.)

“Ugh, Abuelita, por favor,” this time it was Oriana who groaned. 

The matriarch’s eyes snapped to the second oldest, “No seas tan mojigata, Ori.” (Don’t be such a prude, Ori.)

“¡No soy una mojigata! Simplemente no necesitaba escuchar a mi hermanita—,” Oriana snapped back before her lips drew in a tight line. (I’m not a prude. I just didn’t need to hear my baby sister—)

Then, she slammed her fork down, eyes wide with horror as she turned to Rio, her face twisted like she had witnessed something unspeakable. 

“No,” she breathed, pointing a shaking finger at her younger sister. “We are not going to sit here and act like I didn’t wake up to—” she cut herself off with a strangled noise, shoving her hands into her hair. “Oh my God, I heard everything!” 

Rio stiffened mid-chew, feeling the blood drain from her face.

“Oriana,” she warned.

“No!” Oriana shrieked, slamming both hands down on the table like a woman on the brink of madness. “Do you have any idea what I woke up to this morning? Do you—?” 

She broke off, shaking her head so violently the short ends of her hair moved, “I will never be able to look at you the same, hermanita.”

Across the table, Agatha was completely unbothered, sipping her tea with a knowing smirk. Rio, however, wished the earth would swallow her whole. That the roots of the trees would break through their hardwood floors and drag her into the mud away from this.

“I—” she gulped. “I don’t—” 

Oriana whirled on Agatha now, eyes blazing with betrayal. 

“And you!” she pointed accusingly at Agatha’s growing belly. “You’re pregnant! How do you even have the energy for that?!” 

Agatha raised an elegant brow, completely unimpressed, “I have my ways.” 

Oriana let out a wail, throwing her head back dramatically, “To the mother, I need therapy!” 

Rio groaned, covering her face with both hands, the red burned her cheek as she wanted nothing more than to go back in time and stop herself. She would burst through the room, wake herself up before her wife woke up and stop this whole mess.

Abuelita, who had been quietly buttering a pancake, finally spoke, her voice calm and entirely unbothered, “Pues, al menos sé que mi Riocita está cuidando bien a su esposa.” (Well, at least I know my little Rio is taking good care of her wife.) 

Oriana gasped like she had been personally attacked, “¡Abuelita! ¿En serio?!” (Seriously?!) 

Abuelita simply shrugged, biting into her pancake, “¿Qué? Así es el matrimonio.” (What? That’s marriage.) 

Oriana screamed into her hands as Rio choked on her drink when she felt a hand on her thigh. Fingers gave her upper thigh a light squeeze before rubbing towards her inner thigh. Rio shook her away, throwing her a look, but her wife didn’t care, barely hiding her amusement

She was completely shameless, reaching for the syrup before flashing the smirk at Rio, “Pass the bacon, my love.”

 

*** 

 

The moment they stepped into the baby store, Rio realized she had made a grave mistake. Shopping with her family was a different type of torture— one she was sure could have been used during the Inquisition. First, Abuelita had demanded that Rio push Agatha around in a wheelchair, which both Rio and Agatha had immediately shot down. Agatha wasn’t bedridden— she was pregnant, not incapacitated. She could still walk, still move, still exist without being carted around like some delicate Victorian maiden. But Gods, that didn’t stop Abuelita from clicking her tongue every time Agatha so much as paused to rub a hand over her belly. 

“Mija, ¿estás cansada? ¿Deberíamos descansar?” (Are you tired? Should we rest?)

“Mija, no levantes eso— Río, ¡tómalo para ella!” (Don’t lift that— Rio, grab it for her!)

“Mija, estás creciendo, mi bisnieto, ¡deberías estar sentado!” (You’re growing my great-grandson, you should be sitting down!)

It was endless. And as if that wasn’t bad enough, Oriana— still deeply traumatized from this morning’s events— was no help at all. She scowled every time Rio placed a hand on her wife from soothing touches to her stomach, to a hand on the small of her back, and even to when Rio placed a light kiss on her wife’s forehead. 

The nightmare only grew when they reached the things for the nursery. Abuelita and Oriana were fighting in the middle of the store about what colors they wanted and what the theme should be without regard for the actual parents. Rio and Agatha sat back rolling their eyes and walking to the next aisle where without a word or even the slightest bit of a conversation, they decided they were going to do a forest theme for the nursery with the addition of stars and moons. It was a moment where Rio felt herself relax— her and her wife were on the same page without words. 

When Abuelita and Oriana found them, they finally relaxed, seeing them pick out decorations and talking about paint. It wasn’t even an argument. Rio said she wanted the walls a grass green and Agatha agreed. Agatha said she wanted a star and moon mobile and Rio nodded, saying she would look on Etsy. They both said the bedding would be purple but the only issue was the crib which both Rio and Agatha agreed none of the cribs they came across would do. 

Somehow, Rio convinced Abuelita not to buy clothes yet in case her son was actually a daughter— not that she really cared about the gender but, Rio had a bet to win. Her abuelita had simply smacked her saying that she was never wrong and that Agatha was most certainly carrying her great-grandson. 

The real nightmare hit its climax when Abuelita dragged them all down the aisle where breasts pumps were. Rio never wanted more than anything to bash her head into a wall, or be horizontal in grave as even her wife grew bright red— her wife who would only react to Rio, was now the brightest shades of pink, her mouth parted open, as her eye grew wide in embarrassment and disbelief. Yet somehow, Oriana’s expression was the one of pure mortification. The moment Abuelita started talking about suction power or how one machine mimicked the suction of a baby, she was gone. 

Rio and Agatha stood there rigidly, thanking all of the Gods that Abuelita refused to speak English as she went over the descriptions of each electronic pump. Eventually, the one that mimicked the suction of a baby was placed in the cart, along with a smaller portable one and a hand pump if they ever needed it. Rio was sure her and her wife would happily agree to the afterlife the moment they went to check out and it was one of their old students. If it was one of their current students, Rio was sure they both would have passed away on the spot. 

 

By the time they got everything in the house, Rio guided her wife into their bedroom for a nap. Before she left her, she placed a longing kiss to her wife’s lips where, the moment her head hit the pillow, she was out, snoring lightly. Rio adjusted her pillows, placing one behind her back and another for her belly to rest on. She knew she should have fought her wife harder on buying her the pregnancy pillow. Rio wouldn’t always be there as her wife’s personal pillow, no matter how much Rio adored having that perfect belly on her while they slept. 

Rio turned her gaze to her wife’s belly. A hand came over it, rubbing it softly as her wife smiled in her sleep. Rio chuckled, kissing the gentle swell of it before heading back into the living room where Oriana was passing out tea. She sat down with them, sighing and closing her eyes. A warm hand made her freeze, and she opened her eyes to Abuelita staring at her with nothing but affection and pride. 

“No te culpo, Riocita. Lo que sucedió fue parte del ciclo. Era su momento y no era el tuyo. Los estás haciendo sentir orgullosos y cuando tu hijo finalmente llegue, serás una excelente madre,” her abuelita’s voice was quiet and firm, carrying all the assurance understanding a human could be capable of and more. (I don’t blame you, little Rio. What happened was a part of the cycle. It was their time and it was not yours. You’re making them proud and when your son finally comes, you will be a wonderful mother.)

Rio finally broke again, but this time not out of grief, not out of anger of her sister, but simply hearing the words from her abuelita. She hiccuped as her tears ran down her face, drowning her eyes where everything became blurry. The withered hands wiped them away as Rio put a hand to her mouth trying to cover her sobs. Instead, Abuelita pulled her hand away and wrapped her arms around her, holding her close. Her body shook as she buried her face into Abuelita’s shoulder and for a moment she felt like a teenager all over again. 

It wasn’t often that Rio would sob like this when she was a teenager, unsurprisingly, she was always angry and cold. But when she did break, when she couldn’t hold it all together, Abuelita was there without judgement, holding the pieces together that Rio desperately clung to as if she was over the edge of a cliff, holding the thread by its thinnest part, begging it not to fall. Abuelita would be there, pulling her back and taking the thread as if it weighed nothing and would wrap Rio into her arms, sewing her back together. 

Abuelita was always the one without judgement when it came to her. At least to the things that mattered to Rio. When she was caught with a girl in her bed, Abuelita didn’t blink. When Rio went through her first, very cliche- very traumatizing, sapphic breakup, Abuelita knew and made Rio’s favorite tea and together they sat down and watched a nature documentary. However, when Rio got tattoos and piercings— got caught with weed, she received a long lecture and a few harsh slaps to the back of her head, worst case the chancla. She was hit hard the day she revealed she’s been dating Agatha for three years and never once mentioned her. And then hit again when Abuelita met Agatha and was enamoured by the fact she spoke Spanish. Agatha had laughed at her. 

She clung tighter to her abuelita’s forest green sweater, taking in the sweetness that was the familiarity of her perfume. Hands moved through her hair as her shoulders shook, her sobs loud, but muffled into her sweater, probably spreading her snot and make up on it, but she didn’t care, because Abuelita’s warmth, her love, her safety was everything. She felt a hand on her back and knew it was her sister. And slowly, Rio melted into the perfect embrace that was her family. 

 

Oriana convinced Abuelita to leave Rio and Agatha that night, telling her there was more to do later with them and they would plan dinners later. Oriana had booked an Uber and a new hotel, one closer to Rio and Agatha’s and away from Maria. Oriana was also due to meet Jack and to pick up the ring from Maria to return it to him. She wondered how nasty that fight would be and prayed that Oriana would finally grow a spine to tell the cunt off. 

 

Her steps were slow and heavy as she walked down the hall to her bedroom to check on her wife. In truth, everything in her screamed to lie down with her and fall asleep with her wife in her arms, but she had to make dinner and she knew her wife would be hungry soon. Instead of meeting the darkness of her room and her wife lying in bed, Rio found the lights on, and her wife was gone. 

Panic crept in fast, flowing up her veins from her hands and feet and straight to her throat. Then, her hearing clicked to the sound of water running and the light humming of her wife from the bathroom. She approached slowly, expecting to find her wife in the tub, but instead, she was knelt on the ground, dressed in sweatpants and a sweatshirt with her long hair down to her waist. 

Rio stared for a moment, leaning against the door frame watching her. Candles flickered around them, the low glow highlighting the perfect curve of her wife’s nose, her sharp jawline, and the gentleness in her wife’s eyes. She watched as a slender hand, the hands she would always drool over, with a few perfect veins shining through the skin, made its way under the water before reaching for the bubbles— Rio’s bottle of bubbles. It was a pine scent with some type of woody undertone that Rio adored. Her wife was methodical and attentive, pouring in the perfect amount before flicking on the jets. 

Rio shifted her weight, causing the tile underneath her foot to creak. Blue eyes stared up at her, a knowing smile on her lips as a hand reached out towards her. Rio took it, allowing her wife to pull her closer before raising the other hand, a silent ask to help her stand up. 

“What’s all this, mi amor?” she asked, her voice coming out more shaky than she wanted. 

Agatha tilted her head, before cupping her cheeks with both hands and suddenly, her vision blurred. She blinked rapidly, feeling the tears spill over and her wife’s careful thumbs were brushing them away. 

“Don’t think I didn’t hear you, my love,” she spoke softly as Rio grasped at her wrists, licking away the tears that Agatha missed. 

Her eyes fluttered closed as her wife kept her tender grip on her cheeks. More tears flowed as lips pressed to hers, ignoring the saltiness that probably laid there and maybe even Rio’s snot as she sniffed hard. 

“Let me take care of you, my love. You deserve the world and more—”

“You are my world—”

Her wife rolled her eyes, “Don’t get sappy on me now, Vidal.”

“Harkness-Vidal,” she corrected, a small smile tugging at her lips, but it was weak as more tears flowed. “And you like me sappy.”

Agatha shook her head in defeat before pulling her into another gentle kiss. When they pulled away, her wife’s hands dropped to Rio’s black shirt, lifting it over head before starting at her jeans, unbuttoning them and the zipper undone. Before she could get further, Rio stopped her, taking them off herself along with her socks and throwing them in the hamper. A finger hooked on her boxers, her Plants VS Zombies pair that her wife got her as a joke. She had changed into them after this morning with her wife, her regular ones ruined. 

Soon, she was standing naked before her wife and all Rio felt was safety and warmth, despite the cold air making her nipples stiff. When Agatha tried to lead her into the tub, Rio stopped her, pulling at the sweatshirt.

“My love,” her wife stopped her with a hand on hers. “This is for you. Please, cariño, let me take care of you tonight and then you can go back to being my overprotective wife.”

Rio only shook her head, “You can take care of me by joining me, my love.”

Her wife sighed in defeat, allowing Rio to undress her and the moment her belly was on display, Rio’s hands were like a magnet, both hands resting there and caressing the skin. She smiled, already noticing the faint red lines of stretch marks blossoming. Her thumb brushed over them, a stupid grin on her face. Before she could think, she knelt down before her wife, her lips pressing kisses to them. 

“Gods, mi amor, you are so beautiful” a kiss was placed on a dark line “so stunning” another to a smaller web forming “so perfect,” and a final one to the other side where the stretch mark was darker. 

She looked up, finding tears in her wife’s eyes, as she laughed lightly, shaking her head, “Goddamnit, Rio, I’m supposed to be taking care of you.”

Rio pulled her underwear down before standing up before her wife, pulling her flushed against her, “We will take care of each other then.”

Agatha pushed her, shaking her head as tears poured faster down her cheeks, “Just get in the damn tub.”

She laughed lightly, stepping into the hot water before extending the arm to her wife. She lowered them slowly, her skin stinging slightly, from the hot water. Her wife always made the baths a little too hot but it was perfect, easing the ache in her body and once Agatha’s back pressed to her front, it eased the ache in her heart. 

Her wife ducked her head underneath Rio’s chin as the sound of bubbles and soft breaths filled the room. Rio gathered her wife’s hair, using a hair tie off her wrist to put it in a low bun, the ends already dripping, but her wife didn’t seem to care. Then, Rio’s hands subconsciously found her wife’s stomach, rubbing gentle circles there as the last of anger and hurt left her body

Rio was right where she belonged. She was with her wife and unborn child, holding them close and knowing, wherever her parents were, they would be proud. And her mother would be so shocked to see Abuelita cry, or maybe she wouldn’t. She wondered what happened when they told Abuelita they were going to have a third kid. Did Abuelita cradle her the moment she landed in her awaiting hands as a newborn, swaddle her in a green blanket before being placed in her mother’s arms? Yet, all she knew was that when Abuelita would hold her son for the first time, they would form an unbreakable bond the moment he looked at her. 

She kissed the side of her wife’s head, missing the hitch of breath from her and the way Agatha tensed. A hand came underneath Rio’s rubbing in circles before a louder hitch of breath made her thoughts cease as she lifted her head abruptly.

“Agatha? What’s wrong?” she sat up, one hand on her belly, the other cupping her cheek, but her wife didn’t turn to look at her.

No, she was staring at her stomach confusion knitting her eyebrows together. Rio’s heart raced as she waited for her wife to speak. She pulled her closer, nuzzling into her wife’s neck to calm her own nerves so she could be there for her wife. 

“It’s- it’s too early, right?” Agatha’s voice wavered as her blues glistened with tears.

“Too early for what, mi amor?”

Agatha sucked in a breath for saying almost too quietly, “Too feel them?”

Rio’s heart stopped as her mouth opened, her voice in quiet awe, “No, um, it’s rare but not uncommon or unusual. What do you feel?”

“Fluttering, everytime you stroke your thumb,” her wife replied, making Rio press a gentle circle at the swell. 

Her wife gasped again before letting out a soft laugh, “Oh, my love, I feel them. They’re there.”

 

They’re there.

 

He’s there. 

 

Their son, quietly making himself known to his mama and soon to his mami.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

Also, next week is my midterm exams, so I won’t be posting next week until after I am done. Maybe I’ll slip a chapter in but I cannot guarantee anything.

See you after midterms my darlings 💚💜

Chapter 18: Guess

Chapter Summary

Ever since they find out her wife was expecting, Rio has been the one on the giving end, spoiling her wife, but there is that certain fire in her wife’s eyes, a fire that wanted to burn Rio from inside out until she was nothing but a shaking, quivering mess– a fire that wanted to ruin her. And fuck, Rio gladly brought the gasoline.

OR:

3k of plot
6k of smut

Thank the writing demon

Chapter Notes

OKAY the writing demon got the best of me, and I had to write smut before locking in for midterms. Thank the demon.😈

Also, i did a poll of twitter for the use of "good boy Rio" but then still didn't get that great of stats and got nervous, so I didn't do it, however, if there are any requests in the comments, go ahead and lmk what stuff yall don't like and do like— or message me on Twitter @krvidal2119

This is like the craziest smut I have written, if it's not your style, you can stop after William's POV. If you know me... no you don't

‼️Please check the updated tags.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

William

 

William stood straight and tall, gazing out the window into the night. His shift had barely begun– only two hours in– and already, exhaustion clung to him like a second skin. He and Ireland had been roped into working at his parents’ new restaurant, a place that leaned into the witchy charm of Westview. “The Tarot of the Lovers” –a name so sickeningly romantic that William wanted to gag. The concept was simple, painfully so. It was a restaurant designed for couples. Instead of chairs, velvet loveseats were placed, forcing people to sit shoulder-to-shoulder, their hands free to wander, their gazes locked in dreamy, lovesick exchanges. The menu? Equally nauseating. Every dish was crafted for sharing. If one partner craved seafood carbonara and the other wanted chicken Alfredo, each would receive a half portion, perfectly placed so they could– of course– feed each other. William found the whole thing ridiculous, overpriced, and a blatant cash grab. But, to his dismay, it worked. His parents were making an absolute fortune. 

It was the third night since opening, the day before Christmas Eve, and the place was packed. William was darting between tables like a man possessed, dodging flirty touches, rolling his eyes at whispered sweet nothings, and doing his best to ignore couples getting a little too comfortable. He and Ireland, however, had found a silver lining: the drama. They lived for it. Husbands arguing in hushed voices, girlfriends shooting death glares across the room, the subtle tension of an affair unraveling right before their eyes– it was all prime gossip material. 

Finally, William found a moment of reprieve at the hostess stand, inhaling deeply before scanning the list of upcoming reservations. One table remained open, nestled by the window, its occupants due to arrive at any moment. Ireland scribbled notes beside him, her expression neutral, though William could tell she was just as eager as he was to finish this night. 

“Who’s my next couple?” he asked, leaning against the podium. 

Ireland scrolled down the list but didn’t get the chance to answer. The chime of the doorbell cut through the chatter of the restaurant, and when William turned his head, his breath caught in his throat. A woman in a tailored red suit stepped through the entrance, each click of her heels resonating against the hardwood floors. The fabric hugged her frame effortlessly, the flared pants giving way to impossibly long legs. A single button fastened the blazer, revealing a hint of lace from a black bra underneath, and across her collarbones, black ink swirled in elegant designs. Her dark eyes landed on them, cold and assessing, before she turned slightly as the door chimed again. Another woman stepped inside– just as stunning, yet in an entirely different way. She was wrapped in a sleek black dress that traced every curve, the neckline plunging in a way that should have been scandalous but somehow remained impossibly elegant. Her chestnut hair cascaded down her back to her waist in thick waves, and around her neck sat an amethyst pendant, the symbol of the Triple Goddess shimmering in gold. 

But it wasn’t her beauty that had William’s eyes bulging– it was the roundness of her stomach, cradled naturally by one hand as she reached for the other woman, their fingers lacing together with ease. His stomach dropped. The woman in red– the one with the piercing gaze and icy expression– spoke first. 

“Reservation for Rio Harkness-Vidal.” 

Harkness-Vidal. 

Harkness-Vidal. 

Harkness. 

Vidal. 

William’s brain short-circuited. He barely registered Ireland’s sharp inhale beside him, too lost in his own realization. His professors. Dr. Vidal. Dr. Harkness. Married. And now– holy shit– Dr. Harkness was the one pregnant . 

He glanced at Ireland, her eyes darting between the couple in rapid succession, her face draining of color. William knew exactly what was running through her mind: the mortifying memory of accusing Dr. Vidal of being pregnant when– right here, in front of them– the answer had been obvious all along. He kicked her, snapping her out of it, and she straightened, clearing her throat as she put on her best customer service smile. 

“Dr.– I mean, Mrs. and Mrs. Harkness-Vidal,” she greeted smoothly, “welcome to The Tarot of the Lovers. Mr. Kaplan will be your server tonight. If you follow him, he will show you to your table.” 

Neither of them acknowledged her attempt at warmth. If anything, Dr. Vidal’s expression remained unreadable as William grabbed a menu and led them through the dimly lit restaurant. He placed the menu down at the secluded table before stepping aside. He barely concealed his awe as Dr. Vidal– ice-cold and intimidating to students– turned to her wife with a breathtaking smile. To him it was foreign, unreal, a look that didn’t belong on her face, and yet here she was, a smile that was downright devastatingly charming. Then, just to add to the foreignness of it all, Vidal extended a hand as if she was presenting royalty, guiding Dr. Harkenss into her seat, sitting her down with grace before taking her place beside her. Harkness didn’t move as Vidal immediately had their thighs touching, their shoulders brushing as she opened the menu for them. The contrast was staggering. 

William swallowed hard, grasping for professionalism, “If I may offer the wine of the night, a Chardonnay straight from Burgundy, France–” 

He held up the bottle, ready to pour it into Dr. Harkness’ glass but paled as a hand covered the glass, an amethyst ring shining up at him. 

“We’re fine, thank you,” Dr. Vidal said sharply, her attention still focused on the menu. 

He nodded quickly, “I’ll give you two a moment.” 

He hurried back toward Ireland, gripping the wine bottle like it had personally betrayed him. When he reached her, their eyes met in mutual horror. 

“They’re married.”

“Dr. Harkness is pregnant.”

They spoke at the same time, whispering like they were uncovering classified government secrets. From across the room, they could just make out the couple in their quiet conversation. Dr. Vidal murmured something, her hand instinctively stroking over Dr. Harkness’ stomach, while the latter rolled her eyes and swatted her away. It should’ve been a mundane interaction, but there was something about it– an easiness, a familiarity– that made William’s stomach twist. Ireland, however, was stuck in her own spiral. 

“Oh my God, William, I fucking accused Dr. Vidal of being pregnant and it was her wife– her wife who is the Purple witch– this whole time– the whole fu—” 

His hand shot out, slapping over her mouth before she could complete the sentence. Nearby guests glanced at them, and William forced a sheepish smile before removing his hand. 

“Lower your voice,” he hissed. “They don’t even recognize us. Plus, Vidal just shooed me away after I offered them wine–” 

“You offered them what?” Ireland screeched, then promptly burst into laughter. 

William frowned, “What’s so funny?”

She wiped at her eyes, still giggling, “William, pregnant people can’t drink alcohol. How do you not know this?” 

He shrugged, “How am I supposed to know? It’s not like I pay attention to women.” 

Ireland rolled her eyes, but sighed, “Fair. Before you go back, don’t offer her caffeine, alcohol, or anything with raw fish. If you’re unsure, just ask about dietary restrictions. Honestly, Vidal strikes me as the type to avoid anything Harkness can’t eat.” 

That part, at least, made sense. William nodded, turning back toward their table. But then he stopped, something catching his eye. Dr. Harkness was gazing at her wife with a softness that didn’t match the sharp, unyielding woman he knew from class. Dr. Vidal– always so composed, so untouchable– leaned in, brushing a strand of hair from her wife’s face with a touch so gentle it nearly made William feel like he was intruding. 

Vidal was the first to move, tilting forward to claim her wife’s lips in a kiss that was neither rushed nor hesitant– just effortlessly natural. Harkness followed with ease, their connection resonating with a quiet intensity. It wasn’t as drawn-out or dramatic as some of the other couples in the restaurant, yet it carried a weight that made it feel more profound. He froze, his eyes impossibly widening as he stared at the professors that actively made his and his classmates’ lives hell. They were not just married, they were a perfect match.

When they finally parted, William discreetly placed a basket of warm bread and a dish of oil on their table. 

“Shall we start with drinks?” he asked, attempting to sound casual. 

“Water is fine,” Harkness answered smoothly, her tone leaving little room for discussion. 

“And have you decided on your meal?” William swallowed hard, his gaze flickering– unbidden– to Harkness’ rounded stomach. 

He tensed when Vidal’s hand swept over it in a slow, absent-minded caress. For a moment he feared that she had caught him staring, but no. Her focus remained on the menu, her fingers tracing gentle circles over her wife’s belly as if the motion was second nature. Harkness, for her part, didn’t even acknowledge it. She was scanning the menu too, completely unfazed. 

Vidal finally looked up, her gaze cold, “What do you recommend? No fish, no raw dairy, and nothing with soft cheese.” 

William nodded, regaining his composure, “The wedding soup is a favorite. It’s served in a large bowl for two to share. I also highly recommend the beef stroganoff or the chicken carbonara. If you’d prefer something heartier, the steaks here pair wonderfully with a creamy mushroom sauce, and the schnitzel comes with a rich pomegranate glaze.” 

Vidal closed the menu without hesitation, nodding, “We’ll have the wedding soup and the beef stroganoff. Absolutely no red in the meat.”

 William took the menu from her, his brow creasing slightly. She hadn’t even checked with Harkness? He cast a glance between them, curiosity stirring. Everyone knew Vidal was a control freak in the classroom, but so was Harkness– how did that balance work? More than that, why was Harkness so comfortable letting Vidal decide for them both? He thought back to the murmurs about what had happened between Vidal and Maximoff, about how Vidal had snapped. Then, to how she had immediately taken Harkness’ hand, fingers locking together with quiet possession, or how her palm had barely strayed from the curve of her wife’s belly. Shaking his head, he punched in their order and returned to Ireland, his expression pensive. 

She arched her brow, “What?” 

He hesitated for a moment before muttering, “Vidal seems like a control freak.” 

Ireland snorted, “Yeah? And you’re just now realizing that?” 

“No, I mean with Harkness. She seems… I don’t know, possessive? She just ordered for them without asking, and her hand is constantly on her stomach.” 

Ireland groaned, exasperated, “Dude, she’s pregnant with Vidal’s baby. Of course her hand is on her stomach– she’s probably soothing cramps, or if the baby’s moving, she’s feeling that.” 

“No, just… just watch them,” William insisted, nodding toward their table. 

They both turned in time to see Vidal tuck a stray strand of hair behind Harkness’ ear, murmuring something that made the literature professor roll her eyes. Harkness said something back, her tone sharp, and pushed Vidal away lightly, exasperation flickering across her face. 

“See?” William whispered. 

Vidal, undeterred, simply smirked, reaching out to tilt her wife’s chin up with a single finger. Harkness huffed but didn’t resist, rolling her eyes once more before reaching for a piece of bread. 

Before William could comment, someone called out, “William, your water for table six.” 

With a sigh, he grabbed the tray and made his way over, catching a snippet of their conversation as he poured. 

“My love, don’t be ridiculous– I’m perfectly fine working at eight months. Plenty of other women do it,” Harkness said smoothly. 

Vidal shook her head,“You’re not other women, and we get paid leave. I already have everything scheduled with Pepper. At six months, you’re taking maternity leave.” 

Harkness scoffed, “Rio–” 

“Agatha,” Vidal interrupted, voice edged with warning. 

Harkness flicked her hair back, “Just because you’re my boss doesn’t mean you get to dictate this.” 

Vidal’s smirk deepened, “Oh, it absolutely does, mi amor .” 

William expected irritation from Harkness, maybe another protest. Instead, the literature professor’s lips curled in a way that sent a glint of challenge into her eyes. 

“So does that mean you’re going on break with me?” 

Vidal’s sharp features suddenly softened. Her head tilted slightly to the side as her hand now moved in slower, more deliberate circles over her wife’s belly. 

Her eyes sparkled and her voice dipped in a tender softness, “Of course, cariño . The few classes we have will be partnered with other professors. Dr. Kale has already agreed to combine her sophomore class with ours, and Dr. Crawdad is thrilled to take over for you.” 

Harkness stilled, her eyes darkening, “You told Crawdad? The bird couldn’t keep its mouth shut even if you cut it off.” 

William winced, recognizing Dr. Harkness’ brutal bluntness, but Vidal didn’t even flinch. No, the woman merely laughed.

“We knew it would come out eventually, cariño . That’s what you told me.” 

Harkness huffed but softened when Vidal pressed a lingering kiss to her cheek, entirely unconcerned with William standing awkwardly beside them. Taking that as his cue, he shuffled away, his mind reeling from the interplay between them– the push and pull, the bite and the tenderness, the sharp edges that somehow softened against each other. He found himself glancing back at them throughout the evening. When he finally returned with their soup, Vidal had both hands on Harkness’ stomach, thumbs stroking slow, lazy patterns as if drawn there by instinct. 

“Ah, mijo –” 

“You don’t even know if it’s a son, my love,” Harkness interrupted with another roll of her eyes. 

Vidal only grinned, “ Abuelita nunca se equivoca. Vamos a tener un hijo. Admite la derrota ahora, mi amor.” 

William blinked, not understanding what she had said as he set the hot bowl down, grabbing the professional cheese grater as Harkness didn’t miss a beat. 

“ Sigue regodeándose y descubre qué pasa esta noche,” she hissed back at her wife before Vidal snapped his gaze up at him. 

He flinched as she flicked him away with a simple twist of her wrist. William made his rounds to the other tables, receiving kinder interactions from the couples, but his gaze remained on them. Harkness’ eyes lingered, sharp and heavy, filled with something that William couldn’t quite grasp yet. The intensity in her eyes as she watched Vidal– tender yet possessive– made his stomach tighten. His mind scrambled, caught somewhere between confusion and fascination, as though he’d stumbled into an unspoken language. 

Vidal, seeming to block out the rest of the world, met the gaze without hesitation. There was something unspoken between them, a connection woven into the very air, thick and potent. The woman was entranced by her wife, her eyes soft with adoration, her fingertips grazing over the pregnant woman’s skin as if the very act grounded her. 

“Let’s see how you fare tonight, love,” Vidal teased, her voice low, playful, yet carrying that unshakable authority William had learned to fear. 

But Harkness, as always, played the game with a deadly grace. She leaned in, brushing her lips against Vidal’s cheek with a quiet promise– a promise he could feel, even from where he stood. The edges of their words and touches wrapped together, a mixture of love and tension that William, still fumbling in his own confusion, could only observe. He quickly averted his gaze, shaking his head as though he could dislodge the sharp awareness prickling down his spine. But there was no avoiding the simmering charge in the air, the kind of quiet intimacy that ignited the room in unseen ways. 

When he looked back at them, Vidal was feeding Harkness with more care than a chef had for a delicate dish– holding the spoon as though it were a sacred task, a ritual of deep connection. 

“Is she feeding her like she’s starving?” William murmured to Ireland when he reappeared at the hostess stand, the unease still evident on his face. 

Ireland’s sigh was practically a groan, frustration mixing with a deep sense of knowing, “You’re an idiot, William. She’s pregnant. She’s carrying a piece of Vidal, and that little piece is probably going to be a terror like its parents.” She shot him a pointed look. “Pregnancy isn’t just about food, it’s about patience. Support. Love. Vidal’s doing the best she can to make sure her wife is comfortable. She’s not some ice queen, no matter how much she acts like one, no matter how terrifying she is. Now stop asking questions and do your damn job.” 

William’s frown deepened, but he couldn’t help glancing over once again, watching the silent conversation unfold between the two women, even after he served their dinner, in which the Dean was still feeding more of the food to her wife. Her touch was constant, soft yet firm, as Harkness relished in the pliable connection between them. There wasn’t the normal armour, the normal walls that seemed to hold her above everyone else. No, here the pair were as equals, sharing soft laughs, smiling at each other, and speaking a language only they knew.

 

He set down their dessert, the crème brûlée glistening under the soft light. As he retreated, the tension between them thickened again, the love, the devotion too palpable to ignore. Harkness’ eyes, locked on her wife, held a hunger that was not for the crème brûlée, and William felt a strange, involuntary shiver. 

“Here’s your dessert,” he murmured, his throat tight, not quite meeting their eyes. 

He could feel it– the awareness, the bond– trickling into him, even if he didn’t fully understand it. It made his skin prickle, a deep ache spreading through him, as if something had been set into motion that he couldn’t name. He retreated quickly, his mind a whirlpool of confusion as he waited for them to finish. 

 

They had set the dessert aside minutes ago, yet neither moved, leaning close and laughing softly. And just as William went to approach, something indeed did change. He stood there awkwardly, his arms at his sides as he watched as Harkness cupped Vidal by her cheeks, pulling her in for a kiss. This time, she wasn’t sweet or tender, it was desperate, deep, and fuck– I’m gonna puke.

Yet, William couldn’t move, his eyes locked on the professors as Vidal grabbed at Harkness’ wrists, pulling her somehow closer as he heard himself gag as he swore he caught a motion of tongue. He snapped out of it, backing away from the scene, having felt he just walked in on his parents doing the deed, yet somehow this was worse. These were his two horrifying professors, making out in the middle of the restaurant. 

“Nope, nope, nope,” he said to himself as he made his way back to Ireland. “I’ll buy you Chappel Roan tickets if you go hand them the bill.” 

Ireland blinked at him, shaking her head exasperated, “What? Why?”

“They’re sucking faces.” 

 

 

Rio

 

Rio was in trouble—deep, delicious, inescapable trouble. She had spent the entire evening teasing her wife, testing the limits of Agatha’s patience with lingering touches—her fingers brushing over warm thighs, the ghost of her arm grazing sensitive breasts, the deliberate drag of her foot along a shapely calf. A glance, a smirk, a whispered promise in Spanish against the shell of Agatha’s ear, each one a spark in a steadily growing fire. The restaurant was new, a place she knew Agatha had been eyeing, despite her wife’s stubborn refusal to admit she was a romantic. So, Rio had made the reservations, knowing full well where the night would end– in tangled sheets, in breathless moans, in the kind of intimacy that left her trembling. But she had pushed too far, poked and prodded the bear one too many times. 

The moment Agatha had yanked her into a searing kiss right in the middle of the restaurant, Rio had known exactly how the night would unfold. And the thought of pregnant Agatha taking control, pressing her down, leaving no room for escape? It had her mouth dry, her pulse frantic, her boxers soaked before they even reached the car. A wiser woman would have eased back at the final warning in Agatha’s eyes. But Rio– despite holding a PhD– was, at times, a fool. A fool who let her hand rest on her wife’s inner thigh during the drive home, tracing slow, deliberate circles, inching the fabric of Agatha’s dress higher and higher, daring fate itself. 

Now, she was paying the price. 

Her back slammed against the front door, the wood rattling, the glass panels threatening to crack under the force. She barely registered the sharp bite of pain, too lost in the sensation of Agatha’s mouth consuming hers, of a tongue curling deep, tasting her, claiming her. She moaned into the kiss, a sound swallowed whole as Agatha pressed closer, as skilled hands found their way under her blazer, cupping, kneading, setting every nerve ending alight. She was going to die tonight. And she prayed– desperately, reverently– that it would be with her wife straddling her face, thighs clenched tight, her nose rubbing into her wife’s clit as her wife rode her into oblivion. 

She hummed into her wife’s taste, fingers scratching into her scalp as another hand moved up cup her breast through her blazer. Her whole body was on fire as her mind turned to mush. Agatha pushed her harder against the door, pressing, taking the air between was thick– charged electric, humming with something primal and for a moment Rio was transported back to their honeymoon where they barely even explored Greece, staying in their luxury beachside condo, fucking hard with the sound of the ocean providing a contrast rhythm to their harmony. 

Rio barely had a second to breathe before her wife’s teeth found her jaw, then her throat, and finally that pulse point between her neck and shoulder that always made her moan. 

Her knees nearly buckled as she gasped, “ Cariño.”

The word was swallowed by a sharp nip to her collarbone, a warning wrapped in her wife’s fire. 

“Not so cocky now, are we?” Agatha’s voice was low, sultry, but laced with amusement and something so dark, Rio knew she was ruining her pants as well. “Thought you could keep teasing me all night without consequences?” 

Rio’s mouth opened– maybe to apologize, maybe to provoke her further– but Agatha was faster. Her hands raked down Rio’s sides before a hand cupped her core, pressing into her as she felt her thighs start to grow slick with her own wetness. 

“Fuck,” she breathed, her head thudding against the door. 

Agatha smirked, fingers slipping under the waistband of Rio’s slacks, dragging her nails just above the soaked fabric of her boxers. 

“That’s what you wanted, isn’t it?” her fingers curled, just barely grazing. “To push me until I snapped?” 

Rio’s breath hitched, her hands finding purchase on Agatha’s waist, holding on as if she was the only thing tethering her to this world. And maybe she was. 

“ Querida… ” she rasped, voice wrecked, desperate. “Please.” 

Agatha hummed, considering. And then, with agonizing patience, she pulled away. Rio nearly whined at the loss. 

“Bedroom,” Agatha commanded, stepping back, crossing her arms as she arched a brow. Her stance was nothing short of lethal. “Now.” 

For a second, Rio hesitated– not in defiance, but in sheer awe. Her wife’s cheeks were flushed, her stomach round with the life they created eighteen weeks ago– and fuck Rio felt herself clench around nothing, staring at her fuller breasts and her round stomach. She looked almost like Gaia– she looked like life– she looked like she belonged to her. Rio felt a smirk come to creep in, but she swallowed, knowing her wife would perceive it as a challenge and maybe it was. With one final once over, Rio bolted through the livingroom and down the hall to their room.

The bedroom door had barely swung shut before Rio dropped to her knees, helping her wife out of her shoes before anything started. She had already kicked off her own along with tossing her jacket somewhere. When she looked up, she saw a soft gaze from her wife, before she was motioned closer to bed, but Rio remained on her knees. She didn’t need to be told where to be tonight. She just knew. “Mi amor,” she nuzzled against the top of her head, “if you need to cry into my tits some more, I am here, but first I really do need you to breathe at some point.”

OR :

Yule, a surprise, and non spoiler, because we already know, Rio wins her bet.
