See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha

 

The living room buzzed with warmth, alive with music and laughter, bodies swaying and shifting in front of her like flickering candle flames. The scent of cinnamon, pine, and cider wrapped around her, rich and heady, nearly overwhelming. She wrinkled her nose, exhaling slowly in an attempt to settle her stomach. Her hand drifted over the curve of her belly in slow, reverent circles, as if to soothe the restless energy stirring beneath her skin. The sensation had been constant lately– a ceaseless fluttering, delicate yet insistent, like feathers dusting against the inside of her womb. She shifted slightly, trying to ease the odd swirling sensation, already suspecting that their child was bound to be restless. 

Of course , she thought wryly. Just like their Mami . 

A soft laugh pulled her from her thoughts, wrapping around her like the first twinkle of evening stars, as sweet as a mourning dove’s call, as gentle as water trickling over smooth stone. Before she could stop herself, she was already smiling, drawn instinctively toward the source. 

Rio. 

By the tree, her wife stood with easy grace, her dark curls catching the glow of the twinkling lights. Brown eyes, warm and bright, sparkled as her shoulders shook with laughter. She dipped her head, a rare flush creeping up her sharp cheekbones as Alice nudged her, teasing her about something Agatha couldn’t quite catch. Agatha’s heart swelled at the sight. As if sensing her gaze, Rio’s laughter softened, her eyes flickering toward Agatha as if she’d known all along she was watching. She watched as her wife drew back to her friend, Alice playfully hitting her before a presence loomed over, a mug of hot tea in hand being set slowly on the glass coffee table in front of her. 

Ximena sat down beside her, a soft smile on her face, “ ¿Te sientes bien, mi niña?” (You feeling okay, my girl?) 

Agatha nodded, her hand drawing more circles across her belly, “ Sí, simplemente se están moviendo mucho esta noche.” (Yes, they are just moving a lot tonight.) 

Abuelita beamed, a frail hand placed beside hers, “Está recogiendo la energía de Yule.” (He is picking up the energy of Yule.)

A loud cackle from her wife snapped both of their heads over to Rio who wore a dark glint in her eyes as Oriana playfully smacked her. Ximena sighed knowingly, standing and sauntering off somewhere in the house, but Agatha’s eyebrows raised, watching the scene that was slowly folding out. 

Lilia had sidled up beside Oriana, a smirk playing at her lips,“Well, at least you didn’t live with one of them when they were together in college. I have become immune to the sounds they make.” 

Oriana groaned dramatically, burying her face in her hands. 

Oh , Agatha nearly laughed. So that’s what this was about.  

They were teasing Oriana over what she and Abuelita had overheard the morning before they were forced to all go shopping. Predictably, Rio did not let up. 

“How else do you think my wife got pregnant, Ori?” Rio drawled, her voice thick with mockery, stretching her sister’s name like a cat playing with its prey. 

Oriana shot her a glare, her hands still partially covering her face, “You do not have a dick, Rio!” 

Agatha saw it before it even happened– the shift in Rio’s expression, the slow curl of her smirk into something dangerous. That was the look of a woman about to make everything worse for the sheer pleasure of it. 

“Yeah?” Rio tilted her head, eyes gleaming. “And how would you know?”

Oriana’s glare sharpened, an unspoken death threat passing between them as their friends erupted in laughter. Rio, ever the menace, flicked her hair back and pushed her tongue against her cheek, feigning nonchalance. 

“If you must know, my dick is very real when it needs to be,” Rio mused, the corner of her mouth curving upward. “And trust me, it’s long enough that my wife isn’t complaining.”

Laughter erupted, loud and raucous, as Oriana let out a strangled groan, slapping both hands over her face as if she could block out the sheer audacity radiating from her little sister. Lilia wheezed, clapping a hand on Oriana’s shoulder for support, while Alice nearly doubled over, struggling to catch her breath. Agatha exhaled a slow breath, shaking her head fondly as she smoothed a hand over her belly. Their child was particularly active tonight, rolling and fluttering beneath her palm like they, too, were amused by their mami’s antics. 

But then came the sharp thwack of wood against skull. 

Rio’s head snapped forward with a hiss, her hand flying to the back of it. 

“ Mierda ! What the hell?” she grumbled, turning with a dramatic wince. 

Ximena stood there, unimpressed, wooden spoon in hand like a weapon of divine justice. She didn’t need to say anything, the glare was enough to have Rio flinch, still rubbing the back of her head, but Agatha couldn’t help herself. She snorted, laughing loudly with the others, her hand on her belly as the swirling became more prominent with each intake of breath. 

Her wife’s gaze snapped to her, a wild look of betrayement before her lip curled, “Of course you think this is funny.” 

Agatha couldn’t respond, sitting up more, her laughter growing addictive as her wife crossed the room in a few strides. Rio crouched before her, her own hand resting on her stomach, easing the flutters in her stomach as her laughter finally calmed down. She hummed, scratching the back of her wife’s head in mock sympathy, but her wife didn’t swat her away. Instead, her hand rubbed in smooth circles before moving under Agatha’s shirt. Rio’s touch was cold compared to her hot body, fingers splayed on her skin finally the flutters started to ease as if sensing their mami ’s presence. 

“Your child is restless tonight, my love,” she said softly. 

Rio smiled softly, her thumb caressing the swell gently, “He is just excited, mi amor. ” 

Agatha rolled her eyes fondly, but jumped when a loud knock banged on their door. Her wife remained for a moment, soothing the sudden flutters that seemed to have jumped to life with her. Her eyebrows creased though, not understanding who could be here. Everyone was here that mattered, except for Hela and a few of Agatha’s own close friends from college. Pepper, Tony, and Jennifer were in the kitchen as far as she knew and everyone else was right in front of her. She swallowed hard as Rio stood, placing a quick kiss on her lips before heading towards the door. 

The only person who wasn’t here was… Maria. 

Before Agatha could stop her wife, a loud familiar laugh rang through the house. She jumped, her heart leaping at the familiar sound before voices came from the hall. 

“You missed dinner, but we have left overs set aside,” her wife spoke as Agatha stood, albeit, slower than she would have liked. 

A cherry-like lilt responded, echoing down the foyer in the usual dramatic flair, “Not to worry, Rio. Sorry, Jarvis couldn’t make it, where is your wife?” 

Just as the words left his mouth, Agatha stepped into the hall and was greeted by the sight of her wife who was reluctantly embraced by a man with hair as dark as hers and a slightly crooked nose. 

“Kingo?” she gasped, making the man finally release her wife, who brushed off the hug like it personally wounded her. 

“Aggie!” he beamed, his voice a little too loud as she cringed at the old nickname. 

She barely had time to react before Kingo enveloped her a warm hug, but for once the hug didn’t break her spine. “You are glowing.” 

She smiled softly, her wife giving her a knowing smile. Gods, she hasn’t seen Kingo in over five years, minus his Hollywood appearances and occasional texts. They were tight friends in college when she met him her junior year, not to mention, their wedding officiant.

“What are you doing here?” she couldn’t keep the shock out of her voice as Kingo let go, allowing her wife to slip past and stand beside her. “I thought you were imprisoned in Bollywood with the occasional transfer to Hollywood.”

She didn’t bother to hide the smile as her wife wrapped her arm around her waist, a hand already on her stomach .

Kingo tilted his head, this time wearing a rare soft expression, “Well, I got a text from the missus that you two were expecting and, an invitation to Yule. But, I wanted to see for myself if it was true that the great Agatha Harkness got knocked up. Let me tell you, motherhood looks good on you.” 

Hiding her blush, she punched him hard in the shoulder, earning a scoff from him and slight snort from her wife. Her wife ushered them into the kitchen, getting Kingo a drink before Kingo and Tony were talking away. Agatha smiled softly, her hand still resting on her stomach. 

Soft lips pressed against her cheek, getting her to greet tender brown eyes. Agatha reached up, cupping her wife’s cheeks as hands came to her wrists like a thousand times before. She lost herself in Rio’s eyes, seeing nothing but love and adoration. Her thumb moved up on her cheek, returning the same smile. 

Before either could close the distance– 

“Hey lovebirds! Get your asses in here!” 

Agatha grumbled, rolling her eyes, her hands falling from her wife’s face, but Rio didn’t let go. No, she yelped when when she was pulled closer, lips pressed against hers. She let herself fall into it, shrouding her in warmth as cold hands landed on her stomach again. Rio smiled into the kiss, pulling away for less than a second before lips were placed back on hers. Her own hand came back up, cupping her wife’s cheek and pulling her closer into the kiss before someone cleared their throat. 

They pulled away, a little reluctantly, facing off the great matriarch who held out both of her hands. The pair each took one, being led back into their living room where two chairs from their dining room were placed in the middle of the room in front of the TV and deliberately centered for those sitting on the couch and chairs to stare at. Both of them tensed at once, sharing a confused look with each other as their eyes spoke. 

What the fuck is this? Agatha raised an eyebrow at Rio. 

Her wife shook shoulders, her eyebrows creasing as if to say, How would I know?

Ximena sat them in the chairs before taking her seat on the couch between Oriana and Lilia. Agatha eyed everyone like a hawk, noting each of their smug expressions but also noticing that Pepper, Tony, and Kingo were missing. Her wife seemed to catch on faster, sitting up rigidly as her voice lowered. 

“What the fuck is this?” she echoed her wife’s question.

Unlike Agatha, who thrived on attention, Rio hated it– which was odd due to their professions, which Rio claimed was different minus the dread of faculty meetings and she guessed in recent light, the rumors… the rumors that would turn to gossip the moment they stepped back on campus. Agatha tried not thinking about it, but she internally cringed when she remembered their dinner two nights ago, realizing the waiter was one of their students. Hopefully, Wybie– or whatever the fuck was his name– was a gossiper and was already talking, that way the fire wouldn’t roar as hot when they made it back to campus. 

“ Relájate, Río, ” Abuelita snapped, her voice sharp as she motioned for Rio to sit back down. 

Rio obeyed, though her posture remained stiff, her eyes flashing with annoyance. 

“Careful, babe. One more step,” came Pepper’s voice from the basement stairs. 

Agatha barely held back a groan, exchanging an exasperated glance with her wife. Both of them were equally irritated, especially with the sounds of Pepper and Tony dragging something heavy from the basement. Agatha’s stomach twisted in anticipation as she heard them struggling toward the living room. As if on cue, Pepper and Tony appeared, lugging a long, foldable table stacked high with mountains of brightly wrapped presents. They set the table down beside Rio before bolting out of the room with barely a word, leaving neither Rio nor Agatha a chance to question them. Rio straightened up, opening her mouth to speak, but Abuelita silenced her with a single, authoritative glance. Rio huffed, frustrated, her eyes narrowing at the table. Agatha’s gaze followed her wife’s to the pile of presents, and they exchanged another silent conversation. 

Please tell me this isn’t what I think this is, Rio’s eyes begged, her irritation clear. 

Agatha frowned, her head tilting toward the table, Unfortunately, it might be. 

I’m going to kill them.  

Before Agatha could speak again, Alice let out a dramatic sigh, crossing her arms with a raised brow, “Ugh, they’re doing that thing again.” 

“What thing?” Oriana asked, looking up in confusion. 

Lilia’s voice came next, dripping with mild disdain, “The thing where they have full conversations with just their eyes.”  

Agatha sat up this time, ready to snap at them, but voices carried from their foyer again, accompanied by the sound of something rolling across hardwood floors until it fell silent on the carpet. Agatha felt her mouth hang open as she stared as Kingo pushed in a large box on a metal dolly. Tony and Pepper came in behind him as she scowled. 

“If you get dirt and snow on my carpet, Kingo–” 

“Relax, sweetness, don’t let your hormones get you all agitated now,” he smirked at her. 

Agatha’s fists tightened, as her jaw clenched. Right, that’s why she hadn’t seen him in five years because he somehow got more under her skin than her wife ever could, and not in a good way. She went to stand, but a hand came down on her knee, holding her in place as Tony and Kingo pushed the box off the dolly, before wheeling it out of sight. Agatha turned back to the cardboard box. It was huge, easily taking up the space if there was another seat of the couch. 

She swallowed hard, keeping in mind of Rio’s Abuelita sitting in front of them, “Okay, what is this? We agreed on no over the top gifts for Yule this year.” 

Every. Single. One. Of. Them wore a shit-eating grin. 

“Yes, but this isn’t for Yule,” Lilia said simply, bringing her wine glass to her lips. 

Oriana smirked, “Yes, plus we needed Abuelita here for this.”

“And you two refused to plan one,” Jen added. 

Agatha and her wife raised an eyebrow simultaneously. 

“Welcome to your baby shower, Mrs. and Mrs. Harkness-Vidal,” Alice now wore the biggest smirk out of all of them. 

 

***

 

Agatha found a gift in her hands immediately, Pepper and Kingo looking far too pleased with themselves as Agatha saw the small scripture of Lilia’s handwriting in the corner of the gift. She tore through the green packaging with ease. Inside she found a stack of burp cloths, each one soft to the touch and embroidered with delicate flowers– some a deep purple like her wife’s ring, others a rich green that mirrored her own emerald on her finger. She traced a fingertip over the delicate stitching, admiring the craftsmanship. 

“Did you make these?” she asked, looking up at Lilia. 

Lilia smirked over the rim of her wine glass, “Of course. Only the best so all that spit up doesn’t mess up your hair.” 

She snorted softly as Pepper set them aside, handing her another present as her wife was tearing through paper carelessly before pulling out neatly folded swaddles in dark, earthy hues. Her fingers skimmed over the soft, breathable fabrique. She smiled softly, looking up and getting out an awkward thank you. She held back her own snort of laughter, Rio was always awkward when receiving presents, even if she loved them, she would stare at you sometimes like a deer in the headlights– unless you were her wife. Rio would express her thanks with a sweet kiss that would always steal her breath away. 

“They’re lightweight,” Oriana supplied. “ Abuelita and I figured you wouldn’t want anything scratchy against his skin as well since you were always sensitive as a baby.” 

Rio nodded in approval, lips pursing like she was trying to hide how pleased she was. Next came a set of tiny onesies in varying shades of green and purple, some with simple patterns, others with tiny messages stitched onto the fabrique. Agatha held one up, reading the words aloud. 

“‘Newest member of the coven’?” she shot a look at Alice, slightly emotional, but she fought it down successfully for the first time in eighteen weeks.

Alice grinned, “Fitting, isn’t it? This baby is going to be spoiled rotten.” 

Rio snorted but didn’t protest when Agatha set the onesie aside with the rest of their growing pile. 

Pepper handed them the next box, her eyes twinkling with mischief. Agatha opened it first and immediately stilled, staring at the contents. A box of breast pads. Alice burst out laughing at her expression. 

“What? You’ll need them.”

 Jen elbowed her, “Maybe don’t say it like that.” 

Rio raised an eyebrow, leaning over to look inside and seeing them before smirking with the rest of the group, “Right, can’t have you leaking during lectures, mi amor .” 

Agatha didn’t hesitate, slapping her arm as Pepper shoved a box in Rio’s hands. 

 

They moved on, both of them being shoved gift after gift. A compact baby carrier came next, small and sleek but sturdy, designed to hold their child snug against their chest. Agatha turned to her wife with a smirk of her own, already picturing it. 

“You’re going to live in this, aren’t you?” 

Rio rolled her eyes but didn’t deny it. 

The next package revealed a set of teething rings, each one uniquely shaped– some smooth, some textured, all designed to soothe tiny, aching gums. 

Pepper grinned, “For when they start biting everything in sight.” 

Another box was filled with diapers– packs upon packs of them, stacked so high that Agatha swore they could construct a small fortress right there in the living room. 

“To the Mother,” Rio muttered, eyeing the sheer amount. 

Oriana shrugged, “You’ll thank us later.” 

It continued, each adding their own commentary for what would be good for the baby. They received diaper cream, pacifiers, a few stuffed animals, baby dinner sets, bottles and storage bottles, bibs, a bassinet, a baby monitor, baby bath soaps, a baby photo album, and even a hooded towel. 

Their friends really thought of everything. 

“A few things are from Thena and Hela too,” Alice smiled, both of them watching Rio being memorized by the plush soft green hooded towel. 

 

Finally, they reached the big box. The wives exchanged a glance before pulling apart the cardboard flaps, staring inside to see more things wrapped up, but they both caught the undeniable color of royal purple.

Kingo smirked, “Best one on the market, ladies– a purple cushion rocking chair with a foot rest. You’ll have to build it, but Rio’s butch enough to figure it out.” 

At that, Rio shot him a glare, but Agatha found herself laughing. Her wife did have an affinity for building things and did present a more masculine most days, but much like Agatha, they easily switched between femme and masc. As handsome as Rio was in a suit, Rio was stunning in a dress. 

“ Ah, será perfecto para las tomas nocturnas, Agatha,” Abuelita cut in, ignoring Kingo. (Ah, it will be perfect for late night feedings, Agatha.)

Her sister-in-law stood, approaching Rio and nudging her, “See, I told you a baby shower would be worth it.” 

Her wife rolled her eyes, “Yes, but it didn’t need to be this fucking dramatic.” 



 

Agatha nuzzled closer into her wife’s chest, seeking her soul comfort in the morning. Her wife hummed, her arms wrapped around her, one on the back of her head, a silent reassurance that she was alright to do this. Her other hand was rubbing soothing circles on her back, which had recently started to ache. She noticed it during the Yule party and her wife was there without a word. To be fair, she was five months pregnant, with not an overly large bump, still hideable with oversized sweaters, but all the effects were slowly catching up to her. Between swollen hands and feet, leg cramps, sore breasts, hot flashes, pain on the sides of her stomach, and now back pain– as much as she loved growing their baby, she was starting to get irritated. Yet her wife was always there, one step ahead, between massages, a cold rag to wipe her sweat away, a hand on her stomach to soothe the round ligament pains, the hot baths and finally, to her cravings. Her wife was there through it all without a single breath of complaint. 

Agatha didn’t move when their door creaked open, knowing exactly who it was as she made it a habit for this week. 

“¿A qué hora es la cita, mis niñas?” Abuelita’s voice was soft, delicate as Agatha figured she seemed to be measuring whether or not she was asleep. (What time is the appointment?)

Through the week, starting the twenty-fifth, Abuelita had been staying with them before she went back home on the twenty-ninth. Originally, she was supposed to come the twenty first, but Rio somehow managed to convince Abuelita to come a little later. She didn’t know how, but was thankful she did, especially after date night. 

“No hasta las cuatro de la tarde,” her wife responded, her movements continuous on Agatha’s back. (Not until four in the afternoon) 

There was a beat of silence before Abuelita responded. “ Deberíamos hacer algo entonces. Podemos comprar pintura o limpiar tu oficina donde estará la habitación del bebé. O elegir una cuna. Eso aún no lo has hecho.” (We should do something then. We can buy some paint or clean up your office where the baby’s room will be. Or pick out a crib. You haven’t done that yet.)  

Agatha stifled her groan. She was too tired, too irritated, and her back was really trying to kill her. As if right on que, the flutters started happening, feather tips pressing against the side where she rested her belly on her wife. It was continuous, each movement seemed to press closer and closer as if their child could really sense that their mami was right there. It was endearing and in the moment, so overwhelming. 

“ Ya veremos, abuelita. Estuvo mucho de pie estos últimos días,” her wife responded unaware of the great war Agatha was losing. 

Agatha squeezed her eyes shut, willing herself to breathe through it, to keep the wave of emotions from cresting too high. It didn’t make sense, there was no moon to be pulling her emotional tides, and yet, she couldn’t stop it. The tiny movements against her belly weren’t stopping, each delicate fluttering, a reminder that their child was right there– alive, growing, feeling– reaching out… It was too much between the exhaustion, the aches– the overwhelming reality that she and Rio were really about to become mothers– that she defied what the Harkness clan thought of her and created this life for herself. She had a very, very loving wife– overly protective, perfectly in tune with her, reading her without words, seeing past her walls without judgment, or sympathy, only perfect understanding. Gods, Rio loved her… really loved her and somehow already, their baby knew it too as the flutters continued against the side Rio was on. 

With that, her throat tightened, her breath shuddering as she tried to swallow it down… but when Rio’s hand slid down to cradle her stomach, her thumb tracing protective runes over the stretched skin, everything exploded. A sharp, gasping sob tore from Agatha’s lips before she could even stop it. 

Her wife immediately tensed beneath her, “Agatha?” 

The concern in Rio’s voice was instant, cutting through the sleepy warmth of the morning. Yet, she didn’t have words. All she could do was to clutch Rio’s shirt, trembling against her as the sobs spilled out in waves. Hot tears stung her cheeks, smearing into Rio’s chest. 

“Mi amor, ” her wife whispered, her fingers threading through Agatha’s hair, her hand remaining firmly on her belly. “Talk to me.” 

Agatha shook her head against her chest, hiccuping through her sobs. 

“They– they won’t st-stop m-m-moving,” she choked out, gripping her tighter as the sobs reigned on. “They-they just k-keep pressing closer, like-like they know you’re here.” 

Rio let out a soft, breathy chuckle, though there was nothing but tenderness in it as her thumb continued to trace runes. 

“They do know,” she murmured, pressing a kiss to Agatha’s head. “Of course they do. They know their mamá , and they know their mami . They love you so much already.” 

That only made Agatha cry harder. 

“I–It’s just–” she sniffled, trying to catch her breath, but another flutter had her gasping, overwhelmed all over again. “It’s so much, Rio.” 

“I know, mi vida, ” Rio soothed, rocking her slightly. “It’s a lot. But you’re not alone in this, okay? I’m right here.” 

Agatha clung to her, still trembling, still crying, but the steady rhythm of Rio’s hands on her back, the warmth of her body, the quiet, unshakable love in her voice– it grounded her. Her wife pulled her closer as she sobbed into chest, the wet cotton now rubbing uncomfortably against her face. A flash of anger washed over her as she continued to cry. She pulled away abruptly, pulling at Rio’s tank and ripping it off her body with a loud tear before her wife could stop her.  Rio stilled for a moment before pulling her closer, holding her as Agatha’s sobs tore through the room. 

“Did you just–” her wife sucked in a slow, measured breath, as if trying to process the absurdity of what had just happened. “...Did you just rip my shirt off?” 

Agatha ignored her… well she couldn’t really answer as the sobs didn’t let up. She buried her face into the familiar warmth of her wife’s skin but nothing stopped it, nothing could stop it as she hiccuped, sucking in a large breath to regain air into her lungs, only to release in a loud, muffled wail. 

 

 

Rio

 

Rio was internally panicking. This had been going on for almost an hour now and she had no idea how to stop it or why it was happening. Agatha’s breath was hot against her chest, which was now thoroughly soaked with her snot, drool, and tears. She had obviously seen her wife cry before, between her being overwhelmed with work, or being exhausted because Agatha didn’t like to sleep (before she was pregnant), and frustrated– but this? 

This was different. 

This wasn’t just quite the frustration of pregnancy aches or the occasional teary-eyed moment over a book– she cried over The Tempest the other day… the fucking Tempest by William Shakespeare. Something about Caliban even though Rio was sure her wife didn’t like him. 

No, this was full-on, body shaking, breath-stealing– borderline tantrum sobbing. Agatha was gripping onto her as if Rio would suddenly disappear. And Rio had no idea what to do. She kept rubbing her wife’s back, kissing her head, drawing calming runes on her stomach, moving her hand through her hair, but fuck– nothing was working. She sucked in her breath when she felt her wife’s blunt nails digging into her breast, willing her to stay, willing her to be her anchor. She silently thanks the gods that her Abuelita gave them space because Agatha’s face was buried between her bare breasts– which you know what, fair. Rio couldn’t blame anyone for that, a great place to cry and when Rio did cry, she needed to listen to her wife’s heartbeat. But, again, this was going on for almost an hour. 

“My love,” she tried slowly, pulling her body away so she could see her wife’s face. 

To her absolute horror and shock, her wife sobbed even louder, grasping at Rio’s ribs to stay, “NO!” 

Rio blinked, pulling her back into her chest, smoothing a hand down her spine as Agatha practically clawed at her, her sobs hiccuping into frantic little gasps. 

“Okay, okay,” Rio soothed, pressing a firm kiss to her wife’s temple. “I’m not going anywhere, I promise.” 

Another loud sob echoed around her, rattling her to her bones. Nails bit at her skin as Agatha pressed impossibly closer, as if she could mold herself to the safety of Rio’s skin and never leave. Her mind raced, grasping for anything to ease her wife’s sobs. Okay, so maybe this was just hormones. There was no real reason for her to be crying like this. Rio took a deep breath, kissing the top of tangled hair. 

“ Mi vida, ” Rio murmured, her voice low and steady, grounding. “You’re safe. I have you. Just breathe, my love, breathe with me.” 

Rio took a slow exaggerated inhale, guiding Agatha with the rise and fall of her own chest. But her wife just whimpered, trembling harder, her body releasing a fresh wave of sobs that crashed into her. 

“ Mierda.”

Rio shifted, earning more cries of protests, but Agatha would drown herself against her tear soaked skin. She lifted her wife slightly as she sat up. With ease she guided her wife into her lap, cradling her like she was something fragile. Agatha’s face was buried into her shoulder before she could see it, a hand placed over her beating heart and a mess of mucus, tears, and spit, but her wife barely acknowledged it. Rio fingers moved through the baby hairs at the nape of her neck, her fingers lightly scratching her scalp. 

“You’re okay, my love. I’m here. You don’t have to explain. Just let it out.”  

Her wife let out a shaky breath, gripping a little tighter. 

“It– it won’t st-stop,” she gasped, her voice so raw that Rio was starting to get concerned  how much of this crying was affecting her body. “I–I can’t stop and it’s– fuck, it’s so stupid!” 

“It’s not stupid, mi amor. ” 

“It is!” her wife sobbed. “I–I don’t even-even know why anymore- just I was thinking– and then– and then– and then I was crying– then I was crying because I was crying–” she was gasping for her words, each breath bigger as her words rambled on. Rio, for a moment, was surprised she could understand. “And–and–and I–I ripped your shirt because it felt wrong– and I don’t even know why!” 

At this, she couldn’t help it, releasing a breathless laugh and pressing a kiss to the bit of damp cheek that was peeking out. And then, because of course Rio should have realized that her reaction wouldn’t be received well, her wife cried more. 

She swore silently, peppering kisses to the skin she could as her wife rubbed her nose across her shoulder as if she were a tissue– at this point she definitely felt like one giant human tissue. Rio reached down, cupping her wife’s cheek, trying to get her to pull away from her skin, but Agatha wouldn’t budge– which made sense, she hated allowing anyone seeing her cry– but her wife still needed to come up for real air. 

It was time to switch tactics. 

“ Mi amor,” she nuzzled against the top of her head, “if you need to cry into my tits some more, I am here, but first I really do need you to breathe at some point.” 

Her wife let out a choked noise, somewhere between that annoyed scoff she does when Rio purposely says something ridiculously stupid, a broken laugh, and a small sob. And then, to Rio’s definitely carefully crafted plan, and the one she definitely didn’t just come up with on a whim– Agatha lifted her head, her eyes red, wet and furious. It would have been that look, the one that could freeze Rio in place, but somehow the lines of tears, the little bit snot from her nose, and how red her cheeks were– it didn’t work. 

“That’s not fucking funny,” her wife growled at her. 

Rio only hummed thoughtfully, taking her ripped shirt, lying helpless next to her, and wiped the snot and tears from her face with it, “It was a little funny.”

Finally, her wife took a deep breath, but the tears didn’t cease, “I hate this.” 

“I know,” she said carefully, throwing the shirt aside as her wife sniffed. “But, my love, you are growing our child inside you. Your body is making a whole new person and keeping you alive on top of that. If you need to cry about it, then cry. I’ll always be here for you, just, if you really do need to cry into my tits, please just ask and don’t tip my shirt.” 

It earned her a watery laugh, her wife shaking her head, “I hate you.” 

“Impossible,” Rio replied, kissing her but Agatha pulled away, wrinkling her nose. 

“I have snot running down my lips–” 

Rio didn’t let her finish, pressing her lips more firmly to her wife’s not caring for the fact that they were a tad sticky. It didn’t matter, her tongue had been inside Agatha more times than could count. Her wife melted a hand reaching up and cupping her own cheek before she felt more tears run into her cheeks. She wiped them away with her thumbs before pulling away, resting her forehead against her wife’s.

“Do you want a hot bath, my love?” 

She shook her head. 

“How about some hot sauce and ice cream?”

She shook her head again.
Rio kissed her lips, “We can cuddle some more before getting lunch?”

Her wife nodded at that, her eyes closed as her breathing was deep, easing the tears back into her eyes. After a moment, Rio guided them back into bed, pulling the covers up and as her wife buried her face between her tits again, making her chuckle. 

“After the ultrasound, my love, we should introduce Abuelita to Nando’s and also to nurse your ego when you lose the bet.” 

And that earned her a playful, sharp bite to the side of her breast. Rio let out a squeal, instinctively shifting her body to escape, but Agatha withdrew her teeth, leaving a trail of lingering warmth. Rio grumbled, but she couldn’t ignore the knowing smirk she could practically feel radiating from her wife.



***

 

“¿Estás seguro de que puedes confiar en este médico?” Abuelita asked coldly. (Are you sure you can trust this doctor?)

Rio held back a sigh, her eyes drifting to the rearview mirror for a moment. Dark assessing eyes were on her wife though in the passenger seat, watching as her wife shifted uncomfortably, a hand rubbing the side of her stomach as she pushed against the seatbelt that was digging into her. She focused her gaze back on the road, merging onto I-80, driving into the main city. Her hand reached over the center dial, rubbing her wife’s stomach before resting it there. 

“Sí, la Dra. Walsh es una excelente doctora. Investigamos y nos la recomendó la clínica que realizó la transferencia de médula ósea,” she said calmly, but her grip tightened on the wheel. (Yes, Dr. Walsh is an excellent doctor. We did our research, and the clinic that performed the bone marrow transfer recommended her to us.) 

Abuelita hummed her acknowledgement before shifting forward in her seat, “¿Y puedo entrar?” (And I can come in?)

“ No veo por qué no, abuelita.” (I don’t see why not.)

She felt a squeeze on her hand, a careful reminder to keep her cool before Agatha’s grip tightened in a way that wasn’t for comfort. Rio looked over briefly, worry filling her features as Agatha was staring at her stomach like it personally offended her. 

“Your child won’t stop moving,” she said simply. 

Rio held back her smile, not wanting a repeat of this morning, “He is just excited to tell his mama that she is having a son.” 

Her wife snarled, “If you weren’t driving, I swear I would end you faster than light can travel.” 

Rio smirked, “Don’t threaten me with a good time, mi amor .” 

Abuelita’s voice cut through the playful tension, rich with a knowing warmth. 

“Oh, ustedes dos... Aún así, incluso con un bebé en camino, no son mejores que dos adolescentes enamorados,” she muttered, her tone a blend of affection and mild annoyment. (Ay, you two… even with a baby on the way, you two are no better than two teenagers in love.)

Rio felt herself soften, a smile on her face as she met her abuelita’s gaze in the mirror for a fleeting moment, but it was her wife who answered. 

“ Y no querías que fuéramos de otra manera.” (And you wouldn’t want us any other way.) 

Ximena didn’t answer, but Rio caught the barest hint of a smile before she turned her gaze to the passing city.

 

 

By the time they reached the clinic, Rio had barely pulled into the parking space before Abuelita was unbuckling herself, ready to march inside like she owned the place. Agatha sighed, leaning back with a hand on her stomach, staring daggers at the door. Rio leaned over, kissing her cheek and receiving a small grumble. She simply got out, rounding the car swiftly to open her wife’s door. She offered her hand, her wife taking it with a slight grunt as she stood. Rio was quick to interlock their fingers, guiding her wife inside with Abuelita on their trail. 

Inside, they were met with the familiar bright fluorescent lights and the deep smell of antiseptic. She caught the way Agatha wrinkled her nose, taking deep breaths to steady the nausea as they made their way to check in. As she spoke, she placed her palm over her wife’s belly, rubbing slow, familiar circles. The waiting room was half-full, quiet murmurs of other expecting parents filling the space. As they turned to find their seats, she had her wife and Abuelita sit as she crouched down by her wife, filling out the form for her, allowing her to point to some of the information as nurses filtered in and out grabbing couples. 

The nurse called them in after a short wait, and Rio placed a guiding hand on Agatha’s lower back as they followed. The halls were the same, quiet except for a distant baby’s cry to which her wife slightly stiffened. Rio swore in her head, wondering if she would start crying again. She pushed her along a little faster, her hand moving up and down on her back until the nurse guided them into a room. Abuelita immediately took a seat in the corner as Rio quietly helped her wife out of her coat, sitting on the chair beside Abuelita before taking her hand as she sat on the exam table. 

Her thumb rubbed in circles on the top of her hand, “Ready to lose, my love?” 

Agatha shot her a glare, rolling her eyes as Abuelita chuckled behind her. 

Before any of them could speak, Dr. Walsh entered with the same warmth and professionalism, her smile widening, zeroing in on her wife’s stomach, “Agatha, Rio, it’s good to see you again” she smiled before turning to Ximena. “Hello, I’m Dr. Walsh, you must be the proud grandmother.”

“Great-grandmother actually,” Rio smiled softly.

“Ximena,” Abuelita replied, shaking her hand. 

Dr. Walsh beamed at this as she made her way to the computer, the clacking of keys filling the room, “Ah, so this little one is extra special. Well, let’s begin. Mama, how are you feeling?” 

“Tired, my back hurts, and the kid moves a lot,” her wife replied, refusing to release her hand. 

Dr. Walsh chuckled as she rolled her stool closer to the exam table, “That all sounds perfectly normal, though I know that doesn’t make it any easier. But you’re halfway there– eighteen weeks, almost nineteen.” 

Rio squeezed Agatha’s hand, pressing a kiss to her knuckles,“You hear that, mi amor? Halfway there.” 

Agatha groaned, “That’s still twenty more weeks of carrying your little troublemaker.” 

“Our little troublemaker,” Rio corrected, grinning. “And he is already proving he takes after me.” 

Agatha muttered something that sounded distinctly like a curse, but Rio just leaned in closer, her smirk widening. 

Dr. Walsh shook her head with an amused smile, “Alright, let’s check on baby, shall we?” 

She rolled her stool toward the ultrasound machine. The machine upped to life, the room dimming as Abuelita stood, coming beside Rio as they watched the screen flicker on. It was the same routine– gloves snapping before something sterile was placed over the wand and the doctor grabbing a bottle of gel. 

“Go ahead and lift your shirt and we will get started. Today, we will spend a little bit longer looking at baby as this is the anatomy scan. I’ll be measuring baby’s growth, heart and lungs, and we will see if baby cooperates for a gender reveal. You would like to know the gender, correct?” she looked up at the three of them. 

“Yes,” Rio and her abuelita said at once. 

Dr. Walsh smirked, catching the dramatic eye roll Agatha gave as she begrudgingly lifted her shirt. The cool gel met her skin, and she flinched, a soft curse slipping past her lips before the transducer pressed down, smearing the gel in slow, practiced movements. And then, just like that, the image slowly formed. Rio had seen it before– twice, in fact– but the moment the screen crackled, filling with that familiar static grey and white, it still hit her like the very first time. Her breath caught in her throat as the grainy silhouette sharpened, shaping the unmistakable form of their baby. A tiny foot kicked out. Then another. The baby stretched, arms reaching, legs following, moving with a restlessness that Rio already knew all too well from Agatha’s complaints. And then– a small hand pushed against Agatha’s stomach, as if knocking, as if announcing itself to the world.

Dr. Walsh let out a delighted laugh, “Well, someone is definitely making their presence known. I’m guessing you feel that a lot, huh, Mama?” 

Agatha scoffed, her free hand pressing to her belly, “Every damn minute.” 

Rio didn’t even try to hold back her grin, her whole body humming with excitement. It was absurd, the way the doctor gushed over something she probably saw dozens of times a day, but how could one not? How could anyone not be swept away by the sheer miracle of it? Her heart was soaring, overflowing with so much love that she wanted nothing more than to pull Agatha close, to kiss her senseless, to whisper ‘thank you’ a thousand times over. Instead, she clung to her wife’s hand, squeezing so tightly she was surprised she hadn’t cut off circulation. Beside her, Abuelita stood in stunned silence, her gaze locked onto the screen. Silent tears brimmed in her eyes, slipping down her lined cheeks as she took in the sight of her first great-grandchild. 

Rio swallowed hard, pressing a lingering kiss to Agatha’s knuckles. Dr. Walsh hummed as she adjusted the wand, shifting the angle, fingers flying over the keyboard to type down notes. But Rio barely registered it. Because all she could see– all she could feel– was the undeniable, breathtaking reality of the tiny life growing inside her wife.

“Alright,” Dr. Walsh smiled, looking up at them finally. “Everything looks good. The heart and lungs are strong, no deformities, good size– they’re a little on the bigger end but nothing concerning. And, baby is cooperating today. Shall we, ladies?” 

They all nodded, Rio almost bouncing as she gave her wife’s hand a gentle squeeze. Dr. Walsh moved with a steady precision, angling the image around their baby’s legs. 

“Well–” she said, her voice warm with a gentle certainty. “Congratulations. You’re having a son.” 

For a moment, silence filled the room. The kind of silence that only existed in the space between a breath and something inevitable. Then– Rio threw her head back and laughed. A full-bodied, triumphant sound, rich and golden, echoing against the sterile walls of the exam room. Oh, she knew she was right, everything in her telling her that they were destined to have a son, and now hearing it allowed, she couldn’t help but laugh. Particularly, she didn’t care– she did– that she won the bet, all she knew was that something told her and that weird gut feeling was right. She grinned so wide it hurt, her eyes gleaming with the light of a thousand victories as she turned to her wife, pure delight written across her face. 

“I told you,” she all but purred, squeezing Agatha’s hand with all the smugness in the world. “You lost, mi amor. ” 

Agatha, still staring at the screen as if she could will the baby to prove the doctor wrong, exhaled sharply through her nose. Her other hand came up, covering her face for a moment before dragging down, revealing the pure exasperation in her expression. 

“Of course, you’re gloating,” she muttered, but there was no real heat behind it.

 Rio leaned in, pressing a teasing kiss against her temple, “ Mi amor , I earned this.” 

Their doctor chuckled as she turned off the machine, handing Rio a towel, “So mother won the bet, huh?” 

Her wife groaned, but there was a soft smile aimed at her as she cleaned off her stomach, “She’s never going to let me live it down.” 

Rio sighed, all faux sympathy as she squeezed her wife’s fingers while handing the towel back, “That’s where you’re wrong, mi vida. I will let you live it down, eventually. Maybe after his third birthday or something.” 

Her wife sat up with Rio’s help, ready to retaliate but they both froze as a soft sniffle seemed to echo through the room. Rio turned, her eyes wide as her heart clenched at the sight of Abuelita. Like before, it was a foreign sight seeing the strongest woman she knew with glistening dark eyes, a hand over her heart as she stared at the screen that once held the image of her great-grandson, nestled safely within Agatha. 

“Oh mis niñas, gracias por hacer crecer esta familia,” she whispered, her eyes never leaving the blank screen. 

“The pictures will be at check out. I asked for extra so you two can share with your family. I don’t see the need to order any more ultrasounds. You and son are doing just fine, mama. Although, don’t hesitate to call if you have any concerns. Week twenty-two, you can go ahead and start talking to him. It will help with bonding for both of you. You may notice some flutters in response. The earliest mom can feel him will be week twenty-four. It’s rare if you can feel him at twenty, but possible. As you approach the third trimester, give yourself some grace. You will be slower, a little clumsy, but that’s all moms. If I don’t see you sooner, I will see you for your son’s first appointment.” 

Her wife gave a small nod, fighting back a smile that was pushing to be set free. Dr. Walsh only smiled knowingly, giving them all a final handshake. 

“It was wonderful to work with you both. Take your time, and don’t forget to grab the scans on the way out. Congratulations again, moms.” 

The moment she left, the room grew still, Rio staring down at her wife with nothing but unfiltered joy as her wife wiped a few tears that slipped. She moved before she could stop herself, kneeling before her wife where she stood. Her hands found their rightful place on Agatha’s stomach, her forehead pressed gently to the swell before kissing it. 

“ Mi hijo,” she murmured, pressing another kiss. (My son.) 

A hand caressed through her hair, a thumb brushing against her temple in a delicate manner. 

“ Nuestro hijo, ” Agatha corrected. ( Our son.)

Rio chuckled, closing her eyes, exhaling softly and allowing herself to sink into the quiet, allowing herself to sink in the feeling of unconditional love– in a moment where nothing else mattered but her wife and growing son between them. 

 

 

 

Chapter End Notes

Chapter 20: Two Unveiled but One Anew

Chapter Summary

And no matter how much Agatha tried to reason with herself, tried to tell herself the fact that Rio was absolutely obsessed with her, she still couldn’t shake the thoughts that maybe Rio was cheating. That maybe Rio was bored that Agatha couldn’t use the strap on her or bored from how much Rio had been giving her. Maybe Rio was finding her body repulsive. She was getting bigger, fuck she swore she was waddling like Meredith Grey.

OR :

We return to Uni, but Rio is hiding something...?

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha



Rio Harkness-Vidal did not have a dick– no matter how many times she fucking claimed otherwise. 

 

Let it be known that Agatha Harkness-Vidal was one hundred percent certain that her wife did not have a fleshy, mushroom-looking thing swinging between those sinfully sculpted, muscular thighs. If she did, Agatha would’ve been knocked up the moment they started fucking in college, and by now, their house would be overrun with at least ten little ‘mini-me Rios’ tearing through the halls. 

And if Rio did have a dick, Agatha knew damn well she’d constantly feel it pressing against her– especially now that she was really showing. No sweaters or oversized hoodies were hiding it anymore– and also because Agatha couldn’t stop herself from resting a hand there to soothe their little one. Twenty weeks pregnant, and truth be told, they’d been fucking like rabbits, but it wasn’t just Agatha initiating. To the Mother, her wife was always ready for her– drawn to her, mesmerized by her– so the idea that Rio could be losing interest or, worse, cheating? Absolutely not. No chance in hell. 

But that still didn’t explain why, for the past two weeks, Rio had been acting secretive. Sneaking out in the middle of the night– or rather, in the middle of Agatha’s naps– leaving her to wake up to an empty house. Or that one time Rio ditched her for that so-called “girls’ night” with Lilia, only to rush off in some big damn hurry. And when she did finally come home? Straight to the shower before she’d even spare Agatha a touch. 

Something was up and Agatha was going to get to the bottom of it.

Well, once she got through today without complaining about her back or her legs, or really anything. She was miserable and as much as Rio has been weirdly secretive, her wife was still watching her like a hawk, always there to give her a foot massage, rub the knots out of her shoulder, or run her hot baths which Agatha found herself taking more of as her pregnancy progressed. She wondered if she could live in the bath for the last two months of pregnancy– oh, she finally convinced Rio to at least let her work until she was two months away from the birth. March fourteenth she and Rio would go on maternity leave, miss the fall semester before Rio would go back next spring as only the dean.

But the what the fuck was Rio hiding? 

It started some time after the ultrasound, when they started clearing out the office that was far enough away from their room to still grant them privacy, but close enough to make sure they could get to him as quickly as possible. In truth, Rio did all the work. Agatha had sat in the new rocking chair, knitting the blanket’s flowers still and fussing with every thread while her wife had sat on the floor in the middle of the room, her hair pulled in bun, wearing black stained overalls with an old green crop top underneath Agatha knew Rio had worn while they were in college. She watched her wife build the bookshelves, assemble the last of the dresser, and the toy chest. Rio fitted new shelves into the closet while Agatha started organizing the presents everyone had gotten them, including the stack of diapers. Rio built the nightstand table, setting up a new lamp on it and putting Agatha’s breast pump there before they turned to the walls. 

Agatha was a little shocked that it truly wasn’t a fight. They had walked into Home Depot and went straight to the paints, grabbing the little swatches of different greens. The moment they got home and held it up to the wall, they both picked a perfect light sage green, and Agatha had sent her wife back to Home Depot to buy the cans of paint. They didn’t paint that day. No, Agatha got distracted by toned biceps and a perfect round ass shaped by those black overalls.

When they finally did start painting, her wife was hovering, not even being remotely subtle about it. 

 

 

Agatha reached over grabbing the paint roller and dipping it into the tray before listening to the stick of the roller and paint against the wall. As she stretched higher, a hand pressed against her belly, another snatching the paint roller away. Her breath was hot by her ear and it took everything in her not to shiver as the woman behind her placed a kiss on her messy bun. 

“Don’t strain yourself, mi amor, ” her wife murmured, like she hadn’t already said it a hundred times that morning. 

Agatha rolled her eyes, snatching the roller back, “I’m pregnant, not fragile.”

“Mm-hmm, ” Rio nodded absentmindedly, just humoring her. 

Agatha’s jaw tightened and of course, she reacted in the most mature way possible. She swiped her fingers along the roller, collecting the sage green and smearing it across Rio’s cheek. Her wife blinked once like an owl, processing the cold paint smeared against her. Then turned to face her, slow and deliberate, eyes narrowed. 

“ Agatha .” 

Agatha raised an eyebrow, hiding her smirk as she shrugged, “What?”

Silence and stillness. 

Total and utter stillness for a beat… and then a second… and then…

Rio lunged, the paint roller clattering to the tarp-covered floor. Agatha shrieked, barely twisting away in time, laughing as Rio grabbed the paintbrush and swiped a bold streak of green across her arm. 

“Oh, that’s how you wanna play?” 

Agatha grabbed her own, flicking paint at her wife’s face and what followed was not Agatha’s fault. 

It was absolute chaos. Paint splattered against the tarp, against half-painted walls, against them as they abandoned the careful precision and utmost order that Rio wanted to keep while they painted. They flicked, smeared, and dodged until Rio wrapped her arms around Agatha’s waist, laughing and nuzzling into her neck as Agatha tried to break free. 

“Let me go, you freak!” Agatha gasped, giggling. 

“Not a chance, my love,” Rio purred, arms tightening. 

With one last triumphant move, Rio spun her around, pinning Agatha against the nursery wall, their laughter breathless, their clothes smeared with green. Her breath left her body as the wall held her in place, her wife’s body flush against hers, warm, solid, and everywhere. The change in the air was instant as something warm pooled in the lower part of her stomach. Of course, her wife knew her, knew exactly what she wanted… what she needed. 

Rio drew closer, a smirk on her face as she leaned forward, their noses brushing as fingers trailed down her arms, along her belly, and to her waist. Then, a hot mouth was against her jawline, a teasing mize of lips and teeth, her breath fanning against her as she dragged her hands down, tracing more of her round belly before tugging at her cargo pants. 

“You’re covered in paint,” Agatha muttered, though it lacked any bite or annoyance she wanted to show, especially when Rio was right there, pressing against her and making every other thought nonexistent. 

“So are you,” Rio countered, voice husky, her hands tightening their hold and her lips placing a hot, wet opened-mouth kiss to the spot on her neck that always made her moan. “But I think you are an absolute mess right now.” 

Agatha smirked, her fingers gripping at the straps of Rio’s stained overalls and tugging her closer, “Then clean me up.”

Her wife chuckled low against her throat, the sound vibrating straight to her core as the sound of the zipper thundered in the room with her racing heart. 

“Anything for you, mi amor,” she husked, her voice thick with amusement. 

A warm hand ran down her stomach, dipping into her pants and cupping her firmly. 

Agatha arched into her, wanting more– needing more as dark eyes met hers, a twinkle of mischief there as her wife lifted her head, their noses brushing once again, “Already this wet for me, my love?” 

“Stop fucking teasing and just fuck me already.” 

Rio cooed at her, “Aw, you are desperate for me.” 

“Rio,” she growled, gripping the overalls tighter. “If you don’t fuck me in the next five seconds, I swear to the Mother– ah!” 

Fingers plunged into her without warning, moving in a steady pace as a wet sound filled the room, “Mierda, mi amor– look at you. So ready for my fingers.” 

Agatha whimpered, her nails digging into Rio’s shoulders as her wife’s fingers moved, slow, purposeful, setting her nerves ablaze. 

“Fuck, Rio–” she gasped, her back arching against the wall, her thighs trembling as Rio pressed a thigh between them, adding the perfect amount of friction. 

“I’ve got you,” Rio whispered, pressing a trail of kisses up her throat, her touch unrelenting, coaxing, teasing. “Let go for me, my love.” 

Embarrassingly, that was all it took and with a sharp cry muffled against her wife’s lips, she shattered. The coil in her lower stomach released as she clasped on her fingers, white hot pleasure crashing over her in waves. Her wife held her through it, kissing along her jaw, whispering sweet nothings in Spanish, and firmly grounding her. When Agatha slowly came down from her high, her body boneless against the nursery wall, Rio chuckled, pressing a final kiss to her swollen lips. 

“I can clean you more in the bedroom, if you prefer, m’lady,” her wife gave her a mock bow, extending her hand for her take. 

Agatha smacked it away with an annoyed huff and made her way to their room. 

 

 

The room was eventually painted, but it wasn’t by Agatha’s hand. No, she had promptly fallen asleep afterward and woke up hours later to an empty bed. She waddled her way back to the nursery, finding the walls painted, even the ceiling. The paint cans and brushes sat in the corner, brushes and pans cleaned, and cans lidded. She had smiled fondly, her hands soothing the flutters that woke her. She expected to find her wife in the kitchen making dinner, even though it was technically Agatha’s turn, but semantics– but no, Rio was nowhere to be found. 

The kitchen light was on and in the center of the island was a green phone– Rio’s phone. So it meant she was home somewhere. Agatha had called down to the basement, really hoping her wife wasn’t playing that damn video game. It was dark and she didn’t hear her muttering to herself or cursing at the screen. She made her way to the back of the house, looking out the window and seeing the greenhouse light on– target spotted. 

She let her go, drifting into the kitchen to make, for the fourth time that week, arroz con gandules. Sue her, it was good and kept their son happy– which Rio wouldn’t shut the fuck up about being right and telling her to plan their Yellowstone trip for when their son was three. She didn’t argue, just scowled and tried doing the whole ‘ don’t touch me, you’re being insufferable ’ act, but her pregnant body betrayed her, immediately seeking her wife’s warmth. 

As Agatha made her way through the kitchen, her eyes kept drifting to Rio’s phone, her brows furrowed on why her wife was acting so strange and why she was in the greenhouse at this hour. Rio’s phone lit up and she just peaked, she didn’t do it on purpose. She was just passing by, the phone capturing her face and unlocking to reveal a message. Her eyes narrowed, seeing Rio’s cousin was texting her that Abuelita wanted updates about her every day. She sighed, breathing in and trying to control her emotions. 

Rio wasn’t cheating. Rio wasn’t bored of her. If anything, the fact that had just spent hours worshipping her body and finishing their nursery, and also that Rio’s phone was still unlocked for her meant she wasn’t hiding anything… Rio wouldn’t be fucking someone in her greenhouse. That was preposterous and yet…

Agatha found herself going to the front of the house, scanning the street for unfamiliar vehicles before making her way back and had every plan of marching to the greenhouse… that was until her wife came in, a dopey grin on her face and Agatha melted, seeing those adoring brown eyes light up. Her wife had practically pranced up to her like a dog greeting their owner after they had been gone all day.

“ Cariño, ” she cooed, placing a long kiss on her lips and hands cradling her stomach. “ Y mijo.” 

If Agatha melted and then stuck permanently against her wife for the rest of night and week, that was no one’s business but hers… but it still didn’t stop Rio from sneaking around and acting weird. 

 

 

Wanda

 

She groaned, the shrill of her alarm echoing through her bedroom. She reached over with a heavy arm, turning it off before moving to get up, but a strong arm around her waist held her in place. She chuckled softly, rolling over to find Vision beside her, smiling at her. 

Wanda had thrown herself into a relationship with Vision, forcing herself to get over Agatha… Agatha who was with Rio Vidal. She would be lying if she said that she didn’t feel her eye twitch every time at the thought. Agatha wasn’t hers and never had been, but what twisted the knife deeper was that she never said anything, never told her that she was married, never said that she was taken. The woman let her be a fool, an embarrassment, and that was the cruelest twist of all. She wanted to hate her, she wanted to take everything she had, and make her pay… but she couldn’t. There was still a part of Wanda that was absolutely enamoured by Agatha Harkness. Sex with Vision did nothing for her, unless she thought of Agatha, pictured her and each time was lucky she didn’t say her name when she did eventually reach that peak. 

So, Wanda had crafted a new plan, one that worked with her reality, that formed to her own script, one that involved a real university scandal. Vidal was off limits, fine, but the eager students and gossiping professors that were just as bad– all game. Her plan would start slowly, apologize to Agatha, become her friend, and slowly get close, and once she could make sure they looked like ‘besties–’ She could feed little lies around the university. Vidal would be visibly pregnant by the time they got back, and that made things so much easier. The baby would solely belong to Vidal with IVF, so it was not like Agatha would be that attached. If anything, she was probably bored in their marriage now and there was probably a lack of attraction. But, if she couldn’t get Agatha to sleep with her, fine. They would be friends and all she had to do was a little whisper there and another there, and word would get back to Vidal… and goodbye marriage. 

 

“Nervous?” Vision’s voice broke her back into reality.

She turned her head, her eyebrows creasing. 

“The meeting today? Before classes resume next week?” he supplied. 

Wanda shook her head, placing a chaste kiss to his lips, “No, it will be fine.” 

She walked over to her closet, pulling on her clothes as Vision stayed in her bed, “Yes, well, forgive me darling, but Dean Vidal didn’t seem too keen on you.” 

She scoffed, but her lip curled, her eyes darkening as she yanked her pants up, “Well, you know what they say about her, Green Witch of the science department and all that.” 

This was the first time Vision had ever talked about Rio and Agatha after the night at the Yule market. She figured they had a silent agreement not to talk about them and now she did feel nervous. As she made her way into the bathroom, she had every urge to throw up her dinner. 

“Well, I just thought it was strange. She was so relaxed when we spoke and she was so proud to introduce her wife– they are a beautiful couple, don’t you think so?” 

Wanda felt like she had been slapped in the face, her foundation dripping onto the counter. She swallowed hard, her hands shaking as she finished her makeup, heading out into the bedroom. She ignored his question, kissing his lips goodbye and practically running out of her own home. Landing herself at a café, she bought a latte with four shots of espresso. She was fine. She would go to this meeting and everything would be normal. She could start anew with the faculty, get on Agatha’s good side and start her plan. 

Everything would be okay. 

 

***

 

Wanda stared at the email again, not understanding why the Literature Department Semester Start-up meeting was in the science building and not in the haven. It didn’t make sense unless Rio was that pregnant that she didn’t want to walk across campus to her building. She was a couple minutes early, well late for any drinks or food they would have placed out or any small talk. She would show up, listen to the Dean ramble on and then be done. Simple. Uncomplicated. And part of her plan. 

Next week, she would run into Agatha, start off with an apology and not push. She would wait, wait for Agatha to come to her and everything would be set in perfect motion. She couldn’t help but wonder how ridiculous Dean Vidal looked as pregnant, how tired and uncoordinated she would be. 

She smirked to herself as she made her way down the halls, wrinkling her nose at the fresh scent of chemicals. The conference room was set in the same hall as Vidal’s office wing, tucked in the corner and providing professors privacy from prying students. It was cold and as she grew closer, she could smell the fresh fries and burgers already that were set out as some sort of apology to have the meeting at noon. 

Walking in, everyone was either standing or seated, but as always, her eyes found their way to Agatha. She was sitting at the far end of the table, across from an older lady who did not belong in the literature department, in fact half of the professors here were not from the literature department. A tall woman with flawless skin sat next to the older professor and she immediately recognized her as Dr. Kale, a chemistry professor. At the head of the table stood Vidal, leaning against the chair as they all spoke. Vidal was in her usual clothes, a green blouse and fitted pants with her hair in that stupid claw clip. Yet, her stomach remained flat as ever…

That didn’t make sense. She heard her so clearly that day, talking about her little one, unless that was all hypothetical… so, the plan wouldn’t be as easy…but it still stood. She narrowed her gaze to Agatha. Her hair was down, black rimmed glasses sat that gorgeous nose, and a blue blazer on her shoulders. Leaning forward, her arms sat folded on the table against her chest in front of her. To the left of her seat Dr. Crawdad joined in on the conversation and nodded at Agatha and Rio. She bit her tongue, fully stepping into the room and finding assigned seats. She was seated all the way on the opposite end of Agatha, on the same side so there were dozens of bodies between them. She scowled, taking her seat, waiting for the meeting to begin. 

“Good afternoon, everyone,” Vidal’s cold voice finally droned on. “I hope you all had a well rested break. Thank you for coming today and I do apologize for this meeting may take longer than necessary as you can see I have combined the departments. There have been some administrative changes that affect both faculty and curriculum planning, so it’s essential we address them together.”

Wanda barley listened, her focus kept drifting back to Agatha as she leaned forward, pretending her gaze was on Vidal. Harkness’ expression was unreadable– composed, and almost detached, except for the occasional glance towards Vidal. It was subtle, but there. Something passed between them, something Wanda couldn’t quite place, and it made her grip the armrests of her chair a little tighter. 

She was so certain she heard it that day, “my little one.” Was she mistaken? Was it just talk of the future? Vidal sat back in her chair, posture as straight as ever and no curve of her stomach. She didn’t even look different. She was the same cold bitch as before. Maybe they were in the mists of trying and only now she was pregnant. It was her first child, so it would make sense. Some women didn’t show right away. Besides, if it were true, she had a feeling Agatha would be carrying not Vidal… a bitter taste shot through her mouth as her gaze lingered on Agatha a little longer than what was safe or necessary. She couldn’t see anything below her chest and she looked the same– had the same radiance and glow to her. Yeah, there was no way fucking way she was with child. 

 

The meeting droned on. Discussion of faculty changes, funding allocations, and research projects blended into a low hum in the background. Wanda clenched her jaw, watching Agatha, watching the way she sipped her tea with deliberate ease, the way her fingers traced the rim of the cup absentmindedly. And suddenly, Agatha didn’t seem so sharp, she looked lost in thought, as if she wasn’t even listening to her wife present the meeting. There was a soft smile that graced her lips as her eyes turned, looking Vidal up and down. Vidal caught it, raising an eyebrow at her but continued on without missing a beat. 

“As we move forward,” Vidal’s voice finally cut through, “we will be trying out combined classes, having professors work together for a common goal in the curriculum. We will be testing an interdisciplinary collaboration that I will personally be involved in. Dr. Kale and I are combining one of the classes full of the freshmen twos. Meanwhile, Dr. Crawdad and Dr. Harkness will combine their classes. That also being said, I have one final announcement before the meeting concludes.” 

Her eyes scanned the room, cool and assessing. 

“March fourteenth, I will be taking a leave of absence and won’t be returning until next spring semester–” 

Wanda could have screamed in victory. She could have done a cartwheel right there in the middle of the conference room– hell, she could have kissed Vidal on the damn mouth . This was it. This was the moment she had been waiting for. The iron grip Rio had on Agatha would finally loosen. No protective shadow trailing after her. No possessive glances or casual touches to remind the world who Agatha belonged to. She would be free.

“–where even then, I will only be working as the dean. For those who don’t know, this is Dr. Calderu, an esteemed professor in our music department. She will be stepping in while I am on leave and everything will be reported to her. As this semester progresses, I expect you to start turning your concerns to her as she will be stepping up even while I am here. Now–”

“Dean Vidal,” a dark, grumpy voice spoke up. 

Everyone’s eyes landed on Dr. Morgan, an old anatomy professor who clung to tenure like it was a raft after a plane crashed in the ocean.

“This is entirely inappropriate for you to be taking leave in the middle of a semester, especially with how you put it : there will be so many changes starting soon. You can not possibly expect this change to actually commence on your leave, having Dr. Calderu do your work while you sit and do nothing,” he snapped.

Wanda relaxed into her chair, sitting back and wishing she had popcorn. This was perfect, and despite it being Vidal, she did have to nod her head towards the Dean– she could put on a show. 

Vidal’s mouth twitched, her gaze darkening as she leaned forward, slow and deliberate, pinning Morgan under the weight of her stare. She didn’t interrupt. Didn’t cut him off. She let him talk, let him dig . And then– 

“This is utterly unprofessional,” Morgan barked. “And after all the rumors last semester, I’m starting to wonder if there was any truth to them.” 

His eyes flicked between Vidal and Agatha with pointed accusation.

Silence. 

The air grew thick. Vidal didn’t react. She examined her nails, her expression blank– no, bored, as if she weren’t being publicly accused of a scandal that could rip her career apart. She let the weight of it settle over the room, let the discomfort sink into every crack and crevice. 

Then, finally, she lifted her gaze, “Are you done, Dr. Morgan?” 

He grumbled loudly, but ultimately said nothing. 

Vidal sat back, a picture of confidence and calmness, “I understand that we had a very entertaining fall semester with mine and Dr. Harkness’ names being spewed through the mud. I was hoping very much to know where that all stemmed from.”

Not a soul breathed as Vidal met each of their eyes, looking for any sign or hint it was them, and yet each one of them looked guilty as they all took part in gossip that ran rapidly in the fall. Wanda controlled her face, meeting Vidal’s gaze head on, and to her luck, her eyes snapped back to Dr. Morgan, an eyebrow raised. He didn’t flinch, keeping her gaze, daring her to get on with it. 

“If we can actually act like adults this semester, instead of a bunch of inconsiderate, gossiping, teenage–”

A sigh, sharp and pointed, cut through her words, “Rio.” 

Vidal’s head snapped to the side, her expression unreadable as Agatha spoke her name– a quiet reprimand. It was like watching a lion be leashed. Vidal inhaled slowly, then exhaled through her nose, regaining composure.

“If you know where those rumors came from, I expect an email in my inbox and an apology to both me and Dr. Harkness.”

Dr. Morgan leaned forward, baring his teeth, “It still doesn’t excuse your absence, Dean Vidal. You are–”

Vidal’s eyes flashed with something, shutting Morgan up as she practically lunged forward, “ If you could shut your mouth for two seconds, I will happily explain, Dr. Morgan.” 

Everyone watched, knowing not to utter a word as Dr. Morgan foolishly waved his hand as if to say the floor was hers. 

“I am taking a leave of absence,” she spoke purposely, enunciating each word with devastating precision. “Because my wife is carrying our first child that is due in May.”

Wanda froze, her eyes widening as she looked between Agatha and Rio, but neither of them paid any attention– they probably didn’t even care that Wanda had the biggest headache of her life, her mind running in circles, trying to place any of the pieces together. 

Agatha? 

Pregnant?

Impossible.

“Now, if you have any questions that don’t pertain to my personal life, please do stay. If not, I will see you next week. Please take your folder with the new budgets, calendars, upcoming department meeting topics.” 

 No one spoke, just nodding as they gathered their stuff. Wanda watched as the dean raised an eyebrow, eyeing the crowd before rising from her seat. She turned to Agatha, extending her hand for the woman to take. Turning the chair around, her back to the professors, she stood, her hands in Rio’s as they spoke quietly and what seemed to be in Spanish, but Wanda was too focused on trying to see Agatha’s stomach. 

It felt like eternity. Agatha was moving with the same unhurried grace she always carried, her fingers briefly brushing against Vidal’s wrist before she turned. And then the air in the room shifted. The conversations that had barely begun to pick up again died in an instant. Papers stilled in hands. A few audible gasps broke the silence, sharp as snapped twigs. Wanda felt her breath catch in her throat. 

The navy-blue slacks and crisp white button-down should have been unremarkable, just another polished ensemble in Agatha’s collection. But now, with nothing obscuring her frame, there was no mistaking the gentle, yet unmistakable curve of her stomach. 

Pregnant.

Vidal made it undeniable. Without hesitation, she slid an arm around Agatha’s waist, her other hand pressing firmly, possessively, against the swell in a silent claim and warning. It was a declaration to the faculty, one that seemed to rip everyone’s voices away. 

Pregnant.

Agatha, as always, seemed unfazed. If anything, Wanda was sure there was a smirk ghosting her lips that was one of quiet amusement. Electric blue eyes flicked over the stunned faces before settling on Wanda just for a beat longer before Vidal was guiding the woman out the door and into the hall.

Pregnant. 

She couldn’t move.

Pregnant. 

She couldn’t breathe… because…

Agatha Harkness was pregnant with Vidal ’s baby. 

 

The stunned silence stretched, thick, heavy, and smothering, like a whale was pressing down on all of them. It was as if Agatha and Rio had taken all the oxygen with them when they left, leaving each of them gasping for breath, searching for the smallest bit of it. Wanda watched with dazed eyes, still unable to move as the others seemed to be in similar states, except for four of them. 

Dr. Crawdad, who would have been screeching in shock by now like a teenage girl, merely smiled softly, gathering the scattered papers on the desk as even Dr. Lune, her annoying fucking partner in crime did the same. Completely and utterly unbothered. 

The other two, Wanda was less shocked about. They packed their stuff, talking quietly with each other as they even seemed to be laughing at something. Wanda’s ears were ringing so she couldn’t quite catch what they were saying until Morgan’s face grew red with rage. 

“You knew, Kale?” he snarled. 

Calderu and Kale rolled their eyes, “Obviously. Why do you think she has me, Calderu, and Crawdad filling in for them?” 

Crawdad nodded as if she and Lune weren’t part of the problem, “It wasn’t that hard to figure out. They aren’t exactly subtle.” 

The room bristled. 

Dr. Archer, a fellow literature professor, looked like he had seen a ghost.

“How long?” he rubbed his temple as his voice was strained, struggling to wrap his head around what happened. 

Calderu finally spoke, shooting him a bemused look, “Together? Since their college days. Married? Ten years. Pregnant? Well, the baby is due in May.” 

The reaction was instant. 

“ Since college ?!” 

A ripple of disbelief swept through the faculty. Conversations overlapped in hushed, frantic whispers, a tangle of shock, confusion, and sheer embarrassment at having been so blind. 

Dr. Finch, from the language department, gaped, “But they never–” 

“They did ,” Calerdu interrupted, looking positively entertained by the mess unfolding before her. “You just never noticed.” 

And that was the truth of it, wasn’t it? All the signs had been there– the way Vidal would linger near Harness’ office a moment longer than necessary, the way Harkness’ sharp wit always seemed to soften just slightly when Vidal was in the room. The stolen moments, the quiet, fleeting touches, the way Vidal’s sharp temper flared the most when Harkness was involved. It had always been there. Right in front of them. And yet, none of them had seen it. 

Dr. Morgan’s chair scraped against the floor as he stood abruptly, his face red with anger– or was it humiliation? “This is– this is unbelievable.” 

“Oh, give it a rest, Morgan,” Dr. Kale cut in, rolling her eyes. “What exactly are you mad about? That two grown women didn’t announce their personal lives for your entertainment?” 

“That the Dean–” 

“Took a leave of absence to raise a child with her wife ?” she shot back, arching a brow. “How dare she.” 

Morgan sputtered, clearly searching for something, anything, to justify his rage, but he came up short. Dr. Archer shook his head in disbelief, still dazed.

“I just– Harkness?” his brows furrowed as if his brain was physically rejecting the thought. “I thought she didn’t–” 

He stopped himself. 

A fresh wave of murmurs spread across the room as the weight of the revelation finally settled in. Agatha Harkness– sharp-tongued, elusive, untouchable Agatha Harkness– was married to Rio Vidal. And she was carrying her child. The implications were staggering. The meeting was all but forgotten, faculty members too wrapped up in their own disbelief to care about the budgets or calendars left in front of them.

And Wanda, she felt something sting in her eyes and without a single word, she stormed out of the conference room. 

 

***

 

Agatha

 

Agatha’s breath hitched as Rio’s lips pressed a soft, heated kiss to the curve of her neck, just below her ear. The touch was both tender and demanding, a seamless blend of affection and the quiet, possessive dominance Rio wielded with such natural ease. The warmth of Rio’s hands against her waist and stomach grounded Agatha, a subtle reminder of the life growing within her– their life. She tilted her head back slightly, offering more of her neck to Rio’s ministrations, savoring the feel of Rio’s breath against her skin, the gentle press of her body as she pinned Agatha to the side of the car. The sounds of the world around them were muffled, distant, and for the moment, nothing existed but the two of them in their little bubble of intimacy.

She smirked, her own hand on her wife’s waist, the other combing through the baby hairs on the base of her neck. They were waiting for the Coven to join them for a late lunch, and now, her wife was happily getting the possession out of her system. She let her, tilting her head back, granting her wife more access as the kisses were hot, wet, and perfect, sending shivers through her body. If Rio had her way, Agatha knew they would have been home and Rio probably would have taken her up against the door, but for now this would have to do. She hummed when soft lips made their way to her jaw, teeth nicking softly. 

“You enjoyed that too much, my love,” she smirked, making her wife pull away.
