Rio raised an eyebrow at her, but her gaze was down on her stomach, hands smoothing out her white button up, “Enjoyed what?” 

She leaned forward, tucking a piece of hair behind her ear, “Telling them that I am yours.” 

She then chuckled darkly, not able to help herself, “Did you see Maximoff’s face? I thought she was about to pass out.”

Rio’s smirk deepened, a quiet, satisfied gleam in her eyes. But before Agatha could continue her teasing, Rio’s hands moved lower, brushing over the gentle curve of Agatha’s stomach with a tenderness that made her breath catch. Their son was growing, a little heartbeat just below the surface, and Rio’s touch was the softest reminder that this was real, that this moment wasn’t just about them as partners, but as parents, too. Their son, even at this early stage, seemed to reach out for his mother’s touch. Agatha could feel it, the gentle flutter beneath her fingers, as if the child was responding to the warmth of Rio’s hands. Rio’s gaze softened as her fingers traced over the swell of Agatha’s stomach, a quiet smile playing at the edges of her lips. 

“He’s growing in there,” her wife murmured, the brown lighting up to gold as if the sun was shining straight down on her. 

Agatha rolled her eyes, knowing her wife was dodging the conversation. 

“An astute observation, my love,” she deadpanned before cupping her face, getting her wife to look at her. “Did you have to be so possessive about it?”

She teased her calmly, knowing not to poke at her too much as her fingers traced the outline of her jaw, soothing that fire she knew was kindling inside her wife. 

“Not that I entirely minded, but you really enjoyed making that statement.” 

Rio’s grin widened, a playful glint in her eyes, but there was something more behind it– something that could only be described as pride. 

“I have no shame in claiming what’s mine, Agatha. And if the whole world needs to see it, then so be it.” 

Right, how could she forget the number of bar fights this woman had been in over her. 

“You’re impossible,” she murmured, shaking her head in mock disapproval. 

Rio’s smile softened, the playfulness fading just slightly as she leaned in once more, brushing her lips against Agatha’s forehead. 

“No, mi amor . I’m yours,” she whispered, the words an unspoken vow, a promise that resonated deep within Agatha’s soul.

 

 

Lunch with the coven was wonderful, minus all the teasing about how their students will react. Jen and Lilia told them about the immediate scene they caused after the meeting, but the weight she didn’t even know was there left her shoulders. As much as she loved the secrecy, the fleeting moments knowing it was just them and no one else, getting it off her chest was needed. They didn’t have to tiptoe around the faculty or students, they could just be Dr. Harkness and Dr. Vidal, two professors who are married and are expecting a son. 

If only Rio would feel the same about whatever fucking secret she’s keeping. 

The moment they got home, Agatha changed and fell asleep immediately on the couch. When she woke up, Rio was gone. Again. There wasn’t a single sound in the house and the light was on from the greenhouse. And no matter how much Agatha tried to reason with herself, tried to tell herself the fact that Rio was absolutely obsessed with her, she still couldn’t shake the thoughts that maybe Rio was cheating. That maybe Rio was bored that Agatha couldn’t use the strap on her or bored from how much Rio had been giving her. Maybe Rio was finding her body repulsive. She was getting bigger, fuck she swore she was waddling like Meredith Grey. There were new stretch marks on her from her thighs to her belly to her ass. She used the cream for the ones on her stomach, but they were still there and fuck– tears were welling up in her eyes as her chest tightened. 

Agatha shook out of it, sitting up abruptly and spotting that green phone sitting on the coffee table, mocking her. Before she could tell herself that this was a bad idea, she snatched it, unlocking it with tears in her eyes and going straight to Rio’s messages. Agatha’s contact was pinned at the top and well, she knew Rio sucked at texting as much as she did. The amount of blue dots that filled the messages were ridiculous. She spotted some from Alice and some from Lilia, even a few unread from Hela. La Familia Vidal group chat was silent for once and she half wondered what Abuelita must have told the rest of the family about her wife’s cunt of an older sister. 

So, no concerning messages there. 

She moved to Instagram, where her wife used to be so much more active, but it looked as if her wife hadn’t opened it in months. She went to her home page, and unsurprisingly the seven posts on her wife’s private instagram were all of her, minus one where it was Rio’s greenhouse, but her wife still tagged her. She rolled her eyes fondly, scrolling through the pictures of herself, each post tagged with their favorite songs: Sailor Song, Until I Found You, Those Eyes, Alkaline, honey, Somewhere Only We Know, and that one Taylor Swift song– specifically “You drew stars around my scars”– Rio Harkness-Vidal is a lover girl through and through. 

And yet, she went to her direct messages, finding absolutely nothing but her own instagram account staring back at her with the few memes they shared with each other. She swiped out of the app, deciding to go through her wife’s photos, maybe that would explain the secrecy. The moment the photos opened, Agatha’s eyes rolled harder than ever. The woman had pictures upon pictures of her. Most of them Agatha was definitely not paying attention to as the most recent favorite was taken from yesterday morning. She had stood by the glass door leading to their back porch, a mug of tea in her hand while the other cradled her stomach. Her hair was in loose waves down to her ass and of course her wife caught her wearing Rio’s Snoopy boxers and her oversized t-shirt that went with it.

Agatha bit her lip, scrolling down to the recently deleted and finding nothing but images of paper work and wood? She froze, zooming in and just seeing it was pictures of different types of wood. She shook her head, figuring her wife must want new floors in the basement and wanted hardwood. Rio had been complaining about the basement floors and wanting something new put down. She moved on to the Hidden, her face unlocking it once again, and Agatha immediately felt her cheeks heat up. They were all the photos that she sent to Rio over the years. She ended up scrolling to the bottom, finding one of the very firsts she ever sent Rio. 

She cursed in her head, staring at her twenty year old self, in nothing but a thong and a purple thin crop top with “dyke” written across her tits in green. Scrolling up, she watched herself become more bolder from her own hand down her pants, cupping her breasts– yep, too bold, sitting entirely naked in front of a mirror with her legs spread. She remembered that well. It was during their engagement and Rio was taking too long at her lab. 

Swiping out of the apps, she sat back, chewing at her lip as guilt crept in, burrowing its way inside her. Agatha knew she was overreacting, there was truly nothing in Rio’s behavior or phone that would suggest she was cheating. As if to ground her back to reality, a flutter rippled in her stomach gaining her attention. 

She looked down, lifting her shirt to stare at her stomach, feeling the flutters growing in strength day by day. At this point, she was certain she could really feel their son’s mouvements. She knew when he kicked, when he pushed against her, or even when he turned, and right now, he seemed to be kicking at her ribs, trying to get her thoughts to stop spiralling. 

“I know, mister. It’s a ridiculous thought,” she muttered to him while caressing the side he was moving on. “Your mami hasn’t even met you and she is obsessed with you.” 

He moved in agreement, making her chuckle. 

“Goddess, she knew you were our son before I even did,” she whispered in awe, her chest aching now.  

Agatha turned her gaze towards the back of the house where she knew Rio was in the greenhouse. The thoughts were ridiculous. Rio had been attached to her ever since their first ‘I love you’s. That woman would be the last partner on earth to cheat on her, and she hated that she let her stupid emotions get to her. 

 

Whatever secret Rio was holding, Agatha knew she’d have to trust that her wife would unveil it in her own time. But Agatha Harkness was not a woman known for her patience. It was only a matter of time before the tension inside her reached its breaking point, and when it did, the storm that followed would be impossible to ignore.

 

 

Chapter End Notes

what could Rio be hiding 🫣👹💚💜

it WILL be revealed next chapter -- calm your tits down

also, missed writing Wanda's delulus

Chapter 21: Welcome to the Family

Chapter Summary

“Then why have you been sneaking around?!” Agatha demanded, her voice wobbling despite herself. “Why are you hiding things from me? Why am I hearing that you were hugging someone else? Do you have any idea how that feels? Fifteen years together and when I am pregnant with your child– I hear this!”

Something cracked in Rio’s expression. Just for a moment. Then, to Agatha’s shock, Rio exhaled a sharp breath– almost a laugh, but not a happy one. It was the sound of someone who had just realized how deeply they had messed up.

“Oh, fuck,” Rio whispered, rubbing her hands over her face. “Oh my Gods, mi vida, I– ”

 

OR:

we find out how Wanda spread the rumor or Rio's secrets are revealed

Chapter Notes

To the guest "revealthetruth" who commented last chapter, I am so in love with you 🖤

okay, so this was longer than I thought it would be, but enjoy !

Love,

Rioallalong_719💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

 

 

Students

 

Ryan had a secret. 

A horrible secret that he even kept from his friends… something that ate at him throughout all of winter break. Yet, it was no secret that Ryan wasn’t particularly the brightest, but he was trying. He was trying so hard for his family and most importantly his little sister who was in middle school and looked up to him. So when a chance showed up that his little sister would get a good word in with Dartmouth University, how was he supposed to say no? It was stupid really. He didn’t understand how he fell for it, but pulled all nighters, nose buried in a textbook, desperate to understand the major he chose– in the moment, it felt like the right call. 

It was supposed to be small, something inconsequential. Students had their fun with rumors all the time and Dr. Harkness and Dr. Vidal barely seemed fazed with the marriage rumors, even though he was dead set it was true. So what harm could a little baby rumor start? Dr. Maximoff had approached him, promising recommendation letters, before finally threatening to cut him from the football team– something to keep him in line before telling him what she overheard. He thought nothing but of his career and of his future… so he let the words slip, careless whispers turned wildfire, crackling through the halls of the university. 

At first, it was just a joke. A harmless little thing, nothing but a whisper in passing– Dr. Vidal, pregnant? It was almost laughable. She was the most no-nonsense professor on campus, the kind of woman who could silence a lecture hall with a single glance. The idea of her, of the Dean herself, carrying a child? It felt absurd. But then it spread, mutating, twisting into something beyond his control. He hadn’t thought much of it when Dr. Maximoff first pulled him aside, her tone all honeyed promises and veiled threats. A letter of recommendation, a future at Dartmouth secured– and all he had to do was repeat what she had overheard. Just a little rumor, nothing more. 

And Ryan, desperate and foolish, had taken the bait. Now, it was everywhere. Professors constantly congratulated Vidal at finals week and at first, yeah it was funny. Seeing how pissed she got, how she snapped at other brilliant minds. In his defense, he did not take responsibility for what Ireland did. That was all her and he and the others had tried stopping her. What person in their right mind went up to someone, accusing them of being pregnant when they weren’t showing, they hadn’t even said anything, and when it wasn’t even a concrete fact whether she was married? So, no, Ryan didn’t have guilt for that. Despite Ryan not being the brightest person when it came to books, he was sure it took simple common sense not to do that. 

But the rumors turned, building up continuously like ripples. Professors and students whispered alike that Dr. Harkness was using Dr. Vidal, sleeping her way through the faculty. Despite Harkness being scary, Ryan enjoyed the professor’s lectures, even if most of the time he was confused. Yet, Harkness was an excellent professor, meeting him during her office hours, calming going over what it was step by step even though she looked like she was about to kill him– it was the mere fact she didn’t turn him away unlike the other professors who would tell him “it’s in the notes,” “tutoring is available at the student center,” or “look at the syllabus.” 

And now he stood outside of Harkness’ door, staring at the handle, unsure whether telling her everything would get everything to stop eating at him. But then his mind flashed, maybe Harkness wouldn’t listen. Maybe she would go straight to the dean– neither of them believing that Dr. Maximoff had threatened him. All he knew was that Dr. Vidal and Dr. Harkness didn’t deserve their names to be dragged through slander, not if Dr. Vidal was actually pregnant. Just as he lifted his hand to knock, a presence loomed behind him, making him turn, only to be engulfed in flame. He stuttered back, avoiding green eyes and locking into red. His heart staggered as he struck, forcing himself to be smaller than the woman even though he towered over her.

“Dr. Maximoff,” he said, taking another step when she stepped forward, the smile on her face too bright, too forced. 

“Ryan,” she said in faux excitement. “How was your break?” 

He nodded his head, “Great– yep, great.”

Dr. Maximoff’s lip curled slightly, “That’s wonderful. Well, welcome back to spring semester. When is your first class?”

He glanced at Dr. Harkness’ door, continuing to back away from the red headed professor, “In thirty with Dr. Harkness. I-I was just going to check in about textbooks before classes began– and-and ask about the final exam from last semester.”

She nodded her head slowly, her head tilting and it was communicated clearly, I know you are trying to tell Dr. Harkness. Utter one breath, and your football career is gone. 

“Well, I think Dr. Harkness is in a meeting with the dean. You can talk to her after class,” she shooed him away. 

Ryan didn’t fight. He turned on his heel and practically ran towards the lecture hall, needing a moment to himself. He couldn’t tell his friends. They would get mad at him for what happened with Ireland and even more so– for being so foolish when the rumors were all over that Dr. Maximoff crossed some sort of line with Dr. Harkness, prompting Dean Vidal to yell at her. He was so stupid, so full of guilt that it was practically choking him. 

He didn’t know how long he sat there, but it must have been thirty minutes as the doors opened, students pouring in and sitting at the benches and long tables. Somebody nudged him, making him jump and he stared up into the bright eyes of Ireland and William beside her. Eva sat on his other side, looking tired as the friend group all smiled at one another. 

“Hey dude, how was break? What kept you?” William started. 

Ryan shook his shoulders, “Oh, just busy with work and you know how my family is.” 

They all nodded, before Ireland was practically jumping at him, grabbing his arm harshly, “Did you see our texts?” 

“What ones?” he questioned and they all gasped at him. 

The three radiated with excitement, as if they were about to drop the biggest announcement to ever grace their lives, but before they could, the doors opened, silencing the room immediately. Dr. Crawdad walked in with Dr. Harkness beside her, but something was different and Ryan felt the colour drain from his face. Dr. Harkness looked soft… in a long brown skirt and long-sleeve pink shirt, her hair pinned up in a messy bun, but that wasn’t the thing. Ryan’s mouth opened to the floor, everything else draining from him as underneath the high waisted skirt was the unmistakable roundness of Harkness’ stomach. In truth, it was like a perfectly round watermelon was tucked underneath the skirt and dress. He turned to the others, his mouth still opened, but they were staring in awe of her. 

“She’s grown from Christmas,” William whispered. 

Ireland smacked him, “No shit, but don’t say it like that.” 

Ryan whipped his head towards them, “You’re saying you guys knew ?” 

They both nodded, “We texted you and you want to guess whose baby that she is carrying?”
 He shook his shoulders, his mind swarming. 

“Dr. Vidal’s,” Ireland dropped. 

“What?” he squeaked out a little too loudly, the students who didn’t care for the rumors were giving him a weird look while the others were just in shock, staring at their professor. 

His friends nodded, “Vidal and Harkness came to ‘The Tarot of the Lovers’ together before Christmas under the name, Harkness-Vidal.”

“And that was with a hyphen,” William smirked. 

“Damn, I figured Vidal would have her name first, or like their last name just be Vidal,” a different student butted in. 

The group smiled, more of the students acknowledging the ‘news’ from the group, but if the students were being honest, it wasn’t really news– rather a book in a library, water in the ocean– if the students noticed last semester how much Vidal was in Harkness’ office, or vice-versa… they didn’t say anything… at least not in front of them. And when the rumors first started, sure it was a slight shock. Two badass professors– both who could silence a crowd with their mere presence, who could say one word about an essay or lab and make the student cry, both who dominated panels– were married? It would seem like a match made in hell– a match that could only be toxic, something that was bound to explode– but the ‘subtle’ looks between them, the way they even walked together, trying to maintain professionalism– a blind person could see how strong their love was… unless you were Dr. Maximoff. 

That bitch’s delusions had delusions. 

But as the whole class was now talking about the marriage and then gawking at Dr. Harkness’ stomach, Ryan felt his life flash before his eyes. He started the rumor that Dr. Vidal was the one who was pregnant… not Dr. Harkness. And he went and made her wife, Dr. Vidal’s pregnant wife, seem like she was the center of a career ending scandal. Yeah. He was dead. Deader than Ireland. Deader than anyone who looked at Dr. Harkness the wrong way… but maybe not as dead as Dr. Maximoff… so there was hope. But first, he would have to draw the courage– but also who would he tell? Dr .Vidal would obliterate him with a single glance. Harkness? He wasn’t sure. How do you tell the woman who was actually pregnant that you were the one who started the rumor that her wife was pregnant? 

“Good morning, students,” the silent cold voice was cacophonous. 

The students all stopped, like a flock of birds they were in sync, falling into dead silence and perching themselves as the edge of their seats as Harkness’ piercing blue eyes roamed over them, but they were all entranced by the way she slightly leaned on the podium, instead of her usual strong, straight posture. A hand was on her stomach, rubbing the side of it absentmindedly. 

“Before we began, I am sure you saw the change in schedule. This semester will be the first time we are implementing a split syllabus. Until March fourteenth, you will be stuck with me. We will be going over the fundamentals of Shakespeare, dissecting the structure, the themes, and how Shakespeare built the text itself. After, you will be diving into mythology in literature with Dr. Crawdad, exploring the themes of romance in the Arthurian Legends and a few Greek stories such as Icarus” she paused for a moment, her eyes unfocused as her hand rubbed her stomach more. “I will not be returning next fall, but the fall after. My freshmen advisees, please schedule a meeting with me and I will help you request a new advisor.”

A hand shot up, bold and quick before the professor could continue. Dr. Harkness nodded towards the hand.  

“When is the baby due?” 

The question seemed to shock her. She stilled, her hand frozen mid circle on her stomach as her eyebrow raised. No one asked Dr. Harkness personal questions, and yet, the students couldn’t contain themself. Their curiosity ate at them, making some on the verge of falling out of their seats as whispers started up immediately. Students leaned towards her, eyes wide, ready for the answer, as if Dr. Harkness would give it. But before she could snap, the dam holding the river of curiosity finally collapsed. 

“Is it a boy or girl?” another student asked.

Dr. Harkness, for once in the students’ lives, looked truly speechless. There was no quick retort, no sneer in her voice, not even a glare. She just blinked, as if she was trying to process how the conversation had derailed so quickly, vibrating with barely contained excitement, students leaning forward like a flock of birds ready to take flight. No one asked Dr. Harkness personal questions—no one dared. But now? Now the mystery had cracked open, and they were all tumbling through, eager for answers. 

“Wait, so you were pregnant last semester? Not Dr. Vidal?” someone from the back called out. 

A gasp rippled through the crowd as Ryan tensed. 

“Oh my god, it was you all along.” 

“So is Dr. Vidal the father?” another voice called out earning a few snickers. 

“Obviously,” another student answered. 

“This is why Dr. Vidal was extra terrifying during finals?” another person called out.  

 “Oh my God, did she find out during finals?” 

Another gasp. 

“Wait, did Dr. Crawdad know too?” 

Dr. Harkness parted her lips to speak, maybe to finally put an end to this chaos, but it was too late.

“Oh my gods, do you think this baby is going to get Dr. Harkness’ attitude or that glare Vidal does?”

“Both, obviously.” 

 “Then we’re all doomed.” 

More laughter. 

More voices layering over one another, students tossing theories back and forth with increasing enthusiasm. 

“Wait, does that mean Dr. Vidal’s taking leave too? Are you both leaving us?” 

“What about names? It has to be something with Shakespeare like Ophelia if she’s a girl.” 

Someone gasped as others threw their heads back in laughter. 

“Romeo if it’s a boy.” 

“Or Hamlet.” 

“Macbeth!” 

“Henry!” 

“Juliet, obviously.”

Laughter echoed in the hall as Dr. Crawdad stepped next to Dr. Harkness, trying to hide her own laughter. The sheer audacity of the class was unmatched, and Dr. Harkness, for all her usual composure, still looked utterly caught off guard. But she finally snapped out of it as the most basic Shakespeare character names were screamed into her hall. She exhaled slowly, her lip twitching as her head cocked to the side. 

“ This ,” she said, voice sharper than the blade that plummeted into Julius Caesar’s back, “is a literature class. Not a panel on my personal life.”  

Silence. A few guilty coughs. The students weren’t sorry– not in the slightest– but they were smart enough to recognize the thin ice beneath them. 

Dr. Harkness let the quiet settle before adding, in a tone that was almost bored, “For the record, no, we will not be naming our child after any Shakespeare characters.” 

The reaction was instant. 

“So are you naming it after a plant?” 

Harkness’ eyes darkened, a glare attacking them and convincing them to take a blade as Othello did… as Juliet did… as Cassius did… you get the point. 

 

(No one tell Agatha about A Midsummer Night’s Dream. ) 

 

***

 

The students were buzzing through the rest of the day, the news spreading not only from their mouths, not only from Dr. Harkness’, but from the one professor they never thought they would hear a peep about her personal life. 

Ryan felt as if he was going to throw up as he followed his friends into the science building later that day. His feet dragged as the students practically clamoured their way to their seats at the lab tables. Dr. Vidal was already there, typing on the computer as Dr. Kale stood beside her, both of them talking calmly, but Ryan shrank as he heard Dr. Vidal. 

“Sure, but I wish she would take off sooner,” she told Kale. 

Kale shook her head, “You worry, too much, Rio. Agatha is fine holding her own and as you said, the baby is healthy.” 

Vidal just shook her head, standing up straight to face the chemistry professor, “Yes, but as you already heard, the gossiping has started, and she doesn’t need anymore stress.” 

With that they turned and faced the class, Vidal in her signature green and black, forest green pants and a black tight shirt, but for once, she was wearing short sleeves. Ireland and Eva exchanged glances, practically drooling over the purple flowers on her bicep and the snake wrapping around the other. Ireland remembered the ink peeking from the collar bones when she saw her in a suit at the restaurant and everything in her was begging to know all of them. 

“Harkness is so lucky,” she whispered, her eyes still flicking up and down Vidal. 

Eva nodded, “But so is Vidal– it’s almost not fair how good they look together– legit power couple.” 

They giggled at that, but froze when Vidal turned, grabbing the syllabi when they caught sight of ink covering her back from the low cut in the back. Ireland and Eva were actually drooling, staring at their married professor. William kicked them both under the table. 

“Guys, Harkness literally just announced they’re having a baby,” he reminded them just as Vidal passed the packet to Kale. “And we both know how possessive she is of her wife.” 

They snapped out of it, Ireland remembering when they came into the restaurant– how Vidal fussed over her wife, teasing her, but taking care of her. Her eyes were only on Harkness with a tenderness she didn’t even know existed. 

“Welcome back, students. I hope you all had a restful break. Before we begin, you will notice there is a change in this syllabus compared to the online one which Kale and I are working on now. We are the second class splitting this semester’s curricular where first we will cover floral biology before moving to environmental chemistry. March fourteenth, I will be taking leave and won’t return until next spring. My advisees, go ahead and email me, and I will connect you with a new advisor.”   

A student who wasn’t with the gossip raised their hand, and asked with a nonchalant tone, “Why are you leaving?”

Everyone shrank back as Vidal’s eye twitched, “My wife will be having our baby in May.” 

Silence. Absolute, deafening silence. And then–

“Oh my God,” someone whispered, as if the reality of it had just hit them. 

“She actually admitted it,” Eva breathed, gripping Ireland’s arm like she needed to steady herself. 

“Dr. Vidal said it out loud,” Ireland whispered back, her voice somewhere between awe and terror. 

The classroom vibrated with barely contained energy, students exchanging glances, some covering their mouths to stifle their reactions, others gripping the edges of the lab tables like they had just witnessed history in the making. Because Dean Vidal– stoic, terrifying, no-nonsense Dr. Vidal– had actually confirmed the rumors. 

William muttered, “If anyone even thinks about asking her more questions, I’m leaving.” 

Which, of course, meant someone raised their hand immediately. 

“Are you taking leave because you want to or because Dr. Harkness forced you to?” 

A few students gasped. 

Someone in the back whispered, “Bold move.” 

Dr. Vidal’s eye twitched again, “My wife and I agreed that I would take leave with her.” 

William rolled his eyes and muttered, “Translation: Dr. Harkness forced her.” 

Another student jumped in, emboldened by the fact that they weren’t dead yet, “Does this mean you’re both gonna be out at the same time? No Dr. Harkness or Dr. Vidal?” 

“It’s not a hostage situation,” Dr. Vidal said dryly, but the students barely paid attention, too caught up in the rapid-fire conversation. 

“Are you staying home to take care of her?” 

“She definitely made you.” 

“She’s the only one who can tell Dr. Vidal what to do– power move.” 

“Wait, does that mean Dr. Harkness is actually taking it easy?” 

“Oh my God,” Eva whispered, eyes wide with something dangerously close to awe. “Harkness won.” 

Ireland nodded, equally stunned. 

“Vidal let her win.” 

Dr. Vidal exhaled sharply, rubbing her temple, “If you’re all quite finished– ” 

A hand shot up, and without thinking, she nodded toward it, “What’s the baby’s name?” 

The collective hush that fell over the classroom was thick with anticipation. Dr. Vidal stilled, her gaze flickering for just a second– too quick for most, but Ireland caught it. A softness. A shift. 

“That…” Dr. Vidal said, her voice quieter now, “is something my wife and I will share when we’re ready.” 

And just like that, the storm paused. Because for all her sharp edges and coldness, there was something undeniably warm in the way she said “my wife.” And for a moment, the students saw a flicker of someone different, someone who wasn’t Dean Vidal, Dr. Vidal, the Green Witch of the science department– they saw a flicker of Rio Harkness-Vidal, a soon-to-be mother and a loving and protective wife. The students looked at each other, and for the first time since the entire chaotic first day of classes began, they hesitated… well, that was until–

“Okay, but one last question,” a student said, grinning. 

Dr. Vidal sighed, “Of course.” 

“Are you naming the baby after a Shakespeare character?” 

Dr. Vidal’s lip curled much in the way Dr. Harkness’ did, her eyes set with a coldness that would radiate from a dead body. The fearful hush came back, as if the students finally realized who they were talking too. They all bowed their heads as Kale placed her hand over her mouth, stepping beside Vidal, and continuing the lecture before the dean snapped. 

 

 



Agatha

 

Twenty-two weeks pregnant, not even two weeks into the spring semester, her wife was still hiding something. Agatha had been surprisingly patient, well if you count patience as the fact that she was actually too exhausted the moment they stepped home. The moment she was in the comfort of her own home, Rio took them to their room, either ran a hot bath or hopped in the shower with her, before laying her down to take a nap. She would fall asleep with half her body on her wife, fingers running through the ends of her hair, a hand on her stomach or Rio would be tucking pillows around her, Agatha refusing to buy an actual pillow made for her, as she always wanted to cling to Rio. She didn’t want her wife forcing a pillow at her when it wasn’t warm like Rio, when she couldn’t listen to the steady heartbeat underneath her ear, nor have her head rise and fall with her wife’s chest.

So when the pillows were set, Rio would sit on the floor, her hands on Agatha’s stomach, muttering soft Spanish that Agatha was too tired to comprehend. And when her eyes were too heavy, a kiss would be pressed to her stomach, her forehead, her nose, and finally to her lips and Rio, the sneaky bastard, would slip away. 

And this was when Agatha hated being pregnant. She hated constantly feeling like her emotions were crazy, constantly not having a grasp on them– always overreacting, between snapping at her wife or crying over something simple.

She was reading a passage about Ophelia, comparing the original text to movie with the understanding agency– of Ophelia taking the narrative and whether her death was truly a suicide or something on accident. The passage was highlighting the floral imagery of her death in the original text, talking of her mermaid, haunting-like appearance in the brooke surrounded by flowers, and for whatever reason, Agatha thought back to her wife. She thought of the flowers Rio got her for every date up till they were married, even now, Rio would love surprising every now and then with flowers. And with that thought, her throat tightened up. It was embarrassing, the tears welling in her eyes as she had to take a few calming breaths before continuing. For once, the little fucking brats kept their mouths shut. 

And she hated her strange cravings. She hated wanting to eat pickles with ice cream, mac & cheese with applesauce, the random Nando’s cravings– or especially craving that one pastry from a café twenty minutes away from campus and it absolutely ruined her day when neither she or Rio could get them. She hated that she was stealing Rio’s portion of lunch too. She hated waking up her wife at three am, begging her to go get one of the cravings, and then having to wait in a cold bed for her to come back absolutely exhausted. 

Then came the pains, when her back would kill her, aching from carrying the new weight of their son. She hated that her fingers were starting to swell, that Rio had to pry her wedding ring off of her with dish soap and then having to wear it around her neck along with the Triple Goddess necklace. She hated she could no longer wear her heels and envied the gorgeous pregnant women dancing at Carnival she saw on Tiktok. In truth she was stunned and awed. There were days where she didn’t feel beautiful, where she was scared Rio wouldn’t look at her with that usual stunned and bright-eyed look she always did– but of course Rio did. She swore Rio was always ready to jump her bones, and when she did have a rush of energy, moving around the house or adding to the nursery, her wife was there, leaving teasing touches, always trying to get Agatha into their bedroom… and it worked every time. 

But then there were the little moments that always outweighed the rest. The moments where Rio held Agatha after sex, talking of their son’s future, going back and forth on names, laughing at the students wanting it to be a Shakespeare name. They argued over cribs, and every time Agatha found a crib her wife might like, Rio would shake her head and promise they had time. 

They spent quiet evenings talking to the baby, lighting up when he responded and Rio was impossibly more enraptured by their son– not that Agatha would ever admit, but he responded the most when Rio cooed at him. Her wife was so excited to hopefully feel their son, constantly having her hands on her stomach and begging him to kick a little harder for her to feel. During lunches and stolen moments at work, Rio would be in her office, fussing over her, making sure she was eating enough and talking to her stomach. Agatha couldn’t stop laughing the one time Lilia had walked in unannounced and caught Rio on her knees, talking rapidly in Spanish. Rio tried playing it off, trying to act like she wasn’t soft, but Lilia and Agatha melted. 

She loved the moments when she was by herself in the office, well she wasn’t by herself– more like mama and son time. Where she could freely hum to herself or sing softly while caressing her bump. He seemed to love the song she and Rio wrote all those years ago when they first realized they both practiced. It was something to pass the time during the hot summers, where Rio would play on her electric drum set and Alice would be there with her keyboard. But it was mainly Rio and her though, writing the Ballad of the Witches’ Road. When he was particularly fussy in her stomach, all she had to do was hum the melody and their son would still, hopefully falling asleep. 

She would speak softly to him, feeling him flutter in response, but she was hopeless when Rio was there. She swore the baby got so excited the moment he heard Mami. Normally, Agatha would have pushed Rio away from what she would call being weird. At first she thought it was weird to be constantly talking to stretched skin and muscles, but she really did melt when she realized her wife was building her connection with him. 

The final thing she loved about being pregnant was the fact that Rio could barely say ‘no’ to her. She loved watching the gears turn in her wife’s head, the panic in her eyes as she knew Rio wanted to tell her no, but her eyes would flick to her stomach and she would give in… except when it came to whatever the fuck she was hiding. Somehow her wife would barely bat an eye when she questioned her, lie on the spot, and then distract her easily. 

 

And somehow, today she got her wife on her knees in her office, the door closed, hopefully locked. She smirked, leaning back in her chair, pleasure flowing through her body as one hand gripped on the armrest while the other was on her belly. She stared down, just making out a head of black hair between her legs, the tongue warm and insistent. The sight alone sent a shiver of satisfaction through her. Agatha let her head tip back against the chair, her fingers tightening around the armrest as her other hand drifted lower, stroking over the swell of her belly. 

“Mmm, my love,” she murmured, her voice like honeyed silk, lazy and indulgent. “You really are so much sweeter when you’re on your knees.” 

Rio didn’t respond with words– only a low hum against her, the vibration making Agatha’s breath hitch. Her grip on the armrest turned vice-like as pleasure curled warm and slow in her stomach, spreading like wildfire. Her wife was relentless. The tongue against her was both worship and demand, devotion and control. A contradiction, just like her– all that quiet intensity, all that restraint, finally given over to this. 

Agatha smirked, watching the dark head between her thighs, watching the subtle shift of Rio’s shoulders as she moved, the careful way her hands stayed on Agatha’s legs, bunching up her purple skirt just enough to allow her to work. Her free hand slid forward, fingers threading through the inky strands, nails scraping just enough to make Rio groan against her. The sound sent another rush of pleasure through her, making her hips tilt forward, just a little.

She felt her wife’s tongue swirl around her folds, sucking lightly before her tongue was shoving its way and inside her. She bit down on her lip, stifling her moans as her wife worked, happily lapping at her from underneath her desk. The sounds were wet and wanton, and part of Agatha was still grounded enough to pray that the sounds didn’t reach outside her office, but the sex-induced part of her found the risk a little more sweet. 

Agatha’s breath hitched as her wife’s tongue moved deeper, unrelenting, drawing out sharp little gasps she barely managed to swallow down. Her fingers curled tighter in her wife’s hair, both anchoring and urging her on. She could feel Rio smirk against her, the slight curve of her lips almost teasing before she sucked harder, dragging a strangled moan from Agatha’s throat. 

“ Fuck ,” a breathless gasp left her lips. 

Her wife was always thorough– whether it was in the lecture hall, at home, or in between Agatha’s legs. She was methodical in everything she did, and this was no exception. Agatha writhed in her seat, thighs squeezing around Rio’s head, but the hands on her hips kept her down. The coil in her lower stomach was tightening. She was getting close, her eyes fluttering shut as she pulled her wife closer against her, needing a little more. 

“Dr. Harkness?” 

Agatha jumped, trying to sit up but Rio did not stop. Her tongue somehow pushed inside her deeper as she hummed against her, sending a tremor through her entire body. But Agatha had had to move, she threw her skirts over her wife’s head as she moved her chair forward, forcing her wife further under the desk. She barely was able to catch her breath as the head of the literature department came in, already talking her ear off, but she could barely hear him, everything in her trying to not jolt as Rio sucked on her clit. She quickly pushed her wife off of her, slowly tuning in to the boss that wasn’t between her legs.  

“You see? I don’t trust anyone else. I know you have your-er condition– but it would be a great help.” 

Agatha blinked, going to close her legs when they stopped thanks to a familiar head that moved its way back there. A hot tongue trailed against her thigh, making her breath hitch. She flicked at Rio’s head, trying to get her stop. She was pregnant, too sensitive, and the Dean of the faculty was about to get caught between her employee’s legs– granted her wife’s but still on work hours. Rio wasn’t stopping and she gasped, quickly covering it up by rubbing the side of her stomach and tapping three times on Rio’s head as their safe word. Her wife finally pulled away, but Agatha kept her hand on her head, not wanting Rio to hit it and give herself away. 

“Are you alright?” Dr. Jackson spoke up. 

She nodded slowly,  hoping her cheeks weren’t that flushed, clearing her throat, “Yes, the little one is moving a lot today.”

Their son was actually fast asleep. She started noticing during this planning period, he goes dormant. 

“You feel that?” he eyes wide, his lip raised upward in a poorly masked form of disgust. 

She rolled her eyes, giving him a pointed look, “Yes, I am literally growing a whole ass human inside me. Now, what do you want ?” 

She growled at the last part, leaning forward. 

He took a step back, “Um, actually, I will have Maximoff look at it. You just rest up.” 

Agatha raised an eyebrow, watching him scramble out of her office and the door slamming shut. She let out a breath, rolling the chair back pulling her skirt off her wife’s head. Rio sat there, a smirk on her face as she exaggerated licking around her glistening lips, but before Agatha could fully take a breath, hands were on her thighs again, a strong grip spreading them apart before she could even register the movement. 

“Rio–” 

Her wife didn’t care, instead she was interrupted by a sharp gasp leaving her lips as Rio dived back between her legs, tongue stronger, purposeful, greedy . Agatha’s breath hitched, one hand flying to grip the edge of the desk, the other tangling back into her hair, tugging hard. The moan went straight back to her core, Rio’s tongue circling around her folds, flicking at her clit before swallowing more of her arousal that gathered. Agatha let her head fall back, her hips lifting up, chasing her wife’s tongue. Rio’s response was sinful. She hummed, sucking audible, making her body shudder. Her tongue worked faster, deeper– deliberate in a way that made her thighs tremble. Her wife was drowning in it, her mouth dragging her higher, and higher– until she could barely think. 

Agatha bit down on her own knuckles to keep from crying out as the coil snapped. Her other hand fisted hard in Rio’s hair as her body arched, pleasuring cresting so violently, she nearly knocked over the stack of books on her desk. White-hot bliss seared through her, her body shaking with the force of it. 

Her wife guided her down slowly, easing her through it until her body melted into her chair. She closed her eyes as her body relaxed, the tension in her shoulders gone, the pain in her back easing, and her headache gone as a tongue cleaned her up tenderly. Just as Agatha regained her breath, a kiss was placed against her core, making her flinch. 

Her wife finally leaned back, but surprising even herself, Agatha was quicker, placing her hand behind her wife’s head before she could hit it against the desk. She smiled softly as Rio was a picture of calm– like she wasn’t just eating her wife out like a woman starved in the middle of a work day. Her wife reached up, grabbing tissues and cleaning her legs before moving her underwear back in place from the crease of her leg. Then her skirt was pulled down fully, Rio smoothing her hands over the bump. 

“Feeling relaxed, my love?” Rio spoke softly. 

Agatha arched an eyebrow at her, both of her hands landing on either side of her stomach, “You almost got us caught– you, the one who set the boundary that we should never have sex at work, you absolute freak.” 

Her wife’s face lit up, her eyes sparkling as she laughed, “Says the woman” she stood finally “who seduced me” Rio leaned over her, placing her arms on either side of her chair, blocking her in “and shoved me to the ground under her desk.” 

 Agatha couldn’t stop the smile on her face as her wife leaned forward, her nose brushing against hers. For a moment, the both seemed to forget their teasing banter, their noses rubbing softly against the other’s affectionately. The world faded and Agatha forgot they were in her office, in the middle of school day, right before their lunch. For now, she breathed in her wife’s scent, nuzzling into her, and softened when lips pressed against hers. To her dismay, the kiss wasn’t long, it was a tender press, lasting a little longer for something simple, but it was still sweet, still pouring all the love she knew her wife had for her. 

Her wife leaned back against the desk, her hands resting on her rounded stomach. 

“How’s our son?” she asked, her voice a gentle whisper. 

“Asleep,” Agatha replied with a smile, a hint of affection in her tone. “He usually sleeps during my planning period, but he never sleeps through a lecture.” 

She laughed softly, the sound light and warm. Her wife raised an eyebrow, her gaze softening as she considered the idea. 

“Well, that’s because he’s listening to you, mi amor ,” she teased, her lips curving into a mischievous grin. “I swear, if he comes out quoting Shakespeare–” 

Agatha jabbed her lightly in the ribs, a playful glint in her eyes. 

“Don't you dare start,” she warned with a chuckle. “I swear, if I hear one more student ask me the name of a Shakespearean character–” 

A sly smirk tugged at her wife’s lips, and her eyes sparked with that familiar mischief, the unmistakable sign that she was about to say something ridiculous. 

“ ‘To be, or not to be, that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind–’ ”

Before she could finish, Agatha’s fingers found the spot just beneath her ribs, causing her wife to squeal and pull back in surprise. 

“Okay, okay–” she gasped, laughing. “We won't name him Hamlet.” 

Agatha raised an eyebrow, arms crossing as she leaned in slightly. 

“Don’t you have a meeting soon, since you interrupted my planning hour?” she asked, a playful edge in her voice. 

Rio rolled her eyes, rubbing her side as she slowly stood. 

“Unfortunately,” she muttered, her voice dripping with mock annoyance. 

Agatha’s smirk softened into something tender as she kissed her wife’s lips, Rio’s hands gently moving back to her stomach, before cupping her face in her hands. Rio’s thumb gently traced Agatha’s cheek. 

“Thanks for the early lunch, cariño ,” she whispered, her voice full of affection. 

Agatha reached for her side again, but Rio was quick to dodge, her laugh filling the air as she found the mirror on the door and began adjusting her hair and makeup. 

“The keys are in your purse, cariño ,” Rio said softly, her tone quiet but full of care. “I have a meeting running late tonight, so Alice will drop me off around six.” She stepped back to her wife’s side, pressing another sweet kiss to her lips, “Text me if you need anything.”

Agatha’s eyes softened as she smiled, “Yes, my love. I’ll see you tonight."



***

 

Agatha felt the breath catch in her throat as tears welled up in her eyes. She couldn’t stop it, everything in her tightening up. Even her son stilled inside her as all of her fears were coming true, everything that once thought was impossible was slamming into her. She was about to walk into her final lecture of the day when she heard it, the gossiping students– she was used to it by now. Wide eyes always trained on her stomach whether she was teaching, walking in the halls, and especially when she and Rio took walks around the quad after eating their lunch together. But this was truly the thing she thought she would ever hear from a student’s mouth. 

“Yeah, I saw Dr. Vidal hugging some woman and no, it was not Dr. Harkness.” 

The other student gasped, “Are you for real? Hugging another woman? Dr. Vidal?”
 “No, yeah, I am for sure. I literally saw it earlier today and Dr. Vidal actually smiled. You know she never smiles unless she is with her wife.” 

That was all Agatha needed to hear. With that she turned on her heel, moving as fast as her pregnant body would allow her as tears blurred her vision. No one moved in her way and if they saw water pooling in her eyes, they didn’t say anything. 

The moment she stepped into her office, she slammed the door, locking it, and opening up her phone to cancel class on the spot before sinking to the ground, her back against the door. Her heart was in her head, pounding continuously and it made it hard to think as sobs wracked her body. She sucked in each breath, trying to control her breath as the baby moved now, sensing her distress, but she could barely bring herself to react to their little one– to their son– to Rio’s child growing inside her. 

Her suspicions were right then. Her wife was cheating on her– the love of her life foolishly caught by students. She could barely contain her sobs, muffling them by bringing her skirt up and crying into the fabric until she could cry no more. Her body went numb, slowly accepting that Rio really had grown tired of her. And for the first time in fifteen years, she felt like that little girl, scared to death under her mother’s care, undeserving of love, an abomination– something evil… because it was her fault. 

Why else would her wife cheat on her? 

It was all Agatha’s fault. 

 

Agatha didn’t know how she managed to drive home, but the next thing she knew she was on the couch, crying into a tub of ice cream balanced on her stomach as she dipped sour cream and onion chips into the tub. Agatha couldn’t even process thinking, let alone wrapping her head around what she had heard– all she knew was that she felt unlovable and somehow it was her fault.

And yet, when she heard the front door open, the tears dried in an instant, leaving only  blotchy trails as evidence she had been crying, but anger swallowed her like a blackhole as her wif– as Rio came in, hanging up her coat and kicking off her heels. Agatha sat up, on her feet just in time for Rio. 

“ Cariño, are you up?” she said just before she turned around. “I have a–” 

Rio froze, her eyes widening as she took in her wife’s appearance. Her chest was heaving, a single hand to her belly, to ease the little one inside her as she trembled. 

“How long?” she asked, her words breaking as she spoke, her breath shaking as the question burned her throat to even ask. 

The woman in front of her had the audacity to blink at her, mocking her as she rattled for an answer before deciding on, “ Mi amor, how long for what?” 

“Don’t you fucking dare play this with me!” Agatha snarled, her voice dripping in venom. “How long have you been fucking cheating? Who is she?” 

The woman in front of her stared at her as if she had announced she was a time traveller, “Agatha–”

“Don’t! For the past few weeks you have been secretive, disappearing, brushing me off when I ask what you are doing in that greenhouse– that I had made for you. And then today I heard you hugged another woman,” she snarled, her fists clenching so tight, her blunt nails were drawing blood. 

Rio didn’t move at first. She stood there, completely still , her face blank in a way that only masked the panic crawling up her throat. Then, slowly, carefully, she took a breath– too measured, too controlled, like she was forcing herself not to react. 

“ Mi amor ,” she said softly, stepping forward, her hands out like she was approaching a frightened animal. “I need you to listen to me, okay?” 

But Agatha wasn’t in the mood to be soothed, not when her entire body ached with betrayal, “Oh, now you want to talk? Now that I caught you– ” 

Rio flinched. A small thing, barely noticeable, but Agatha caught it. Not from guilt. Not from anger. But from something else– something anxious, frantic, barely concealed behind the gentleness in her voice. 

“Agatha,” Rio tried again, her tone patient but tight, her shoulders tense in a way they rarely ever were. “I swear to you, I have not cheated on you. Not now. Not ever.” 

“Then why have you been sneaking around?!” Agatha demanded, her voice wobbling despite herself. “Why are you hiding things from me? Why am I hearing that you were hugging someone else? Do you have any idea how that feels? Fifteen years together and when I am pregnant with your child– I hear this!” 

Something cracked in Rio’s expression. Just for a moment. Then, to Agatha’s shock, Rio exhaled a sharp breath– almost a laugh, but not a happy one. It was the sound of someone who had just realized how deeply they had messed up. 

“Oh, fuck,” Rio whispered, rubbing her hands over her face. “Oh my Gods, mi vida , I– ” 

She stopped, sucking in another breath, steadying herself before she reached for Agatha’s hands, carefully, gently. Agatha stiffened, but Rio didn’t let go. Instead, she squeezed, grounding her, anchoring her. 

“ Cariño ,” Rio murmured, looking at her with something so raw, so aching, that it made Agatha’s stomach twist. “The lunch meeting I had today? That was Hela. I didn’t even realize she went back to work at her university so soon after having Lilith. I was shocked and just hugged her. That’s it. That’s all it was.” 

Agatha’s lips parted, a hot tear finally falling down her face. Rio was quick to wipe it away, her eyes tender, her face softening as a single hand cupped her cheek as the other rested on her belly. Rio was trying to calm, trying to reassure her while internally, Agatha could see the panic and guilt suffocating her. 

“And the greenhouse– ” her voice wavered slightly, and Agatha could see the moment she debated saying it, see the panic still curling in the tightness of her jaw, the way she was trying so hard to stay calm, to keep Agatha from spiraling again. “I– mierda – I didn’t mean to make you think– Agatha, I wasn’t hiding anything bad, I was– I was just…building something.” 

She froze, her eyes widening as the woman wiped more of her tears away before pulling back, taking her hand. She allowed her wife to gently tug her towards the back door, “Come with me. Please, my love.” 

Agatha hesitated. But something about the way Rio’s fingers trembled slightly, the way her chest rose and fell just a little too quickly, made her follow. Her wife was trying not to rush, trying to be slow as if she would spook her like she was a doe. Rio helped her with her boots before placing her coat on her. She smashed her feet into her own boots, sliding into her coat before taking Agatha’s hand again, leading her into the cold evening air, through the snow, and to the greenhouse. Her wife’s hands trembled as she pushed open the door, leading them in. Together they stepped out of their shoes, throwing their coats to the ground before Rio turned to her, taking both of her hands. 

“I finished last night and this is your surprise tonight, but I promise it would have been more special than this,” Rio’s voice was soft, but the weight of it pressed deep into Agatha, sinking into the marrow of her bones. 

She swallowed hard, her breath coming in shallow bursts as her wife squeezed her hands, grounding her, steadying her. Then, with precise unrushed movement, her wife turned, guiding her deeper into the warmth of the greenhouse, passing the fragrant herbs, the azaleas, and the vines that twisted throughout the glass panels. And there, in the very heart of the greenhouse, was something that made Agatha freeze. Rio didn’t pull her any further, instead she stepped towards the large thing covered in a giant green table cloth. Brown eyes peaked back at her, and for a moment, Agatha caught sight of a prideful smirk before her wife ripped the table cloth away.

Agatha’s breath ceased for a moment, all the anger and confusion evaporating from her body as she stared. Because there, in front of her was a crib. Not just any crib, a handmade crib. One that was freshly polished and painted, carved with intricate details, each curve and line carefully etched into the wood like it had been shaped by love itself. Each of the side panel posts, meant to keep the baby in, were round, no sharp edges, but the outside of them had protective runes, each painted a mix of green and purple to highlight them from the black, but the headboard and footboard were intricate. Life-like vines wrapped along the framework. The headboard had the triple moon, small carvings of ravens spilling out from moons. The footboard had a tree similar to the tattoo on Rio’s back. The roots wrapped down the feet of the crib, the branches blending into the posts on top. 

Her fingers hovered over the wood, tracing the delicate vines, the way the carved ravens seemed to take flight from the crescent moons. It was stunning. It was theirs . Her throat felt tight, too many emotions tangled in her ribs making it impossible to breathe. Agatha’s knees nearly buckled, but Rio’s arms were there, slipping around her waist, holding her steady. 

“Do you like it?” her voice was softer now, hesitant.

Agatha couldn’t speak. She just nodded, a small, jerky movement, her fingers still ghosting over the wood. Rio exhaled, her body molding behind hers as her face nuzzled into the back of her neck, her hands resting on her stomach, before she rested her chin on her shoulder. 

“I– I wanted to make something with my own hands for him. Something that would keep him safe, always,” she swallowed. “I should have told you. I– I was just trying to make it perfect first, and then– ”

Agatha barely heard the rest of the sentence, because something was moving out of the corner of her eye. Her head whipped around– the subtle shift in the shadows near the base of the crib, something tiny… something alive. 

She stiffened, “Rio.” 

“Hm?” her wife hummed against her shoulder, her arms still snug around her waist, hands rubbing her stomach. 

“Is that what I think it is?” 

Silence. 

Both of them stared as a tiny fluffy ball of white and tan that hopped out from the shadows. Agatha felt her eyes widen. It was tiny, so unbelievably small— a baby. A small light brown nose twitched up at her, black eyes wide and curious as impossibly small floppy ears perked up. 

Her wife grew rigid behind her, fingers briefly clenched against the stomach, the way her breath caught in her throat before she slowly exhaled. She could practically hear the gears turning behind those big brown eyes. 

“ Mierda ,” Rio muttered under her breath. “How did you get out?”

Agatha turned slowly, one eye on the ground to keep the creature in sight, “Rio.” 

 “I can explain,” her wife shifted nervously. 

“ Oh, can you?” she bit back, folding her arms and arching a sharp brow.  

Rio froze in front of her, her eyes flicking down to the baby bunny, completely unaware of the tension cracking in the air.  It gave a tiny stretch before hopping closer, its soft nose tickling at Agatha’s feet. 

“He was abandoned,” her wife started slowly, her voice soft as her eyes remained on the baby bunny nuzzling against Agatha who fought to keep up that she was angry. “During one of your three am cravings, when I didn’t come home until three hours later and you were pissed at me the next morning– it was because of him. I walked out of the gas station and some teenager ran up to me and shoved a blanket into my arms and when I opened it, there he was. He was just old enough to leave his mother, but he was so frail. I rushed him to the vets, got his shots, and took him home. He is supposed to be in his pen, but he keeps escaping and eating my plants.” 

“Rio,” she sighed, pinching the bridge of her nose and closing her eyes. “You got a rabbit, while I’m pregnant.” 

Her wife cleared her throat, “He’s very small.” 

“Oh, fantastic, that make everything just fucking peachy–” 

“And he’s soft, too,” Rio added quickly as if that would help her case. “And he doesn’t eat much, and very quiet–”

Agatha pointed a finger at her, her other hand cradling her belly as their son moved, “ You disappeared for weeks, let me spiral into a full hormonal meltdown, and now you’re telling me that it was for a crib and a fucking rabbit?!”

Her wife had the nerve to bite her lip, looking uncharacteristically sheepishly. She looked nothing like the power dean of the Westview University– just a wife getting scolded by their wife. 

“In my defense, I knew you would be mad if I just brought home a bunny without something to soften the blow and I had already started the crib…”

Agatha stared at her before staring down at the bunny, and back to her wife. Her eyes sharpened, her firsts curling, everything ready to lash out at her wife for being so fucking stupid– for letting her think the worse– and then have the audacity to just be worried when Agatha finally accuses her instead of being mad or hurt. Before she could ponder on how her wife isn’t hurt by Agatha thinking that, a soft little rumble echoed in the silence. 

Her eyes darted down as something in Agatha’s chest cracked. She didn’t even think. She just bent down, albeit, struggling a little, and scoped the fluff ball into her arms. The bunny let out more purrs, nuzzling into her chest and fuck, all the was gone as she burried her face into pure fluff. 

“I cannot believe you.”

Her wife, her stubborn, determined, strategist of a wife, took this as an open opportunity to step forward, hands back on her stomach. 

“You love me,” she murmured, pressing a kiss to her forehead as she inhaled the bunny’s soft scent. 

Agatha lifted her head, correcting her, “I tolerate you.” 

Her wife smiled, pressing a kiss to her lips and then to her nose, “And you always wanted a bunny.” 

Her heart soared, tears ready to slip from her eyes from just that one sentence because that… that was true. She never explicitly said it, maybe one or twice the words came out, but they were fleeting thoughts or just a whispered daydream. Usually, if she and Rio were somewhere and there were bunnies, they always stopped. Agatha would always look, would always stare the moment she saw the little creatures, but it was Rio who always dragged them to their enclosures– at first she had thought it was because Rio loved looking at them too, but now… Rio had noticed her longing stares, the way she would prefer anything with little bunnies on them. She had remembered, had truly seen her– truly loved her. 

Agatha nuzzled back into the bunny’s fur, smiling as it purred contently in her arms as she turned back to the crib before her, trying to hide her tears. Her shoulders were shaking though, and no amount of bunny fur was going to muffle her ragged breaths. 

“Gods, you absolute fucking idiot.” 

Her wife laughed, wrapping her arms back around her and kissing the side of her head repeatedly. They stayed like that for a while, Rio swaying them as Agatha held not one, but two babies close to her, staring down at the crib her wife made for their son. 

The moment she calmed down, the bunny was asleep in her arms, his face burrowing into the crook of her arm. Her wife was still pressing kisses to her head, hands easing the flutters. Then her lips came by her ear, her voice hesitant and soft. 

“So does that mean we can keep Señor?” 

Agatha stepped out of her grip, “I am not calling him that.” 

 

Later that night, Rio had managed to bring the crib into the nursery, setting it up next to the rocking chair before heading back out into the cold. Agatha watched her wife bring in a black bunny enclosure, a few blankets and toys, a litter box, and a bed. She had sighed, shaking her head, deciding not to question this as she held him closer to her chest. 

 

Agatha laid on the couch with the bunny lounging contently on top of her round belly, her wife behind her, pressing continuous kisses to her lips, each one of them longer and longer as Agatha’s hand tangled into black hair and she tried bending her head back more, needing to deepen the kiss, to spell out her apologies and her love. 

But something more in her cracked as she pulled away slowly, “I’m sorry, my love. I shouldn’t have–” 

“It’s alright, mi amor. I know you hate surprises and I should have told you what I was doing,” Rio cut her off, placing another longing kiss to her nose. 

Agatha smiled softly, “Well, this surprise wasn’t horrible.”

They both laughed, Rio leaning back and pulling her wife closer as the bunny nuzzled into her stomach more, his little paws scratching at her. Agatha smirked as her wife settled her against her chest better. 

“Señor Scratchy.” 

“Mm?” her wife hummed. 

“Señor Scratchy. We can call him Señor Scratchy.”

Rio chuckled, nodding her head, “Welcome to the family, Señor Scratchy.”

 

 

 

Chapter End Notes

DAMN

not all of you guessing the surprise like wtf- i hope Senor Scratchy was a curve ball.

ANYWAY

next chapter, a certain cunt makes an appearance 👹

💚💜

Chapter 22: Lessons in Love

Chapter Summary

“I ought to have killed you the moment you left my body, and I won’t allow another mistake like that to happen again.”
Something blinded Eva for a moment, a reflection– a ray from the sun hitting off something metal– something silver… and for a moment, the world froze. Eva’s eyes widened, her strides weak as she just reacted, unsure how she was going to get there faster, but Eva wasn’t needed. Vidal pushed through, a blur of black and green, cutting through the weight and severity– and she lunged

 

OR :

a lot shit happens

Chapter Notes

This chapter is dedicated to @paybacks_a_witch on TikTok aka Scarlett_witch15 as a thank for plotting with me and reading my random rants that I text you with my ideas.

Please also thank her for this chapter as we spent a couple hours plotting this scene and she helped figure out what was best for Evanora among other factors.

Thank @Xoi_xoxo for the Spanish translations for this chapter 💜

Buckle up for 9.9k words my loves.

can you count how many different cameos I have in here ?

Additionally, can you guess what I am ? French ? American ? British ? or Russian ? Lesbian ? (if i haven't already said...?)

Will answer comments soon !

ALSO, the Shakespeare quotes are from Folger Shakespeare Library- as a lit minor, it is my best friend

Thank you for votes on twitter my loves 💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

 

Evanora 

 

Evanora had spent her life crafting perfection, each move precise, each achievement another jewel in her crown. A childhood devoted to study led her to Oxford, where she graduated early, brilliant and untouchable. She seized her father’s empire, bent it to her will, and became one of the most powerful CEOs in the world. 

Wealth? Endless. 

Influence? Unmatched. 

She even found a decent enough man to procreate with, securing her legacy. And she got exactly what she wanted– at first. 

A thriving dynasty, an heir born to her– a gay thot daughter…

 

So much for perfection. 

 

She was so close to having it all, so close and then her husband’s sperm had to be that weak to give her a daughter– she could have handled a gay son, possibly , that was more tolerable than the filth that came out of her body. 

If she were ever put through any of the three Trials by Ordeal, only death would hear her say that Agatha was a perfect child at first. Agatha was beautiful with eyes so stunning, she was shocked when they first shined up at her. Her daughter was a gentle baby, barely cried, barely made a fuss. The only time she screamed was when she needed her diaper changed. It was immediate too. She was an intelligent little thing, picking up on her movements, bright inhumane eyes following her everywhere. She was six months when she started crawling, seven months when she started walking, and eight months old when she said her first word. Her child was exceptional, surpassing all the expectations by doing everything earlier than what the doctor said. 

When she was pregnant with Agatha, she was small. She didn’t start showing until a late six months. She was worried. Not understanding why her baby was so small, but the doctors assured her it was only how her body was reacting as a new mother. Agatha kicked a lot though, dancing on her bladder, aiming for her ribs and every fucking organ in her body. That part wasn’t fun, but when she was born, she was a perfect bill of health. 

Everything was fine as she grew until she reached elementary school. That’s when everything changed. Agatha was malicious, but only Evanora saw her in action. She pushed kids on the playground, playing soccer, she watched her daughter subtly foul– stepping on the backs of opponents heels (flat tiring), tripping them when the ref wasn’t looking, and Agatha’s signature move? 

Well, if you don’t understand the rules of soccer, know that you are allowed to push, as long as it is shoulder to shoulder. For Agatha, she found joy in dipping her shoulder down, hooking it into the opponent’s side before lifting them up and sending them sprawling.  

 

(Soccer players, you would have hated me because I may or may not be taking from experience when I used to play) 

 

She’d slap, scratch, pull at other girls’ hair, and as she got older, she got bolder. She snapped two girl’s ankles– told both of them to ‘walk it off’– one knee, and Evanora was pretty sure Agatha also armbarred someone during a fight that she started. And this was only when Evanora was present during the few games she came too. 

But it didn’t stop there. No, Agatha was in the orchestra and sure, call violinists bitchy, self-righteous, snobby– 

(don’t come at me, I’m a violinist) 

Agatha was worse. She would snap violinists’ strings, cut bow hair, steal the screw from the bow– but that had nothing to do with the fact that Evanora would punish Agatha if she didn’t get the solo or be the concertmaster. It was nothing to do with the fact that if Agatha came home with anything less than a ninety-five, she was forced into their library with no dinner, no breaks, and if Evanora decided to give her daughter the time, a ruler to her knuckles, maybe a slap across the face if her rings were on, or a kick to her ribs if she was feeling so very benevolent.  

Evanora didn’t know when she started punishing her daughter like this, but the moment it started, she couldn’t stop. There was something so satisfying seeing the fear in those large blue eyes the moment Agatha walked into the house, the way all of that– fuck Evanora never had a word for it. Her daughter carried something, some type of strength, of intelligence, that infuriating self-assurance that Evanora never had– and she loved watching it crumble underneath her. She had some much fun watching her daughter shake, but at the same time she was molding her, forming her to be cold as she was, for her rise and taking the Harkness name– no, Evanora never changed her name when she married. She was never particularly interested in men, just needed attachment for her empire– so when her husband died with symptoms of ARS– vomiting, lymphopenia, anorexia– no one thought to look for polonium-210 poison or point fingers at the poor suffering pregnant wife. Case unsolved, put in the vault to collect dust. 

Agatha was slowly learning though, becoming her perfect daughter– until she was sixteen. Evanora was meant to be gone on a business trip for a week. She ended up coming home earlier, finding clothes thrown around the house, a trail of them leading up to Agatha’s room. They weren’t just Agatha’s, but they also weren’t a man’s. Evanora didn’t care, she stormed right into her daughter’s room, slamming the door open, and she almost fainted at the sight– another girl between her daughter’s legs. Evanora saw red and well, the girl was thrown out of the room, and Agatha was beaten until the bruise was too dark to conceal, so she was forced to be kept home. 

Yet, that didn’t stop her daughter from doing something so unholy. No, Evanora caught the way Agatha would stare at other girls, the way she would flirt when she thought no one was watching, the moments during her charity events or other banquets, she was also dragging her daughter by the hair away from some poor girl who was pinned to a wall by her, or in some random bedroom– Jesus Christ it was disgusting. So at seventeen, she shipped her off to a  conversation camp and at eighteen, it seemed to have worked. Agatha finished high school the first of class. Evanora got her into Oxford, ready to ship her daughter off, and prepare her to take over the business. 

 

The morning after graduating, everything changed. Agatha was gone, most of her clothes missing. Valuables gone, except for her phone which was wiped clean. Evanora tore through her room, finding all of her suitcases gone, all of her bags– Agatha was just gone. Ran away, and Evanora could do nothing. She was eighteen and she just finished high school. She called Oxford and they informed her Agatha withdrew from their program. She tried asking her neighbors– asking the one weird Italian woman who lived down the street– she was sure her daughter was Agatha’s best friend, but the Italian woman always refused to speak English. Stupid plebeian. 

 

So, she lost her for a couple years. That was until she was invited to speak at Harvard for their business program. She was walking on campus when caught sight of wild long brunette hair. She turned and right there was her daughter. She was beautiful, stunning… happy. Evanora had never seen her face lit up the way it was. Her smile was soft, raw– so real . Arms were wrapped around her and behind stood a woman, raven dark hair, sun kissed skin, and eyes dark and intense, but they were soft as the woman drew Agatha closer against her, saying something to her. Agatha had turned, laughing loudly, her arms thrown around the woman’s neck. And then it fucking happened, they kissed, long and languidly, woman’s hands on her hips, drawing her daughter closer. 

Evanora gagged, her blood boiling watching the scene. She stood frozen, her mind struggling to process the sight before her. Agatha, her Agatha, who had been formed and raised into something respectable, something obedient, something perfect, was standing there– untamed, radiant, and utterly wrapped in sin. She wanted to scream, to storm across the courtyard and rip her daughter away from the woman’s grasp. But she knew, deep down, that Agatha would not come willingly. That smile, that ease in her posture– it was real. Agatha was no longer the rigid, controlled thing Evanora had molded. She had slipped through her fingers like smoke, and no amount of grasping would bring her back.

Instead, she had turned sharply on her heel, teeth grinding, fury crackling through her bones like a storm. She couldn’t confront her here, not when she was about to give a presentation. So she waited her time, hired someone to watch her daughter, and found out she lived with the woman named Rio Vidal. Agatha was deep in her fantasy, blinded by the manipulation of this woman, obsessed with the woman– but Rio, some random bitch from Puerto Rico– was protective, possessive even. Her touch was always on her daughter, her eyes always watchful, and yet of course, her daughter flourished under it, under the attention of being wanted, but if Rio knew who her daughter truly was, she would leave her. 

Evanora tried, oh did she try, but when Evanora first met Rio, well it was an accidental run in. She found her daughter’s apartment, the one she was staying in over the summer, figuring the woman would have gone back to Puerto Rico. She pulled up to the rundown complex, slipping past the desk attendant and walking up the stairs until she made it to the seventh floor. 

She reached forward to the handle, finding it turning until it opened, creaking as it went. She stepped inside and immediately regretted it. The apartment was small. A kitchen to her right, and a small dining table as well. A small couch was on the other side, facing the television, and on the far wall was a bed… an occupied bed where the bodies were moving slowly together. 

The raveness was over top of her daughter, her back on display and to her luck, the covers were over her waist. Their lips were locked, Agatha fingers clawing at the woman’s back as a single arm disappeared between them. 

Then, there was screaming. 

The raveness pulled the covers over them, but didn’t move from her place over Agatha, almost as if she were protecting her. 

Finally, the next thing she knew the raveness was standing over her and slamming the door in her face with the fatal click of a lock following. 

 

So she calculated that wrong. 

 

The next time she ran into Rio, it was by chance. She was in her home town of New York City when she entered her usual café. A woman brushed past her, a little too quickly, throwing her off balance. She caught herself just in time to look up into the darkening eyes of the woman. The raveness looked right through her, as if she were nothing. Rio had simply raised an eyebrow, clutching her coffee close to her chest, nodded, and walked around her. She had never been ignored like that. It was… wrong. 

 

Things unnecessarily escalated when Evanora found out her daughter was about to be married to the woman years later. Worst of all, she didn’t even find out through her daughter, nor through social media. She was at dinner with a few, hopefully new, clients, professors at Salemite’s University when one of them asked if she was related to Agatha Harkness. Evanora was shocked. There was no way her daughter got into Salmite’s University. 

 

She only nodded and gave a snakelike smile, asking with a voice full of honey, “Why, yes. How is my dear daughter?”

The professors’ smiles grew brightly, both of them sitting up, more engaged than ever in the conversation, “She’s a wonderful student. She is my advisee–” 

“Rio is mine– they make quite a beautiful couple.” 

“You must be so excited for the wedding.” 

It took everything in Evanora not to spit out the wine she had just sipped. Her fingers tightened around the glass, nodding politely as the professors continued with their voices filled with nothing but admiration and awe.  

“The rings are insanely gorgeous. I know they had been saving so much for them– but I do have to ask, Mrs. Harkness, why didn’t you help with the rings?” Dr. Carvallo, the literature professor, asked, raising her eyebrows. 

Evanora stiffened, freezing as the professors’ grew calculating. 

“Yes, Rio was so worried the jeweler would turn them away when they said they didn’t have the money. I just figured, a woman of your standards and as a mother, would gladly step in for her daughter,” the other professor, Dr. Dylan leaned forward. 

She swallowed hard, realizing why they had accepted her invitation. She needed this partnership with Salmite’s University – one so could kick her daughter out and Rio, for that matter, and two– her company would absolutely flourish with a partnership with the world’s number one university. But now, it was a trap. They had been skittish to accept her invitation and it all because of her spoiled insolent brat of a daughter. 

“Well, I have always taught my daughter independence. She needs to learn how to carry herself before leaning on me,” she recovered quickly, or so she thought. 

Dr. Dylan cocked her head, blonde hair falling to the side.
