Rioallalong_719 💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

 

 

Rio



Rio was going to fucking die tonight. Her body buzzed with the faint power of electric hunger, her very veins becoming the heated wires– if that’s how electricity worked. She didn’t know. Perhaps it was just a witch with purple strings of power encasing in her a spell that was too tight. A sliver of her mind worried about her dark green pants– were they dark enough? 

Gods, please let them be dark enough.

Agatha had already walked out of the room, short heels clicking like the drums of death on polished floors, her hips swaying, knowing exactly where Rio’s eyes were as she made her way to the elevator. Rio grabbed her wife’s purse and followed, her eyes betraying her, staring at the way the dress wrapped around her wife’s figure. She wondered if Arachne cowered before her wife, drawing her silk, worrying if a single thread out of line, Agatha would pluck her legs off, one by one . Her hands reached out, having a mind of their own as the succubus stopped before the doors, pressing the button. The moment Rio’s hands landed on the curve of her ass, her wife’s lips formed into that grin. The grin that should have come with a warning label and a safe word, but Rio knew her wife. Agatha Harkness-Vidal never played safe, especially when her prey would gladly burn for her. 

The lights above the elevator lit up as it made its slow ascent, rising to cage Rio in with a feral predator in sheep’s clothing. Her hands explored her wife’s figure, around her ass, to her hips where she was used to feeling bone– now she moved along the rise of where their son grew. Before her hands could move any further, a small chime alerted her to the doors opening where her wife was already stepping in. Rio followed as if Agatha had the handle of her leash, dragging her into the mirrored tin-can. Agatha turned before the doors even closed, but it wasn’t a kiss that met her. It was a claiming. Rio’s back met the mirror with enough force she would be shocked if the glass didn’t crack. Hands were gripping her jaw, the curve of her skull, pulling her in with a kind of urgency that could shatter bone. Lips waltzed down her neck like they belonged– they did– teeth grazing the tender skin, tongue flicking in a cruel apology. 

“Eighteen floors,” Agatha murmured against her, her breath molten lava. “Eighteen chances to beg me for mercy.”

Rio couldn’t respond. She couldn’t think. Her brain had melted from the heat as her breath hitched, her spine arching as Agatha pinned her to the mirrored wall. One hand dragged along her waist, slow, deliberate, leaving heat in its wake. The other was firm beneath her jaw, keeping her still as that mouth– those lips– bit down hard enough to leave a mark. 

Then the doors closed, a slight jolt as they began their descent. Rio fought to open eyes as her wife attacked her neck, trying to keep her pathetic whimpers quiet as from her hooded eyes, she watched the elevator ding with each floor they passed. And suddenly, eighteen floors felt like both eternity and finity. Her hands gripped her wife’s waist tightly, unsure if she would be able to stand without her. She trembled, soft sounds betraying her and leaving her lips without permission as her wife continued on her neck. And then, ding! The elevator not only tolled the arrival of the lobby, but Rio’s absolute lack of dignity as something slick, soft, and searing trailed up her neck and Rio whimpered when her wife pulled away, walking out like nothing at all had happened. 

 

But that was just the first offense. 

 

The second? 

 

It came in a long sleek black vehicle. 

A limo. 

A fucking limousine ordered like they were some incredible actrices or queens or some type of rich assholes– which they were now– to be taken to Da Andrea in Greeenwich Village, like they weren’t just two wives, mad and completely enchanted by the other. Agatha leaned into her, Rio wrapping an arm around her like it was instinct– supporting the swell of her belly, steady and sure– when a hand landed, innocent at first, on her thigh. Rio stiffened though, her instincts kicking into four wheel drive as she knew what was coming next as their driver turned on Rio’s favorite band : Sleep Token. She tried to ignore her wife’s hand, aiming a snarky comment about dramatics and birthdays, but her wife’s hand was already on the move, smoothing, gripping… creeping higher until, just as the limousine rolled to a stop, Agatha’s palm pressed firmly against her. Rio jolted, her hips bucking forward as her breath caught. Everything in her coiled as she chased the friction. Before she could speak, before could scold or whimper or plead, her wife had already pulled away, stepping out of the limo with the kind of smile that could make a cosmic entity fold. A soft, sweet thing that had no right belonging to a woman who had killed her with touch alone. 

Rio cleared her throat, composed herself, and followed her wife out into the churn of the city. Noise crashed into her– car horns, shounting strangers, the hideous order that burned her nostrils. Right, this is why they both hated New York. But then her gaze lifted and the restaurant stood alive with color and music, every inch of its wooden exterior wrapped in blooms of pink, climbing vines wrapping it in its own type of love. Inside, almost every table was full, couples’ laughter blended in the rich, but soft Italien music that changed the ambience. For a moment Rio believed they were no longer in Lower Manhattan, but rather in Italy together, one of the legs of their honeymoon on their way to Greece. Inside, she could make out the floral energie and fuck, she was falling in love all over again with the woman before her.

Blue eyes glittering up at her, Rio’s heels exaggerating their high difference only by a little, but it was enough. Her wife stepped forward, her expression oddly soft as she fixed the lapels of Rio’s blazer. Rio’s own hands rested on her wife’s stomach, smiling as her wife then fixed her necklace before kissing her cheek with infuriating sweetness. Agatha took her left hand in hers, the lamps and little fire lights flickered around them, making their wedding bands sparkle together. A hostess held the door open for them, greeting them as they stepped in only to meet a line of couples waiting impatiently before the stand. At the front, a woman was arguing with the host– loud, nasal, righteous– a fucking Karen. 

“For fuck’s sake,” Agatha breathed out a little too loudly, but it seemed that the people in front them agreed, letting out their own sighs as the Karen continued. 

Rio drowned her out, stepping behind her wife and wrapping her arms around her waist. And for a flicker of a second, she remembered seeing something on TikTok about helping her wife while they were standing. Slowly, Rio pressed her body closer against her wife’s back, her hands moved underneath her stomach and lifted it slightly. Peering down at her wife, she watched as Agatha’s eyes closed in relief, her hands moving over hers as she took some of the strain off her wife’s lower back. Her head rested back on Rio’s shoulder, her lips forming something softer, away from the teasing from minutes ago. 

But Rio should have known that wouldn’t last. No, her wife was a terror, a she-demon– a seductive succubus ready to eat her alive and Rio– her willing victim, who would welcome Death with open arms. Fuck, she would actually fight Death and make her trade places so she could have the succubus for eternity.

In life and in death, Agatha Harkness would be hers. 

That teasing smirk crashed in hard, her wife not even bothering to hide it before Rio felt a subtle shift of her hips pressed back against hers. She sucked in breath, her body growing still as she continued to gently hold her wife’s stomach. Her wife didn’t stop. Pushing back against, her legs shifting where one her ass cheeks grinding right into her. On the outside, Rio looked like a tender wife, trying to help her pregnant wife who shifted uncomfortably– if anyone knew the truth? Her wife was truly a demon. 

Rio leaned down, nuzzling her nose into her wife’s neck, taking in the fresh scent of lavender, basil, and something distinctly Agatha, “ Cariño, if you keep this up, I will take you right here, against the host stand, in front of every bored couple in Lower Manhattan.” 

Her wife only hummed, her eyebrow raising as they shuffled forward with the line, the Karen grumbling past them, muttering something about being a “paying customer.” But it fell on deaf ears as Agatha allowed her weight to fall a little more on her wife. 

“Mmm, would you now?” she whispered, her lips now crazing her cheek, a picture of innocence. “Guess we’ll just have to find out if your thirty-fourth birthday is worth the misdemeanor.” 

Rio groaned in defeat, shifting behind her wife to try to stop the pressure against her. But it was no use. Agatha was already winning, taking the high ground. So, she stayed there, taking the torture, but making sure her wife felt the little bit of relief from the pressure of their son. When they finally reached the stand, Agatha interlaced their fingers a little tighter as she lifted her head at the hostess. 

“Reservation for two under Harkness-Vidal.” 

The girl swallowed nervously, nodding quickly, “Of course, Mrs. Harkness-Vidal. Sorry for the wait. You can follow Matthew as he will be your waiter for tonight.” 

A college student stepped forward, but Rio barely glanced at him, allowing her wife to pull out of her hold, keeping their hands locked together as they were led to a cozy corner, kissed by the flickering light of twin candles and the fragrant twist of lemon trees growing in pots nearby. To Rio’s shock, the table wasn’t like something Agatha would normally have them sat at. Her wife would normally have them seated side by side, but not as close as the one restaurant they went to made for couples in December. No, this table created a 36”x40” divide between them– flaunting something that Rio couldn’t directly touch or have in the moment. It was another ploy, another play– another way to leave Rio extra sexually frustrated. She held back a sigh as she moved before the waiter could, helping her wife sit down before taking her seat, a little more roughly than she should as she yanked out the chair, sitting across from the demon. The demon’s lips curled into that vixen grin as she sat back, both hands on her stomach, a painting of maternal grace as the waiter cleared his throat. 

“Everything has been pre-ordered, yes?” 

Her wife nodded, refusing to take her eyes off of her prey. Rio gulped nervously, wondering what her wife had done. Part of her wondered if she knew Rio would look at the menu, and try to estimate the time it would take before they were back in their hotel room. But maybe, maybe, if their order was already in place, time would go faster. 

“And we are still going with the beverage choice of water? We have a nice white wine,” he turned to Rio and for a moment, Rio’s eyebrows raised in surprise as he only addressed her for alcohol. 

“No, thank you,” she waved him off, but her wife cocked her head. 

“My love,” her wife’s voice was fucking honey. “It’s your birthday, you should have wine if you want some.”

She shook her head, “ Mi amor, we already made a deal– I don’t drink when you can’t.”

Her wife rolled her eyes as another waiter set bread, oil, and water down in front of them and they were left in the low light ambience. They reached forward in sync, Rio interlacing her left hand– her non-dominant hand– with Agatha’s right, her wife’s non-dominant hand. 

“Happy birthday, my love,” Agatha’s voice was quiet, almost too quiet where the overlapping conversations made it hard to hear. 

She was uncharacteristically soft for a moment, Rio couldn’t stop the bright smile that stretched her cheeks. She felt as if she was back on their first date, how nervous she was not to fuck it up, but also not able to stop staring at the woman who sat in her arms as she pointed out the stars. Their second date then became more vivid, and red threatened to give away the memory to her wife. Rio had taken her to a nice sushi restaurant, and for some reason, she was just as nervous as the first date. Rio had successfully embarrassed herself. She had tripped over her own feet, barely catching herself as Agatha had tried pretending not to notice, but she covered her mouth, holding back her laugh poorly. Then, Rio had spilled her tea everywhere, shattering the glass, sending boba all over the floor. Agatha had laughed– not the one with her favorite snort, but damn well close to it. 

She laughed until her eyes shone like twin lapis catching in the firelight, and then without missing a beat, she had leaned across the table, taken Rio’s stained hand in her own, and said, “Oh, you’re so cute when you’re flustered. ” 

And Rio knew– had known right then and there, there was no turning back. Now, years later, Agatha held her again, differently and maybe even more tenderly. She was glowing from her pregnancy, her hair shining, more voluminous, running down the front of her chest. Her skin was clear, her lips laced with that knowing smile, as the free hand rubbed circles on her round stomach. The silence between them wasn’t silent. It was laced and brimming with a love that grew over the years between the chaos they had faced, the nights where they laid together too exhausted for sleep, but unable to talk– just holding each other, and to the nights where their bodies told each other how much they loved the other. 

Rio swallowed, trying to remember how to breathe under the weight of her wife’s gaze. Her wife was studying her, but that glint had never left, and despite the aching, loving moment between them– Rio knew the succubus was lurking. 

“You’re plotting,” she whispered. 

Her wife’s lips curled into that siren’s smile, eyes half-lidded and wickedly amused, “Always.” Agatha took her hand back, reaching for her glass of water. “Almost ten years of marriage, my love– fifteen years of being together. I have to keep you on your toes.” 

Rio rolled her eyes, going to reach for her own glass, before something made her freeze. She looked up, watching her wife drink, both of them forgoing the plastic straws. A stream of water ran from her wife’s chin. Time slowed for a moment, and Rio watched as it dripped from her sharp chin, down her check, just a hair away from the red lipstick mark still staining her neck and down defined collarbones. She was entranced, almost as if she was a kid in the backseat of a car, betting which of the two raindrops would win the race down the window. But this was only one, and she wondered how reasonable it was to be jealous of the water that caressed her wife’s chest before running right between her breasts. Her wife raised a knowing eyebrow at her as Rio cleared her throat, unable to tear her eyes away as Agatha took a napkin, reaching into her dress to dry it. 

Mierda, she is trying to kill me , Rio thought as her tongue pushed against the inside of her cheek. 

Before either of them could speak again, their first course arrived– two plates placed in front of them with the food looking more like art than for dining. It was a small portion, and Rio figured– and hoped– that there would be another course… but she also regretted it. Because it meant more time in the restaurant– more time waiting to take her wife to bed. 

“For you, our ravioli di crostacei – lobster, crab, salmon filled ravioli mixed in our special saffron sauce,” the waiter smiled before turning to her wife. “And the gnocchetti di patate : potato gnocchi, gorgonzola sauce, and arugula– completely cooked as requested, Mrs. Harkness-Vidal. I will be back with your second course later.” 

Great , there was a second course . 

But then Rio finally registered what he said, the meal– it was something Rio had mentioned in passing about craving months ago– reminiscing of the time she had it in Italy with her wife, and Agatha, of course, had remembered. She blinked at the plate, her throat tightening. 

“You really didn’t have to do all of this,” she murmured, her voice quiet. 

Agatha took her hand again, “Of course I did. It’s your birthday and we are about to have a baby. What else were you expecting?” 

Rio huffed as slid the food in her mouth, humming at the taste as her heart fluttered, “A quiet dinner. Maybe some cake.” 

“Oh, there’ll be cake,” her wife smirked. “But it won’t be the kind you eat with a fork.” 

Rio choked, her body stiffened as she tried forcing the ravioli down. Her wife just lifted her glass like it was a fluke of champagne in a toast, feigning innocence like a devout nun. Rio hit her chest, swallowing harshly as her wife just smirked, and they knew this game was far from over. 

Blue eyes glinted at her, like a sapphire as the main jewel of a necklace– sharp, flashy, and something mystical– enough to allure someone close. She set her glass down with the grace of a predator wrapped in silk, already knowing all the weak points of her prey and reached for her utensils, fingers curling around the fork like she was plucking a forbidden fruit. 

“Cariño, ” Rio murmured, meaning to scold her wife, but it came out as a plea. 

 Her wife didn’t look up, only focused on her food– but her eyes told a different story. Her lashes low, her eyes hooded as she deliberately dragged the edge of the spoon down her plate, gathering the round gnocchi and sauce into a neat display upon it. She took a bite, a soft noise leaving her lips that belonged more to the bedroom than it did dinner. Rio squeezed her legs unconsciously, swallowing hard as she stared wide eyed at her wife, watching as a drip of the gorgonzola gathered at the corner of her wife’s lips. Taking her pointer finger and wiping the dot, her tongue circled the tip of her finger, those azure eyes locked on hers before she took her finger completely in her mouth, her cheeks hollowing as she sucked. 

Rio stared, unable to tear her gaze away, watching those lips before a loud clatter silenced the restaurant, startling herself as the fork in her hand bounced off her plate and rattled on the tile floor. Her face heated in an instant as her wife chuckled under her breath, a waiter already handing her a clean fork and taking the one from the floor as the chatter started up again. 

“Are you alright, sweetheart?” fingers tangled with hers, stopping her from burying her face into her hands to hide from the world. 

She pushed her tongue against her cheek with a chuckle, “You’re evil.”

Her wife scoffed at her, “I haven’t even started, darling.”

“Agatha,” she groaned, picking at her food. 

Her wife went back to eating, shrugging, “I’m pregnant and it’s your birthday. We can have a little fun.”

Rio fought the roll of her eyes, but they wandered back to her wife’s plate, watching as Agatha played with the sauce. Her eyes watching the thick off-white cream drip back onto the plate before taking a couple more bites. Then, she sighed, her eyes looking out over the table as if she was deep in thought, but Rio knew better– forcing the last couple bites of food down, needing this meal.

“You know,” she started softly. “This sauce is incredible. I read reviews on the menu before booking–” 

“Agatha,” Rio warned, but her wife ignored her. 

“And what were the words they used?” she tapped a long finger on her chin. “Creamy, bold… sinful. Reminds me of those nights in Greece on our honeymoon, if you remember.”

Rio’s jaw went slack as the images hit her right in her core. 

“Specifically that night where we were standing, watching the waves, the moon just hiding from the clouds–” Agatha’s voice dipped low, and sultry, a whisper Rio could just hear over the music “– and I bent you right over that railing. You took me so well.”

Agatha rolled her eyes, “Don’t act all smug, my love. You still came untouched, perv.” 

Rio laughed for real this time, something soft that echoed like a summer storm in the distance– warm and thunderous all at once, shaking Agatha right down to the bone. Her wife pulled her closer, caressing her cheek, their eyes speaking their own language as it grew quiet again. 

Her wife broke it softly, “Are you tired, mi vida ?”

She snorted, “Cute, but no.” 

Agatha moved off of her wife, moving back to the bathroom where she had her bag. She heard Rio sit up, her eyes burning a hole into her as they followed her to the bathroom. She grabbed the green toy from on top, smirking as she moved back into the bedroom. 

“I have another gift, my love,” she smirked as Rio’s jaw dropped again. 

In her palms rested the toy, vibrant and bold, its surface glistening under the low bedroom light. It was a deep, enchanted green, smooth, sleek, and slightly curved on the longer end of it– exactly six inches. There was a curve closer to the end, soft to the touch, something to nestle against Rio’s clit, and on the other end– it was shorter, bulbous and meant to nestle inside. It was made for her wife, molded for her body. She knew how it would sit within her wife– how this end would rest deep, kissing that soft, tender spot, ridged to grind against her walls as she moved.  

With a crook of her finger, she motioned for her wife to stand– she practically scrambled, hands on her hips as Rio didn’t leave her gaze from the toy.

“You’ll wear it,” Agatha said softly, her voice low and velvet, laced with possession. “You’ll feel me every time you move.” 

She watched as her wife swallowed hard, her throat bobbing as she nodded like a woman possessed. Agatha kissed her lips slowly, distracting her as the kiss heated. Hands travelled up her body, Rio undoing her bra with one hand before hands were squeezing her boobs, a finger rolling over a hardened nipple, making Agatha groan low. Rio’s mouth descended, mouthing at her skin, kissing along her breasts and marking them as her hands pinched at her nipples. Impatiently, she pulled her wife back, kissing her jaw. 

“Do you want lube?” she asked quietly, staring up at her. 

Rio shook her head, a lazy smile etching on her lips as she took the toy, “Pick your position, mi amor. ”  

Agatha flashed her a smirk as she crawled onto the bed on her hands and knees where she patiently waited. She heard a slight moan from her wife, a wet sound of the toy moving inside her before she felt hands holding her hips. 

“Tell me when you need to move, mi amor. We can’t put pressure on your stomach,” her wife leaned over, kissing her back as the toy slipped between her thighs.

She moaned low in her throat, feeling the drag between her folds, dragging wetness up her slit, teasing her clit before slipping lower. She arched into the sensation, her knees spreading further apart, offering herself up to her wife with a hunger so bold it could kill. She swore and hissed as the head nudged slightly inside her, and then it slid in slowly, obscenely. Their moans harmonized as the toy stretched Agatha and her fingers clawed at the sheets. Fuck it was perfect– customed made for both of them. Something so it would fill both her and her wife. Her wife moaned behind her, hips twitching as the toy rubbed along her own clit with every slow push into Agatha’s soaked cunt. 

Her wife’s voice was breathless as hands drew her back,” You’re so fucking tight, mi amor. Gods, you’re swallowing it.” 

Agatha shuddered, her body grinding back to meet each thrust as she panted, “That’s the point. It’s yours. I’m yours. Use me.”

Something flipped in her wife, the control being transferred to Rio. Her hips snapped harder, the toy plunging deep– so deep Agatha’s cry pitched into a sharp whimper. She could feel every curve, every pulse– like Rio was really inside her, dragging against her walls and her clit catching on the ridge when Rio bottomed out. 

“ Mierda – you feel that?” her wife growled, but her voice was just as wrecked, her hands bruising. “I can feel your cunt trying to keep inside. You’re dripping, mi vida, making such a mess on my cock.” 

Agatha whined, her eyes rolling as she nodded, unable to speak as the room filled with wet slap of skin on skin. The toy pistoled in and out, the bed beneath them moving, hitting against the wall with every thrust as their moans became a chorus. Her body trembled, sweat dripping down her arm and face, her belly heavy with their son, jostled just enough with each brutal thrust. A warm hand slid under her, fingers finding her clit within seconds, circling it just as she liked– for once not teasingly, just giving what she needed. 

“Come for me, mi amor, ” her wife husked, hips never slowing. “Come while I fuck you with my cock.” 

She cried out, almost there, she just needed a little more– the coil was tight in her lower belly– just another nudge to snap the wires. 

Rio moaned low, “If you could see yourself, mi vida. So full of life, so perfect, so beautiful– round with child but begging for more. You want me to fill you, baby? Is that what you need, my good girl?” 

Agatha was gone. The coil exploded through her whole body as her eyes rolled back. A silent scream left her lips. The air left her lungs and she swore she was flying– the toy so deep inside her she could feel it in her throat. Her walls clenched greedily, pulling on it, milking it, trying to hold onto the sensation as she pulsed and squirted around it. Rio held her through it as her body trembled, hands on holding her hips up as she came with her. 

She couldn’t stop coming, her breaths coming out in loud moans, her wife’s name loud into the room as hands guided her. Her wife didn’t pull out, keeping them tethered as they laid on their sides, her wife spooning and holding her closely until Agatha’s body only trembled from the aftershock.  

“Breathe, my love,” a soft voice murmured against her ear. “That’s it. I have you. You’re safe. You’re mine.” 

She buried her face into the ruined quilt below them, overwhelmed, undone. Sweat drenched her, her breathing hard. Her wife curled closer behind her, soft kisses to her neck, sweet and perfect. They were soft, grounding, easing her down as a hand brushed along her hip and to her belly, rubbing circles on her side.

Agatha didn’t know how long they laid together, her wife pressing soft kisses to her until they both felt a sharp kick to Rio’s palm. They chuckled, Agatha pressing her hand just beside her wife’s to receive another hard, visible kick.

“You woke him, my love,” she teased, smiling as she knew Rio was rolling her eyes. “He’s already like you– cranky and fussy when woken up mid-nap.”

Lips pressed to her cheek, “That’s all you, mi amor. And technically, we woke him up.” 

Agatha laughed, the sound still frayed at the edges making her realize how sore her throat was from moaning, or screaming… whatever. Her head fell back against her wife’s shoulder, their sweat-slicked skin, making them stick together. 

“Well,” she whispered, her hand running over the spot where their son began another tantrum. “He’s got your temper.” 

“He’s kicking like he’s trying to complain to upper management.” 

“He probably is,” she chuckled. “Complaining at how loud I was.” 

Rio lips pressed a smile into her neck, “Or maybe he doesn’t like to share the attention.”

Agatha turned her face slightly to look at her wife, “Jealous? You’re calling him jealous? Goddess, he really is yours.” 

Another laugh rang in the room, one full of love and tenderness as hands pulled her closer. A hand slipped around her belly, tracing the shape of him– or least his tantrum. They fell into a quiet hum, her wife placing kisses still to the back of her neck, a hand calming the ill-tempered within, and Agatha laid, still full of her wife, deep inside her.

 

It was only later did Agatha realize the toy slipped out, the quilt kicked to the floor, and her body wiped of their activities. She heard her wife swear somewhere in the room, the lights now turned off before she heard two thumbs of a certain traumatized rabbit. 

“Señor, just go to bed– it’s not even that late,” she heard her wife grumble to the rabbit before footsteps grew closer and the bed shifted. 

Agatha turned, immediately resting half her body on her wife before a pillow was being placed behind her back, adding to her comfort as she breathed in her wife’s raw scent. She nuzzled her face deep into Rio’s neck, letting out a content sigh as lips pressed to her head. 

“Happy birthday, my love,” she whispered, before her eyes became too heavy, and she fell deep into sleep, feeling loved and sated.

Chapter 28: Conejito

Chapter Summary

A featherlike kiss pressed into her side before, “Tranquilo, conejito. No tortures a tu mamá. Necesita descansar.” (Easy, little bunny. Don’t torture your mama. She needs her rest.)

Agatha exhaled slowly, molten fury cooling into thick fog. Her fingers twitched where she rested her head on them, still itching for a dramatic gesture– perhaps just a firm poke to Rio’s forehead, or jab at her sides– nothing life-threatening– but the storm had passed for now. Rio had that effect. Always had and probably always will. It was both comforting and fucking irritating, how quickly she could unravel Agatha’s frustration with the gentlest of touch, with that voice– a lullaby underneath the stars, sweetened with even when laced with worry.

“I am resting,” she muttered, her voice sharp-edged, but dulled like a sword after striking too much. “Or I was trying too until someone bothered the little gremlin.”

OR :

Rio is stressing, Agatha is sleepy and needs Rio to relax. Coven chaos ensues

OR :

FLUFF AND CRACK

Chapter Notes

First off, THANK YOU FOR 3K KUDOS OMG THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU

I LOVE YOU ALL.

This chapter is fluff and crack, please enjoy while I prepare chapter 29, the chapter you and me both have been waiting for.

Love,

Rioallalong_719 💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha



Agatha wondered if she could kill someone temporarily… like just put them asleep for a moment… minutes… hours ... couple days… weeks maybe– more like seven weeks to be exact. Nicholas was due in seven weeks and a certain someone, if she didn’t calm her fucking tits down, would not be around to meet her son. It was dramatic, totally something over the top that crossed her mind, but nonetheless, it was Agatha Harkness-Vidal totally over being pregnant, especially with an overdoting wife. Don’t get her wrong. She loves the little shit with all of her heart. She didn’t think there was a possibility to love anything else as much as she loves Rio… until they decided to have a kid together, but that’s besides the point. The point was that little shit was driving her up a wall. Call it hormones, call it the lack of sleep she was getting– blame it on Rio’s spawn growing in her belly and never being able to sit still like his mami– Agatha was at her breaking point. She was ready to throttle Rio, ready throw her out a window, ready to scream, ready to–

Soft lips brushed against her cheek, immediately smoothing the crease between her eyebrows. Her body betrayed her, the tension vaporizing off of her in slowly lulling waves. A hand was on her stomach, comforting the little kicking bunny inside her before cold air was hitting her now bare stomach and those soft lips were now worshipping with warm, tender presses. She heard pianissimo Spanish whispers, dancing across her skin– lyrical murmurs that eased their son’s squirming.

A featherlike kiss pressed into her side before, “ Tranquilo, conejito. No tortures a tu mamá. Necesita descansar.” (Easy, little bunny. Don’t torture your mama. She needs her rest.) 

Agatha exhaled slowly, molten fury cooling into thick fog. Her fingers twitched where she rested her head on them, still itching for a dramatic gesture– perhaps just a firm poke to Rio’s forehead, or jab at her sides– nothing life-threatening– but the storm had passed for now. Rio had that effect. Always had and probably always will. It was both comforting and fucking irritating, how quickly she could unravel Agatha’s frustration with the gentlest of touch, with that voice– a lullaby underneath the stars, sweetened with even when laced with worry. 

“I am resting,” she muttered, her voice sharp-edged, but dulled like a sword after striking too much. “Or I was trying too until someone bothered the little gremlin.” 

Her wife only hummed her laughter, kissing her stomach again before her touch pulled away. She didn’t bother opening up her eyes and for once, she believed her wife got the hint to leave her alone, but not to be too far. To be quiet, but never silent. To stop worrying, but to never stop caring. To stop hovering, but to never stop holding her. 

She didn’t. 

(Not that Agatha knew what she wanted in all honesty.)

Instead, Agatha listened to small jars being opened with a silent pop before a hand was on her stomach again, fingers tracing the oil in protective, strengthening, and loving runes on her. She could only imagine what their altar looked like, covered in ashes from Rio burning bay leaves, surrounded by offerings, candles burned outside with good intentions for pregnancy. Gods, Rio was so sweet and worried, it was nauseating… but it also worried her. Her wife was so overprotective, so overly doting ever since they went on maternity leave. Well, even before that, she believed it started after her wife’s birthday. 

It was the night after she made Rio come in her pants (which was a huge boost to her ego and so fucking hot. Sue her. That was her own gift that day) Agatha had woken up with Rio sitting on the bed, pulling out sweets from the Two Magpies Bakery . (This is in England, not New York… just go with it.) It was their favorite place and somewhere where Agatha had planned to take them before taking Rio to lunch and back to the hotel to get ready for the Broadway play : The Outsiders. Instead, she looked at the time and saw it was noon. She had an alarm set– something to wake her and her Rio to get ready and go, but instead, Rio went and picked up the food and brought it back to her. When she questioned her wife, her wife just shook her shoulders and handed her two breakfast brioche rolls, fried eggs, and extra crispy bacon before settling on her weird ass choice of mushrooms on toast. They had sex again after, apparently getting fucked into the mattress the night before wasn’t enough so they kicked out the bunny from the bathroom where Rio and her definitely did not save water… and then ruined the mattress more.

At the Broadway show, Agatha totally didn’t cry into her wife’s shoulder at Dally’s death and the song “Stay Gold.” But walking down to their seats at the beginning, Rio had a firm arm on her, guiding her carefully down the steps. During intermission, her wife pushed through the crowd for her and when they did reach the bathroom, Rio made them cut the line and distracted a Karen while Agatha went. In truth, if Rio hadn’t had them cut the line, she was sure her son would have publicly embarrassed her. 

When they were finally home, things escalated. Her wife would appear after her lectures, freezing any approaching student in their tracks, and in real time, Agatha saw them rethink their life decisions at the moment Rio would walk into the classroom, parting the wave of leaving students like Moses did the Red Sea. Her wife would be at her side immediately, never openly touching or revealing her softer side, just a firm gaze that kept students far away from both of them. Rio would always have lunches with her, and would block out that time specifically for her. During faculty meetings, which Agatha was always late to, even before pregnancy, to mess with her wife– well, she came in late and there was always an empty seat available next to her wife that was never open before. Normally, another dean or head of a department would assume the chair as closer to ‘power,’ but then, it was left open for her where lemon tea was ready and her favorite craving beside her folder. And Rio would stand or come over from where she was up by the board, and without breaking her flow of talking, would pull out the seat, take her hand, and help her sit. 

Then came her last day. She received a few gifts, some chocolates, and Eva even gifted her a Shakespeare onesie that said “To Be or Not to Be” which she pretended to be disgusted by and rolled her eyes. Ireland gifted her two bottles of wine, in which later she read “One for Dr. Vidal” and a non-alcoholic sparkling grape juice : “One for Dr. Harkness.” It was cute, sweet even. Her older students made a card for both her and Rio, and even William and Ryan gave their own gifts of sweets. From other professors, their mailboxes were full of cards and chocolates. 

Let it be known, Rio went through all the food like a hawk, throwing any out she deemed wrapped weird, she didn’t like the vibe, or if it wasn’t dark chocolate. Agatha hid the Dubai chocolate that the head of the department gave her and gave it to Rio when her wife caught her with three bites left only for the little shit to throw it away claiming it had too much sugar… which it probably did. 

For the last few weeks Rio had been home cooking their meals, refusing take-out Thursdays or when Agatha suggested it. Her wife would just ask what was her order and just cook it. Which was fine for Agatha, but it wasn’t the end of the worrying and the doting. When they went into maternity leave and because Agatha wasn’t moving like she did on campus, Rio would trick her into doing daily walks whether it was the backyard, up and down the street, the park, or in town. It was perfect though, Agatha wasn’t complaining at all. She never did if it meant being by her wife’s side. The problem came when Rio wanted to sign her up for prenatal pilates. Agatha was a pilates queen before her own instructor went on maternity leave for a year. And then when Agatha got pregnant, she stopped… but pregnant Agatha, surrounded by other pregnant mothers who would all be bragging about what their husbands did, meanwhile the husband was likely screwing the assistant… well Agatha has said enough. 

All it took was one glare and one snide comment for Rio to shut her mouth and continue on another topic like it never happened. A few days later her wife asked her to do prenatal yoga which Agatha only agreed if they did it in their basement together. Well, most of the time those sessions led to sex on the couch down there, and Agatha did plenty of yoga then. 

But it grew worse… well, she wasn’t sure what was the word for it. When Agatha would take her naps, Rio would be researching and researching. The one night she caught her wife after Nicholas had kicked her bladder, on her home laptop in the dining room. Three notebooks were spread out, Rio’s coloured pens were lined in front of her as she kept alternating, and don’t get Agatha started on the sticky tabs Rio used for labelling. Agatha didn’t bother to look, just closed her wife’s lap top, grabbed her hand, and dragged her to bed. Then were the apps– plural – Agatha found on Rio’s phone. She wasn’t snooping. Her wife asked her to check to go in her emails when Agatha saw the three pregnancy tracking apps. 

Then came their go-bag. Rio packed four . two for the baby, and two for them. Agatha had caught her reorganizing them about five times now. But that was only when she was awake, she wasn’t sure what her wife was up to after she was asleep. But the final straw came early that morning when Rio was nesting . Not Agatha, her non-pregnant wife. She should be the one nesting, but it was Rio. Rio was reorganizing the nursery, refolding onesies onto a different shelf, and organized the sock drawer. Her wife rebuilt the changing table, adjusted the diapers before settling the books by height and colour in the bookshelf… Agatha would have to change it back. What kind of monster organized books like that? It was by genre first, and then by the author’s last name. And she would have to refold the onesies and other clothes… okay, maybe she was starting to nest. She was already thinking about changing the decorative pillows on the couch, changing the pictures in the hallway, and getting new paintings… she was definitely nesting, but why was Rio? 

Agatha kept her eyes closed, ignoring the lotion now being placed on her stomach as she realized she couldn’t remember the last time her wife went to the greenhouse to garden other than watering the plants. Rio’s new gloves that Agatha bought for her birthday were still sitting on the kitchen counter unused. She couldn’t remember the last time Rio sketched, played her drums, or found some project to keep her busy where she would disappear for hours. The playstation was starting to collect dust and Rio had also stopped working out as much, because her wife was always at least in a ten foot radius. Fuck, she wasn’t even sure if her wife had texted Alice or Hela.

No, Agatha had no problem with the doting, with Rio always ready to hold her when she was unsteady, to make her craving food, to hold her in the bathtub, rub her swallow ankles, talk to conejito, or to fuck Agatha through the mattress until she was sated. No, the problem was that Rio hadn’t done anything lately to make herself happy, to relax her own nerves… and this was stemming from an unforeseen weight… something so much deeper than what the surface showed. 

And the first step she needed her wife to do was to get the fuck out of the house and be away from her for at least half a day. 

She needed her wife to be herself. 





The plan was easy to enact. All she had to do was text Hela and Alice. They didn’t ask questions, didn’t prod at why Agatha needed Rio to get out– they knew. Agatha told them to create a separate group chat without her and Rio and to plan whatever– bowling, a bar night, karaoke night, axe throwing, one of those rage rooms– something to get Rio back to being Rio. Sure, Rio was still her, still loving and sweet like she has always been– but Agatha couldn’t go an hour without Rio worrying about something with their son instead of enjoying the leave before they were sleep deprived, covered in spit up, and running solely on a baby induced fever. Her wife was running herself into the ground. 

It was four am the night before when Agatha had woken up to an empty bed. She had looked towards the master bathroom, not finding a low glow and immediately stood. The hallway was dark, nothing moved, just sitting in an eerie silence. Towards the floor, a white light illuminated by the doorway to the nursery where Agatha spotted the door open. Stepping inside, the door creaked low as a chill wrapped around her. She hugged her arms to herself, missing the warmth of the bed while her wife’s cool body held her; instead she was up, staring at the open window of their nursery. Moon light draped in, shining directly on the crib. 

We need new blinds and maybe to angle the crib a different way, her nesting mind stored the information away for later. 

Her eyes had narrowed back to the crib, finding her wife standing over in just her oversized Stevie Nicks shirt. Black boxers hugged her hips, but Agatha couldn’t even admire her wife. Her back was stiff, drawn tight and rigid. In her arms was the security blanket with a rabbit head, ears, arms, and a patch in the side where Agatha removed the tail and stitched the hole back up in case their little one decided to explore the world with his mouth. She nuzzled the thing under her chin, her head tilted back, as she stared up into the ceiling. Rio was terrified, terrified of messing up, of not being enough, and most of all, she knew her wife was horrified if she couldn’t protect either of them. Rio was always a protector, a caring, slightly possessive, protector, but Rio was hers.

“My love,” she had whispered softly, causing her wife to whip around with the rabbit clutched tightly to her chest. 

Her eyes had gone comically wide, like a child being caught doing something they were explicitly told not too. Then, Rio was lying the security blanket back into the crib, approaching her quickly. Hands were on her stomach, lips pressed just beside her lips. 

“ Mi amor, what are you doing awake?” 

Agatha leaned into her wife, nuzzling into Rio’s neck, “The bed was empty and I went searching for you. What are you doing in here?”

 Rio ignored her question, pulling back to take her hand. She followed her wife into the bedroom, curling up on her chest as lips peppered touches to her head. Her wife didn’t answer her question, but she did – in her silence she did. It was how she held Agatha a little tighter, a hand resting on her stomach protectively, pressing the tiniest bit firmer. It was in the way she seemed to hold her breath, and her heart beating a little faster underneath her ear. She lifted her head off Rio’s chest, turning to find her wife watching her. She met her lips softly, trying to ease the tension she carried. Rio held her a little tighter, keeping her as close as possible and Agatha let her. 

And before Agatha had drifted off, she placed another kiss at the corner of Rio’s mouth, “I love you, Rio.”






Agatha was going to kill someone. 

 

Her sights were set on Rio. 

 

She was going to be the first person in her line of sight anyway.

 

Pourquoi pas ? 

 

It was the end of March and whatever the fucking humans did to the planet was about to set Agatha off on anyone who didn’t believe there was something wrong with our planet. It was eighty degrees fahrenheit or twenty-seven celsius… in the Northeast of the fucking United States of America. Fuck this place . Fuck the sun. Fuck fate for deciding their airconditioning wasn’t working. 

And worse, Agatha couldn’t stand the feeling of her own skin against her, especially after her shower. So, she found Rio’s green flowy Boho pants, thanking the gods that the elastics weren’t tight against her skin as they hung low on her hips and under her belly. Okay, maybe she needed that sweet pine scent of her wife. She grabbed Rio’s sleep shirt off the bed and put it on, tying the shirt up over her belly with a hair tie in the back. Her belly could breath, and her skin wasn’t touching that much and she could still be cool. 

She grumbled as she made her way down the hall, figuring Rio would be in the basement fixing the damn thing, but froze when she saw their coven sitting on their back porch– Lilia, Hela, Alice, and Jen– all laughing together as Rio passed out water. At least the stupid utility belt was hanging around her hips. Agatha licked her lips, starting at the way it pulled at Rio basketball shorts, revealing the lace– fuck she is wearing a thong– of her underwear and revealing Agatha’s favorite V-line. She tore her eyes away, looking for Señor Scratchy, hoping the bunny was alright. She looked in his pen, finding it empty before checking the kitchen, wondering if he climbed on to lay on the cool counters. Don’t ask Agatha how he managed to do that. Rio and her were still trying to figure it out. 

The sound of a low whistle made her whip around to her wife coming back into the house. Her lips were formed into that irritating smirk as her eyes shamelessly wandered Agatha’s body. They travelled the length of her before resting on her bare stomach. Her tongue pushed against her cheek as she pulled the belt from her hips, sitting it on the counter before hands were on her hips pulling Agatha against her wife. Her arms wrapped around broad shoulders, fingers already playing with the sweaty hairs there. Lips kissed hers softly before hands were on her stomach, the little rabbit kicking at Rio’s hands. 

Agatha leaned into her wife, completely forgetting about killing her as she breathed in her wife’s scent. Pine, juniper, and something distinctly her wife– something strongly her wife. Rio technically stinked, but Agatha never cared. She was used to it from their college days with Rio coming back from the gym, soaked in sweat, wrapping her arms around Agatha, and covering her too. Most of the time, if Rio wasn’t elbowed or hit, Rio would be getting her post workout in the shower with her. 

Agatha might have grown to like it– sue her. Rio was her wife, the father-mother of their son– the love of her life. If she did… per se, didn’t mind the scent of her wife’s sweat, that was no one’s business but hers. 

Her nose brushed along her wife’s neck, taking another quick breath in– a chuckle rang out into the room, tolling that she had been caught. 

“Are you smelling me, you perv?” 

Agatha pulled away abruptly, “No, you just stink–”

Rio only hummed, chuckling darkly–

“Whatever you are about to do– need I remind you that I have been graciously carrying and growing your son.”

Her wife pulled away, opening her arms up in mock surrender before taking a towel and wiping her face, “I ended up calling someone. She’s on her way to fix the AC for us, but why is the coven here? They just said you were expecting them– Agatha?”

She had stopped listening. There was a tightening feeling in her lower stomach, something that was very much like cramps. Her wife was quick, a cold hand to her naval before another caressed her cheek, her eyes wide with worry. But she didn’t say anything, just held her close until the tightening stopped. 

Agatha breathed in sharply before releasing the long breath as Rio kissed the side of her head, “It’s alright, mi amor. It’s just a Braxton Hick. Your body is practicing for when he comes.”

Hands were now in hers, guiding her to the back porch where the coven was arguing, “I have the sprinkler on outside with them. Stay with them and I’ll get a shower and be right out, mi amor. ”

She nodded as the door slid open and immediately all of their eyes were on her stomach. Alice looked like she was ready to launch out of her seat at her, Lilia held a hand to her chest as Jen swirled her margarita in hand. Hela was the only one who stood to cautiously approach her, handing her a cold bottle of water.

“Congratulations, Mama Harkness. It’s been a while.” 

She scowled at her, making the others laugh as Rio guided her to sit down before Rio was behind her, fingers in her wet hair. She closed her eyes, breathing in as her wife started a French braid on the top of her head. 

“So how’s the little one?” Lilia sat up, her voice soft, not insisting. 

It was careful, giving her enough room where she could, if she wanted, not answer. This was why Lilia was her best friend. She knew when to be soft with her and when she could absolutely annoy the shit out of Agatha. If it weren’t for Lilia… and Alice, her and Rio might have never been together . 

“He kicks me a lot,” she replied, holding back a moan as her wife’s fingers ran through her hair before continuing the braid.

Her wife laughed behind her, “ El conejito never sits still. When Agatha is asleep, he kicks me at night through her stomach.” 

Hela tilted her head, “ Conjito– what is that?”

“Little rabbit,” Alice answered, her eyes still not leaving the baby bump. “Can we feel? Goddess, I can’t believe how real this is– you really are growing a baby.”

Agatha went to open her mouth, figuring it couldn’t hurt. As long as strangers didn’t ask, Agatha didn’t mind if it was their friends. 

But before she could, her wife was tying off the braid and a hand was on her stomach, “Maybe later Alice, when she cools down. Hela, if the handywoman comes, can you show her the AC unit. I’m gonna hop in the shower.”

She held back the roll of her eyes as her wife kissed her cheek before heading into the house. All eyes were on her, but her wife either didn’t notice or didn’t care. The moment Rio was out of ear shot, Agatha sat up, her hand on her belly. 

“Please tell me you have a plan to get her out of the house.”

They all stared at her sympathetically, Hela and Alice even more so, and Agatha’s fingers twitched. 

Hela sat up, “Agatha, she doesn’t seem off. She’s her same overprotective self. I would have killed to have someone like Rio during my pregnancy.”

Agatha froze at that, swallowing hard as she remembered Rio leaving on random days to help Hela emotionally during her pregnancy. Alice would be there too and well… Agatha’s emotional range, unless it was Rio but even then it wasn’t great, was the size of a tea spoon. She blinked for a moment, her mouth opening awkwardly as both hands were now cradling her stomach. 

“Please, don’t take me as complaining,” she started quietly. “I know how lucky I am to have Rio, but I am just… concerned. Rio is worrying herself to death.” 

The coven all grumbled, Jen swallowing the last of her margarita in one gulp while Lilia sat back and groaned with her pointer finger and thumb pinching her eyebrows. 

Alice sat up, “Agatha, respectfully, you realized who you married, right?”

She shook her head, “No, this is different. Rio, when she’s stressed, she plays video games, finds random projects to do, works out, and we all know the greenhouse is her safe space. She hasn’t been in that green house unless she is watering them, and even then, she doesn’t take her time– those plants are her babies” Agatha gestured around them to the evergreen growing on the porch. “It’s not like her.”

They all stared at Agatha like she was stupid. Lilia and Jen looked away while Alice and Hela shared a look. Hela was the one to sit up, frowning slightly at her. 

“Agatha, can I just remind you who you are growing right in here?” she said softly, a hand on the curve. 

She watched as the stoic woman actually cracked a smile when conjito kicked at her. She pulled back, all of them staring at her .

Hela chuckled, “You are carrying her real baby. Of course, he and you are going to be her entire focus.”

Agatha exhaled slowly, the weight of their gazes pressing than her belly for a moment. Her spine pressed into the cushion behind her, hand still resting instinctively over the firm swell between her palms.

“I know,” she whispered, barely louder than the small breeze that graced them. “I know. But, she’s unravelling and stitching herself back together a hundred times a day. So when will the threads fray? I need her to go out and be herself.” 

Alice leaned forward then, elbows on her knees, gaze fierce, “You think she’s falling apart, but from where we are sitting, she’s stronger than ever. She’s holding up the world and sure, she’s quieter, maybe not obsessing over her plant babies or gaming– she is transforming to be your son’s other mother. You know, the one who isn’t breastfeeding him– or rather the one who he didn’t come out of– OW!” 

Jen pulled her hand back from slapping Alice on the arm.  

Lilia sighed, blowing a stray curl from her face, “What she means to say is that Rio is preparing– nesting in her own way.” 

Agatha’s lips parted to cut in, but nothing came. The wind shifted– another needed breeze blowing on them. She looked down to their son kicking again, a visible distortion in her otherwise smooth curve.

“Rio’s never half-assed her way through anything. She’s not gonna start now, especially not with your son.” 

“And,” Jen added with a lazy wave of her glass, “she’s not used to being afraid. Not like this. It’s different when love has a heartbeat, you know?” 

Agatha’s heart twinged at that. Not because it was new– but because it was right. Rio, fearless and kinetic, had never flinched in the face of any chaos. But this… this was a different kind of unknown. A future with tiny hands and cries in the night and lullabies in a language neither of them had grown up with. And Rio, for all her fire, was terrified. 

“Still,” Agatha’s voice stayed soft, “I just want her to breathe. Really breathe. Not for me, not even for him– but for herself. So please, tell me you guys have a plan.”

Hela smirked this time, sitting up, “Oh, we do. She is not going to like it by the way. Don’t let her sweet talk her way out of this, Agatha.”

Alice nodded, “We are going in the center city to see the new Jurassic movie and then taking her shopping.”

“And not baby clothes,” Jen tilted her head. “I can imagine you two have enough.” 

“And I am staying here with you,” Lilia smiled, placing a hand on her stomach to feel him kick. “That way, your wife isn’t freaking out about you being by yourself and we can catch up if you would like. The girls and I figured we can cook dinner and when they come back, depending on how you’re feeling, we can take the to-go containers or all eat here.” 

Agatha smiled softly, “That sounds perfect.”



The screen door shut, causing them to turn to find Rio in black shorts and a tank with her hair a wet mop on her head, “What sounds perfect?” 



***

 

The Coven

 

Alice stared wide-eyed as she watched her best friend hastily whispering to her wife in the corner of the house as the handywoman that came over finally fixed their AC. It was rapid Spanish– none of them able to keep up as Agatha scowled at Rio, her eyebrow raised, her hands cradling underneath her stomach. It was a strange sight– maybe strange wasn’t the word, incredible maybe. From what Lilia had told her and from what she knew of the brunette, if anyone told Agatha that she would be married and now pregnant, she would have laughed in your face and possibly spit in it. Yet, it was the same for her best friend. Rio had never looked long term… that was until Agatha. They all knew how possessive and Rio was over her wife now, but this was different. Rio was more than protective of her pregnant wife– in fact ever since the incident with Evanora, she hadn’t seen the worry lines leave Rio’s face.

Her best friend raised her voice higher, the Spanish fluid and quick to the point where her wife even looked lost for a moment before cupping Rio’s cheeks to calm her.

There was a heavy sigh before Agatha continued in English, “My love, it is like six hours away from me–”

“And you starting with Braxton Hicks–” 

“And they’re just Braxton Hicks contractions. We have seven more weeks, my love. He isn’t coming anytime now. Go have fun. Drink for me– eat sushi for me. And when you come home, Lilia and I will have dinner ready.”

The stressed wife shook her head, her hands on her wife’s stomach, “Pero ¿y si–”
 “Rio.”

Alice walked away, standing next to Hela as she nodded the handywoman out of the house. She didn’t mean to eavesdrop– well, she kind of did– but the weight of the moment between Agatha and Rio was too sacred to interrupt. Not that she could’ve if she tried. When Rio was locked in like that, love curling around her like a hurricane refusing to let go, only Agatha could untangle her. 

“She’s so gone,” Hela murmured beside her, her lips quirking in a knowing smile. 

Alice didn’t respond, only crossed her arms and watched. Rio was brushing her thumbs along Agatha’s sides now, nose tucked close, breathing like she was trying to memorize her. Maybe she was. Alice had known Rio as long as any one else, for the matter, maybe a couple months longer as they met the summer Rio had moved from Puerto Rico. She was independent, strongwilled, laughed with reckless joy on rooftops, and walked away from people who tried too hard to hold her. But Agatha was the only one who’d held her without chains. 

“Fine,” Rio finally exhaled, forehead pressed against Agatha’s. “Okay, but you text me.”

“Every hour on the hour,” her wife promised. 

“No sarcasm or jokes.”

“One joke.”

Rio huffed, rolling her eyes before their lips sealed in a brief kiss that made Alice turn around like it burned. Beside her, Hela made a gagging noise as Jen visibly shivered, coming beside her. Alice wrapped her arm around her waist, all of them ignoring the couple in the corner as Lilia approached them. 

“You realize you’re basically kidnapping Rio, right?”  

The group nodded, a chorus of “yep”s echoing around them. 

Lilia cocked her head, “And you realize you are not keeping her still for the movie?”

They shared looks with each other as the kooky woman smirked, “Agatha and I already talked. Just keep her busy for at least four hours. Shop, book stores, craft stores, plants stores or whatever. Just keep her away from baby stuff. Agatha says their nursery is overrun.”

“Lilia,” Rio’s voice sounded behind them, making them all jump.

All eyes narrowed to the mother-to-be as the pregnant wife sat down on the couch, rearranging the pillows around her like a poised queen. Alice startled, hearing a light patter coming from the hallway until a caramel spotted bunny emerged, hopping towards the couch and jumping straight in Agatha’s lap. He curled up against her stomach, bunny eyes closing, nose twitching, and fucking purring. 

A stern voice brought Alice back to her best friend listing off things for her wife, “ –she needs her vitamins with food, not on an empty stomach, or she’ll get queasy. And don’t let her convince you she’s not tired. If she starts blinking too slow, that’s it. Nap time.” 

Alice glanced at her girlfriend, who mouthed ‘ blinking too slow ?’ before stifling a laugh. 

“And if she says she doesn’t want to eat even though she is fidgeting” Rio continued, hands on her hips now like a general issuing wartime orders, “she’s lying. If anything she wants the lavender bread or strawberries with salt. Now the AC works, keep it cold. She likes being cold– have the blankets and my hoodies ready. But she only likes the purple blanket that we keep in our bedroom and…”

Jen leaned against her, kissing her cheek, “Please tell me you won’t be this overbearing if we ever have a kid in the future. It’s like watching the overprotective scared first-time dad in a 90s sitcom.” 

“She is a first-time dad,” Alice muttered back. “If that dad knew how to kill a polar bear with just her bare hands.” 

At that, Lilia straightened, her tone gentle but commanding, “Rio, I will treat your wife like she is British Royalty. She will rest, we will read together, or put on Friends . Now go. Before they drag you out by your hair.” 

Rio looked between them all, eyes narrowed, clearly calculating whether or not she could really trust her coven with the most precious thing in her life. Then her gaze landed on her wife– legs curled, bunny honking softly, and a knowing smile blooming like a fresh tulip on her lips. Her eyes were closed, already dozing off even with the coven present. Alice jumped when Rio brushed by her, cupping her wife’s cheek before helping her lay down. They all groaned again as they kissed before Rio muttered something in Spanish, but Alice had enough. Jen and her shared a look before they both grabbed her, pulling her out the door before she could find another excuse. 



They piled in Hela’s truck, old rock songs already blasting from the speakers and windows down, hitting them in the face with summer air instead of spring. They were all screaming and singing, carrying on like a bunch of college students that just finished finals, officially out for the summer. Jen was in the front, leaning back, her arms through the moon roof as her and Hela carried on in the front. Alice was with them, singing her heart out when she turned to Rio– who was staring out the truck window in her own world. 

Hela met her eyes in the mirror, both of them staring at Rio who remained oblivious. In her hand, her phone was clutched tightly, almost enough to break the screen. Her leg was bouncing as she played with the ends of her hair, a true tale of nervousness. Rio was always the life of the party, always the one to go too far with her wife tugging her back at the end of the leash– and yet, Rio didn’t even seem to care that Hela turned on Bombay Sapphires by Stevie Nicks, one of her favorite songs. She didn’t even tap her fingers to the beat nor did her mouth move with the lyrics. They tried Sleep Token next and still got nothing. They even tried Until I Found You, the song Agatha and Rio danced to on their wedding night. Brown eyes narrowed, but still Rio remained unusually quiet. 

Jen, despite being new, recognized Rio was not herself. She glanced back at her girlfriend, wondering if Alice had an idea, but she looked just as lost. So perhaps Hela talking of her daughter would relax Rio’s nerves. 

“Hela, so how is Lilith doing?”

She didn’t take her eyes off the road, but warmth cracked through her cold exterior as a single hand drifted to her empty belly, “Oh she’s wonderful. It was hard leaving her today– she’s in that stage where she only wants me. My partner has her today, so please wish them luck. I’m surprised they haven’t texted me yet.” 

Jen smiled, her heart warming hearing the woman beside had a support system, “Aw, how did you and your partner meet?” 

“Well, I was in my third trimester actually. They were super sweet– we met at a coffee shop– I just wanted a cinnamon roll and I don’t know. Kori and I just started talking and then next thing I knew, they came to the hospital after I had Lilith with flowers and a sushi boat. Gods, Rio, you need to get Agatha a sushi boat after she has your son.”

They all watched as Rio’s head snapped off the window, peering at Hela with unwavering attention. 

Rio’s head tilted as she shifted in her seat, “A sushi boat?”

Hela nodded, “Yeah, it’s free for first time mothers and they deliver right to the hospital too. I’ll give you the name of the place.” 

Rio swallowed thickly for a moment, her eyes darting right out the window before speaking hesitantly, “How… how was it? Like with everything?”

She raised an eyebrow, as the hand on her stomach rubbed softly. For a moment, Hela was transported back to when she first had Lilith. She was so pumped full of drugs and that epidural did fucking wonders. Her eyes drifted off, still focused on the road, but elsewhere as Lilith– the small baby was placed on her chest, her cries already softening, as impossibly small fingers clung to her.

“It was everything, Rio,” her voice was uncharacteristically soft, a tone perhaps she only used on Lilith or her partner. “They pumped me up full of drugs though, I barely felt a thing. Agatha will be fine. Your son will be here in no time.”
Rio nodded, her gaze going back to the window but it ricocheted back, “Okay, but what if– well they said that Nicholas is on the bigger side– what if–”
 The coven froze, tuning out the rambling overprotective wife. Each of their eyes widened, realizing that the mother-to-be slipped up, releasing the name of her unborn son in her rambling. Hela’s foot eased off the gas, Jen lifted off her sunglasses, turning around, her mouth parted open, and Alice twisted in her seat, her whole body turning towards Rio in pure wonder. No one spoke for a moment, except for Rio who continued to ramble on and on about wayward possibilities. 

“Nicholas?” Hela finally said, her voice lilting at the edges like she didn’t want to spook a wild animal. 

Rio blinked owlishly, stunned like she hadn’t realized she had said anything at all. Her lips parted and then closed, repeating like a stupefied fish. Alice took Rio’s hand aiming for the contact to jolt Rio out of it. It worked. The hand was ripped away like it was burned as Rio sat up too fast, the seat belt pinning her back in her seat. 

“Gods, Agatha is going to really kill me this time,” she spoke to herself before covering her face with her hands. “ Mierda!”

 The group laughed softly as Jen turned to her, “You guys picked a name?” 

Rio’s voice was fragile, “Yeah, on my birthday in a botanical garden. I was talking about hemerocallis, also called ‘Fire Chief Nicolas.’ He kicks every time we say the name.” 

Brown eyes stared up at the sky through the moon roof, watching the clouds pass by quickly as Hela pulled off the interstate to the mall. 

“He feels real now, huh?” Hela said gently. 

Rio didn’t answer, her gaze now on the huge building of the mall. Oh, she was going to kill them. She liked shopping when it was for her wife or for conejito. She hated shopping for herself, not as much as Agatha, but still enough. This was going to be torture. 



***



Alice was secretly fuming, not even the gentle circles on her back from her girlfriend’s fingers could soothe her. They were sitting and eating a small lunch, drinking– well Hela and Rio weren’t drinking. At least Hela had an excuse. She was still breastfeeding Lilith and she was driving. Rio wasn’t drinking and she didn’t have an excuse, just eating a few fries here and there, while staring at her phone. They knew Agatha should have texted by now but she hadn’t. They didn’t know why, and in fact it was strange to them. She would never break a promise to Rio and starting now– when she was close to giving birth to their son– well, Rio was about to snap the fucking table. 

It was Hela who received the call from Lilia. She set her tea down, quickly pressing the green button before putting her on speaker. 

“Lilia?” Hela asked, concern leaking from her voice as Rio lunged across the table, taking her phone. 

“What’s wrong? Is she okay? Is it our son? Did she–”
 Lilia cut her off softly, “Rio, dear, she is fine. I was calling to let you know that she is asleep so you can quit worrying. I figured I would call Hela so you didn’t blow up when my name flashed across the screen.” 

Rio remained unimpressed, “Are the pillows there to support her back and belly? She needs good rest now that he stopped kicking her. He was kicking her all night.”
Lilia hummed, still whispering into the phone, “Yes, and Scratchy is at her feet too. I think the baby kicked him when he was against her stomach. I see why you too call the baby a little rabbit.” 

Small smiles all formed on their faces, Hela’s eyes light while Jen leaned against her girlfriend, staring at Rio. And in the moment, it really struck Jen for some reason. Dean Vidal  was really going to be a mom. It wasn’t just rumors, or conversations– neither passing glances or Alice telling her about what Rio was up to. This was real . She always thought Rio was cold-hearted, uncaring, and detached. She was scary in her own right, but as she got to know Rio over these months, through Alice and working with Rio more, she saw the closely guarded warmth, only kept for those who truly deserved it. Staring at Rio now, Jen already saw a mother before her eyes. 

As the conversation between Lilia and Rio continued, Jen leaned over to Alice, “She’s gonna be one helluva mother.”

Her girlfriend smirked, leaning into her shoulder, “Good luck to any kid that bullies their son.” 

They laughed, Jen kissing her cheek softly, “Who do you think is going to be scarier? Agatha or Rio?”

Alice scoffed, “Definitely, Agatha. She’ll tear into the school board or some shit if anyone lays a hand on her baby. Rio is probably going to teach him how to fight.”
 “Oh, this kid is going to be a mini her, isn’t he?”

“Gods help us all if he is like Agatha.” 



***



Hela made a mistake. A deadly mistake.

She’d thought they’d chosen wisely by avoiding the mall. After all, within minutes, she, Alice, and Jen had been wrestling Rio away from anything baby-related– body-slamming her out of Build-A-Bear , dragging her by the arms from Babies "R" Us , even yanking her back from some random boutique none of them remembered seeing before. Hela had pulled Rio by her hair out of a bookstore, sending toddlers into tears as baby books clattered to the floor. Rio had shrieked at her, but Hela hadn’t cared. 

Then came the final straw. A full-grown adult– a Dean of a goddamn university– had bolted for a store called The Precious Garden . 

All three of them had tackled her, grumbling as they decided, Fine . The theater.  

Maybe sitting the Green Witch in a dark room with her favorite silly dead overgrown lizards would finally calm her down. 

It did not. 

Not even close. 

The movie had started and Jen and Alice were curled up in a love seat, leaving Hela and Rio in stiff, single seats. Hela kicked up the footrest, tossing buttery popcorn into her mouth as the Jurassic Park theme roared through the speakers. Rio loved these movies. Or she should have. Instead, the woman was vibrating with restless energy, her dark eyes fixed on Hela’s purse– where they’d stashed Rio’s phone, rerouting Lilia’s messages to Hela’s own. Rio’s jaw twitched, fingers drumming against the armrest, the other hand rubbing her chin– a nervous habit she’d picked up from her wife. Hela exhaled as a dinosaur’s roar shook the theater, the screen flashing red with a kill. She didn’t care. 

Her focus was on one thing : praying to every god she knew, Just let us survive this movie. Let us hit the five hour mark. That’s all. Five hours without Rio losing her damn mind . 

She glanced sideways– 

The seat was empty. 

Popcorn littered the floor. 

Hela’s blood turned to fire. She shot up, footrest still raised, hands gripping the seat like she could crush it. Alice noticed first, sitting upright. 

“Oh, come on–” 

Rio was gone. 

Ignoring the angry shushes from the audience, all three of them bolted for the exit.



Rio

 

Rio exhaled the breath she hadn’t realized she’d been hoarding, lungs burning as fatigue rolled through her in tremors. Her body pulsed with the raw sting of effort– sweat sliding down her temples, her pulse thudding like drums of war beneath her skin. She glanced down at herself, catching the sharp scent of her own exertion, and grimaced. She soaked her shirt again, sweat dripping down her back, covering her in stink. Despite it being late, the sun starting to set, it felt worse than a Puerto Rico July. Maybe she was exaggerating or maybe she was growing used to the moody weather of the Northeast. Maybe it was because she had been skipping her main gym workout. 

She hadn’t worked out in weeks, not really. Unless you counted carrying every heavy thing in the house– moving the crib for her wife, building the new book shelf because of course they didn’t have enough bookshelves, and building a toy chest made from mahogany wood while her wife sat with her in the garage. It was better than building in the green house, but it still led her to be running up and down the steps for her and her wife– fetching water, wool for her wife to crochet or knit or whatever, books, her phone– all because she wanted to sit with Rio even if they didn’t speak… Rio’s heart leapt at that. But it wasn’t like she was totally inactive. She strolled with her very pregnant wife on walks in the park or in their yard and coaxed her hips open in yoga poses and breathing exercises– and, oh, yes, if you counted sex. Rio definitely did. She deserved a goddamn medal just for lifting Agatha off the floor and carrying her all the way to their bedroom without trembling.  

The theater her friends kidnapped sat across from the mall, a sea of asphalt between the two buildings. And the store? It was all the way on the opposite end of the mall. But Rio’s mission was crystal clear, heart hammering with urgency as she was pretty sure she knocked a kid over. 

Oh well.  

Not her kid. 

The store was closing in ten minutes, but Rio only needed two– three tops. She’d seen it earlier, the onesie. Spotted it before her utterly clueless coven had yanked her away, ignoring her protests saying it was just one thing. Which it was and she hadn’t forgotten. If anyone told Rio in the past that something could pull her away from watching her favorite franchise, she would have laughed until she threw up. But here she was, movie forgotten, ignoring the ache in her knee, and blowing right past the protest of the store employee letting her know when the store was closing. Nothing was going to stop her. 

She made a beeline for the far wall, where rows upon rows of tiny newborn onesies hung and begged her to pull out the shiny new black credit card. Pastels and florals, soft colours and softer fabrics all staring down at her, begging to be the one that holds her newborn, but Rio’s eyes locked onto one in particular. It was white with caramel coloured spots, delicate to the touch and so familiar. She lifted it as if it were made of glass instead of wool and cotton, brushing the sleeves between her fingers. She found the little legs sleeves were wrapped on the bottom, little built in socks with soles of patched pink paw prints. Her breath caught as across the belly was a soft patch of wool, giving way to white and caramel fabric. Her hands moved before her brain could catch up, lifting the tiny hood, and there they were– two wool bunny ears, the tips dipped in caramel. The sight hit her with a strange glowing force, pounding into her heart and twisted it where her face was hurting with how strong her smile was. 

Next thing she knew, she was stepping out of the store where Hela, Alice, and Jen were waiting, rolling their eyes at the bag in her hand before turning on their heels back to the truck. Rio followed, humming The Ballad of the Witches’ Road under her breath as she practically skipped back to the parking lot. 

On the ride home, she made them promise not to say anything to her wife. It was surprise, one where the moment her front door opened, she bolted to their room, tucking the onesie into a go-bag before coming back out to the living room, finding her wife exactly where she left her : asleep on the couch, cradling her belly as her heavy breathing filled the room. Rio didn’t hesitate. She moved with practiced ease, sitting beside her wife, and guiding her wife’s head into her lap. Agatha moved in her sleep, a sleepy smile ghosting her lips as she nuzzled into Rio’s thigh before falling deeper into another realm, dreaming of what was to come. 

 

 

Chapter End Notes

Also, sorry for taking so long. My dog is super old and needs some extra love between getting up and down steps and attention.

For those of you who saw my post on twt, she is okay. She has lived a long, healthy, and loving life so far. I am cherishing these days I have left with her.

Ironically, life really hit today. Today, my last goat passed on to the next life. Rest easy, buddy. He was the oldest goat we have ever had. He lived over 18 years, gorging on fresh grass and any of our vegetable left overs. Just wanted to share.

Finally, out curiosity and because I would love to hear from you all, what are your theories for how the birth will go with Nicky ? Keep in mind he WILL be alive at the end !

Chapter 29: I Hear a Symphony

Chapter Summary

Agatha’s water had broken.
She was in labor.
Their son was coming.
Hospital.

OR

They spend a day with Oriana and then two weeks later Nicholas decides to come into the world a little bloody and raw

Chapter Notes

First off, HUGE THANK YOU to futureghostie23- a labor & delivery nurse who reached out to me and answered all of my questions so I can bring this scene as accurate as I could to you all !!! I learned a lot of terrifying things and also that amniotic fluid smells like cum, so you guys have to know that too. Huge shout out to the mamas here reading this, respectfully, you all are terrifying for doing this, but I know its worth it in the end. 💚💜

Big thank you to my Spanish translator : Xoi_xoxo thank you queen 💚💜

Okay, buckle up, keep in mind of the new tags. Can't believe this part of the story is coming to a close !!!

reminder that there will be a part two to this, so be sure to subscribe to the series.
Part two : Until I Found You

That being said my darlings,

Happy pride !

Love,
Rioallalong_719💚💜💚💜

See the end of the chapter for more notes

I Hear a Symphony ~ Cody Fry

 

Rio 

 

Rio breathed hard through her nose, flaring it as her lip curled. The black steering wheel in her hands was close to cracking as her grip tightened. Her jaw was clenched, her tongue playing with her cheek as she stared at the road in front of her. For a moment, it felt as if she was on auto-pilot, her body knowing when to stop, when to turn as her mind was elsewhere. Her wife was fearless, insane, and fucking stubborn.
