She was thirty-eight weeks pregnant– round, completely full, and radiating life as if she was a descendent of Gaia herself. Once a week, they went to their appointments, this time with a new OBGYN as their previous one was also on maternity leave. As far as Rio hoped, they had two more : one next week and the one in two weeks in which their son would be gracing them with his presence. Agatha was close. Rio had read all the symptoms : 

  • Nesting like crazy 
    • Agatha ordered new throw pillows for both the upstairs living room and for the basement, bought them a new bed set up where the sheets were just a lighter shade of purple, and the comforter was just a darker shade of green. Rio lost count at how many times her wife refolded the clothes– the baby’s and theirs, how many times Rio moved the crib for her, and not to mention that Rio could no longer find anything in the kitchen anymore despite being the only one who cooked in their house.
  • Lower back pain
    • Agatha’s back was killing her, and no matter what Rio did– back rubs, creams, yoga stretches, and baths– nothing seemed to comfort her wife. 
  • Braxton Hicks Contractions
    • They scared the shit out of Rio. She never knew what to do when her wife froze mid-sentenced or when they were laying together, Agatha would press closer into her, seeking comfort. In truth, Rio didn’t know what comfort she could provide for her wife. All she knew was that when the contractions did start, she was expecting for her wife to claw at her. 
  • The baby dropped 
    • Agatha was carrying low now and from what Rio read, their son’s head was in her pelvis. 

Now, it was just a waiting game and the new doctor had said that she was on tract– nothing was out of place and their baby boy was strong, healthy– heart was powerful and lungs in perfect condition. From what the technician could tell was that Nicholas already had a full head of hair, which had Rio hopeful that he would have her wife’s thick locks. 

Rio wasn’t mad about any of this though. In fact, she would call herself relaxed now, knowing that her and her wife were about to have a healthy baby boy. Agatha was almost in the thirty ninth week stage, meaning they were in full term if she were to go into labor then, for now, she was in early term, and Rio was perfectly relaxed… was. She was more than happy to stay home with her wife for the last few weeks, instead of going everywhere. Ever since the coven dragged her out of the house that day, her wife was dragging her too– dinner dates, lunches with the coven, symphonies that her wife wanted to see, and she even convinced Rio to see the new ‘live action’ How to Train Your Dragon that Rio did want to see. It was fun and it did relax her, but now that her wife was two weeks away from Nicholas, she was only slightly fidgety. 

Yesterday evening, Rio was on the couch with her wife curled up in her arms, her belly completely on top of hers, and her wife’s face buried in her neck. She was asleep already, conejito finally sitting still enough for her to slip off. Friends played in the background, Rio half-listening, only tuning in at the juicy parts like Ross and Rachel fighting in the early seasons when he cheated on her. Rio hated Ross. Never liked him. Never would. She had just gotten to the part where the rest of the gang, all trapped in Monica’s bedroom, were left to eat the wax when her phone rang. Agatha had jumped, her head lifting up, staring at it on the table and allowing Rio to stretch for it before burying her face back into her skin, her hand joining Rio’s on her stomach. She stared at her phone surprised. 

It was her sister. 

“Oriana?” she had questioned when she answered it. 

Her sister had practically squealed in excitement before rambling on about how she moved out of the place she shared with Maria finally and bought a new house. Where? A ten minute drive from her and Agatha. As her sister droned on, Rio was planning when to call their real estate agent. Her wife and she had already talked about getting a bigger house, but that also meant redoing the nursery they both worked so hard on. The plan was already in motion though. They would move when Nicholas was around two, weaned off of breastmilk, and hopefully, easy to handle. Agatha won the fight when it came to the moving people– Rio and her would barely lift a finger and it would go faster– which her wife argued was what they needed for Nicholas if he inherited Rio’s impatience… Rio didn’t fight, but they both knew her wife meant her own impatience. Rio was as patient as a cosmic entity. Her wife… was as impatient as a dragon, especially now. 

Lost in her thoughts, she didn’t even realize her wife had taken over talking, her voice soft, quiet, hot breath against her neck, and then her wrist was being dragged closer to her face as her sister politely asked for Agatha to speak up. 

“Sorry, but yes, we’ll be there after the appointment tomorrow.”

Rio had froze, staring down at the phone as her sister practically squealed, making both of them wince, “Perfect! I’ll have lunch ready– Abuelita’s arroz con gandules and oh, Agatha, I have a pool– don’t worry I did research. You will be fine to swim and Abuelita said it will help with your back by lifting the weight from you.” 

Her wife chuckled, “That would be great, Ori. We will see you tomorrow.” 

“​​Adiós!” 

So that’s how Rio was in their truck, hospital bags packed just in case– despite the doctor saying she was fine today– and her wife humming to Phoebe Bridgers next to her, either completely oblivious or ignoring the frustration rolling off of Rio in waves. Perhaps she was overreacting, and that thought was like a bucket of ice water splashing over her head. If something happened– if Agatha went into her labor– Agatha would communicate with her, or Rio would easily read it off her body language. Despite what others thought, Agatha was a fairly easy read… well…

Okay, despite being with her wife for almost fifteen years, when her wife put up her walls, it was a little more difficult… but Rio has been doing fine for now. But if she knew one thing for sure about her wife– Agatha Harkness didn’t make anything easy. 

As she pulled up into the driveway with the address of her sister’s new home, a hand fell onto her thigh, caressing her softly. Turning off the truck, her eyes glanced over and immediately, the bricks she carried on her shoulders crumbled. Blue eyes were soft, warm, calming– enchanting her to breathe normally as her eyes wandered her wife’s figure. The braid Rio braided that morning sat heavy on her shoulder, moving over heavy breasts, and resting over the large baby bump that sat lower than usual, even jutting out slightly. The pool would do wonders for her wife’s back, and part of Rio wondered if that was the only reason why they were here– Agatha using her sister… it was so her wife that the very thought made her chuckle as her hand smoothed over the bump, her cheeks stretching feeling the excited kicks from within. 

“ Cálmate, mi amor. He still got two weeks left to cook in there,” her wife smiled, making Rio’s gaze land on the crinkles of the corners of her eyes. 

She wanted to kiss them, instead, she met her wife’s lips before getting out of the truck. She hurried around, lifting her wife out with ease, placing her on her feet before smoothing out the purple sun dress– a black nylon swimsuit peaking out from underneath. Agatha leaned against her, a habit she had picked up lately, which Rio didn’t mind. If it brought her comfort, then so be it– her wife already slept with her stomach completely on Rio– and all night, right along with her wife until she was asleep, she felt their son doing target practice on her wife’s organs. 

 

Oriana was quick to greet them outside, ushering them in. Inside, the interior was a mix of blues and beige, the walls decorated with shells and paintings of bays filled with ships, seagulls, and even the classic white and red lighthouse. Rio and Agatha eyed each other as Oriana droned out, squealing when showing them something exciting, and humming to herself in the halls as she even showed Rio her bedroom. With a language of their own, Agatha and Rio judged in silence– Oriana’s house looked as if a Pinterest board called “Sanctuary of the Sea: Sea House Decor” threw up in it. And yet… as they did judge, covering their mocking laughs with coughs, or her wife saying it was the baby– they were secretly proud. 

They knew– Rio especially– how controlling Maria was, and living with Maria– forget about even having an opinion. Growing up, the twins shared everything: a room, clothes, makeup, shoes– anything you can name they shared. When they went to college, they were roommates, and after college, they got a house together… but Maria decorated everything– even Oriana’s own room in the house. So, for the first time, Oriana was able to choose for herself. 

“I was even thinking of making one of the guest rooms a playroom for my sobrino, ” her sister smiled as they headed to the back porch. 

Her wife stiffened beside her and Rio didn’t even have to look at her wife to know her maternal, overprotective instincts were firing. 

“That sounds perfect, Ori, but don’t change your home for us– plus he is not even here yet. We aren’t even sure what he will like.” 

Her sister’s eyes still sparkled, becoming golden thanks to the sun as her wife squeezed her hand, staring at the pool excitedly. 

“Whenever, of course, Riocita,” Oriana nodded and clapped her hands together, squealing with excitement. “Ohh, I can’t wait to see him. He’s going to come out looking like you, Riocita, but I hope those blue eyes, Agatha– but if he’s Rio’s copy-and-paste, he is going to love dragons and dinosaurs. He will roll in mud, bring you bones he finds in the woods probably– Abuelita used to scream when Rio found skulls and brought them home–”

“You did what ?” her wife hissed, swirling around to level her with a sharp glare. 

 Rio chuckled low in her chest, rubbing the back of her neck like a child caught red-handed. 

“They were cool skulls. One was a snake. The other might’ve been an iguana. And I washed them.”

 “With what, bleach and bad decisions?” Agatha snapped, though the edge in her voice was dulled by the amusement twitching at the corner of her mouth. 

“Don’t give her that look, Agatha,” Oriana piped in with a smirk. “She used to swear they were dragon bones. Would cry if you said otherwise.”

 “And I was right,” Rio grinned, leading her wife down the stone path toward the patio lined with a glass table, loungers, and softer seats. “You’ve just never had vision.” 

Agatha snorted and leaned into her, holding Rio’s arm for balance as they stepped into the shade of a wide umbrella. Her breathing had grown more labored in recent days, each motion slower, more deliberate. The odd summer heat in spring made the stray hairs cling to her sweat streaked neck, pooling there before a few drops ran from her forehead. Rio gently nudged a lounger with her foot and sat her wife down, kneeling to remove her sandals.

“The water should take some weight from you, mi amor ,” she murmured, already rubbing lotion up her legs. 

Her wife didn’t reply, just tilted her head, watching the few clouds in the sky, but flinched when the cool cream was placed on her belly. Rio rubbed it in, smiling as her son took the opportunity to push against her, almost as if he could also feel how cold the lotion was compared to the sun. Once her wife’s delicate pale skin was lathered in protection, she guided her wife to the shallow end. Both of them grew silent as Agatha slipped into the water with a low sigh of relief. The tension in her shoulders melted almost instantly, her body floating into weightlessness, the braid half sinking, half floating– long and delicate like a mermaid’s.

Rio crouched at the edge, arms crossed on her knees, watching every flicker of ease that returned to her wife’s face. Her gaze lowered, staring at the crack of her breasts peaking from the black bikini top and before her eyes could travel more– water drenched her, soaking her clothes that she had over top of her own black and white checkered bikini. The water was a wake up call… a really rude one as she spit out what had fallen into her mouth. 

“ Ay Riocito– I will have a repeat of that morning in my own pool. Stop eye-fucking your wife,” her sister scolded her as Agatha laughed, the sweet harmony echoing around the backyard. 

Despite being closer to her mid-thirties, Rio was always a child at heart. Quick as a flash and faster than thought, she reached behind her, grabbed Oriana’s ankle, then snatched at her sister’s waist, fingers curled into the hem of her t-shirt. With a single practiced tug, she yanked her down into the water. A great wave erupted, dousing them all. Agatha threw her head back, laughter snorting bright and unrestrained over Oriana’s sputtering protests. Rio leaned back on her hands, the sun glinting off her wet skin, watching her sister rise like a furious sea creature. Oriana’s neatly cropped pixie cut now clung to her forehead in damp defeat. 

Wiping her eyes just in time for another splash to hit her full in the face, Oriana barked, “Still a fucking child, hermanita .” 

Rio merely stuck out her tongue in reply, raising a finger, and drawing another snort from Agatha, who now reclined in the water like a queen in her court, amused and untouchable. Rio watched her, seeing the water lift her, cradling so carefully as her belly floated with ease. Rio went to move, to the t-shirt clinging to her skin so she could wade in towards her wife. Instead, a wave crashed into her before the air vanished. Chlorinated water filled her mouth and nose. Her limbs thrashed as her lungs screamed, her sister’s surprisingly strong hands pushing her under with a vengeance honed by years of actual sibling rivalry. But Rio was stronger and with ease, she gripped Oriana’s waist, lifted her life dead weight, and slammed her back beneath the surface as she surged up, gasping. Laughter clawed up her throat between coughs– but her sister was already back, dragging her under again, making her nose burn. 

For a moment, the world became a swirl of bubbles and muffled joy, the rhythm of breath and water blurring into chaos. At last, Rio couldn’t take it anymore, her lungs protesting as she lifted her sister off of her, throwing her away from her. She pushed off the bottom of the pool like a seal, gliding toward the queen who floated with her hands holding her stomach, watching her court jesters with an amused smirk. 

Rio came behind her, arms around her waist as she pulled her back against her chest, kissing her neck softly as her sister surfaced, gasping for breath. She smirked, happily using her wife as a double human shield, knowing it would work as Oriana approached. 

“Splash me, and you’re not meeting your nephew for a year and that pixie cut ends up floating in the pool,” her wife snapped, her eyes blazing at her sister who raised her hands up in surrender. 

“ Mierda, no wonder she tamed you, hermanita,” Oriana grumbled. 

Her wife only raised an eyebrow unimpressed as Rio chuckled, “Well, she has no problem splashing me in–” 

An elbow found her stomach, taking the breath from her. She clung to her wife tighter, hiding from pointy elbows as her wife happily leaned back against her, “Finish that sentence, my love. Go on.”

Rio didn’t fall into the taunting, knowing to keep her mouth shut as her sister chuckled underneath her breath. 

Agatha only groaned, rubbing her stomach, “He senses your stupidity, by the way. He was perfectly still before.”

Her sister smirked, floating out like a starfish, “Just wait till you two give him a sibling. It will be pure chaos.”
“It already is,” her wife huffed.

 

***



Agatha

 

She leaned back in the pool, the weight finally off her back as her belly had her float like a buoy. She bobbed up and down slightly and only left the pool to use the bathroom or when her sister-in-law brought food. The flutterings and kicks underneath tight skin eased up, her son only reacting when his mami laughed or was close by, talking to him softly. But her plan was working out perfectly. Although Rio had loosened up, not as freaked out as she forced her and her wife to go out on dates, each time getting less and less protests and excuses, Rio needed someone to remind her to let go. Oriana was perfect for that… plus she had a pool. So win-win. 

And now, everything was explicitly going to plan. She floated in the water as the sun began to set and up on the patio her wife and Oriana were talking lowly, Agatha pretending she wasn’t listening to them. 

“ Lo estás haciendo bien, Riocita. Tu esposa tiene suerte de tenerte. ” (You are doing fine, Riocita . Your wife is so lucky to have you.)

Rio shook her head, swallowing hard, “Pero, ¿Y si no suy suficiente para ella o él? Ori– ”  (But what if I’m not enough for her or him. Ori–)

Her older sister snapped, “ A la mierda, Rio– ¿si quiera te escuchas? Tienes cuatro malditos bolsos para el hospital preparados, tu esposa me dijo cuántas aplicaciones tienes en el teléfono, y no quiero saber cuántos cuadernos tienes llenos de anotaciones.  ¿Qué? ¿Acaso contactaste a una enfermera privada también– ¡No es una invitación para hacerlo! ” (Fucking hell, Rio– do you even hear yourself? You have four fucking hospital bags packed, your wife told me how many fucking apps are on your phone, and do I even want know how many notebooks your probably have filled with notes. What? Did you contact a labor and delivery nurse too– That is not an invitation to do so!)

Oriana took a deep breath, sitting up, but not touching her little sister, a boundary finally listened to. 

“ Riocita, vas a ser una madre tan increíble, solo necesitas tomar aire y calmar el maldito culo. No va a ser fácil, maldición, tú sola casi volvías alcohólica a Abuelita. Pero tú y tu esposa son un gran equipo. Se tienen la una a la otra y eso será suficiente. Tendrán sus altos y bajos– momentos dónde ninguna de las dos estará segura de que lo que hagan es perfecto– pero ese es el punto... ambas intentarán ser perfectas para él y eso es todo lo que necesita– que ustedes intenten. Pero no hasta el punto en que mueras del cansancio. Solo respira, hermanita. Y cuando Agatha este dando a luz estarás ahí para ella. Solo llévala a tiempo al hospital.” ( Riocita , you are going to be such an amazing mother if you just take a deep breath and chill the fuck out. It’s not going to be easy– hell, you alone could have sent Abuelita into drinking. But you and your wife are such an amazing team. You have each other and that is what is going to be enough. You will have your ups and downs– moments where you both are not sure what you are doing is perfect– but that’s the whole point… you both will try to be perfect for him and that’s all he could ever want– is for you both to try. But not to the point where you tire yourself out. So just breathe, little sister. And when Agatha goes into labor, you will be there for her. Just get her to the hospital in time.)

They both laughed at the end, but Agatha’s stomach dropped at that. 

The hospital. 

Something rose inside her, a feeling that clawed at the very recesses of her mind. She hated hospitals and she thanked her dearest dead mother for that. She breathed out heavily, panic crawling up her chest at the thought of bright fluorescent lights shining down at her as doctors and nurses worked between her legs. She swallowed down the bile that rose, Nicky kicking at her with her nerves rising. Her mind flashed back to the lonely nights left there. Her mother never came with her, leaving her alone to the white coats… okay, yeah– she definitely had white coat syndrome– unless her wife was in a white lab coat and then she would happily jump her. But she couldn’t bear the idea of sitting in a hospital, pacing under too many watchful eyes, too many people probing at her vagina– even more compared to when her and Rio got their bone marrow and then their eggs ready– at least her wife would carry the next. A lab somewhere had both her eggs and her wife’s eggs fertilized with each other’s bone sperm in a freezer. 

Agatha’s eyes narrowed as the sensation didn’t leave her. Conflict made her eyebrows crease as her son fluttered beneath her skin. She knew she had to go– Hela already talked about the wonders of epidurals and drugs. She would need them– and she was not going to have her son rip open her vagina without them…but that also meant telling her wife when she was in pain and Agatha hated admitting pain. Again, blame it on the dead cunt of a mother. She half wished her mother was cremated so she could flush Evanora’s remains into the city sewer. But that would be offensive to the sewer. 

Her wife was still talking Spanish with her sister, but she seemed calmer, her shoulders no longer tense, her whole body falling into the chair instead in a position where she was always ready to leap up for her. Something in Agatha softened, the feeling gnawing at her quieting as she stared at the way her wife tilted her head, absentmindedly playing with her fingers– she was everything. Maybe, for once, Agatha would be alright going to the hospital as long as Rio was there by her side. And she knew her wife would rather die than to leave her. Perhaps that could be enough. 





Two weeks passed in a blur for Agatha. Her wife was hovering less, but there when she needed her, and oh how it made Agatha’s heart ache in such a way as she watched her wife slowly step into a place of calm and readiness. She was no longer frantic, no longer always on the edge of the seat, and she finally listened when Agatha told her when she was fine. She still was there when Agatha needed her ankles or back rubbed, still glued to the bump when she could, talking to their son, singing to him in hushed Spanish lullabies. She still held her when she slept and gave up whatever she was doing the moment Agatha raised a hand at her to join her in bed or on the couch. She didn’t press when Agatha was moody and waited to be told what to do when the baby kicked too hard or Braxton Hicks hit her. Usually, Agatha would send Rio away in the middle of the practice contractions, but the moment they were gone, she demanded that Rio hold her tight, which she did without question every time. 

Oriana’s talk with her wife worked… maybe a little too well. 

 

It was two days after her due date, May sixteenth– and no sign of conejito making his appearance. The OBGYN even commented that she hadn’t even started to dilate but not to worry. Agatha fucking did– more-so, she wanted him out. She was tired, her back still hurt, and she wanted to see her feet. She already made her wife trim her down there to feel more comfortable. Every position was uncomfortable, not to mention she was peeing all the damn time. She refused anyone from coming over to their house, even Lilia– but Rio wasn’t safe from her either. Although her wife never strayed far, Agatha couldn’t help but yell as if she were across the house. Her wife would simply walk in with that stupid ass puppy dog look, kiss her sweetly, before doing whatever she grumbled out at her wife. 

Rio, her sweet darling Rio, who really only wanted to help, tried suggesting sex or masturbating– a natural way to induce labor according to every gods be damned website, app, and their OB. For once, Agatha didn’t like that idea. She didn’t like the idea that it was only to get him out– and she really couldn’t get in the mood for even Rio’s mouth…

And yet, even when Agatha lasted another day without contractions or their son ripping his way through her vagina, Rio was calm. Maybe it was because Agatha was not. Maybe that helped, something clicking in her wife’s brain that the moment Rio started freaking out with her, Agatha would actually lose it. Instead, Rio was just a call away or really right there, watching her mess with the nursery, pace the hallway before sitting down and passing out wherever she pleased. She basically hand fed Agatha food, trying to get her to eat when it felt like her nerves wouldn’t let her. 

So, May sixteenth was no different… except it was, because the pain rolling through her definitely was not Braxton Hicks. 

It had been happening all day, and yet she kept trying to convince herself they were just Braxton Hicks. They were uneven, neither of them lasting the same amount of time, but they grew stronger, each cramp tightening her stomach, making her breathless as it traveled around her midsection. She stayed somewhat hidden in their window seat, holding onto Señor Scratchy or a pillow, muffling her groans from her wife who was now currently making dinner for them. 

She had time, she knew she did. Agatha had taken Rio’s phone, going through her apps and double checking about the 5-1-1 rule : contractions five minutes apart, they are about one minute long, and the pattern had been consistent for at least one hour. From what she timed they were only thirty to four seconds long and well, there was more than twenty minutes between them… well, now there was only fifteen minutes between this one and the last. 

“ Mi amor?” a voice made her snap her eyes up as the contraction eased off. 

She met chocolate ones glowing down at her with patience and worry. Rio was in her normal sweats and a green wife-beater shirt, her hair pinned in a claw clip out of the way. Behind her the table was already set, a candle glowing low and warm tea steeping. And yet, Agatha could barely concentrate on the perfect smell of beef and thyme that was filling their home. It felt as if her insides were tearing apart. 

“Mm,” she hummed, stretching out lazing as if she were trying to get comfortable– but that was futile. 

Her wife cocked her head, stepping forward to sit on the edge of the green cushion, “Dinner is ready. Do you want to eat, cariño ?” 

Agatha took a deep breath in, able to calm herself as the contraction passed. She was fine. He was fine. They would be fine… unless it happened in five minutes. She reached forward, allowing her wife to stand her up before taking her hand and leading her to her seat where she sat down, staring at the potatoes, roast, and noodles. Before she could move to serve herself, Rio already had the ladle, making, luckily, a small bowl for her to eat at first. 

“Just small bites, mi amor,” her wife murmured before yawning. 

Right, Rio was just as exhausted as she was. They both have been surviving on barely any sleep. When Nicholas kicks her bladder, Agatha is jolted awake and hurries to the bathroom which in the process wakes up her wife, no matter how hard she tries to be quiet or not jostle the bed while simultaneously trying to make it in time. Her wife always woke, Rio reaching out to her the moment she stepped out of the bathroom to hold her. And when she would fall asleep, she was sure their son was kicking Rio, keeping her up a little longer before falling asleep and the cycle repeating itself. Only a few times during the day did Rio nap, but her gaze was always on her, always worried, but calm. 

Agatha reached across the table, finding Rio’s hand and threading their non-dominant fingers together, grounding herself in the quiet warmth of skin and gold. The dim light caught her wife’s wedding band, making it glint like a small star between them. Rio blinked slowly, owlish and tired, her fork idly stirring the food in her bowl without lifting it. Agatha lifted their joined hands to her lips, brushing a tender kiss across her knuckles. 

“My love,” she murmured, voice low and sure, “you need to sleep tonight.” 

Rio straightened, blinking out of her drowsy haze. 

“So do you,” she countered gently, always ready to match her in stubbornness. 

Agatha smiled softly, shaking her head. 

“We both know your son won’t let me,” she said, her tone almost amused.

 She took a bite, chewed, and spoke around the weariness they both carried like extra limbs. 

“One of us needs to be rested for when he finally decides to arrive. Please, my love. I promise I’ll wake you when it’s real.”

Rio nodded slowly, accepting the truce. She drew her hand back to rest it against Agatha’s belly, her palm open, fingers splayed as if trying to commune with the quiet storm beneath the skin. 

“He’s taking his time,” she murmured. “I thought he would’ve been here by now.” 

Agatha gave a crooked smile, eyes heavy-lidded but warm. 

“Who’s ever truly ready to face this world?” she reached to rest her hand over Rio’s. “At least he has you.” 

Rio looked up, meeting her gaze. “He has us, cariño– ” 

Thump . 

The sound cracked through the air like a punctuation mark. They both turned their heads toward the floor, where Señor Scratchy sat, twitching with conviction. A breath of laughter escaped them at the same time. Agatha watched as the rabbit bounded forward, pausing beside her chair with theatrical determination before thumping again– louder this time, pointed. The message was clear: something’s coming . 

“Fine, you too, Señor,” her wife said dryly. 

But the rabbit wasn’t done. He rose on his hind legs, tiny forepaws pawing at her lap, eyes fixed with eerie focus on her belly. His nose twitched in an urgent staccato. Then he tried to leap into her, frantic and unwavering. Agatha went stiff. 

He knows . 

Her hands hovered like she might push him away– or confess. Because Scratchy was about to blow her cover. He knew something was shifting, and he was seconds away from outing her to Rio before she was ready to say the words herself. 

He was frantic, bunny nose twitching before looking straight up at her wife, as if to say, You dumb bitch, she’s in pain.

Carefully, Agatha eased him back to the floor. Rio narrowed her eyes, sighing as she bent to reach for him.

“What’s up with him?” 

The rabbit thumped once more, indignant, his fluffy body vibrating with determination. His ears stood tall, every inch of him alert and bristling with warning.

Agatha sat up straighter, flicking the braid behind her, “He probably smells the carrots, or he’s not getting enough attention lately. Maybe you should put him in his pen.” 

Her wife stood, scooping up the rabbit, but not before he could land another thump in protest. 

Shithead, she’s going into labor soon, he would say if he could speak. 

To Agatha’s luck, the rabbit couldn’t and exactly then– seventeen minutes after the last– a contraction hit. Agatha dropped her fork, letting it clatter into her bowl, bending over slightly as she breathed through it. Her eyes were on her wife though, who walked Señor Scratchy into the living room where they moved his pen into. He squeaked, fighting Rio but it provided her enough time to compose herself and start eating again. 

When Rio returned, she was none the wiser. Their hands intertwined as they ate, drifting in and out of soft-spoken dreams– murmured plans of the future, too delicate to name aloud. Afterward, they cleaned up together, Stevie Nicks curling through the air like incense, her voice settling into the corners of their quiet kitchen. Agatha dried the dishes, humming under her breath, hiding the contraction that rolled in thirty minutes later. But it was fine. Nothing to worry about. It wasn’t active labor. Not even early labor. Too short, too spaced out. And so, with practiced denial, Agatha filed them neatly away: Braxton Hicks. Again. Still. 

Everything is fine, nodded to herself in her head . Just strange Braxton Hicks. Not labor. No hospitals yet. 

Then came the first few notes– eighth notes of a piano– echoing into the air like a breath held too long. They both paused, glancing toward each other as they set the last dish down. 

Keane’s voice rose like a memory : “ I walked across an empty land. I knew the pathway like the back of my hand… ” 

Without a word, Rio reached out, her hand settling low on Agatha’s waist, steady and warm. She drew her in gently. Agatha let out a soft laugh, her rounded belly pressing between them as she looped her arms around her wife’s neck. Together, they swayed, singing quietly into the space between their lips. Normally, Rio would twirl her, dip her, lift her off her feet with that effortless strength and delight– but not tonight. Tonight, she simply held her close, like gravity had chosen to settle between them instead of the earth. Their lips met in the middle, tender and sure, their own language unfurling between each brush of mouth to mouth. The music dissolved into a hum behind them, as if even the song had stepped back to give them room. Agatha tilted her head, deepening the kiss, noses brushing, breath shared. Her body responded instinctively– heat blooming through her chest, her heart pounding so loudly it seemed to ricochet inside her ribs, searching for Rio’s in the dark. 

But just as her tongue pushed into the kiss, another ‘false’ contraction gripped her abdomen. Her breath caught. She gasped against Rio’s mouth– but masked it with a moan, too smooth to question. Her tongue flicked hastily into the kiss, tangling with Rio’s, trying to ground herself in sensation instead of pain. She took a shaky breath, pulled away just enough to swallow, then leaned back in, lips crashing into hers once more, tongue sweeping deep, searching for something to hold on to. Rio made a soft noise in return, her hand sliding up to cradle the back of Agatha’s head.

And then it came in almost as sharp and fast as the Braxton Hicks, pooling low in her belly– desire. She tugged her wife closer, moaning softly into her mouth as they tasted each other like they were starved. Their tongues battled and danced a rhythm only they knew, speaking easily as Agatha’s mind raced. 

Why did ever think she didn’t want Rio’s mouth on her? Rio’s mouth and tongue were blessed by the gods. What the fuck was wrong with her? 

She needed her now, and there was only one way to be absolutely sure she would get what she wanted. Her mouth worked against Rio’s before moving down caramel skin, tasting her before her teeth were scraping, biting into that perfect jaw line and leaving indents. Her wife gasped against her before moaning lowly as Agatha took her tongue, licking one long stripe down her neck. 

She had her.

Large black pupils stared down at her, her mouth hung open where she could just see the bottom of those gaped teeth. She grabbed Rio’s hand, guiding her to their bedroom. Just as she turned around, lips were on her, hands on her hips before going to her stomach. She sighed, her head tilting back, lips were on her neck, teeth nipping and tongue soothing. Her own hands travelled her wife’s body, moving under the tank, her fingers clawing down the ripple of abs. Rio groaned low in her throat, a sound that came from deep inside. 

“You’re trying to kill me,” she murmured, dragging her mouth along Agatha’s collarbone. Agatha tilted her hips just enough to tease. 

“If I was trying,” she whispered. “You’d already be dead.” 

Rio’s laugh was breathless, hands gliding down over the swell of Agatha’s belly. She rested them underneath it, half holding it as her lips glided back up again, slow, sure. Hands moved underneath the oversized band tee, her mouth hovering at the edge of a moan. 

“You’re sure, mi amor ?” she asked, voice rough, but her touch was already moving, pulling at the hem of her panties. “It’s just you said–” 

Agatha grabbed her chin harshly, forcing her wife to look up at her, “If I ever say I don’t want your mouth on me again– double check that it’s not more than pregnancy brain.”

Her wife nodded, swallowing hard as her very finger tips on her naval sent sparks up through her body. Agatha smirked, pulling at Rio’s tank before she complied, taking it off to reveal her bare chest. Her hands reached up, squeezing at her wife’s breasts, her fingers spreading in a V, and squeezing a hard nipple in place. Chuckling as her wife moaned, she felt her panties being peeled down her legs, the gusset cold and wet, caressing down her inner thigh before landing on the ground with a soft thud. She wasn’t given time to breathe before fingers were working through her labia, slipping through easily as her own hands grasped for purchase– already so close to the edge.  

Agatha laid a hand on her wife’s chest and panted, “Not inside, love.”

Hands went to her waist again, walking her backward until the back of her knees were hitting the bed. Rio was kissing her like it was their last, her fingers playing with her swollen clit as she moaned into her mouth. Agatha’s mind blanked, everything becoming hazy as pleasure took over. Rio didn’t let her sit though, not yet– the hand on her waist moved to her back, holding her in place as her fingers worked. Her head rested against Agatha’s when she pulled her mouth away– a line of spit connecting them. Agatha panted and whimpered into her mouth, struggling to keep her eyes open, her legs shaking, but her wife merely lowered the hand on her back, palming her ass to keep her upright and close. 

“Fuck– yeah– Rio, right there– oh my fuck, yes!” 

It didn’t take long. In fact, it was probably the quickest she’d ever come from just clitoral stimulation. Her orgasm hit hard, brutal and fierce, her head tilting back as she clenched around nothing, her wife’s fingers slowing but never leaving– guiding her gently through the aftershocks. Her arms instinctively wrapped around Rio’s neck, holding on as tremors wracked her body, her knees giving out beneath her. That’s when Rio lowered her, slow and tenderly, as if she were spun from glass, easing her onto the bed with care. But Agatha didn’t let go. She clung to her wife, burying her face in the crook of her neck, nuzzling in with a sigh as the last ache dissolved like mist in the morning sun. 

See ? She was fine. 

Warm lips pressed to her temple. A gentle hand found her belly and rubbed in slow, tender circles. 

“Let me take care of you, cariño ,” Rio whispered. 

And who was Agatha to deny her wife that? 

She hummed softly as Rio’s lips traced lower, down the slope of her throat. Her arms slipped around Rio’s back, holding her close, grounding herself in the worship of her body. Her head tilted, offering her neck like an invitation, her moan barely more than breath. 

“What do you want, cariño ?” Rio murmured into her skin, the words a vibration that seemed to ripple straight to her core. 

Agatha pulled back just enough to catch her wife’s chin, guiding her gaze up until their eyes locked. 

“Your mouth, baby,” she whispered, her voice sultry and deep. “Don’t stop until I tell you to.” 

Rio didn’t hesitate. She reached for their pillows, arranging them carefully around Agatha, cradling her like royalty in a throne of down. Her smirk was playful, reverent, but hungry. She peeled the t-shirt from Agatha’s body, trailing kisses across her breasts, her palms cupping and lifting them with care. She pinched her nipples just enough to draw a sharp gasp, Agatha’s back arching high before she sank back into the sea of plush. Her wife hummed in delight. 

Agatha kept moaning, soft and blissful, smiling faintly as she felt how the pillows relieved the pressure from her spine. Rio’s mouth moved with unhurried devotion, lathing at the underside of her breasts, her fingers still teasing and tugging. 

“Mmm, so perfect, mi amor,” Rio hummed, pitching her nipples again. “So big and full–” 

Agatha’s hand struck like a viper, wrapping around her wife’s throat, but not squeezing, just a quiet authority, “What did I say?”

Each word was slow and measured, but controlled as the uncomfortable feeling in her gut rolled in again. It took everything in her not to reveal the silent pain behind her eyes, and she almost breathed out in relief when her wife nodded obediently. Rio descended like a knight ordered by her queen on a mission, her mouth hot and insistent, but kissing gently down the expanse of her stomach before burying her face between her legs. Agatha’s head fell back, her thighs already tight around Rio’s checks as her fingers twisted in black silk. Rio’s mouth consumed her so perfectly, her breath hot as her tongue explored every tiny nerve, lavishing over her, making her whole body grow taunt. She started around her labia, sucking each fold into her mouth before teasing her clit. Agatha’s hips bucked, her whole body flying as Rio swallowed everything she had to give her. 

She whimpered and moaned with each warm stroke of that tongue, hands clinging to her thighs, hanging on to her before Agatha reached for her wife’s hand. Rio took it, squeezing it– something to say she was here– Agatha was safe. The pressure in her stomach was building up quickly– too quickly as the coil became tighter and tighter.

“Just like that– yeah– yeah– uhh Rio!” 

It wasn’t a snap. It was an explosion, the wrong wire cut, causing everything in her to fly. Her head flew back as her thighs clamped harshly around wife’s head. Whatever was left of her nails dug into Rio’s head and hand as she was floating in the Northern Lights– waves of green and purple encasing her body, numbing everything. When she floated down, she felt it, her body squirting straight into her wife’s waiting mouth, and her wife didn’t stop– she just swallowed. 





Agatha woke with a jolt.

A pulse of pain tore through her lower belly, sharp and sudden, dragging a gasp from her lips as she sat up too fast. Her hands flew to her stomach, cradling it, as if she could hold the pain in– keep it from blooming outward like fire on dry grass. She was trembling before she even realized it, the quaking of her body beyond her command. Around her, the bedroom was still and dark, soft with the hush of the witching hour. Beside her, Rio slept deeply, her breathing loud and steady– nearly a snore– after days of sleepless nights keeping watch over her. Agatha turned her head, watching her for a moment. Guilt tangled with fear in her chest, but the pain returned before she could dwell in either. 

This one was worse. It slammed into her like a prizefighter, the kind with cinderblocks for fists. She doubled over, biting back a cry, her braid slipping over her shoulder as she reached up to push hair from her face. The motion made her dizzy. Her legs swung off the bed and touched the floor like they didn’t belong to her anymore. She stood– or tried to. Her knees buckled slightly. Her back curled with the force of the contraction, and she hobbled toward the door, each step unsteady. The robe clung to her thighs, bunched awkwardly as she fumbled for the knob, opened the door, and slipped out into the hall. She closed it gently behind her, leaning heavily against the wall. She breathed through her mouth, desperate to find a rhythm, but her nose felt useless. Nothing about this pain was orderly. It stretched on, a minute that felt like ten, stealing the air from her lungs and the strength from her bones. When it finally let her go, her muscles sagged with relief. But then– 

Warmth. It was lazy, a trickle that ran down her thighs slowly, as if purposely leaving its mark down and down her legs. She blinked down through blurred vision, through tears she hadn’t noticed until now. Liquid was racing down her legs like ran against a widow, darkening the hem of her robe, and trickling to the wooden floor. 

Okay, so I just peed myself. That’s… embarrassing, she thought as she panted, but she was already moving towards the kitchen. Water, rest and wait to see if it’s actually happening, wake Rio. Water, rest, Rio. Water, rest, Rio.

It was a mantra… it was a life line. One that didn’t make sense, one that had to hold on to as the glass in her hand trembled violently under the running water of the sink. She didn’t care as her hand refused to remain steady, shaking the water out from it– splattering to the floor. But that was okay…she was hoping… something… would be cool…

something… 

anything to stop the sweat pouring down her face and her teeth from chattering… 

she was so hot… 

and– 

the glass fell from her hand, shattering to the floor with a loud crash. 

The pain was burning, shooting all around her pelvis, like a thousand tiny lava infused fire ants were eating her from the inside out. She stumbled around the center counter top, freezing as it grew sharper, the pressure too much as she yelled out, leaning back against the counter and clutching her stomach, possibly lower like she could reach inside herself to stop it all. She barely even processed the loud bang from their bedroom, or the slight squeak of a foot sliding on the hardwood. All she knew was that he was coming, and she needed her wife now.



Rio 

 

A loud crash of glass had Rio up in seconds. Her heart was hammering in her throat, choking her. Her hands reached beside her, searching for the woman she wrapped in a robe after she fell asleep right after her fifth orgasm. She had just lifted her head a little after her wife came down, and found her breathing in deeply before snoring. Rio had smiled, maneuvered her wife into a robe before lifting her properly into bed and passing out the moment her head hit pillows. 

But now she was gone, the sheets left to be wrapped around her legs as her eyes looked to the bathroom, before a loud yell made her heart almost stop. She was moving before her brain even caught up, finding her tank and putting it on as she kicked at the sheets. Rio crawled across their bed frantically, crashing to the floor as the sheets acted like a snare. She didn’t care as her shoulder throbbed, her body rolling over itself before she was up, tearing off their bedroom door as she saw the kitchen light was on. She froze for a moment, eyes searching for Agatha before another scream tore through the house. 

Rio stepped in something wet, her foot gliding forward as she steadied herself with the wall. She stared down at the trail on the floor, her breathing becoming hard as her eyes widened.

Agatha’s water had broken.  

She was in labor.

Their son was coming. 

Hospital . 

Rio ran forward, finding her wife leaning against the kitchen counter, clutching her stomach before looking up. Sweat drenched her, her hair was soaked and in wild tangles, the braid lost as her robe hung on her shoulders, no longer tied. Blue eyes were electric, wide with panic as hair covered her face, staring at her. 

“No, no– it can’t be,” her wife groaned. 

That’s all it took.

Rio was before Agatha in seconds, taking her hands, trying to guide her to her feet, trying to get her at least through the front door first so she could grab their hospital bags there. But her wife protested, tears heavy down her cheeks as she screamed this time, piercing Rio’s ears as her grip grew tight enough to break her hands.

“Ri– Rio,” Agatha breathed out before groaning and whimpering. “I want more time– I want more time.”  

Rio felt herself recede, burying her panicking self to the very depths of her being as adrenaline kicked in. She lowered herself to the floor without another word, moving the ends of the robe away and there… 

Oh. 



Oh. 

 

That… 

 

that was a head… 

 

a head full of black hair… 

 

her wife was crowning…

 

Agatha was crowning…

 

Nicholas was coming now. 

 

Rio froze, everything in her body and her breath caught. She couldn’t breathe. She couldn’t think. She tried to remember what to do, anything– but it was all lost. She swallowed hard as her eyes widened, her true, panicked self resurfacing. 

They were supposed to go to the hospital… they were supposed to be in a hospital surrounded by trained nurses. She was supposed to be by her wife’s side, letting her crush her hands as nurses delivered their son in a sterile hospital room with the right equipment. But no. They were here. They were in their home… in their kitchen… with the only training Rio had was online research and TikTok videos. And this… this was her fault, wasn’t it. Sex was a way to induce labor and at first her wife didn’t even want to be touched, and then she did. It was Rio’s fault… her fault… somehow she induced it… if that even made sense. Was it her fault? 

“Please, my love,” her wife groaned above her, the pain in her voice was like a slap in the face. 

She shook out of her spiral, finally able to breathe as she stood, running to their bathroom and grabbing towels as her wife wailed behind her, the moans of pains echoing off the walls and straight into her heart. She didn’t care as she knocked into stuff, pulling their bathroom closet apart as she grabbed the first few towels before running back to the kitchen. Her hands were surprisingly steady as she laid the towels underneath her wife, before rushing towards the sink, stepping over the broken glass as the water grew hot, scrubbing her hands and arms before rushing back to her wife, kneeling in front of her. 

Agatha was trembling, her whole body straining, curling in on itself like a storm pulling into its own eye. Sweat was gathering in the dips of her collarbones, dripping down her breasts and around her stomach as her body started to crouch, sitting back against the wood of their kitchen island. Her eyes were wild, cracking with lightning behind them as they stared down at Rio whose heart was thundering over her own ragged breath. Her hands were damp, but clean as she placed a hand on the junction of her wife’s groin, keeping her legs open as fluids drenched the towel beneath her. Rio sat up on her knees, caressing her wife’s face with her free hand, placing a kiss to the corner of her mouth as blue eyes closed– her wife groaning low in pain before panting. 

“I’m right here, mi amor. I’m here,” she spoke, her voice steadier than would have thought as she brushed the stray strands that stuck to Agatha’s face. “Just breathe like we practiced.”   

Her wife growled low, “Fuck you, you mother fucking cunt! I am– oh fuck!” 

Agatha was swearing at her… good sign. 

She kissed her again, a quick peck before staring down at the head. It was slick with birth, but the hair was there, full and thick. She couldn’t stop the small smile before taking her wife’s hand, easing her to relax for a moment. She led her hand down, feeling the curls that were there. 

Her wife perked up, staring at her in wonder– but the wonder didn’t last as her wife groaned again, screaming as her hands shot out to grip Rio’s shoulders, clutching like a drowning woman. Agatha’s head flew back as Rio placed her hand on the baby’s head, trying to guide him– ready to catch when he would inevitably slip into their world. Her wife cried out again, her knees buckling but Rio caught her, steadying her, cushioning her the best she could with strong arms that weren’t trembling yet, and soft towels that now were ruined with sweat, blood, and everything that made this moment so terrifyingly real. 

“It burns– Rio– my love,” her wife almost crashed her forehead with hers, Rio leaning back in time to stop the headbutt and Agatha cried out again. “I-I can’t– Rio– fuck you–”

Rio held back a smile, “You are, cariño. He’s right here. You’re doing it– almost there.” 

The crown of black hair was slipping further forward now, Rio catching the sight of his little eyes all while checking to make sure he wasn’t turning blue. She felt her own blood thrumming through her body, barely noticing the way her knees bruised and ached as she knelt on the hard floors, keeping fresh towels an arm’s length away. 

“Push when your body says to, mi amor. ” 

Agatha nodded weakly, breathing out hard once before sucking in a breath. And then, she bore down– the kind of push that came from somewhere ancient, somewhere maternal. A scream tore from Agatha’s throat, primal, bloody, and raw, shaking Rio’s very bones and the foundations of the house. Plants curled back as Scratchy thumped anxiously in the background. Her body bowed, staining and contracting as her nails cut into Rio’s shoulders, possibly drawing blood. And for a breath, the world froze, the air thickened as Rio… Rio watched before her very eyes… her and her wife becoming mothers. 

The baby slipped, the umbilical cord attached but not tangled. He was bright pink, wet, and… she blinked… her hands full of him, slippery and small… warm.  She reached for a clean towel, clearing his mouth and nose, and the moment she did, a cry echoed… silencing everything around them. 

Impossible tiny limbs flailed as he continued to cry a bright, but almost angry sound– his eyes wrinkled shut, and his face covered in the slime of birth. Rio blinked again, staring at him. 

Their son. 

Their son. 

He was finally here and all Rio could hear was music, despite how loud he was for such a tiny body. And in that moment, sitting in the kitchen at three o’clock in the morning, Rio’s life felt truly forever changed. Instead of some wayward melody, she heard a symphony. 

Rio was sobbing before she even knew it, the sound mixed between a laugh and gasp as she kissed her son’s downy head before placing him in her wife’s outstretched arms. 

Her wife laughed weakly, cooing at him breathlessly and chuckling tearfully, “Oh, hi.” 

Agatha was quick to take him, holding him close to her bare chest– skin to skin, subsiding their son’s cries. Rio moved, replacing the soaked towel with a new one before easing her wife to settle down, her back against the cabinets. She grabbed another towel, whipping her wife’s legs clean before snapping her head up when she heard his first coo. 

Their eyes locked, a light, melodic silence settling between as she watched her wife and newborn son. Hands cradled his tiny feet, barely filling the palm of her wife’s hand. His head was resting against her breast nuzzling into her warmth as tiny hands reached out, one grabbing onto pale skin– the other, wrapping tightly around a chestnut lock of his mother. 

“Congratulations, my love,” Rio murmured, cleaning her hands before cupping her wife’s cheek. “He’s here. Our son.”

Her wife sighed, her eyes closing as she pressed her face against her hand, weakly kissing her wrist. Rio couldn’t stop the smile as she pressed forward, capturing her wife’s lips tenderly as Nicholas continued to gurgle and coo on her chest. She pressed a kiss to his head again, everything within her sobbing as he pressed closer into his mama. 

“Let me grab more clean towels and call the hospital, cariño,” she murmured against her lips, pressing another kiss before hurrying off. 

They weren’t done. Agatha still had to deliver placenta and the paramedics could take her. She grabbed a couple more towels before finding a phone on their nightstand. Before she could open the emergency part, a low groan made her run back to her wife. 

“Rio– what’s–” her wife’s eyes were wide again, panic creeping around her irises as she pulled Nicholas to her chest.

She knelt down, holding her wife’s legs open, “Just the placenta, mi amor. ”

The placenta came easy, the cord still attached as she wrapped the thing in a towel. The phone was ringing then, 9-1-1 answering as her wife never left her gaze from their son who breathed evenly against her chest, his ear kept close to the only thing he has ever known in his life : Agatha’s heart. 

“Yes, he’s fine, still attached and placenta is out,” Rio spoke quietly into the phone. 

The operator crackled in the silent room, the keys clicking like teeth on the speaker. Then his voice returned, calm and maddeningly steady. 

“Okay, Rio, two ambulances are en route from the hospital. How’s mom doing?” 

She smiled, ready to see those gentle, river blues. She pulled away from the tiny rise and fall of Nicholas’s back to her wife, but something was wrong. Agatha’s eyes were hollow, distant, dim of life. Her body had started trembling and her teeth weren’t exactly chattering, just trembling in place. Her head dipped, listing sideways as if her neck had lost the strength to hold it up.

Rio bolted upright, “ Mi amor ?” 

She pressed her hand to her wife’s cheek. 

Cold. Too cold . 

She tapped. 

“Agatha?”

Slapped, gently. 

No answer. 

The body beneath her sagged, boneless.

“Agatha?!”

Then came the warmth– something unnatural, wet, sticky… wrong. It crept to her thighs, seeping into her sweatpants, slow and merciless, like tea leaves soaking in water. She didn’t want to look because she knew– her whole body knew. But her eyes fell anyway, watching her world turn red as she reached for their son, holding him to her chest as if his tiny breaths alone could regulate hers in the horror movie she stepped into. The towel beneath her wife was soaked through, just a mere base of a puddle as their hardwood tried to take the rest. The red pool was thick, glistening under their kitchen lights, spreading with a horrible calm, and it didn’t stop. 

Rio gasped, clutching Nicholas to her chest with one hand, the other on her wife’s cheek. Her own heart pounded so hard it felt like it might shatter her sternum, her soul begging to be released to reach her wife. 

“Agatha,” she croaked on sobs that rose high into the head winds. “Agatha!” 

Louder and desperate. 

“Agatha– ¡ No, mi amor! Por favor–” 

The phone on the floor lit up, a distant voice yelling at her, mixing in the symphony of her own son’s wails– as if he knew too the grief that was before them. Rio was choking. Her eyes wide and unblinking– jaw clattering as if that alone could stop the crimson pool inching around her.  

Then, her lungs stopped working and something else took over as she shook her wife, trying to wake her up. All the while, Rio watched on in a third person point of view– out her body– watching her life crumble around her as sirens completed the symphony– and paramedics pulling Agatha away before her family could even sing.

Chapter 30: So, Why Don't We Go...?

Chapter Summary

“Rio– Mrs. Harkness-Vidal– please,” a nurse’s voice finally broke through the film.

She stepped towards the nurse, knowing how wild she must look, covered in blood, her hair in tangles, clutching a newborn, “Where is she? Where is my wife?”

OR

does Agatha survive ?

OR

the final Chapter of "Somewhere Only We Know"

Oh, simple thing, where have you gone?
I'm getting old, and I need something to rely on
So, tell me when you're gonna let me in
I'm getting tired, and I need somewhere to begin

"Somewhere Only We Know" ~ Keane

 

 

Rio 

“Agatha!” Rio stormed into the hospital, clutching her wailing son to her chest as her eyes squinted against bright fluorescents. “Where is she? Agatha!” 

 

The ambulance was a blur before that, a mess of lights and too many strangers in her home. She had frozen, her mind going blank, and the only thing she was able to do was soothe the newborn in her arms as her wife was placed on a stretcher and ran out of their house before she could follow. The ambulance left, leaving her standing in the night, the leftover paramedics grabbing her bags and locking up her house before she even knew she was being led into the back of another ambulance. The medics there worked around her, her son fighting in her arms as they maneuvered him, severing him from the last tether to his other mother before wrapping him in a plain white baby blanket. Rio clung to him, her tears pouring down her cheeks as she faintly heard the medic beside her, trying to calm her, trying to coax her as they took her blood pleasure- which she was sure with how high it must be– she should be dead… but if Agatha…

 

Before she could take another step, nurses were surrounding her, and it took everything in her not to jerk her body back feeling a hand on her arm. The infant in her arms was squealing, impossibly tiny fingers clutching into her skin, the other hand latched to her hair, refusing to let go. She couldn’t let him go– she couldn’t– everything in her body was shaking, working twice as hard as her very blood– it felt as if her veins were swelling, ready to pop as the light hurt her head. 

“Rio– Mrs. Harkness-Vidal– please,” a nurse’s voice finally broke through the film. 

She stepped towards the nurse, knowing how wild she must look, covered in blood, her hair in tangles, clutching a newborn, “Where is she? Where is my wife?” 

“Doctors have her– she lost a good bit of blood, and they’re doing transfusions now.” 

Rio shook her head, this time slowly yanking her arms away from a nurse making grabbing hands at her son, “I have to be with her–”

“Mrs. Harkness–” 

She didn’t let her finish, her voice frantic, her eyes pleading with the shorter nurse to understand, “No– no way! You don’t understand. She can’t be alone when she wakes up.”

“I assure you ; we will have doctors present–”

Rio’s eyes grew wide, her heart ready to jump from her throat and run through the damn hospital itself to get to her wife. Her wife couldn’t be by herself, couldn’t wake up alone– and each thought of her waking to no one, sent Rio into a spiral. 

“No, she’s scared– scared of hospitals– please, don’t let her wake up without me there!”

The nurse faltered, just for a moment, as if something in Rio’s voice– hoarse and breaking, so raw it didn’t even sound like pleading, just truth– was sharp enough to fray through the clinical cool of protocol. The baby cried harder, his voice small but unyielding, the same as his mother’s resolve.

“We will see what we can do, but please– let us take a look at him.” 

Rio felt the protest on the tip of her tongue until another nurse was in front of her, her eyes gentle, a simple tilt of her head, “You can come with us. The doctor just needs to check a few things, his lungs–” 

At that, her son screamed harder, his mouth rooting against her upper chest, making some of the nurses smile.

“His lungs do sound strong,” another nurse cooed. 

The younger nurse, blonde and spirited, smiled up at Rio, “Then we will check his heart, see how much love you and your wife put into it. Doc will also redo his vitals and measurements.”

An older nurse stepped forward as Rio’s protectiveness cracked, “Please, dear, will you let us? It’s important for his survival too.” 

With that, she nodded, allowing the blonde to step forward and cradle him into her arms with practiced ease before two others were there, chuckling under their breaths as they unwrapped his tiny fingers from her hair.

Rio was alert as she followed them, her eyes wide but pinned on her son who continued to cry. She followed close on their heels, stalking behind in the shadows like a mountain lion, her heart pounding a ragged rhythm behind her ribs. Every time he wailed, it tore something deeper into her. It felt wrong, her skin itching from the absence of him– of her– and he had only just arrived in the world, still caught in the first echo of cries while his mother had been ripped away from her with no warning, no goodbye, just an orchestra of blood and flashing lights. 

The hallway opened into a quieter wing– soft beeps, warm-toned walls, the kind of hush and sterile reserved for new beginnings. The nurse guiding them slowed, opening a door to a smaller room full of supplies and machines Rio did not have a name for. It smelled of clean linen and something gentler– something like a fresh meadow on a warm spring day… something to calm a newborn, and her own newborn was placed into a tall bassinet before the nurses cleared out, trading places with a doctor who swept in with patience and a tired grace Rio had recognized from her own self. 

“My, he looks amazing for a sudden home birth and those lungs,” she chuckled softly, deep red hair pinned up in a bun, before she turned with a gentle smile on Rio. 

She was older, but the lines were light that carved her face, her eyes a dark blue, that radiant peace, “Hello, I’m Dr. Aubrey Montgomery. You must be Rio– his mother and a very worried wife.” 

Rio would crack a smile, but she couldn’t as her eyes narrowed behind the doctor to her son. Instead, she nodded in greeting– Montgomery turning back to Nicholas. 

“You did a wonderful job, Rio, for the delivery. Did you train or study somewhere?”

She cleared her throat, finding it hoarse and responded slowly, “No– uh, just read a lot on the internet and some books.”

Montgomery didn’t look at her as she smiled, “Well, Rio, you delivered a healthy baby boy– his vitals look great, lungs clear, and his heart is strong. Let’s get his measurements– does he have a name yet?” 

She smiled softly, her mind drifting to the day lilies, “Nicholas, Nicholas Scratch… Harkness-Vidal.”

“Nicholas,” she hummed, smiling at the babe as he wailed even louder. “You are an even nine pounds and five ounces, and twenty-one inches, mister.” 

Rio’s mind was running though, through the blood, through the way her wife just went unresponsive, and her fists curled. She replayed everything, as if her life was recorded on a screen, watching her wife through their dinner, dancing together after doing dishes, and sex… Gods… 

“Is it my fault?” she blurted before she could stop herself. 

This time Montgomery blinked up at her and for a moment, they only listened to Nicholas’s whimpers as he was passed back into her arms. The doctor didn’t smile, didn’t react– her gaze unnervingly steady, making Rio want to grab her by the neck and shake the bitch. 

“No, it’s not your fault. Hemorrhages happen, even with professionals on staff. They’re working on her now and doing everything they can.” 

Rio sat up, holding her son to her chest, “And what if–” 

The door opened, interrupting her as the older nurse was back with a bottle, “Alright, let’s get him fed. You want to feed him, dear?” 

The world stopped, just a second– a second that felt like hours as her gaze turned down to the baby in her arms. Gods , he was gorgeous– he was still pink, but the goo and the birth were wiped away, those fragile wrinkles, and alienated looks gone… just a baby– one clinging to her tightly, nuzzling into her with trusting whimpers. Her son… their son… and a different dam broke through Rio’s walls. Tears ran down her face as her son wiggled, trying to bring himself closer to her, almost as if he was already done with the outside world and wanted to be cradled back inside. 

Rio didn’t notice the doctor leaving, just the nurse kneeling in front of her, murmuring directions, steady hands guiding him on his back in her arm, one wrinkling one cupping under his head and neck until it was placed perfectly against her breast. The white blanket held him but didn’t stop his hands from reaching for her. Rio looked up, locking gazes with the old nurse who smiled at her, holding a tiny bottle for her– it was only two ounces, the amount newborns drink within the first hour of life. She held the bottle up, bringing the nipple to his tiny mouth, and he took it quickly. His cries ceased entirely and a soft suckling filled the room. Then, a hand found her again, not pulling, just holding as once more the world stopped.

Her heart was fluttering, her body flying as she fed the tiny body made of both her wife and her. Her eyes raked over his face, already seeing both of them. His chin was Agatha’s already, a small dimple that would grow– his hair also her wife’s, but the color was an even shade between them : darker than chestnut, but lighter than her raven hair. And then, his eyes opened, and Rio felt her heart skip a beat, more tears running down her face as rich brown eyes stared up at her, really stared up at her. 

Oh. 

Oh. 

It was her . 

Rio was staring down a mini version of herself. 

The hand in her hair moved up, reaching for her face and next thing Rio knew, her head was already bowing, bringing it closer until five tiny fingers, no longer than an inchworm, were against her chin. 

They didn’t grip, just rested there, feeling and exploring his world– so curious already. She closed her eyes, pressing a kiss to his hand and the tears kept falling. She didn’t bother to wipe, just allowed herself to feel everything– feel the pride deep in her chest, the love that was already so strong it made her body hurt, the worry for her wife that felt like oxygen had depleted. She didn’t notice the nurse walk out either– silent as a breath from a candle. The room was only filled with the steady suckling of milk and a few tiny hums from Nicholas, blissful in his mother’s arms. She cradled him closer, her cheek brushing those curls, before inhaling– a mix of milk, a tiny bit of hospital, and something distinctly him and Agatha. 

Nicholas stared up at her, mouth still greedily working the bottle, but slower, drowsy, and trusting– and it was that trust that broke her and rebuilt her all in one breath. And suddenly, she couldn’t stop talking. Her voice came in low, thick with emotion, words trembling but tumbling free softer than an owl learning to sing through the night. 

“ Ay mijito, tu mamá va a estar tan feliz– o bueno, va a fingir estar molesta conmigo, con estos grandes ojitos marrones, pero te enseñaré algo,” she whispered when his mouth opened, allowing her to set the now empty bottle aside, his eyes fluttering. (Oh, my little son, your mama is gonna be so happy– well, she’s gonna feign being mad at me, with these big brown eyes, but I’ll teach you something.)

“Primero, mamá siempre está a cargo. ¿Quieres algo? Tendrás que preguntarle. Si me preguntas te voy a mandar con tu mamá a menos que podamos esconderlo de ella. Pero el secreto, mijito, es que tienes que usar esos ojos marrones– mi regalo para tí. Son su debilidad y juntos podemos hacer que se desmorone– pero ella te enseñará mejor como manipular– como defenderte, como dominar el mundo con solo una mirada– te enseñará a cantar, a leer, a escribir– te enseñará nuestra canción .” (First thing, Mama is always in charge. You want something? You must ask her. Ask me and I will send you to your mama unless we can hide it from her. But the secret, my little son, is that you have to use these big brown eyes– my gift to you. They are her weakness and together, we can make her crumble– but she will teach you how to manipulate the best– how to stand strong for yourself, how to dissect the world with a single glance– she’ll teach you to sing, to read, to write– teach you our song.)

And it seemed, at the mention of his mama, his face scrunched up, his breath sucking in before he let out a few soft, cranky cries. Rio brought him up to her chest, settling his ear against her heartbeat as she kissed his head, taking in his scent. 

“ Ow, yo sé, conejito, ” she soothed, her body on autopilot as she stood, swaying herself. “ Tengo otro secreto para tí– uno que no le puedes contar a mamá. En una de nuestras primeras citas me sentó a ver mi primer musical– The Sound of Music y la manera en la que su cara se encendió– sus ojos azules brillan cuando se emociona– cuando la película empezó, escuché cantar a tu mamá. No le digas pero es lo único que me gusta de los musicales. Su voz es celestial mijito, mejor que la mía, y yo sé que te cantará nuestra canción cada noche antes de dormir, así que, por ahora, te cantaré una canción distinta. Ella merece ser la primera en cantar nuestra canción. ” (Oh, I know, conejito. I have another secret to tell you– one you can never tell Mama. It was one of our first couple dates and she sat me down for my first musical– The Sound of Music and the way Mama’s face lit up– her blue eyes get this extra shine when she is excited– when the movie started, I listened to your mama sing. Don’t tell her, but that’s the only part I like about musicals. Her voice is heavenly, my son, so much better than mine, and I know she will sing you our song every night before bed, so for now, I will sing you a different song. She deserves to be the first one to sing you our song.) 

 

“ Edelweiss, edelweiss 

Every morning you greet me 

Small and white, clean and bright 

You look happy to meet me 

Blossom of snow, may you bloom and grow 

Bloom and grow forever 

Edelweiss, edelweiss… 

You look happy to meet me ”




***

 

He had slept peacefully in her arms as she swayed around the room, trying not to pace as her focus was on him– it had to be on him, or she would break. Eventually, the nurse came back another following with her bags from the ambulance and it was only then that Rio realized she was still in her blood-soaked sweatpants, whatever was on her shirt, and her bare feet frozen against the concrete floor. After convincing her once again to let them take Nicholas, Rio was able to shower and change, luckily bringing her own slides and changing into a different pair of sweats and a crew neck from Salemite’s University.

The moment she stepped out, she rushed to where he was sleeping in a nurse’s arms, the same one who promised she wouldn’t leave the room. She didn’t, thank the gods, and with ease, Nicholas was back in her arms. She clung to him, pressing kisses to his head, humming every song but their song. Her wife deserved that– and something gnawed at Rio that her wife didn’t get the joys of being the first to feed their son. But she couldn’t change it and wouldn’t if it meant both him and her wife surviving– if she survived. 

Another hour went on, and Nicholas was still happily against her chest, listening to her heartbeat. She was in the chair now, her eyes fighting to stay open, murmuring soft Spanish to her son when a knock jolted her upright, staring at the nurse. 

“We are going to do another check on him and then bring you to Agatha. She’s stable, but we had to give her a dose of Ativan when she woke up–”

Rio’s head snapped up, her mouth parting, but for a moment no sound came. Her fingers curled around Nicky, shielding his body from her own raging storm inside her. 

“When she woke up?” her voice cracked low with disbelief, then steadied like a storm brewing over the horizon. “You let her wake up alone? After everything I said?”

The nurse stumbled back for a moment, her professional façade fading for a moment.

“She was agitated, disoriented. We had to calm her down before she got violent–”

Rio rose slowly like a tidal wave, Nicky still held close. “I told you and every single person that she couldn’t wake up alone.” 

She didn’t shout– she didn’t have to. She kept her voice colder than arctic winds, soundless at times, but holding the frozen bitten punch. A tone she only used in classrooms or board meetings. But this time, her eyes glistened, sharp and wet, as she could imagine the pure panic of Agatha’s face realizing Rio and Nicholas weren’t there. And gods, it was hard to breathe. 

“She needed me, not a sedative,” she growled low, Nicky now fussing softly at her chest as though sensing her shift. 

The nurse didn’t argue, just nodded slowly, “I understand. If I may do one last check on him before bringing you to her?” 

Once again, she had to fight herself, handing him as she disappeared down the hall. Minutes felt like hours, the hand dragging itself until the nurse appeared with a few others. She was quick to return Nicky to her arms as the others grabbed her bags for her, the nurse leading them through long halls as Rio held the most precious thing in the world. Yet the world around them held its breath as the nurses kept a distance behind her until the doors opened to the postpartum unit. It was quiet, a few newborn cries making her son twitch against her. She caressed his cheek, soothing him as they made it to the end of the hall, the old nurse opening the door to a private suite. 

Inside, the lights were dimmed only slightly, the blinds pulled to where the coming sun was blocked entry. The room was painted a soothing baby green, a bathroom to the side, and a cot just under the window. A rocking chair sat across from the cot, a basket of leaflets, baby bottles, blankets, and diapers resting in its seat. On the other side of the wall was a bassinet and an island full of more medical supplies, but in the center was a practical hospital bed and her wife, dressed in a pink gown, sleeping soundly against the pillows. Her cheek was turned to the side, facing the window as she laid there half sitting up. Her arms were limp next to her, an IV stuck in one, a bag beside her dripping with fluid. But the color was back in those high cheekbones, her hair a mess behind her, tied loosely in a tail– probably by a nurse. 

She jumped when a hand landed on her shoulder, the old nurse coming beside her and whispering, “When she wakes, she’s alright to start breastfeeding him. There’s a pamphlet in the basket or you can grab one of us if you need help.”

And with that, her bags were set on the cot and the door shut behind her with a deafening click. Yet, Rio couldn’t move– she was rooted to her spot, staring at the strongest woman she ever knew, the love of her life– the woman who now seemed so small with a nasal cannula to help her breathe. But the relief had more tears streaming down her face– her love was alive ; she was here for her and their son.

Just like that, those blue eyes began fluttering open, flinching at the light before a pained whimper left Agatha’s throat. Rio was by her side before her brain caught up, her body moving, a hand caressing her cheek just under the oxygen tubes. Her wife relaxed into it, pressing against the hand as her eyes remained closed for a moment longer. 

“Oh, mi amor, you’re alright,” Rio couldn’t stop herself from whispering, her heart practically cracking when blue eyes became alert– panicked as a hand wrapped tightly around her wrist– but the hand was shaking. “Shhh, está bien , mi amor. It’s me, sweetheart.” 

Agatha visibly relaxed, blinking now before turning her head away, another pained sound leaving her throat. Rio leaned over, peppering her cheeks and head with kisses until her wife relaxed, turning back to her to meet her lips. Warmth spread through Rio as a thumb was on her cheek, wiping away her tears, but instead she felt more on her cheeks, her wife’s mixing with hers. 

“Where is he?” her wife asked the moment they pulled away. 

She smiled, really smiled as stood up straight, helping her wife unsnap her gown as she spoke, “He’s right here– we’ve been waiting, but I think he’s been more anxious than I have been.”

Her wife stayed quiet, her arms staying at her sides as Rio unwrapped him from his blanket, causing a soft cry to echo into the room before placing him on her wife’s chest, skin to skin– and instantly, he quieted, nuzzling into the crest of Agatha’s breast as more tears ran down her wife’s face. Rio was quick to wipe them away, watching her wife slip into motherhood, a hand on his back, the other softly running through his curls. 

“You my dear Nicholas– you were made from scratch,” her wife whispered, pressing a soft kiss to his downy hair before looking up at Rio with blues brighter than Rio had ever seen.

She didn’t waste another second, leaning down and kissing her wife, a hand back on her cheek as their lips met, setting her heart ablaze with something she couldn’t even name. She loved Agatha– had known that since the day Agatha knocked on her door at three am telling her that they were girlfriends. One of the best days of Rio’s life… one of them, because after that was when Agatha told her yes in their rotten apartment during their PhDs. Then, it was their wedding day, then it was when Agatha told her she wanted to have a kid, and after that was when her wife first was pregnant… and now it was this… her wife’s lips against her, their tears mixing together, and their son, clinging to Agatha’s chest– both of them alive, strong, and whom she was so deeply in love with. 

Agatha gasped, pulling away– and for a split second, Rio thought she hurt her. Instead, they both looked down, seeing Nicholas was moving, clumsily on her chest, his mouth working against her– searching, rooting. Rio’s heart fluttered as she kissed her wife’s head before guiding Agatha’s arms around him. Her wife’s breath trembling as Rio murmured soft instructions, helping her cradle their son.
